> Paper C2 - you didn't solve the predator's dilemma, you are a poseur, and you armed the peripherie nobles. It's not enough for workers to be equal, they must be irreplaceable
> Paper V3 - Verany calls Coltbert a deviant
> Paper V4 - After Coltbert reforms start, Verany rails against the fake banking and the lack of economies of scale
> Paper C4 - Coltbert dismisses Verany's concerns about royal notes as the words of a traitor, saying the royal notes are the key to breaking the predator's dilemma while Verany is a soulless, gold hoarding Herzlander wannabe that wants to destroy Aquileian excellence
> Paper V5 - Verany says Coltbert is spreading deviancy, appealing to moral panic
> Paper C5 - Coltbert equates economic liberty with sexual liberty and says Verany is a poseur who only cares about the rigid patriarchy and griffon superiority to control others, just like a warlord. Coltbert says nobles used to treat serfs as interchangeable objects of pleasure, just like Verany treats workers as replaceable cogs. Coltbert treats his suitors as unique and irreplaceable, every creature has asset specificity when it comes to romance and charm. This is proven economically too; farmers and artisans are not replaceable cogs, but have uniqueness that makes them productive.
> **Note 44:** the Predator's Dilemma was solved not by morality, but by the weaponization of Ambition and Asset Specificity
> **Note 45:** All the element spectrums are examples of the predator's dilemma. The elements of harmony are when there are no predators. The elements of liberty are the choice to cooperate instead of conquer.When one cooperates and the other defects, it's predator and prey; you get the element of tyranny. When they both conquer, it's the element of war. Twilight should publish this in her revision.
> **Note 9:** Concepts - Ambition vs Safety
> Ambition was only possible with leverage and asset specificity, not when the serfs are stuck. Having dignity but still being cogs was not enough
> **Note 23:** Dialectics - Predator vs Prey
> Captains are grifters who want to be Herzlander lions/predators. Followers were misled to think they had to be lions because of the example of lions winning. Minette thought she had to be a lion wielding a needle (socially and literally) to survive, dominate, and not be prey. Until Reni shows a refusal to be a brute and that Minette can be safe and graceful with him.
> The 2nd gen royalists grew up safe, so they exist outside the predator vs prey hierarchy. They reject being brute force lions or humble eagles serving the state and instead pursue their ambitions and cultural deviancy (both an Aquileian lion of ambition and Aquileian eagle of grace and charm)
> The noble griffonesses are imprisoned collateral who want respect. They try to be aggressive like their husbands, asserting dominance over their trapped-into-nonviolence husband and hurting their house pony pets. The 2nd gen Royalist stallions respond by saying acting like a Brute is pathetic, but acting like a cultured Queen gets attention and respect.
> **Note 29:** Demographic - Noble Griffonesses vs 2nd Gen Royalist Stallions
> At the beginning, there are only scarce few Royalists brave enough to engage with the nobles, and they have day jobs. Their side duty as an auditor is to report damage to the King so he can demote the warlord for hurting his wife or servants. The wife abuses the servants to punish her arranged marriage husband for past mistreatment. The Royalists assert cultural superiority and shame the griffonesses for being brutes. Once shamed into silence, the Royalists say they can offer intelligent conversation, education, and passion, but they have to ask nicely. The stallions share notes about which griffonesses are willing to negotiate/engage and which are too dangerous. They arrogantly call it a tableau de chasse (hunting log) and think they are hunters taming predators. Once the griffonesses are tamed, they have to compete with each other for attention since the Royalists have day jobs and there aren't many 2nd gen who are around and brazen enough. The griffonesses collect cutie mark stamps and view their tableau de chasse as a trophy shelf. Eventually the market stabilizes as more stallions want in, and hunter/trophy shelf becomes ambiguous. The reality is that they are partners in a positive sum game of cooperation.
>
> The stallions started the list as literal code words in the king's official audit log, which then became off-record tableaus de chasse for tracking conquests/taming
> The griffonesses then start sharing notes to brag and also blacklist stallions who got too power hungry and tried to act like a warlord
>
> The 2nd gen Royalist stallions learn from their friends at the University of Pridea and teach the griffon artisan techniques to the noble griffonesses in part 1 of Coltbert's game. The griffonesses's machine becomes a total declaration of soverienty; the warlord husband can't take the machine because the parts are only enchanted by the griffoness who made the machine!
> **Note 38:** System Mechanics - Sociology - The Lioness Spell
> Under King Gerad Discret, the "cost of conquest" was artificially high because warlords who acted violent would be demoted. Their ambition to be apex was used to tame them by saying being abusive means being a brute (King's Peace) and deserving demotion. Minette used Law and Social Shame to stop Lord Westkeep from verbally abusing her and her parents any longer. Once the State is decapitated by the revolution, there is nothing to inforce social leverage. Coltbert invents the Lioness Spell to recreate the conditions that force brutes to negotiate instead of conquer. The spell is tested by some chasseurs from 981-987 and then deployed in the city in 987 to tame the thugs in part 3 of Coltbert's game.
> **Note 46:** Urban capitalists of Skyfall and the Herzland have solved resource scarcity, but they replaced it with an artificial scarcity. Because Griffon magic/pride only works on the self, stripping a Griffon of their artisanship to put them on an assembly line destroys their soul. Their innate Griffon pride metastasizes into toxic greed. They hoard wealth not because they are starving, but because the system denies them any other avenue to express their Ego. Coltbert writes his paper to point out that material abundance means nothing if the psychological framework is broken. He focuses on Ego, artisanship, and cross-species passion specifically to cure the soullessness of the industrial cog, giving the wealthy, safe Griffons a productive reason to live.
> **Note 47:** The irredeemables are called "Herzlander Lions" derogatorily, labeling them as having no grace; just brutes.
>
> The redeemable, misled followers are called "Les Sauvageons" (The Little Savages)

### Crystal Trade
* **Category:** Backstory
> **Note 0:** Chrysalis goes to Diamond Mountain herself and purchases the crystals using her blood money from the Skyfall company that her fake parents run and she got from hit jobs and bounties
> Chrysalis then creates Krysallfels Hendelsgesellschaft for mining crystals from an isolated island in the sea far away from Skyfall by Olenia and the Changeling Lands
> **Note 1:** Chrysalis's scientists are drones who showed scientific curiosity. The Herzland noble researchers are disgusted by the bugs and their lowborn status. Chrysalis got the idea by paying attention to Kemerskai's new state and machine in Cloudbury, the very enemy she just helped the Herzland nobles defeat.
> **Note 2:** The Griffonian Republic also buys crystals from Diamond Mountain's black market or from intercepting the supply chain in Hellquil. They need these crystals for their Universal Translator research and development. The GR eventually conquers the sunstriker mountains in order to secure its crystals, which aren't as abundant as they are in Diamond Mountain but they are for the republic.
> **Note 3:** Star Energy Corporation develops magic tech in Pridea. They have to get their crystals from Diamond Mountain or Krysallfels Hendelsgesellschaft too, and they are expensive. The GR initially keeps the sunstriker crystal supply for state use, only trading low grade crystals (for their artisans to stabilize the flow) with the Aquileians in exchange for high caloric food
> **Note 4:** The crystal mountains existed north of Equestria but no one was willing to prospect there because it was too infeasible. When the Crystal Empire returns in 1002, the crystal ponies are willing to mine it and trade with Aquileia. The shipments must go through Equestria, but Equestrian customs officials are so used to viewing crystals as mere "decorative jewelry" or "palace building materials" that they literally do not comprehend their industrial, militaristic value. They stamp the manifests as "Luxury Goods Trade" while Cadance and Shining Armor quietly arm another nation's magical industry.
> **Note 5:** Temberik Mountains have crystals, but are off limits until the events of the war and cooperation
> **Note 6:** Chrysalis attempted to strike a deal with the Temberik to get crystal supplies way before the war started, but the Temberik elders rejected her. Same for Comet Shine. No friend but the mountains.
> **Note 7:** Additional black market crystal sources are Blackrock Mountains, Griffonstone (maybe some controversy for defacing ancestral lands) and check the rest of the map for controversial areas, like the middle east and siberia. Maybe some Zebrican sources?

### Temberik
* **Category:** Organization
> **Desc:** Thestrals who live in the mountains, inspired by the Kurdish people. "Temberik" means "pony" in Sorani.
> **Note 0:** They are isolationist and communal, wary of outsiders. Unlike the Tzinacatl who engage in global maritime trade and historically participated in survival harmony, the Temberik truly have been isolated from the rest of Equestria by choice for 1000 years. They are the warriors who self-isolated after Luna's banishment.
> **Note 2:** They reject Chrysalis's bid to mine the crystals in their mountains for her love harvesters in exchange for material wealth. They do not want industrialization. They want isolation and their traditions. They want to be left alone and in peace.
> **Note 1:** The Kurds have historically lived in the mountainous region between great empires (Ottoman, Persian, Arab) without a unified state of their own. Their society was organized around the Ashiret (Tribal Confederation).
> **Note 3:** In Kurdish culture, the Diwan is a gathering place where men and women sit, drink tea, and discuss affairs. It is radically democratic compared to the strict hierarchies of Griffon knights or Celestia’s court (privy/petty council).
> In-Universe: Temberik villages share grain, tools, and labor. If one family’s roof collapses, the whole village fixes it. This appeals to Applejack’s "Barn Raising" sensibility
> **Note 4:** Applejack’s Perspective: She respects their communal work ethic (it reminds her of the Apple Family), but she sees their isolationism as a trap. By refusing to engage with the world (globalization), they are slowly starving in their mountains while the world moves on.
> **Note 5:** The Temberik are Order without Progress. They have unity (communalism) but no ambition beyond survival. They need Ambition (Red Love) to realize they can be more than just survivors hiding in the rocks.

### Magical Engineering
* **Category:** MagicSystem
> **Desc:** In my world, magic is a branch of physics, just like mechanics, E&M/optics, thermodynamics, etc. (Nuclear and quantum have not been discovered. They are cheat codes that don't suit the narrative.) Magical Engineering is applied magic (as a branch of physics), just like how Mechanical Engineering is applied mechanics and Electrical Engineering is applied E&M.
> **Note 9:** The love harvester uses a spell matrix for the changeling love draining spell, a massive vacuum sealed distillation drum that separates the love into two distinct streams, pink love (friendship) and red love (magic). The love is stored and stabilized in a canister that has a crystal to stabilize the love. The crystal uses the power of the magic to self-contain the magic, like a self-correcting circuit or a ball in a valley. When the red love spikes, a portion of it is used to stabilize itself.
> **Note 10:** Combining the draining crystal with the crystallers' emotion filtering spell that separates friendship from magic and interchangeable canisters makes magic power sources modular and renewable. This is superior to finite power crystals.
> **Note 11:** Power source via lightweight but finite power crystal or reusable canister plus filtering spell and vacuum valve. Both the power crystal and the vacuum valve have a common output piece. It is compatible with input pieces for any spell matrix, or a crystal enhancer directing into a unicorn's horn. Power sources and spells are modular and have biological compatibility too.
> Therefore, the canister + draining spell matrix + vacuum valve + filtering spell matrix are equivalent to an open source smartphone. The spell matrices are software applications.
> **Note 12:** Any sentient creature can use their own magic as a power source for civilian spell matrices. Or they can buy magic canisters. Magic doesn't replace anyone's special talent, it just gives commoditized solutions for things so that creatures can devote their time and energy to their own special talent.
> **Note 0:** Timeline - 965-980 ALB
> Bored 2nd gen Royalists at The University of Pridea are studying novel ways to apply Grover III's griffon magical artisanship (where the griffon has to build the machine themselves to give the metal pieces magical properties). Unicorn Magic is also studied, leading to the theory of the "cutie mark discount" where unicorns have relatively uniform raw magical power (which grows with practice, like a muscle, but with diminishing returns, also like a muscle) and get discounts on spells related to their cutie marks. But there is nothing saying a unicorn can only cast spells related to their special talent. It is just hard to cast someone else's complex spell without their discount. However, anything is possible with an external magical power source. Hence the crystal enhancer is invented. However, it requires only the highest grade crystals from Diamond Mountain to deliver a stable magic flow, otherwise the crystal enhancer will explode and the unicorn will be severely injured.
> **Note 2:** A unicorn's horn has muscles that rearrange their internal microscopic shape to cast different spells. The next leap in magical engineering is taking the pattern within a unicorn's horn for a particular spell and etching it into a crystal, forming a static "spell matrix" that casts a particular spell. The spell matrix can be powered by a high grade crystal to do magic without any unicorn. But it still requires a very stable flow of magic, otherwise it will shatter.
>
> The Aquileians call the etched crystal an "appliqué", based on an extra piece attached to an artisan piece of clothing, which explains what it does (connects with software application)
> This perfectly reflects a society that lacks innate Equestrian magic but possesses immense artisanal pride.
> To the most Aquileians, magic is not a natural bodily function; it is a highly specialized craft that they have to make happen using their asset specificity (griffon metallurgy, unicorn spells).
>
> Calling the swappable crystals an "Appliqué" frames the magic as a beautiful, bespoke addition to their workshop. (The griffon operates the magic stabilizer big hardware machine, feeds in crystals and controls the flow, and the appliqué does something new and unique for them)
>
> The Equestrians simply call it a "spell matrix" because they are familiar with unicorns casting spells all the time. "Spell matrix" describes what it is. she isn't introducing a radical new art form; she is simply mechanizing something they already do organically. Therefore, calling it a "Spell Matrix" is the most direct, practical, and logical term. It accurately describes exactly what the object is: a structured crystalline grid that holds a spell.
> **Note 3:** To get around the supply chain bottleneck of Diamond Mountain's unfortunate slave labor for highest grade crystals, griffon artisans invent and build machines that can stabilize the flow out of lower grade crystals. The machine must be controlled by the griffon who built it for the metallurgic surfaces to not shatter while funneling/stabilizing the magical flow. This is the grand partnership between Aquileian unicorns and griffons. The griffons and unicorns then start bootstrapping the process to scale up. A unicorn with a crystal enhancer can using the lithography spell to etch the new spell into a spell matrix (T1 - the end product). But even better, they can manually etch the specific lithography spell to make T1, which would be T2. T2 can be hooked up to a griffon artisan's magic flow stabilizer machine and the griffon can take in low grade crystals, stabilize the flow, and power the T2 spell matrix aimed at a blank crystal, which produces T1 copies. So the griffons are able to make T1s to sell. So now etching lots of T1 spell matrices is repeatable (but still grueling, since it requires griffon artisans running the machine), but in order to run these spell matrices outside the workshop, you still need the highest grade stable crystals from Diamond Mountain.
> **Note 4:** Timeline - 986 ALB
> Chrysalis's red love drugs flood the international market. Some researchers tried using it but it exploded the spell matrices from instability and was subsequently ignored.
> **Note 5:** Timeline - 1000 ALB
> Twilight knew about Aquileian magical engineering research from books Shining Armor and Cadance slipped her from the Manehattan parloirs. She thought of making a spell matrix to help Applejack harvest apples, like the mass harvest spell at the end of Applebuck Season. But when Applejack yelled at her during Winter Wrap Up for using magic on the farm, Twilight stopped all spell matrix work as she realized it would be insulting to her friends to automated their special talents.
> **Note 6:** Timeline - 1003 ALB
> When the Crystal Empire reappears in 1003 ALB, there is suddenly a brand new source of highest grade crystals. Shining Armor and Cadance are already friendly with Aquileians from the parloirs in Manehattan too. This leads to massive increases in utilization of the existing spell matrix setups, although it all needs to be hidden from Celestia because trading crystals with the Aquileians would be highly suspect and against the principles of the Stagnation of Harmony.
>
> Star Energy scales up hybrid tractor engine production to help earth ponies farm. Aquileian ace pilots use these hybrid engines for their planes.
> **Note 7:** Timeline - 1007 ALB
> Twilight returns from Ain Trotgourait burned out but still urgently feeling she needs to help the zebras of the ruined city. She designs water filtration systems using spell matrices and gets Cadance and Shining Armor to help out by delivering crystals to Ain Trotgourait to run them. As the changeling threat ramps up, Twilight works on Magical Supply Organization, which is a network of teleportation matrices based on her original apple mass harvest spell (she is the most talented unicorn at teleportation and simultaneous telekinesis).
> **Note 8:** Chrysalis literally cannot use the new magical tech, because she doesn't have access to donated love, only extracted love. Friendship and Harmony are not just moral high grounds, they are the ultimate industrial force multipliers.
> **Note 1:** The Etymology of "Application"
> The word comes from the Latin root applicare, which is formed by joining ad- (to, toward) and plicare (to fold, to weave, to attach).
>     Originally, it meant to literally "fold one thing into another" or to physically attach something to a base.
>     By the 1600s, it evolved to mean "putting a general theory to a practical, specific use."
> The Computer Science Shift:
> In the 1960s, computer scientists needed to distinguish between two types of code:
>     Systems Software: The code that simply keeps the machine alive (the operating system, the memory management). This is equivalent to your Luna Nova's base chassis (the draining crystal and the three-way valve).
>     Application Software: The code written to solve a specific, real-world task for the user (like accounting, word processing, or playing a game). It is the code applied to the real world.
>
> In our world, an appliqué is a traditional French needlework technique where a smaller, highly detailed piece of fabric or ornament is physically attached to a larger, general-purpose garment. English loans appliqué for the tailoring technique.
> **Note 13:** The griffon artisans' crystal stabilizer must be operated at vacuum for particle cleanliness and to prevent oxygen from reacting with the exposed crystal and forming slag when the crystal is punctured to release magic
> **Note 14:** If magic is another branch of physics alongside mechanics, E&M, and optics, etc. and mechanical engineering is applied mechanics, electrical engineering is applied E&M, then magical engineering is applied magic
> **Note 15:** The vacuum valve for the crystallers' emotion filtering spell (and the changeling brute force centrifuges that came before it) must maintain a sealed vacuum environment to fully separate the pink and red love, otherwise air resistance would interfere with the separating mechanism and cause the separation to be incomplete and chaotic

### Crystal Pony Biology
* **Category:** MagicSystem
> **Note 0:** Crystal ponies have emotion sense just like changelings. When they crystalize, it's a visual indicator of caloric surplus and charitostatic magic. Crystals are stored magic, so the transparent crystalization effect is stored friendship.  Adjacent to how thymodynamic magic from windigos are stored in crystals
>
> Example from the canon episode: the crystal ponies literally get color back to their dull coats after hearing about the crystal faire
>
> The big blast at the crystal faire makes even non crystal ponies get the crystalization effect
> **Note 1:** Why do changelings and crystal ponies have the same emotion sense and friendship as calories biology?
> Doyalist explanation: Because the changeling episode Canterlot Wedding was about changelings feeding on Cadance's love, and the crystal pony episode The Crystal Empire is about Cadance's love magic needing to keep the shield up until the crystal faire and heart are recovered, therefore it's the same mechanic
> Watsonian explanation: because they both evolved in freezing, calorie-scarce environments. Their magic is about turning friendship and charitostatics into survival/calories/vitality

### Applejack's Star Spade
* **Category:** Technology
> **Desc:** Henri gives Applejack a shovel in chapter 1 to help dig the trenches. Later on, Applejack discovers that it's magical and tied to earth pony pride. Applejack uses it to kill Pagala on Celestia's throne.
> **Note 0:** star spade proves Celestia wrong, that you can have industry without slavery
> **Note 1:** Star Spade fundamentally breaks the economic predation model because it is solely used as human capital improvement. It is not automation, it is enhancement
> **Note 2:** The Star Spade is the most visible proof that earth pony magic is real. Unicorns, Pegasi, and Griffons have highly visible, active magic. The Star Spade turns latent earth pony magic into a concentrated, visible effect of breaking hardpan into soft, diggable loam.
> **Note 3:** Normally a shovel is a symbol of servitude. The Star Spade subverts this because serfs can't use it
> **Note 4:** Fleur invented the star spade as the earth pony version of griffon magic

### Grover V's Stagnation (981 to 1007)
* **Category:** History
> **Note 0:** This phase is characterized by Chrysalis manipulating the nobles who bullied her and extracting wealth out of Herzland to fund her rise
> **Note 2:** The Revolution of 978: An alliance of ambitious industrialists in Griffenheim and peasants across Herzland and symphathizers in the Imperial Army overthrow the monarchy and proclaim the Griffonian Republic
> **Note 4:** The Counterrevolution of 981: Gabriella Eagleclaw takes back Griffenheim (secretly orchestrated by Chyrsalis)
> **Note 6:** Rugged Individualism and Serfdom - industrialists rule the cities, nobles continue serfdom in the countryside while enjoying the benefits of industry. This is a toxic alliance of Divine Right (Landed Nobility) and Social Darwinism (Industrialists). In the countryside
>
> The Nobility realized they needed money, and the "Rugged Individualist" Industrialists realized they needed cheap labor.
> •The Deal: The Nobles provide the serfs (labor); the Industrialists provide the machines. They split the profits.
> •The Culture: This is the era of maximum cynicism. It has neither the "Honor" of feudalism nor the "Opportunity" of capitalism. It is a kleptocracy. ◦Gabriella Eagleclaw represents the "Nobility" side of this.
> **Note 7:** They invented telegraphs and radios. They are in vacuum tube tech, just like in world war 2.
> **Note 3:** Child Grover V was saved by Benito in 978. He returned to the throne in 981 from Eagleclaw's counterrevolution and had a regency council. He was a weak ruler whose rule was undermined by nobles+rugged individualist industrialists. He passed away in 1007 (the start of EAW). Grover VI grew up in Griffenheim with Eros as his regent and an elderly Benito as his bodyguard.
> **Note 5:** The counterrevolution succeeds. Eagleclaw becomes regent until her cousin Grover V comes of age. But she does not have a monopoly on power and is slowly pushed back to just her own Duchy of Strawberry as Grover grows up and the other male advisors push her out. Eagleclaw grants academic and industrial resources to Chrysalis to develop the love harvesters as part of their quid pro quo.
> **Note 8:** Since Griffons are canonically greedy and prideful, the Industrialists don't need to promise equality; they just need to promise superiority. "Don't join a union to get a crust of bread. Work for us, buy our weapons, and you can carve out your own barony." It shatters the working-class solidarity because every griffon peasant thinks they are just a "temporarily embarrassed millionaire."
> **Note 9:** The industrialists forget about selling clothes for all and instead focus on industrial productivity capital equipment for themselves/nobles and luxury/glamour. They also import Chrysalis's stuff which funds her Great Leap Forward. Also arms manufacturing

### Aquileian and Herzlander Cultural Differences
* **Category:** SocietalDifferences
> **Desc:** While they are both griffon nations in Griffonia, The Griffonian Republic and the 2nd Aquileian Republic are vastly different and incompatible cultures.
> **Note 0:** The GR invented the Universal Translator to solve the language barrier. Aquileians embrace their differences (pony and griffon) and learn from each other. They learn Equestrian to understand Equestrian culture.
> **Note 1:** All griffons want to be better than others. They want status. In Herzland, that's by being greedy and having the most capital. In Aquileia, that's by being a snob and spreading your brand.
> **Note 2:** GR vs AR is Universalism vs Particularism
> **Note 3:** Herzlander griffons are essentially the "canonical" griffons who are greedy (also Aquileian urbanites)
> Aquileian (rural) griffons are quirky, diverse with their own dialects and snobby
> **Note 4:** Real life parallels to the griffon/pony divide for humans regarding men and women:
> The "Aquileian" Approach (Harmonic Differentiation): ◦This mirrors Difference Feminism / Maternalism.
> ◦Philosophy: "Women are different, and that difference has value that the market ignores."
> ◦The Goal: Accommodation of difference. The economy should change to fit biology (maternity leave, valuing care work, flexible schedules).
> ◦In Universe: This is how Aquileia treats Earth Ponies. It doesn't tell them "Stop caring about the land and work in a factory like a Griffon." It says, "Your connection to the land is a Specialty. We will build the economy around that."
>
> The "Skyfall" Approach (Standardization): ◦This mirrors 2nd Wave Feminism / Corporate Feminism.
> ◦Philosophy: "Women can do anything men can do."
> ◦The Goal: Erasure of difference. To succeed, women often have to adopt "male" working patterns (long hours, no gaps for childcare, competitive aggression). The "Standard Citizen" is modeled on the male biological clock.
>
> The "Griffonian Republic" Approach (Accomodation): both griffons and griffonesses are given paternity/maternity leave and told it is their duty to take it. It is active rejection of "manosphere" in favor of "patriotism"
> **Note 5:** Aquileia is the Cult of Merit. They are anti-poseur but not pro-proletariat. If you have no asset specificity, you are prey. Dignity must be earned through passionate labor. Griffonian Republic is the Cult of Solidarity. Dignity is an inherent right, not a reward for mastering a craft.
> **Note 6:** Aquileia fights to destroy the "mass-market poseur". They hate mass production and monopolies because it dilutes passion.
> Griffonian Republic fights to destory the "aristocratic poseur". They embrace mass production so that every citizen can afford things, and prosecute hoarders.
> **Note 7:** Herzland and Aquileia two tracks is the geology, the terroir. Aquila, Pelis and Rila are the coastal towns surrounded by flat plains which become industrial cities and their plains fertilized by haber-bosch. The rest of Aquileia is hilly, forested, or mountainous, which is why monsters persisted longer, and why they have terroir economics like wine and cheese growing. So coastal Aquileia and the seat of Gerad Discret's power follows the Herzland route, but 30 or so years delayed because they are subjugated and paying taxes to the Emperor. The rest of diverse Aquileia scoffs at Herzland style industrialization because of their terroir, which is why they become rent seekers of the serfs' terroir instead, and the serfs become bandits, and the warlords have to buy gunpowder from Herzland industrialists and later Gerad
>
> Flat land has low friction. When the Imperial Legions (or early Aquileian protectors) hunted here, it was ruthlessly efficient. The monsters were eradicated quickly, flooding the coastal markets with magical loot, which triggered an early, localized Golden Age. Because the land was flat, once the monster loot ran out, it was incredibly easy to transition to Haber-Bosch agriculture and massive, sprawling Bessemer steel factories.
>
> Mountains, hills, and dense forests are logistical nightmares for monster hunting. The monsters persisted here much longer because they could hide, ambush, and survive. The rural lords enjoyed a prolonged, slower drip of "Monster Loot." They didn't need to industrialize because the old biological magic lasted an extra thirty years in the hills. When Grover III’s mandates (or simply time and attrition) finally wiped out the last of the mountain monsters, the rural lords faced an existential crisis. They had no monster loot left to fund their castles, but because they lived in rugged, mountainous terrain, they couldn't build Herzland-style factories or mass-agriculture either. With the "Honest Racket" dead and industrialization geographically impossible, they had to find a new source of wealth to maintain their "Lion" status. They looked down at their serfs, who were using the unique, mountainous soil to cultivate premium wine and cheese (Terroir). A true "Lion" hunts a Manticore. A "Poseur" points a sword at a peasant and demands 80% of their artisanal wine vintage. Because the lords possessed no Asset Specificity regarding agriculture, they became pure parasites, extracting the wealth of the terroir without contributing any labor or protection.
> **Note 8:** GR united by laws and desire to liberate Herzland from the Empire/Reich, solidarity and 1st gen trauma
>
> Aquileia united by nationalism and individual passion and asset specificity. No rent seeking, only glory seeking
>
> Equestria united by cultural harmony after exorcising ponies first. Not forced by laws or national enemies or economic efficiency
> **Note 9:** Original unions in Herzland and Aquileia were poseurs. The unionized griffons were rugged individualists who wanted to extort the tycoons (like corrupt NYC unions)
> Griffonian Republic unions want the company/The State to succeed. Instead of rugged individualism they exist to ensure proportional earning (record profits, record contracts)

### Knightly Code / Rules of War
* **Category:** WorldRules
> **Note 0:** Traditionally, knights squabble with each other over land, but they don't touch each others' peasants because they don't want their own peasants to get violated or murdered. They need their peasants to abuse for themselves and keep up their powerbase and sick pride of ownership.
> **Note 1:** No guerilla tactics or partisans
> **Note 2:** No terror bombing
> **Note 3:** Surrenders are accepted. Prisoner swaps are formal. They trade their captured veterans with dignity while keeping equipment.
> **Note 4:** Griffonian knights are the traditional army before industrialization. This is because Griffonia is almost all monarchy for centuries. It's elites fighting elites. Knights are not allowed to slaughter peasants. That would be hypocritical, because then their own peasants who generate wealth for them would get slaughtered next time. Peasants basically live in a lie that knights are there to protect them, so hand over your crop. The same ethos carries over to industrial scale war, thankfully.
> **Note 5:** Another griffon knightly rule is "never fly your enemy's flag". Because it's counterproductive in the long run. But changelings disguise themselves as the enemy, literally, all the time as infiltrator units. This is core to changeling predator identity.
>
> So Synovial's war crimes should be really obvious - his changeling soldiers drained surrendering GR combatants and civilians to death. Because what kind of changelings would want to go on a mission to a foreign land where they can learn to use modern weapons to defeat their enemies? Certainly a fair number of predators.
> **Note 6:** Rules of war for nobles is like the things I came up with for serfs in KU
> **Note 7:** In the rules of war, sky ambushes are banned
> Griffons don't fly fast in heavy armor
> Enchanted armor necessary to protect from crossbows. It makes being a knight expensive and skill required
> **Note 8:** he definitions of "Honor" drive the tactical evolution and the psychological radicalization of both Henri Gourard and Hivemarshal Trimmel.
>
> This dynamic creates a tragic mirror: Trimmel adopts the tactics of the Chasseurs but believes he possesses the honor of the Knights, while Henri adopts the ruthlessness of the Peasant Revolt to destroy what he sees as Trimmel's false honor.
>
> I. The Chasseur Doctrine: War as Pest Control
>
> You established that the Knightly Code is essentially a "Gentlemen's Agreement" between property owners: We fight for land, but we don't kill the management (Knights) or destroy the capital (Serfs). [Source 744, 385].
>
> The Chasseurs (1st Gen FJA/Royalists) reject this because they are not Property Owners; they are The Property fighting back.
>
> • The Target: They don't fight to take the castle; they fight to kill the Lord.
>
>     ◦ The Logic: The Warlord is not a "Peer." He is a "Wolf" who eats the sheep. You don't duel a wolf; you shoot it from a distance.
>
> • The Tactic: Precision Decapitation.
>
>     ◦ Instead of massed musket volleys (which kill conscript peasants), the Chasseurs use rifled muskets/precision magic to snipe the Commander.
>
>     ◦ The Message: "We are not fighting your army. We are fighting You."
>
> • The "Dishonor": To a Noble, sniping an officer is "murder." To a Chasseur, it is the ultimate humanitarian act. You kill one Tyrant to free 1,000 serfs.
>
> II. Henri’s Application: The "Poseur" Exception
>
> Henri carries this Chasseur DNA into the Great War. He views the Changeling Officers (Statthalters and Jaegers) through the lens of the Predator's Dilemma.
>
> • The "Poseur" Loophole: Henri believes that Honor is a contract.
>
>     ◦ If you fight a "Fair Fight" (Peer vs. Peer), you get Honor.
>
>     ◦ If you abuse the helpless (Lords vs. Serfs, or Changelings vs. Ponies/Conscripts), you are a Poseur. You are pretending to be a Warrior, but you are actually a Bully.
>
> • The Consequence: Poseurs forfeit their right to the Rules of War.
>
>     ◦ Henri's Logic: "Synovial drains prisoners. Pagala tortures civilians. Trimmel commands an army of drugged slaves. They are not Generals; they are Warlords. Therefore, we'll strike the warlords directly."
>
> • The Validation: When Henri tells Applejack to use these "dishonorable" tactics (deceit/precision strikes) to destroy Synovial, he doesn't see a loss of innocence; he sees the Chasseur Spirit. He sees a Peasant (Applejack) refusing to play the Knight's game.
>
> III. Trimmel’s Tragedy: The Meritocratic Delusion
>
> This creates the perfect psychological trap for Trimmel.
>
> • The Student of Discret: Your notes state that Trimmel studied Moriset Discret’s "Peasant Army" (The Chasseurs/Proto-FJA) to learn Mission Command and Individual Initiative. He copied their tactics (Small unit autonomy, mobility, precision) [Source 681, 1303].
>
> • The Misunderstanding: Trimmel thinks he also copied their Honor.
>
>     ◦ Trimmel's Self-Image: He views himself as a Meritocrat fighting a stagnant Feudal power (Celestia). He believes his war is "Clean" because he offers surrender terms and (mostly) avoids torture in his own unit. He thinks he is the Liberator.
>
> • The Radicalization (2nd Battle of Tall Tale):
>
>     ◦ The Event: Henri directs the Wonderbolts and Twilight/Starlight to "Precision Strike" Trimmel's officer corps [Source 683, 1310].
>
>     ◦ Trimmel's Reaction: He is horrified. He views this as Dishonorable Assassination.
>
>     ◦ Why he snaps: To Trimmel, his officers are "Brothers in Merit," not "Warlords." When the Ponies vaporize them with magic from the sky, he doesn't see "Liberation of Conscripts"; he sees "Cheating."
>
>     ◦ The Hypocrisy: Trimmel thinks, "I offered you an honorable surrender! I fought with tanks and tactics! And you respond by sending a demi-god (Twilight) to magically erase my friends? You are the savages!"
>
> IV. The Synthesis: Radicalized Doctrine
>
> This misunderstanding drives the escalation of the war.
>
> 1. Henri/Applejack's View: "Trimmel leads an army of slaves. Killing Trimmel's officers breaks the chain of command and frees the slaves. It is a moral imperative."
>
> 2. Trimmel's View: "The Ponies refuse to fight a 'Soldier's War.' They use Magic/Alicorns to bypass strategy. Therefore, I will close my eyes from whatever is happening in the north an continue Chrysalis's war.
>
> Conclusion:
>
> • Henri fights without "Honor" because he believes the enemy is a Criminal.
>
> • Trimmel fights without "Mercy" because he believes the enemy is a Cheater.
>
> This validates why Applejack must eventually make peace with Trimmel in the POW camp. She has to show him that she didn't strike his officers out of malice, but out of the same "Chasseur Logic" he studied: To decapitate the Tyrant so the Peasant can live. Only then does Trimmel realize he was the Tyrant all along.

### 2nd Aquileian Republic
* **Category:** Nation
> **Note 1:** 28 million population in total. 20% are ponies
> **Note 2:** Political Weight: They are too big to ignore. The Aquileian Republic must accommodate them to function. This justifies why Aquileia is culturally compatible with Equestria—they are used to living with ponies.
> •Pridea as "Quebec": With 40% ponies, Pridea becomes a distinct cultural heartland.
> •Integration: It implies a society where ponies are normalized citizens, not exotic creatures.
> **Note 3:** Coltbert and Gaudreau basically took Celestia's ways and made it work with industry, democracy, and griffons. But of course the resulting Republic is so messy Celestia cannot comprehend it. Plus she can't look past the fact that Aquileian monarchial culture had historically always abused ponies. The sexual promiscuity is revolting to her too.
> **Note 4:** Gaudreau's FJA (40%) has a plurality (harmonic capitalism) while Verany's PdNA is 2nd (35%) and rules in a coalition government with Gaudreau. That's why Verany is economic minister. The radical communists and nationalist/griffons first parties are on the fringes, but occassionally support legistlation they like.
> **Note 5:** The MPA (10%) is Discret's former secret police thugs, griffon supremacists, violent militarists, dispossesed nobles. They are the thugs who once survived by having no talent except getting bribes to allow the bartering between the FJA and PNdA and also didn't better themselves during Coltbert's Game Part 3. They are now all fired and have to get real jobs, and they hate it. Rodier is the Bonapartist/Fascist apex predator at the top who disciplines the thugs and makes them stop fighting for scraps.
>
> The populist pitch is devastatingly effective because it requires no Asset Specificity: "The FJA elites in Pridea care more about a Zebrican bug or a Changeling prisoner than they care about you. They demand you learn poetry and charm just to be treated like a griffon. I say you are superior simply because you were born in Aquileia. You don't need a Seal. You just need a flag."
> **Note 7:** The PNdA are perceived as rugged individualists by new FJA but the reality is they have the same economics about artisanship and asset specificity but they are urban and culturally conservative.
> **Note 8:** The PSA are pan-griffonians who aspire to join with Kemerskai's republic because Aquileia left them behind culturally
>
> The FJA volunteers in Tall Tale are there specifically to support Equestrian victory and conversion into a Republic that follows FJA ideology for Harmonic Capitalism, making Aquileia untouchable from annexation into a pan-griffonian republic
> **Note 9:** Aquila and Rila are flat coastal plains, like Herzland
> The central government/crown had always suppressed or snubbed local cultures in Vinovia (wine country), Pridea (highest pony minority), Westkeep (mountainous), Adelart (forested), Tarrin
>
> Historically, all versions of the French state have been obsessed with centralization - Absolute Monarchy, Jacobins, Napoleon, and subsequent republics
> **Note 10:** While Kemerskai purged the rugged individualists and formed a pure state in Cloudbury, Verany was completely wiped and went underground. Ironically, it means Verany is still somewhat stuck in the rugged individualist dogma of 980 (although he did adopt strict anti-corruption laws/ethos), and the FJA is the counterbalance that lets his economic ideology continue to be productive instead of self-destructive
> **Note 11:** Safety nets were informal during Coltbert Reforms, since economies are communal. In the city, safety nets were necessary for solidarity against the king's thugs.
> Once the republic is formalized, the safety nets are written into law (and debated)
> **Note 12:** They don't adopt Kemerskai’s "Civic Fiat" (too Herzlander/Statist) or Skyfall’s "Pirate Fiat." They create a Nationalist Fiat.
> The Central Bank manages the money supply via national bonds
> **Note 13:** They have high tax rates but low interest rates nationwide to encourage capital investment. These are national bonds. It's not funded by conquest like MEFO bills or restoration bills. It's not backed by a fleet or piracy. Less emphasis on tax base like Kemerskai. It's funded by export and productivity gains and future labor, and nationalism.
> **Note 14:** Upon throwing out Discret and legalizing the value-based economy with their own national fiat in 1008, the new Aquileian goal is to put Skyfall out of business.
>
> Aquileia's strength - the patriot discount
> Because the farmers sell food/wine/clothes at near-cost to "spread the brand," the cost of living in Aquileia is artificially low (it's harmonic). Urban workers eat like kings (good wine, good cheese) on modest wages. They are happy. This allows the PNdA to keep wages stable while maximizing output. They can undercut Skyfall on shipping rates and steel prices while maintaining a higher standard of living.
>
> Meanwhile, Skyfall's weakness is that they run on greed. Everyone demands market rate or higher. There is no solidarity, only ruthless capitalism. Their sailors, workers, and mercenaries want hig hwages to afford the high cost of living in their trade city that processes luxury goods.
>
> Then when Eros becomes a threat as he reunites Herzland, the national focus shifts to military production and training for a hot war.
> **Note 6:** The PSA (15%) (Parti de la Solidarité Aquiléenne) is not Marxist. They are the exhuasted, the wallflowers, the lonely, the un-ambitious. They oppose Coltbert's "ego-capitalism". Ideology: Universal Dignity
> The Unsealed Men: Male Griffons who aren't violent (MPA), but aren't charismatic or skilled enough to earn a Seal from a Mare. They are "Nice Guys" who finish last in an economy of aggressive peacocks.
> • The "Leftover" Women: Griffonesses who didn't have a "Reformed Thug" childhood friend and are too traditional to visit a FJA Stallion, but can't find a husband because the top-tier males are chasing Unicorns.
> • The Burnouts: Ponies and Griffons who tried the "Artisan Rat Race" and hated it. They don't want to "Spread their Brand." They just want to bake bread, eat it, and sleep.
> 2. Their Political Pitch: "The Right to be Boring."
> • Not Marxism: "Seize the Means of Production" doesn't work when everyone owns a wine press.
> • The New Slogan: "Dignity Without Display."
>     ◦ They advocate for a stronger safety net without the social shaming.
>     ◦ They hate the "Seal" system. They argue that a citizen should be respected because they are a Citizen, not because they impressed a Unicorn.
>     ◦ They look at the FJA social clubs and see a high school cafeteria where they aren't allowed to sit.
> 3. Why they are "Left Behind":
> • In Aquileia, if you aren't climbing, you are invisible.
> • The FJA views them with Pity/Contempt: "Why are you so dull? Don't you have a passion?"
> • The PNdA views them as Dead Weight: "If you aren't hustling for a promotion, you're wasting my time."
> • The Result: They have food (cheap surplus), but they have Zero Social Capital. They are the "Lonely Crowd" doomscrolling in a golden age.
> **Note 15:** The Aquileian Failure (The High Floor, High Ceiling, High Cost):
> • Coltbert: "Look, everyone is fed! Everyone can be a star!"
> • Applejack: "But what if they don't want to be a star? What if they just want to be a pony?"
> • She sees the PAT/Wallflowers and realizes that Ego-Capitalism is exhausting. It demands constant performance. It cured the "Predator's Dilemma" by turning life into a never-ending job interview.
> 2. The Vindication of Celestia (The "Nursery" had Value):
> • Celestia’s Stagnation was a "Safe Space." In Equestria, a pony with no ambition was still loved. A "Blank Flank" was a temporary state, not a social death sentence.
> • Applejack realizes: "Celestia was wrong to ban ambition, but she was right to protect the quiet ones."
> 3. The Synthesis: The Equestrian Republic Applejack decides to build a system that combines the three models:
> • From Aquileia (FJA): Economic Asset Specificity. Everyone gets the tools (Land/Magic/Capital) to be great if they want to be. (Solves Poverty).
> • From Griffonia (GR): Political Solidarity. "No Creature Left Behind." The State guarantees rights, not just the Market. (Solves Injustice).
> • From Celestia (Stagnation): Social Unconditionality. You do not need a "Seal" to be worthy. You do not need to "Hustle." You are valid just because you exist. (Solves Loneliness).
> **Note 0:** Timeline - 1008 ALB Second Revolution
> It took 28 years for the peasantry to go from Grateful Serfs into Entitled Owners. The King is bankrupt. We hold his debt. Therefore, we own the country. It is a bank run and a coup with no military. In game mechanics, it's as if all of the Aquileian subjects supported the republic unanimously. Moriset Discret gets to keep his position as a judge at the state fairs since that is the only thing he was actually good at (his asset specificity) and actually in charge of, since Gaudreau and Verany already ran the economy.
>
> One of the factors of the bank run is Dennis Discret's unsanctioned war against the statthalter slaver islands and the Fall of Olenia due to the volunteers being stretched thin across Yakyakistan and Nova Griffonia and the rise of Archon Eros who rebranded the Empire as the Reich. The Aquileian public needs mobilization.
> **Note 16:** They are a Republic, but the culture is nationalism, not solidarity. It is a continuation of the Coltbert Reforms. The Aquileians are not fighting for world peace, they are fighting an anti-poseur crusade to feed an industrialized, nationalist ego. Coltbert's ego-capitalism is a highly formalized religion of competence. Status is tied to your asset specificity and terroir. Anyone who claims power, status or wealth without having the personal merit is a poseur. The problem with ego-capitalism and the anti-poseur crusade in a post-scarcity/world peace: Honesty without Kindness is still cruelty
> **Note 18:** Aquileia may have solved the predator's dilemma and eliminated rent-seeking with ego-seeking, but they have not solved society. The old Herzlander model was: 1st gen survivors of feudalism = traumatized and grateful for survival, 2nd gen grew up safe and became rent seekers.
> The new Aquileian ego-capitalism model is:
> 1st gen grateful
> 2nd gen = ego-seeking
> 3rd gen is partially exhausted (stagnation) and also reduced due to birth rates
> This is where the final Equestrian synthesis includes unconditional dignity
> **Note 19:** En Marche! as the coalition government name been FJA and PNdA, because it's business owners plus socially fluid professionals
>
> Also tie into "The Aquileians March" event, need to review the context of it, what focus leads to it
> The focus is integrating the peripherie, and the context is cultural integration, marching to unite their cultural cousins
> **Note 17:** The Aquileian military relies on elite, hyper-competent volunteers. Their warfare is romantic, individualistic, and selfish. Kemerskai's military relies on standardized logistics, identical rifles and rigid formations. They have mandatory service. But the army is not built on terror like the Reich or Chrysalis, it is built on solidarity. Their soldiers don't fight for personal glory, they fight for every griffon in the republic. A profound tragedy is that, when the threat of war with the Reich is imminent in 1010, the Aquileians cannot just use lone wolf pairs/small group-style chasseur aces. They still need a standing army for a resilient frontline, and the Gendarmerie has been ceremonial ever since Part 3 of Coltbert's game tamed the thugs.
>
> In peacetime, the FJA’s "Patriot Discount" and the Republic's surplus subsidized a high floor for the "Wallflowers" (the unambitious, the unsealed, the content). But in total war, logistics dictate morality. The surplus food, the best medical alchemy, and the winter fuel must be diverted to the front line. The Republic does not force the Wallflowers to enlist; it simply states the mathematical reality: The welfare state is suspended. The bread is in the trenches.
>
> This preserves the Aquileian illusion of "Choice" while executing a brutal macroeconomic reality. The Wallflowers enlist because the civilian economy can no longer afford to subsidize those who do not possess the Asset Specificity to generate massive capital. It is a tragedy of Ego-Capitalism: if you cannot sell a masterpiece, you must sell your body to the line.
>
> The profound irony of the Aquileian infantry is that the Wallflowers do not view the trench as a prison; they view it as a Sanctuary, much like the traumatized first gen Royalists recruited and standardized by Gerad Discret
>
> Ego-Capitalism (the FJA/Coltbert model) is a gentle purgatory for the introvert. It demands constant performance, networking, and the curation of a Tableau de Chasse. For the Wallflower, this is exhausting. When they enlist in the regular infantry, they are handed a standardized uniform that covers their Cutie Mark or their lack of a Seal. For the first time in their lives, they are not expected to be "Special."
>
>     The Anti-Poseur Relief: In the trench, there is no pressure to be an Aquileian Lion or a graceful Eagle. The standard-issue rifle does not require artisan pride to fire; it just requires a trigger pull.
>
>     Organic Solidarity: Stripped of the hyper-competitive social market of Pridea or Aquila, these infantry units organically develop the exact same "Esprit de Corps" and Solidarity that Kemerskai engineered in Cloudbury. They share rations, they hold the line, and they protect the griffon/pony next to them. They find dignity not in standing out, but in standing together.

### Skyfall Trade Federation
* **Category:** Nation
> **Note 0:** They do not reform into a republic like in EaW canon/default settings. They are merchants of death. It is the libertarian rugged individualist paradise. Mercantile amorality.
> **Note 4:** Walter Whitefeather - cigarettes
> Heinrich Kessler - brings in brain drain from unstable countries, makes cars
> Ferdinand von Hochskiaue - richest and most powerful banker
> Hippolyte Bechard - arms dealer and private security
> **Note 5:** Skyfall represents the boring, soulless, greedy and capital accumulating Herzland stereotype
> **Note 6:** They are inspired by the original Dutch Republic which were merchants of death
> The Burgundian Inheritence putting them under Spain and then the Dutch revolt is exactly like the 978 Ghishard betrayal. Seizing the imperial fleet instead of building the Fluyts themselves
> Just as the dutch produced gunpowder, Skyfall makes modern armaments
> They are a state built on Credit, Gunpowder, and Water
> **Note 7:** Even when faced with the threat of Aquileia building a rival fleet, they don't box out Equestrian companies who buy and sell to Aquileia using Aquileian insurance, because they are decentralized and greedy. They want the highest profit margins.
> **Note 2:** Ghislain Guichard the Iron Chancellor fantasized for years of seizing the imperial fleet and letting banks print money backed by shipping and privateering, which he did in 978, becoming a trade federation.
> Because Skyfall no longer has the Herzland to fall back on, simple guano extraction isn't enough to fund their mercenary armies and the massive upkeep of the stolen Imperial Fleet. Ghislain Guichard planned a closed-loop macroeconomic engine. The system ramps up from "business as usual" Resource Extraction to Systemic Arbitrage. Skyfall isn't just extracting labor; they are engineering a closed-loop ecosystem of dependency.
> With the guano mines tapped out or inefficient, Skyfall's CEOs pivot. They realize that Zebrican labor is more valuable than Zebrican guano. They lure desperate Zebrican males (with propaganda about being a self made lion and joining the civilized world) to Skyfall's sweatshops to manufacture goods for export and send Skyfall Marks back to their impoverished home villages that are terrorized by warlords. Skyfall intentionally pays the workers just enough to send remittances home, but never enough to build wealth in Skyfall.
> Skyfall stops selling Zebrica just basic gunpowder. They start intentionally dumping obsolete "last generation's weapons" to the Zebrican warlords. This ensures the Zebrican warlords have the tools to violently extort the remittances from the families, turning the warlords into unpaid tax collectors for Skyfall.
>
> They sell cheaply stamped water pumps, agricultural tools, and radios that break down after a year, forcing the village to spend next year's remittances on extortionate Skyfall replacement parts.
>
> This post 978 evolution of Skyfall is like the British Empire - putting entire populations in servitude to buy your manufactured goods and take their labor and resources
> Arbitrage. They buy cheap labor from Zebrica, buy cheap "charity" food from Equestria (SAA), and use their monopoly on shipping lanes to sell both back to each other at maximum profit.
> **Note 8:** Citizenship must be sponsored by a company by debt
> **Note 9:** Skyfall taking Chrysalis's red love and "Olenian taxes" and washing them into legitimate Skyfall Marks legitimizes her regimes cruelty
> **Note 11:** Demographic - Skyfall Arcane Cartels
> Highly educated, magically gifted griffon artisans who entered company academies to learn to forge their own enchanted tools, paid lavishly and live in penthouses, but trapped by threat of assassination if they step out of line. Their lifestyle is propped up by the sweatshops.
> **Note 12:** Demographic - Skyfall Sweatshop Workers
> Desperate workers working 60-80 hours a week, and if they collapse they are replaced. Rural griffons who lost their farms, immigrants sold on the manosphere lie or sending money home. They are told if they save up, they can become CEOs.
> **Note 10:** Demographic - Skyfall CEOs
> For high tech industries requiring griffon magic, they are merchant princes/syndicates running legalized mafias. They teach sacred arts of forging magical tools, but require oaths of loyalty. For mass produced commondities, they are the ones who made it big early and pulled the ladder up after.
> **Note 13:** Meta-Narrative - Skyfall is Carthage to Grover III's Rome. Skyfall values gold, leverage and mercantilism compared to Grover III's honor and imperial service
> **Note 14:** Meta-Narrative - Phoebus Cartel of 1924 applied to the entire economy
> Skyfall owners want to maximize profits, so they make their products cheap and use planned obsolesence to manufacture a subscription economy. The hillarious irony is that the same philosophy applies to the companies who sell capital equipment, so the factories stiff consumers while getting stiffed themselves by suppliers. Skyfall factories constantly break down and need extortionate parts and labor to fix. No one ever stops to say this is all stupid and inefficient because everyone only cares about how much they can extract from others while minimizing how much they get extracted. This is why Kemerskai is able to build an industrial powerhouse in Cloudbury out of nothing but solidarity, by outlawing planned obsolesence and instilling a culture of fraternity.
> **Note 15:** In a hyper-capitalist trade hub driven by "Rugged Individualism," the concept of a public police force that "serves and protects" the citizenry does not exist. The line between a Police Department and a Mercenary Gang is completely erased. Skyfall's security apparatus operates like the historical Pinkerton National Detective Agency or the private armies of the VOC. The "Police" are simply the highest-paid, best-armed syndicate in the city, holding a state-sanctioned monopoly on violence to protect corporate property, not human life. Below the Corporate Police are the street-level gangs (smugglers, extorters, strike-breakers). They are allowed to exist by the Corporate Police as long as they pay a cut and only terrorize the slums. In a petrostate or a VOC-style trade hub, corruption isn't a secret; it is the fundamental operating system of the city.
> **Note 16:** Skyfall is pure, unchecked Ego completely divorced from Asset Specificity. They possess the brutal "Predator's Dilemma" instincts of the old feudal warlords, but none of the actual physical courage or artisan pride. They are the ultimate "Poseurs."
> **Note 17:** Rival shipping companies have a shared interest in keeping the Skyfall Mark powerful, so they don't openly fire on each other and play games of chicken with ramming and wake washing
> **Note 1:** During Grover IV's gilded age (914-970), Skyfall was the premier Imperial port city. The wealth extracted from Zebrica flowed through Skyfall and directly into the massive domestic economy of the Herzland. Instead of hunting monsters, the griffons colonized the southern zebrides for guano extraction for fertilizer and began selling gunpowder to Zebrican warlords. This was brutal, but it was fundamentally simple colonialism. They used guns to enslave the locals and brought in some laborers, but the goal was just to pull resources out of the ground.
> This is like the Dutch Republic - he Dutch would show up, shoot you, take your spices, and sell them in Europe. It was brutal, but it was just theft.
> **Note 3:** Remittances are just currency (a claim on wealth), not actual material wealth. The immigrant laborers in Skyfall sending money back don't change the nature of the predator's dilemma at their village. Their labor and value created is all in Skyfall. They have to import goods.
>
> Because the supply of local land and basic necessities is fixed, the sudden influx of remittance cash causes local inflation—especially in real estate. The local elites (warlords, cartels, landlords, or monopolies) own the land, the water, and the ports. They sell or rent land to remittance-receiving families at massively inflated prices. Extortion: Local authorities simply demand a cut of the incoming cash with the guns they bought from Skyfall. Once the elites extract this foreign currency from the peasantry, they don't invest it in local infrastructure. They use it to buy imported luxury goods and more guns/artillery/ammo.
>
> the economy of impoverished Zebrican villages operates on two entirely disconnected levels:
>     The Subsistence Level: The villagers trade grain for fish, or woven baskets for tools. They use basic barter or perhaps regional commodity money (like salt or cowrie shells) for daily survival.
>     The Power Level: To interact with the warlords, or to buy anything imported, they must use the Skyfall Mark. The warlords only care about Skyfall Marks because they need them to buy the "outdated Herzland guns" dumped by Skyfall and Chrysalis's shell companies. A warlord cannot buy a rifle with Zebrican grain; he needs foreign exchange. Thus, the Skyfall Mark becomes the absolute sovereign currency of the continent by default.
>
> The Zebrican warlords think they are kings, but they are actually acting as unpaid tax collectors for Skyfall. They use violence to extract Skyfall Marks from their own people, just to hand those Marks right back to Skyfall merchants in exchange for obsolete weapons. They are strip-mining their own continent's human capital to prop up the Skyfall Trade Federation's balance sheet.
>
> The Zebrican warlords only extort some of the remittances, whatever they can get away with. Also the workers not understanding how macroeconomics works is part of the point of the exploitation, as it happens in the real world too

### The Battle of Mount Aris
* **Category:** Backstory
> **Note 3:** The Storm King's horde has guns from Chrysalis's shell companies. It started from Skyfall and Herzland elites dumping last generation's weapons for a quick buck, but Chrysalis accelerated it. They have a chaotic museum of historical guns.
> **Note 2:** The Storm King army is basically all the existing feudal warlords of the Zebrican continent assembled into a "join or die" horde bound by looting and terror. Mount Aris is the last free kingdom on the continent. When the air force wipes most of the warlord army and kill the storm king himself, the entire hold on the continent collapses.
>
> The Storm King rallies the continent under a "Join or Die" banner to expel the disguised foreign slavers, using their own guns against them, but replacing foreign exploitation with domestic tyranny.
>
> The anti-slaver narrative is the justification that allows proud warlords to submit to the Storm King without bruising their egos. They aren't surrendering to a rival; they are joining a "Grand Coalition" to kick the foreign parasites out of Zebrica.
> **Note 4:** The Zebrican warlords think they are becoming modern superpowers by buying guns, but they are actually just becoming a captive market. A society built on looting and terror cannot sustain the educational infrastructure (universities) or the capital infrastructure (foundries) to do this.
> **Note 5:** Meta-Narrative - Historical Hordes
> The Storm King = Attila the Hun
> Huns, Magyars, and Vikings historically stole or extorted their weapons
> The Mandala System in south east asia would trade spices for guns to maintain dominance
> Other examples: Rugaruga of Tanzania led by Mirambo, Pindaris of pre-British India, Warlord Era of China (1916-1928) or figures like Idi Amin and Charles Taylor
> **Note 7:** The horde is a collection of bullies. A bully relies on the threat of violence (loud noises, massive size, terrifying aesthetics) to make the victim submit before a real fight can happen. The defense of Mount Aris in 1006 ALB is the moment the bluff is called. The Wonderbolts, Aquileians, and Republicans do not cower at the loud noises and the massive airships.
> **Note 8:** The Storm King's army is not a nation. It has no constitution, no supply lines of shared sacrifice, and no civic loyalty. It is a pyramid scheme where the warlords at the bottom only obey the Storm King at the top because he promises them loot and threatens them with his massive airships. When the Aquileian Aces and Republican fighters shred the airships, they destroy the Myth of Invincibility. The moment the Storm King dies and the burning airships fall into the ocean, the glue holding the warlords together dissolves. A Zebrican warlord on the mainland suddenly realizes there is no overarching authority to pay him or punish him. He instantly turns his guns on the rival warlord next to him to secure his own fiefdom. The unified threat to Mount Aris evaporates as the horde cannibalizes itself.
> **Note 9:** Dennis Discret brings the entire Royal Aquileian Navy to defend Seaquestria. The monster hunting crews are ready to take down the warlord poseur horde.
> **Note 1:** The Storm King's airship fleet attempting to invade Mount Aris is essentially a disastrous failed Operation Sea Lion
> The defense of Mount Aris is a triumph of industrial and artisan competence over pure, blustering "Manosphere" intimidation. The Storm King brought a psychological weapon to a physics fight.
> **Note 6:** The massive horde rallies in Ain Trotgourait across the strait from Mount Aris and terrorize the local zebra population while the Storm King summons all warlords in preparation of the invasion. The volunteer air forces can see the horde growing and growing.
> **Note 10:** The Storm King gets the lifting gas for his airships by extracting natural hydrogen and healing vents dry, and then burning guano and forest wood in kilns to capture the syngas (hydrogen and carbon monoxide) for a highly toxic mix of gas to keep the airships running. The airships are highly explosive.
> **Note 11:** Zeppelins can float higher than the range of a musket, and even if punctured, the hydrogen leaks out at a slow rate and there are many compartments. Impoverished warlords with muskets can't do anything against the Zeppelins and get bombed if they try to resist. But the moment they face fighter planes with white phosphorus rounds at Mount Aris, the entire zeppelin fleet explodes and crashes into the ocean.
> **Note 0:** Queen Novo reached out to Celestia for help, but Celestia had nothing to give in terms of military support. Celestia offered Queen Novo to evacuate but cannot tell Equestrians what happened to their home. Novo scoffs and rejects that, saying they already have Seaquestria for civilians to retreat to, she wants to defend their home. Silverstream, having learned Loyalty, wants to go home and help her family and nation survive the Storm King instead of staying put in school. So Queen Novo ended up asking Silverstream to tell her teachers (the Mane 6) that her home was under attack from thugs, hoping that the "Magic of Friendship" will amount to something. Queen Novo says "don't tell the princesses." So Silverstream only tells Rainbow Dash, who is about loyalty. Rainbow goes with Silverstream to Mount Aris without telling anyone else.
>
> Rainbow thinks she's going to be Daring Do taking down a bunch of thugs. Seeing the endless warlord horde with guns breaks her world view. She flies back to Equestria to bring the Wonderbolts to learn how to fly the biplanes, which they are naturals at.
>
> The Mane 6 and Zecora track down Rainbow Dash and learn the reality of the zebra refugees' plight. The stagnation of harmony shatters.
>
> The Kingdom of Aquileia actually answers Queen Novo's call, bringing the Royal Navy and air force ace pilots in order to hunt poseurs. The Griffonian Republic also shows up with volunteers to fight warlords for their hippogriffs' freedom, and donate old biplanes.
> **Note 12:** Twilight didn't get trapped by a cage by Tempest Shadow
>
> The evening before the attack, Twilight and Applejack teleport to the shore and speak to Tempest Shadow with a big shield (subversion of Twilight getting caged).
>
> Twilight still fundamentally misunderstands the root causes of war, relying on her Alicorn power and moral high ground rather than addressing the material conditions of the conflict.
> The talk here should be heavily inspired by the lyrics of "Open up your Eyes"
>
> Behind that glowing wall, neither of them is a hero. They are just two terrified Equestrians realizing that "Harmony" means nothing to a bullet. Shared trauma does not always bring people together; often, it drives them apart if they process it differently. The parley creates a fundamental disconnect in how they view their own self-worth in the geopolitical landscape. Twilight realized she is a fake princess of friendship who isn't solving problems. Applejack feels completely useless, with neither the words (honesty) to fix things or a magical shield. Applejack leaves Mount Aris thinking the world is cruel and she can't do anything meaningful to fix it.
>
> After the decimation of the horde, Twilight pulls Fizzlepop out of the wreckage and offers another chance
> **Note 13:** Applejack does not leave Mount Aris naive; she leaves feeling profoundly emasculated and small. She learns that the world is cruel, but more importantly, she learns that her innate Earth Pony virtues are insufficient to stop that cruelty. After the Aquileian and Republican volunteers decimate the horde and Twilight rescues Fizzlepop, Applejack returns to Sweet Apple Acres. She seeks grounding in the dirt, attempting to cope with her feelings of powerlessness through hard, physical labor. She does not accompany Twilight to Canterlot to present Fizzlepop to Celestia.
>
> Twilight goes to Canterlot and is brutally berated by Celestia. Celestia weaponizes Twilight’s trauma, framing Twilight’s frontline intervention not as heroism, but as a reckless flirtation with the exact supremacist violence that created Nightmare Moon.
>
> Because Applejack is not in Canterlot, she does not see Celestia crush Twilight’s spirit. Applejack only sees the results: Twilight abruptly shuts down the School of Friendship, relocates to Ain Trotgourait with Pinkie to perform top-down charity, and eventually locks herself in an underground lab to build magical supply organization/teleportation networks.
>
> Applejack logically—but incorrectly—concludes that Twilight has become a cold, bureaucratic extension of Celestia’s Stagnation. The "old nerd" who loved grassroots friendship is dead, replaced by a "new nerd" who treats the world like a supply chain. They drift apart because Applejack views Twilight’s new methodology as soulless, while Twilight is too consumed by her "Reactive Atlas Complex" to explain that she is actually hiding from Celestia's geopolitical terror.
>
> Because Applejack felt useless behind Twilight's shield in 1006, she is highly motivated to never feel useless again. When Luna eventually offers her the General's star, Applejack refuses to be a mere figurehead. She volunteers for basic training and masters the machine gun because she believes that tactical competence will prevent a repeat of Mount Aris. Applejack enters 1011 ALB believing she understands the cruelty of the world. She thinks, "I saw the warlords. I know it gets ugly. But this time, I have a gun, and I have the solidarity of my troops." The Imposter Syndrome triggers because Chrysalis’s Heer is not the Storm King’s horde. The Storm King was a disorganized, performative bully. Trimmel’s Panzer divisions are a mechanized, industrialized superpower executing Auftragstaktik. Applejack’s breakdown in Chapter 1 occurs because she realizes her preparation was fundamentally asymmetrical to the threat. She trained for a gritty infantry defense; the Changelings arrived with a macroeconomic war machine. She feels like a "Fake General" because she brought a machine gun to a logistics war. Her hard-earned, grassroots solidarity is instantly vaporized by Blitz-Essenz and artillery.
> **Note 14:** Twilight and Pinkie Pie try to rebuild Ain Trotgourait with top down charity, Applejack goes to bootcamp and eventually becomes a general through peer merit, Rainbow Dash and the Wonderbolts do secret air force R&D, Fluttershy I guess just retreats to her animal sanctuary and Rarity would probably retreat to her fashion business, with overlap with EEEE and the parloirs in Manehattan
>
> Applejack and Rainbow Dash have massive shifts in mindset, abandoning the rules of Celestia's "nursery" in big ways. Twilight and Pinkie Pie try to brute force the old ways against the reality of the real outside world, while Fluttershy and Rarity retreat.
> **Note 15:** the battle of mount aris is the big point that TLTT's backstory diverges away from FiM canon because the equivalent in FiM canon (My Little Pony: The Movie) is the point when the Hasbro Mandates completely took over the show, so from here on I'm replacing season 8/9 with materialist backstory, e.g. the nursery shatters, the army is formed, Spike matures and takes a separate path from Twilight

### Tzinacatl
* **Category:** Organization
> **Note 0:** The Tzinacatl and Temberik as Luna's monster hunting army eradicated all monsters across Equestria and the Tzinacatl jungle before Luna's banishment. Afterwards, the tribes have ritualistic non-lethal combat like the Aztec Flower Wars - ritual wars not to conquer land, but to capture prisoners for ransom and prestige and keep warriors sharp. It's their ambition sink since they can't attack monsters or other civilizations anymore.
>
> During the Frontier Era, the Tzinacatl were not drug cartels; they were the premier medical and botanical experts of the continent (carrying on Mage Meadowbrook’s legacy). Their jungle alchemy produced powerful stimulants for pioneers working 18-hour days, painkillers for logging accidents, and contraceptives for frontier towns. They were respected, wealthy trading partners. They were the masters of the "Night Economy."
> **Note 3:** They also fight over the drug trade. Need to upload this context and get commentary. The Tzinacatl are essentially merchants of death/merchants of hedonism connected to the global black market economy, plus they steal and raid each other out of theological and tribal spite. No wonder Celestia left them alone.
> **Note 4:** Applejack’s Perspective: She sees this as a lack of Honesty. They are killing each other over stories they made up to cope with abandonment, rather than admitting the truth: She just lost.
> **Note 5:** The Tzinacatl are Chaos without Purpose. They have too much ambition (to dominate the theology) but no unity. They need Honesty to stop lying to themselves about the past. They need Loyalty
> **Note 6:** The Tzinacatl felt so abandoned because Nightmare Moon was the unifier. That's why they fight over who she really is and why she left.
> **Note 7:** When Chrysalis brought in industry, she told the industrialists in the eastern cities to hire bat ponies for the night shift. The Tzinacatl are desparate because Chrysalis had flooded the black market with red love and made their native drug obsolete.
> **Note 8:** Tzinacatl have always been connected to the global black market economy, so there are some bat ponies who live in industrial coast cities working night shifts. They abanonded their tribal, jungle and drug lord ways for industrial life, even as nocturnal minorities.
> **Note 9:** Ironically, the factories Chrysalis (posing as a Skyfall businessgriff) set up are a unifying force that pulls batponies from all tribes who want to modernize instead of infight. The rugged individualism of the factory is an alternative to the communal stagnation of the tribe. Most industrious batponies leave the jungle for the Equestrian cities after the factories are shutdown. The remaining Tzinacatl are fiercely traditional and anti-heavy industry. That's why the harmonic capitalism answer appeals to them so well. They are not luddites; they know about the global world very well but don't want to destroy their culture and their jungle. When Star Energy offers capital investment to help them accelerate their traditional alchemical practices, that is the key that gets many tribes on board.
> **Note 10:** Need integrate/draw inspiration from the token thestral reforms
> Especially the shadow economy event
>
> Not just celestia who wanted token reforms
> The isolationist traditionalists were Luna's strongest supporters theologically so she adopted their views of Stagnation?
> **Note 11:** Need to figure out how many different tribes and get Aztec/Mayan/Incan names for them
>
> Total isolationists/pacifists (Mali), most inland
> Selling drugs to global market, low quality (profit driven, PNdA)
> Selling higher quality less destructive but harder to produce (pride driven, FJA)
> Accepted Skyfall funding to modernize, desecrated the jungle, got destroyed
> **Note 12:** Multiple distributed river systems is why the tribes are scattered and not unified
> **Note 13:** Rainbow will map out the shortest connections where magic can be used to cover the gap
> These are the areas that most tribal scuffles occur because that's when goods (drugs) are most vulnerable to capture while moving from one rough river to another by hoof/wing in order to get to a river which leads to the sea for export to Griffonia 
> So no central river like the Amazon, but a bunch of little rivers going to the sea and a central trade route of conflict points
> **Note 14:** These are three general groups of tribes. Each tribe within the group may have slightly different products. These are economic groups. Their theological views of Luna/Nightmare Moon generally line up to their economic ethos.
> Group A: Mali's tribe, isolationist, they don't grow drugs. They are like the Temberik. Inland. These are traditionalist isolationists. It's like the stagnation of harmony.
>
> Their theological stance is that Nightmare Moon corrupted Luna because they were naughty. They must stop fighting. (very stagnation like)
> They call themselves Children of the Night
> **Note 15:** Group B: ruthless capitalists/rugged individualists aligned with Skyfall
>
> Opioids are sedative and unproductive, but give an escape
