> The friendship plants are designed to capture the ambient charitostatic effect of a communal tribe, allowing that friendship to be harvested into medicine, eventually contraceptives, and then corrupted into opioids, and finally forming the blueprint of the combat drugs jaeger geist and panzer haut
> **Note 6:** Should the thestrals' echolocation abilities be why they are effective alchemists? Some mechanic with their noise emitting for evaluating magical plants
> **Note 0:** Thestrals evolved in the dense, light-starved southeast jungles. That's why they are nocturnal and have echolocation. They developed fangs to consume the dense, magically-saturated meet of jungle monsters. Being omnivores is necessary to maintain their big brains, which Equestrian ponies didn't need thanks to their symbiotic agriculture.

### Token Reforms for Batponies
* **Category:** Backstory
> **Note 1:** The shadow market - leave it intact, thestrals get paid lower wages and industrialists get away with it, thestrals tolerate for self-sufficiency. EEEE helps thestrals enter the proper economy and support them
> **Note 2:** Anti-thestral sentiments - Celestia doesn’t do anything to address the racism. EEEE!’s fundamental mission is to educate and spread empathy, it’s in the name!
> **Note 3:** Thestral mistrust - this is referring to the thestrals themselves being reluctant. Twilight’s plan to invite them to the school of friendship and do Equestria wide friendship activities was shot down in a similar manner to her plans to bring all creatures to the school because Celestia said it would be too disruptive (she is accustomed to having a thousand years for change to happen, very incremental. That’s why the changeling invasion breaks her whole ruling model) EEEE’s clubs and meetings are all about getting all kinds of ponies together, a two-way cultural exchange. It goes beyond bat ponies and also includes frontier ponies from the southwest in the exchange too. Maybe even changelings from Acornage? Definitely crystal ponies as well.
> **Note 4:** The final event at the end of the token reforms gives the gist of Celestia’s worldview:
>
> Celestia stood silent for a minute before speaking. "This is the burden we bear, Luna. You know the threats that are coming. You know the challenges we must balance. This land has stood for over a millennium, and our caution might be what ensures it will stand millennia more."
>
> Luna closed her eyes, trembling.
>
> "I know these changes are not as fast or as bold as you wish. But we have started something with these new ideas about thestrals. Maybe not this year. Maybe not this generation, even. But change will come, and one day, we will heal the scars of history."
>
> Luna nodded, holding back her disappointment. As always, her sister bore the hard-won wisdom of centuries. The foolish dreams and fancies of the night must always melt away before the illumination of the sun.

### Crystal Enhancer
* **Category:** Technology
> **Note 1:** Eventually the red love canister and draining spell and vacuum valve become a valid source of magic.
> **Note 4:** Crystal enhancers work just like magic inhibitors that go over unicorn or changeling horns, just in the opposite direction by providing magical energy. The magic inhibitors are common in other fanfiction, and actually are kind of canon too. There's the black crystals that covered Shining Armor's horn from King Sombra in The Crystal Empire Part 1.
> Check to find where the magic inhibitors are canon. There's also Equestria Games, and the movie with the storm king?
> **Note 0:** The Crystal Enhancer is a piece that a unicorn wears around their horn. It is made of a composite metal and crystal material like a rechargeable battery. It must be manufactured by griffon artisans whose machines have cutting edges with max smoothness when operated by the griffon who built it. High grade power crystals can be attached to the crystal enhancer.
>
> The unicorn uses a simple on/off spell to turn on the crystal enhancer's magic intake (like a circuit breaker) to charge the crystal enhancer.
>
> Once the crystal enhancer is charged, the unicorn can cast any spell they know and their horn will take the magic from the crystal enhancer.
>
> They must apply Tzinacatl horn-healing paste as well to heal the horn premptively (and reactively if necessary) from damage/stress from the excessive flow of magic.
> **Note 2:** The University of Pridea invented the crystal enhancer.
> **Note 3:** Crystal Enhancers allow unicorns to teach others their special spells. Previously, there was no point teaching others because they are only able to cast their spell thanks to their cutie mark discount for their own special talent. Crystal Enhancers allow unicorns to cast other unicorns' special spells, but still no one is obsolete due to comparative advantage and personal pride.

### Banking
* **Category:** SocietalDifferences
> **Note 0:** Equestria's gold standard is strict, with no debt creation via fractional banking and no interest profit.
>
> The gold standard effectively capped the Equestrian economy. The money supply was tied to metal, not productivity
>
> Tally sticks were a way to circumvent the bits for big harvests in Tall Tale
> **Note 1:** The Skyfall system - trust in the ledger (based on the Dutch) starts in 978
> The stolen Imperial Fleet guarentees secure shipping, thus the Skyfall Marks are honored across the global economy
> The value backing the marks is not the gold bits in the bank of Skyfall, but the value of the trade and shipping insurance. It relies on "peaceful trade".
> **Note 2:** Chrysalis used the Skyfall Mark as the reserve currency for her red love drug trade.
> **Note 3:** The Reich uses a rigid gold standard just like Celestia because rich nobles don't want their wealth to be inflated away. The zero sum game created by the gold standard is what fuels the predator's dilemma. Trust is physical/hoarded
>
> Skyfall banks lending out money creates the fiat currency of Skyfall Marks, and this unleashes ambition, making Skyfall a global power
> Ruthless Fiat/bank credit
> Money represents Leverage. Skyfall Marks are backed by the "Credit" of the Trade Federation (their ability to enforce debts and control trade routes). Accept this paper or we don't protect your ships; in fact we will raid you. Trust is extorted.
> It favors predators. Banks print monye to lend to tycoons, who use it to buy up real assets (land/factories). It creates massive inequality, it is "ambition without harmony)
>
> Coltbert's model is harmonic fiat/social credit
> Money represents future trust. Coltbert takes the mechanic of fiat (printing money) but changes the backer. Instead of being backed by a predatory bank (Skyfall), it's backed by the royal coffers with a 1:1 conversion and his royal advisor word. Money is command. This paper is worth 10 bits because I am the king (trust is commanded)
>
> Kemerskai's Riks backed by federal law and taxes, the 2nd Aquileian Republic's Francs backed by national production and war bonds, and Equestria's war bonds are backed by the collective labor and future. Trust is mutual.
> **Note 4:** Applejack realizes that Celestia’s strict Gold Standard wasn't actually "Honesty"—it was a lack of faith. Celestia didn't trust her ponies enough to bet on their future productivity. Harmonic Capitalism (Fiat backed by democratic solidarity) proves that the ponies trust each other enough to finance their own survival.

### New Mareland
* **Category:** Nation
> **Note 2:** The original capital of New Mareland from 649 to 930 was New Manehattan.
>
> Sunset started as a small coastal village but boomed with the help of griffon artisans from 854 to 914. Sunset is supposed to imply the beauty of the sunset on the ocean on the horizon, but Celestia cynically decided to move the capital from New Manehattan to Sunset (since Sunset was the primary port for receiving Equestrian expats already). With my lore about New Mareland being a penal colony/quarentine zone for overly ambitious ponies after the enactment of the "stagnation of harmony" petitions, the city of Sunset takes on a new meaning as the landing port of the exile ship. The sun sets on the exiles because they were expelled from Celestia (the sun goddess)'s stagnation of harmony.
>
> By landing at "Sunset," these overly ambitious ponies are physically and metaphorically leaving the Day and entering the Night. Because New Mareland is a place of "ruthless capitalism for ponies," it is an environment running purely on Red Love (Ambition). The name "Sunset" perfectly encapsulates the exact moment a pony's safety net is revoked and they are forced into the Predator's Dilemma to survive. Fresh exiles stepping off the boat are prime targets for exploitation by the established capitalist cartels of New Mareland.
> **Note 4:** They break off from equestria after the princesses retreat to Manehattan, since if Equestria can't even defend itself then why stay tied to it? Also Skyfall griffons led by Chrysalis influence then to break off
> **Note 5:** Then they become a Wingbardian vassal instead of fighting a kinetic war
> **Note 6:** New Mareland proves that biology is not destiny. Ponies are not inherently good. If you put ponies in a ruthless environment without harmony, they become just as greedy and cruel as the griffon barons
> **Note 7:** Because the oceans are full of sea monsters, being exiled to New Mareland is a massive threat that keeps the ponies from being too ambitious.
> **Note 8:** Celestia has a repatriation program for foals. The ships that send misbehaving/ambitious ponies to New Mareland return to Equestria with foals that the irresponsible New Marelander rugged individualists discard. This is the ultimate, tragic depiction of Celestia responding to symptoms instead of fixing problems.
>
> The journey is long and their are sea monsters. They ride a slow moving grand royal proto-cloud carrier where the royal guards provide magic and combat spells and the wonderbolts provide air recon. Hippogriffs and seaponies also accompany the Equestrian cloud clarrier for air and deep water recon.
>
> The hippogriffs and seaponies become part of a wholesome bedtime story explaining where foals come from, on the same level as Hearth's Warming Eve (mirroring the real life story of the stork delivering babies)
>
> The "bad ponies" witness the roughness of the sea during the trans ocean crossing to New Mareland. The cloud carrier docks in Sunset for a month while the New Mareland authorities deliver the unwanted foals and the newly exiled Equestrians are left on their own to find housing and jobs. They are allowed to come back before the month is over after seeing what the world is like without the stagnation of harmony. To return to Equestria, they have to help take care of the foals on the return journey, which indoctrinates them into harmonic caretakers; it is a ruthless move to nuture out their previous toxic ambition.
> **Note 9:** Young Equestrian stallions, bored by the Stagnation of Harmony and infected by imported Skyfall "Manosphere" propaganda, desperately want to be "Lions." They want to escape the Nursery. However, booking passage to New Mareland is expensive, and the ocean is filled with sea monsters. Rockfeller offers them a flawless economic arbitrage. He hands them an outdated, Chrysalis-manufactured rifle and tells them to guard an oil derrick. They get to play out their rugged individualist fantasies by intimidating the Buffalo. When the Royal Guard eventually arrests them for possessing firearms, the punishment is exactly what the thugs want. They are sent to Celestia’s state-sponsored friendship seminars. Because they are ideologically compromised, they intentionally fail the rehab. Celestia, adhering to her policy of removing "bad ponies" to protect the herd, exiles them to New Mareland. The Equestrian state essentially provides a free, secure, Royal Guard-escorted cruise (complete with Wonderbolt and Hippogriff recon) to the capitalist "paradise" of Sunset. Rockfeller gets an infinite supply of highly motivated, disposable muscle who view arrest not as a deterrent, but as a graduation ticket.
> **Note 0:** Founded in 649 ALB by Coltlumbus. Cross-ocean travel was extremely perilous, so from 649 to 854, it was a tiny colony of only the most ambitious ponies who are there for the pride of conquering the swamp and settling it. It is the top of Equestrian pioneer spirit. For 200 years, this colony absorbed the most aggressive, restless elements of Equestrian society. Because the environment was so hostile, they still had to rely on symbiotic Pony magic to survive, maintaining a rough, gritty version of Harmony.
> **Note 1:** 854 to 930, as Grover III's knights and Equestrian seafarers clear the Celestial Sea of monsters, New Mareland becomes the "easier option" for Equestrians compared to the Everfree Forest.
>
> New Mareland transforms from a brutal survival colony into a booming, prosperous frontier. It attracts "moderate" pioneers who possess strong Equestrian ideals of Harmony and justice.
>
> The Threat of the Holy War: New Mareland is geographically adjacent to Aquileia. If the New Mareland militias—flush with pioneer confidence and Equestrian morality—discover that Griffon Lords are keeping Aquileian ponies as abused, dressed-up pets, they will not write strongly worded letters. They will launch a crusade across the strait.
>
> Grover III and Celestia absolutely do not want a world war. To prevent this, the New Mareland elites (the original 649 ALB pioneer families who now control the ports and trade) actively collude with Celestia and Grover III. They suppress all information regarding Aquileian serfdom. They redirect the Tzinacatl refugee rafts to New Horseleans (on Equus) specifically to keep the New Mareland populace ignorant and the lucrative Griffonian trade routes open.
> **Note 3:** After transforming into a rugged individualist capitalist hellscape, New Mareland still refuses Aquileian refugees but for different reasons. The Aquileian Warlords are a massive market for the New Mareland tycoons, so accepting runaway pony serfs would be "stealing the legal property" of their most lucrative trade partners. The Tycoons view the Aquileian ponies not as brothers, but as a geopolitical inconvenience. Meanwhile, the working-class ponies of New Mareland (the descendants of the pioneers and the newly arriving exiles from Equestria) have been infected by the "temporarily embarrassed millionaire" propaganda. They do not want Aquileian refugees arriving because they view them as cheap, desperate labor that will undercut their wages in the factories. Their pioneer solidarity has completely decayed into economic xenophobia.

### EEEE!
* **Category:** Organization
> **Note 0:** Equestrian Equality, Empathy, and Education!
> EEEE! is a pun for the shrieking sound of a happy bat pony.
> **Note 1:** It is a progressive grassroots organization that formed in Manehattan for the cause of equal rights for batponies, then expanded to a social club for all factory workers
> **Note 2:** Celestia's token reforms left the batponies as still second-class citizens. EEEE! aims to bridge the remaining gap.
> **Note 3:** They reject and police the drug dealers from the jungle because one bad apple implicates all of them
> **Note 4:** Meta-Narrative - Unions
> Corrupt Unions like in NYC who love Andrew Cuomo operate like the MPA Thugs. They are rent-seekers who force industrialists to pay high wages for little work, fraud, and arbitrary rules like "only one type of worker can turn screws". They are a labor cartel.
> Strategic Unions like the UAW and Shawn Fain aim to align the workforce and stakeholders to secure long term viability against short term greed or external threats. This is what Verany does to present a unified front against the thugs and Skyfall. He drops Rugged Individualism because it is inefficient in a corrupt state and even the bosses like himself have been humiliated and humbled. Old Verany hated unions because he thought workers would become lazy parasites, but the new Verany realizes the state and thugs are the real parasites. Verany learned that solitary beasts get eaten.

### AJ The Collaborator
* **Category:** Concept
> **Note 0:** This story is a "fork" of P&K (The Princess and the Kaiser) where when Luna gives the Tall Tale retreat order, Applejack refuses instead of agreeing. In P&K, she became a collaborator after the war was hopeless, just like Phillipe Petain, the Lion of Verdun. She ran a huge apple farm of pony slave labor in order to protect her family from further harm after the war was lost. That is the nightmare Applejack has in chapters 1 and 2.
> **Note 1:** Synovial and VOPS think Applejack is a pessimistic patriot. She'll surrender to the bauleiters and work with them to save the soil after seeing Celestia's weakness and incompetence
> **Note 2:** Meta-Narrative - the story's title
> The Lioness of Tall Tale starts out as Synovial's prediction of being a strong defender turned collaborator, then ends as being the one who forces predators to the table (strong to be merciful, exposing poseurs)

### Applejack's Parents
* **Category:** Backstory
> **Note 0:** The feud is because of the Stagnation of Harmony. Since the economy is stagnant, sales are a zero sum game. There's also the boredom of peace. Without external threats or great causes to fuel growth, and the stakes of the world are so low, the feud is the only thing that has higher purpose besides growing the fruit itself. The feud is a manifestation of ambition when all other avenues (growth, profit) are lobotomized from society.
>
> The apple and pear family feud means they tragically felt their family farms were prisons that prevented them from fully loving each other (and also weakened their earth pony magic due to loss of pride and belonging). They wanted to end the tribalism at any cost.
>
> The recruitment of Applejack’s parents highlights the fundamental flaw in Chrysalis and Gilded Lily’s psychological architecture: their inability to process Pink Love (Empathy) as a catalyst for ambition.
> **Note 1:** Around 988, around the time Apple Bloom was born, they got the opportunity to go to Skyfall to learn about industry in a seminar hosted by Gilded Lily, the greatest Equestrian tycoon in Skyfall
> This represents radical autonomy from the feud. Pear Butter wasn't happy at Apple Family reunions. Granny loved her, but the rest of the family didn't.
>
> Gilded Lily misinterpreted the feud as enough motivation for Bright Mac and Pear Butter to abandon their families and embrace the tycoon lifestyle. She didn't expect them to keep their consciences.
> **Note 2:** Throughout Applejack's young childhood, the family went on frequent trips to Manehattan, and her parents were often gone for days at a time
> **Note 3:** Her parents decided to relocate to Manehattan to start the business, but Applejack did not like the rapidly industrializing city life (Chrysalis shell companies and students) and all its posture, preferring the honesty of the farm. When she decided to go against her parents' wishes and go back to the farm, she got her cutie mark. She does not tell her friends the full truth of her cutie mark story; they only get the canon version that she went to Manehattan, didn't like it, and came home. The reality is she had already spent years taking trips to Manehattan.
> **Note 4:** Big Mac stayed on the farm with Granny Smith the whole time because he already had his cutie mark and was soft spoken. He already knew the city was not for him. Applejack wasn't soft spoken, but still didn't like it.
> **Note 5:** Bright Mac and Pear Butter visit home once a month or two months or so. They put on "country personas" when they come home. Applejack's friends know they live in Manehattan, but never ask why.
> **Note 6:** Their company is called Standard Agricultural Amalgamated (same acronym as Sweet Apple Acres). They buy leftover or third-rate crops from all farms across Equestria, can them into rations and export to Griffonia. They accept workers and representatives from all farming clans, so it is standardized/soulless but has solidarity across all families. It is a rebellion against ancient feuds. It is also a lifeline to poor griffons in cities, especially Skyfall.
>
> The ethos is using the excess of Equestria to bring life to Griffonia and use excess profits for practical education and hospitals
>
> Mashing all fruit into a mush and shoved into the same cans is exactly the point. Apples and pears can be mushed together with no judgement or difference. While Applejack views it as a "soulless mash", her parents view it as "acceptance".
> **Note 7:** Bright Mac and Pear Butter host both the Apples and the Pears who are refugees at their company headquarters/mansion. Shared struggle unites them. They realize petty agrarian tribalism is a luxury of peacetime
> **Note 8:** SAA is an invisible empire before the war. They simply use family connections to buy the third-rate leftover crops, mash and can, then export. Ponies don't know about it. The families give their old crops to their wayward family members in the company because why not? It makes them happy.
> **Note 9:** Under Celestia’s "Stagnation of Harmony," the Equestrian economy is artificially capped by the Gold Standard and isolationism. Because there is no export market, the local demand for fruit in Ponyville is finite.
>
>     The Zero-Sum Trap: In a finite market, the Apple/Pear feud is a literal manifestation of the Predator’s Dilemma. If the Pears sell more jam, the Apples lose market share and suffer. Their Ambition (Red Love) has no external outlet, so it metastasizes inward into bitter, generational tribalism.
>
>     The Infinite Sink: By establishing Standard Agricultural Amalgamated (SAA) in Manehattan and securing export contracts with the starving Griffonian Republic, Bright Mac and Pear Butter shatter the finite market. SAA acts as a Monopsony of Excess—they possess the capital to buy everything the families can produce. The economic environment instantly shifts from zero-sum to positive-sum.
>
> Bright Mac and Pear Butter understand that they cannot simply order their families to "be harmonic" and drop a generational grudge. SAA introduces a corporate leaderboard—tracking tonnage, sugar density, and harvest speed. This is the exact industrial equivalent of the Aquileian Tableau de Chasse or King Discret’s Royal Fairs. It gamifies their labor. he families are no longer trying to bankrupt each other; they are trying to out-produce each other to win the title of "Top Supplier." They pour their Earth Pony magic (which scales with pride) into the soil not out of malice, but out of a fierce, competitive desire to dominate the SAA leaderboard. The brilliant irony of SAA’s operation is the canning line itself. The Apples and Pears compete viciously in the orchards to prove whose crop is superior. But once the crops arrive in Manehattan, SAA mashes the apples and pears together into the exact same uniform, life-saving ration cans sent to Skyfall and Cloudbury. The parents weaponize the families' division to produce ultimate, standardized solidarity.
>
> It's like the Aquileian terroir way of funneling ambition into pride instead of extractive greed.
> **Note 10:** Applejack sees how her parents don't eat the canned product they sell. They live fancy and eat at nice restaurants (patronizing local businesses). That seems like a hyprocrisy.
> **Note 11:** Pear Butter's cutie mark is a preserve jar, that's a perfect fit for the canning company
> **Note 12:** The Manehattan canning guild before industrialization was using heavy tin-plated steel cans and sealing them with lead-based solder. The parents want to transition to lines of aluminum cans and pull tabs to alleviate the dangerous manual labor. Early exports were hard cider, apple sauce, fruit jams, pickled root vegetables
> **Note 13:** Pre-war Applejack looks at the SAA leaderboard and sees Skyfall-style "Rat Race" capitalism. She believes her parents turned the sacred, spiritual act of farming into a soulless quota system. She views the mashing of apples and pears into identical cans as the erasure of their identity (a loss of Terroir). This is why she rejects the factory, returns to the farm, and earns her Cutie Mark in defiance of "industry."
>
> Her parents need a lot of industrial growth and recruitment of ponies to solve world hunger, then focus on getting profit margins to pay shipping to the GR

### Chrysalis's Economic Scheme
* **Category:** Backstory
> **Note 2:** The red love is marketed as "Failed Tyrants" and "Olenian Taxes"
> It is based on the British East India Company and the Opium Wars - Britain bought Chinese tea but China didn't buy British goods, so Britain was running out of silver. They industrialized opium production in India and got Chinese people addicted, and the silver started flowing out of China and it left the chinese workforce incapacitated
> Sell red love to Equestria via Skyfall and get paid in bits (gold)
> **Note 3:** Chrysalis flooded the market with red love drugs, getting elites and scoundrels in Skyfall, Herzland, Discret Aquileia, New Mareland, Wingbardy, Petershoof, and Equestria's cities addicted
>
> Celestia is naive. The royal guards arrest drug dealers and sends them to New Mareland (war on drugs) but the underlying problem exists and the bits are leaving the country.
> **Note 5:** Then she tightened supply, the price in Skyfall Marks and bits skyrocketed, and she used the profits to build her submarine fleet, alongside MEFO bills (not backed by treasury or tax revenue, but by future conquest spoils)
> Monopoly supply control is like De Beers, the South African diamond mining monopoly, and also OPEC
>
> Chrysalis essentially drains Skyfall and Equestria's liquidity using a vice with inelastic demand
> **Note 1:** Chrysalis has Gilded Lily run a recruitement scheme to find the most ambitious, ruthless Equestrians who embody "capital has no country" to become tycoons
>
> They exported goods (Oil, consumer goods, food) to the global economy and collected payment in Skyfall Marks. When Equestrians bought goods, they paid in bits. The bits and raw materials flowed out of Equestria in exchange for Skyfall Marks. They fueled the Great Leap Forward.
> **Note 6:** After Olenia surrendered in 1008, the love taxes put the MEFO bills on a path towards solvency and the Chrysalis flooded the global market with red love again for another round of Skyfall Mark financing to buy more weapons and industrial equipment from Skyfall. This is when Rockfeller and other tycoons started stockpiling red love to sell when Chrysalis inevitably tightened supply. Red Love is safer than paper money because it will always have value.
> **Note 7:** In 1011, the submarines deploy and trap Equestria (based on U boats), incentivizing collaboration along with the "happy" Olenia that got civilian investment. Red love supplies for export shrink again to fund the war and because the red love goes to conscripts. Skyfall Marks are useless if they can't import due to submarines, which is why all the tycoons sitting on Skyfall Marks want the war to end. Also, if Equestria wins, the red love supply will be gone forever and the synthetic demand for the Skyfall Mark will collapse.
> **Note 4:** The price of a canister of red love acts as the index for the entire economy. It is the reserve asset to be used for pump and dump schemes.
> **Note 0:** Chrysalis saw Coltbert's parloirs sucking bits out of Equestria and going to the FJA. That was her inspiration to set up shell companies in Skyfall to teach ponies how to industrialize and how to live big. The Equestrian companies would sell mine raw materials and sell commodities to ponies, collecting their bits as payment. Then the tycoons who own the companies would pay the bits to cover loans they took out to build the factories and also fund their own lifestyle. If they export anything, they get Skyfall Marks.
> **Note 8:** There's actually in game lore about a market crash before 1007 which leads to 2nd Aquileian revolution and many more things
> Should be revealed to be tied into Skyfall 
> Direct parallel to Hoover and 1929 market crash and subsequent great depression which leads to global fascism and war. That's a theme. It's why Eros challenges Eagleclaw and the establishment. He is if Hitler or mouselini and Franco were actually less poser and more genuine
> Also fdr running for unprecedented third term and doing radio addresses to the nation is like Kemerskai's big personality
> **Note 9:** Chrysalis also sold drugs and old guns to Zebrican warlords, leading to the Storm King's horde in 1006
>
> Chrysalis's original 981 assembly line of WW1-era rifles that she used to conquer Vraks never stopped producing. Same for any other intermediate models. Those guns were constantly being sold to Zebrican warlords in exchange for raw resources. These are exact copies of Herzlander models, so it looks like all the guns come from Skyfall and Herzland. The Zebrican warlords are never given the factories or engineering education to make guns. They are entirely dependent on Chrysalis's Skyfall shell companies for spare parts even if they can make black powder with slave labor.
> **Note 10:** Griffon capitalists are loud, proud, violent, and boast about their wealth. They are overt predators. Chrysalis realizes that overt Griffon tactics will get a pony deported to New Mareland instantly. Therefore, her Skyfall shell companies teach the ambitious Equestrian ponies a new, terrifying strategy: Weaponized Harmony, lying and deceit.
>
> A Changeling survives by taking the shape of something you love. Rockfeller does not act like a warlord, he is like an HR manager who use words like "Synergy," "Community," and "Harmonic Abundance." When Rockfeller and other industrialists are sent to a Friendship Seminar, he doesn't rebel. He sits in the circle, sings the songs, and writes an essay on how his oil derricks are "bringing the warmth of friendship to every home." He legally passes the test because the Canterlot auditors are only trained to detect Griffon malice (anger, violence), not Changeling malice (sociopathy, greenwashing, deceit).
>
> the royal bureaucracy is totally overwhelmed when Chrysalis through shell companies and tycoons floods Equestria with industrial propaganda and training. It awakens the inevitable 10-15% of ponies who have high ambition, inducing demand for industry. Most of Equestria ignores the messiness suddenly appearing in Manehattan and Las Pegasus and other places. The industrialists keep up an aesthetic of harmony and pass their friendship seminars (which are only equipped to deter plain-faced tyranny, not systemic extraction).

### Polar Bear Communities
* **Category:** Nation

### Dragon Lands
* **Category:** Nation
> **Note 0:** The dragons are initially a force of nature - chaotic, destructive, and ultimately disconnected from the moral struggle of the ponies
> **Note 1:** Ember feels like she is herding idiots. After she saw her father die, she commands all dragons to stay neutral.
>
> The Elders' Delusion: They cannot accept that "prey" (Changelings) killed Torch. They believe it was a fluke. They want to attack head-on to prove they are still the apex predators.
>
> There are several reports of dragons randomly showing up at the front and getting shot down throughout the story
> Applejack witnesses an incident during the southern blitz
>
> The changelings are not scared of the dragons at all because they are such big targets and the AA punctures them. They don't go for headshots. They drain the dragon of red love with mobile harvesters before killing them and then use the scales as armor. The dragons are actively fueling the changeling war effort. When Ember lectures them, it is a massive blow to their ego and only makes them more aggressive.
> **Note 2:** Spike goes to the dragon lands to talk with Ember, asking her to come to Ponyville for the Combined Arms meeting
> **Note 3:** Dragons forbidden from attacking Canterlot because of collateral damage. Ember is insulted and ready to leave. Only her explicit friendship with Spike and the Mane 6 keep her in the room.
> **Note 4:** She offers the dragons for the Bluebell River Spearhead with unicorn support for shielding. It will sooth the elder dragons' egos and serve the Equestrian Army well
>
> The unicorns carry red love canisters to fuel their crystal enhancers, but if they run out, they can drain magic from the dragon for more power.

### Pre-1st Revolution Aquileia
* **Category:** History
> **Note 0:** Chronology - Pre-705: Warlord Chaos
> Aquileia was a chaotic land of small lords who lived in castles on a hill and defended their serfs from magical monsters in exchange for 50% of the harvest and the implicit right to abuse them.
> **Note 1:** Chronology - 705: Grover I Conquest
> The legend says Leonce du Roc rallied the Aquilean lords to hold off Grover I at Verdame, giving him the title The Lion of Verdame. Grover I pivoted to conquer Wingbardy instead, and the Aquileian lords went back to bickering amongst themselves. Years later, Grover I attacked Aquileia again from the southeast, bypassing Verdame. King Discret summoned Leonce du Roc to rally the Aquileian lords once more, but this time Leonce imprisoned Discret and forced him to bow to Grover I, believing the squabbling lords who took his heroic defense for granted deserved to be conquered. Leonce was then remembered as the collaborator who helped Grover I subjugate Aquileia.
> **Note 2:** Chronology - 705-854: Imperial Vassalage
> Grover I strategically left magical monsters alive in Aquileia and the Empire maintained control by providing occasional 'Heavy Support' via Legion interventions to deal with monsters. This created a dependency that prevented the local lords from developing the military independence necessary for rebellion, while justifying the lords' tribute to the Emperor.
> **Note 3:** Chronology - 854-914: Monster Eradication Era
> During the reign of Grover III, the Imperial Legions systematically eradicated the magical monsters that had plagued the region. This action caused the peasantry to revere Grover III as a father figure and the will of Boreas.
> **Note 4:** Chronology - 914-940: Industrial Transition
> Under the decadent Grover IV, the withdrawal of knights and the absence of monsters turned warlords into rent-seekers who diversified into mining and industry. Displaced serfs turned to banditry, forcing the lords to purchase gunpowder weapons from the cities to defend their titles, creating a dependency on the bourgeoisie.
> **Note 5:** Chronology - 940-972: Centralization and Construction
> King Gerad Discret wanted to break free from the rotting Empire and crush his own warlords. He invented the modern Aquileian State from scratch, spending decades constructing a centralized state apparatus, including the massive Le Grand Foyer, while posing as a compliant tax collector for the Empire. He utilized bureaucratic warfare to break the financial secrecy of the warlords and built a meritocratic administration to hollow out imperial authority from within.
> **Note 6:** Chronology - 972: Independence Declaration
> Upon Grover IV's death and the subsequent paralysis of the Regency Council, Gerad declared independence in 972 ALB, ending centuries of vassalage.
> **Note 7:** Chronology - 972-980: Academy of Submission
> Following independence, Gerad forced the nobility into Le Grand Foyer for an 'Academy of Submission,' where they were humiliated and treated as employees of their own estates. They got stipends based on their performance in State Philosophy exams and bootcamp. They have to reside with their arranged marriage wives and servant pets at night in the noble wing of Le Grand Foyer. They are forbidden from hitting their wife or pets, and also responsible for any of their wife's crimes since they are head of the household. Gerad holds periodic formals where the warlords are expected to dress up and bring their wives, and get dinged when their wives refuse to show, and the warlords are unable to negotiate. Simultaneously, Royalist stewards managed the provinces, extracting taxes and harvests with ruthless efficiency for the State.
> **Note 8:** Chronology - 980: The Warlord Explosion
> The revolution of 980 ALB was started by starved, unpaid warlords fighting for their livelihood, bourgeoisie who Gerad thought he could control like his Royalists but didn't realize they could buy their own guns, and a peasantry that viewed Royalist stewards as foreign oppressors. The lack of local community ties for the Royalists led to their massacre, validating the survivors' fanatical loyalty to the Crown.
> **Note 9:** System Mechanics - Gunpowder and Musket Monopoly
> Gerad established a state monopoly on the manufacture and import of gunpowder and muskets, centered at L’Arsenal Royal. This forced the 'Nobles of the Sword' to depend on the Crown for the modern weaponry required to defend their estates.
> **Note 10:** System Mechanics - Cadastre Land Registry
> The Cadastre was a comprehensive land registry created by Royalist auditors who mapped every inch of the kingdom. This tool eliminated financial secrecy, allowing the state to count every asset and ensure precise taxation.
> **Note 11:** System Mechanics - Standardized University Education
> Gerad built universities in Aquila, Rila and Pelis, modeled after Imperial institutions, to mass-produce the engineers and lawyers needed for state logistics. L’École Royale at Le Grand Foyer served as the academy for indoctrinating newcomer Royalists into state philosophy. Pridea as a merchant city has a rustic university that predated Gerad's modern institutions.
> **Note 12:** System Mechanics - Chancery Legal Dialect
> The Chancery codified 'King's Aquileian' as the standardized legal dialect, forcing illiterate or dialect-speaking nobles to hire the King's pony bureaucrats for all legal matters.
> **Note 13:** System Mechanics - Totalitarian Meritocratic Enclosure
> Chrysalis learned about meritocracy with no lineage and the loyalty gained from liberating serfs from oppressors. Gerad had to sell gunpowder in exchange for talented house ponies and serfs. Chrysalis simply hooks the other queens and nobility to love harvesters and earned the loyalty of ALL their drones. Chrysalis saw how Gerad indoctrinated his royalists with "state philosophy" via standardized education. She applied it to her whole population. Gerad allowed second gen kids of Royalists to leave state employment since they were not as motivated as fresh rescues. Chrysalis attempted to solve the 'Second Generation' problem by replacing Gerad's 'Open Door' with a system of 'Totalitarian Enclosure.' She forbade exit and manufactured artificial trauma through a meritocratic rat race to keep subjects exhausted and unable to question the state. From kindergarten, a Changeling is fighting for their life against their peers.
> **Note 17:** Demographics - Apex Griffon Warlords
> The Griffon Warlord, or 'Lion,' is the apex predator who holds a monopoly on violence through enchanted equipment. They maintain a 'Supremacy Ego' that demands they receive all glory for killing monsters to preserve their political legitimacy.
> **Note 18:** Demographics - Griffon Serfs
> Griffon serfs, constituting 80% of the population, function as the 'Eagle' to their Lord. They provide massive labor and food in exchange for protection from being eaten by magical monsters.
> **Note 19:** Demographics - Pony Serfs
> Pony serfs are a 'Double Eagle' minority who submit to both the Lords and the Griffon serfs. Despite possessing magic and speed, they cannot rule because their 20% minority status prevents them from winning a race war. Most pegasi can't fly well due to developmental malnourishment. When griffon warlords went to war with each other, they used unskilled ponies as disposeable bodies (camp followers) for the traumatized griffon serfs to take out their post-combat adrenaline crashes or looting highs out on, or to sexually abuse when they are bored on long treks or sieges. Talented ponies were shielded from abuse by warlords but the threat of abuse was used as leverage to extract their labor (like cooks and tailors)
> **Note 21:** Demographics - Bourgeoisie Merchant Class
> The bourgeoisie merchants were educated in the new universities and transformed into industrialists.
> **Note 22:** Demographics - Royalists
> The Royalists were a fanatically loyal cadre recruited from abused house ponies and neglected brilliant griffon serfs, stripped of lineage, and rebranded as state property. They were subjected to strict social controls, including a two-child limit and mandatory chastity outside the family, to maintain their focus on state service. Gerad acts as a Pragmatic Employer who built a highly functional trauma ward. He provides "Family Benefits" to retain his talent; the children are raised well and without trauma.
>
> The Auditor has no fear of the Lord. To them, he is just a number in a ledger, not the monster who beat their father. The Auditor is a stranger in a strange land. They are hated by the locals. Their only friend is the King. This forces absolute reliance on the State.
> **Note 24:** Demographics - Griffon/Pony Meritocratic Equality
> Gerad established a meritocracy where uniforms superseded race, making Griffons and Ponies equal servants of the King. He explicitly harvested talented individuals from the slums, ignoring social hierarchy to build a 'Cult of Competence.'
> **Note 25:** Demographics - Lord/Serf Protection Exchange
> The feudal contract was based on the exchange of protection for labor and abuse rights. Knights defended serfs from magical monsters in return for half the harvest.
> **Note 26:** Demographics - Merchant/Crown Gunpowder Deal
> Gerad controlled the import and manufacture of gunpowder through a deal with the bourgeoisie. This arrangement allowed him to leverage the merchants' resources to disarm the warlords politically.
> **Note 30:** Dialectics - Feudal Mystery
> The 'Feudal Mystery' allowed lords to hide wealth through vague promises of crops. Gerad destroyed this by mapping every acre, preventing tax evasion and exposing the true value of the warlords' estates.
> **Note 31:** Dialectics - King's Math
> The 'King's Math' replaced feudal dues with standardized imperial taxes based on hard numbers. This shift to mathematical precision ensured 100% tax efficiency and trapped the warlords in a system where their financial worth was known and vulnerable. It also maximized the extraction of wealth from serfs, who could no longer hide the fruits of their labor from stupid warlords. Before, the oppression was arbitrary (The Lord might get drunk and beat a serf, or a bandit might burn their house). Under the Royalists, the economic oppression is calculated and systemic.
> **Note 32:** Dialectics - Religious Honor
> Griffon Lords were susceptible to betrayal for 'Religious Honor,' adhering to the Herzlander faith that blessed them as Lions. This contrasted with the secular loyalty of the Pony bureaucrats.
> **Note 33:** Dialectics - Secular Safety
> The 'King's Peace' provided physical safety from bandits and arbitrary beatings, creating a loyalty based on security rather than divinity. The Royalists served Gerad because he provided this safety, not because he is "King" or the greatest "Lion".
> **Note 34:** Dialectics - Survival Gratitude
> The First Generation served the leader out of gratitude for being saved from the 'Starving Times' or feudal hell. They viewed the leader as a father and did not question their cage because they remembered the wolves outside.
> **Note 35:** Dialectics - Moral Critique
> The Second Generation, born into safety, took their safety for granted and had the luxury to critique the system's morality. Gerad viewed these 'Palace Brats' as bad investments because they lacked fear.
> **Note 36:** Dialectics - Gratitude vs Critique Gap
> Both Gerad and Chrysalis faced the 'Second Generation Gap,' where the transition from survival-based gratitude to safety-induced critique created a sociological inevitability.
> **Note 37:** Dialectics - Agency vs Enclosure Conflict
> Chrysalis thought Gerad was weak for letting Coltbert roam free, but Gerad's open door kept his system stable. Chrysalis's strategy failed because trapping high-agency individuals in a low-agency system turned them into saboteurs. Thorax defected due to empathy. He is the Coltbert of Chrysalis's system. Hans Kessel broke due to burnout, realizing the "Rat Race" had no prize. Even if you won, you were still just a cog. Both aided the enemy with the ferocity of betrayed professionals because the system denied them agency and erased their achievements.
> **Note 38:** Le Grand Foyer featured perfectly manicured, mathematically precise gardens reminiscent of French formal gardens.
> **Note 39:** Gerad allowed the bourgeoisie to coexist, and couldn't scale his royalists. Chrysalis uses the love harvesters to eliminate all other apex predators.
> **Note 14:** System Mechanics - Law - Vicar Liability
> Gerad makes the warlords responsible for their wives' abuse of house ponies, forcing them to attempt to negotiate to please their wife and failing due to years of disconnect and grievance
> **Note 15:** System Mechanics - Military - Gerad Discret's Gendarmerie
> Uniformity, no special talents, marching in a line with muskets like Redcoats. Unicorns cast shields while the line reloads their muskets. The unicorns may be unique from the rest of the species but they are uniform amongst each other.
> **Note 16:** Demographic - Royal Army (Gendarmerie)
> The traumatized house ponies and serfs find solace in being cogs because before they were victims of arbitrary feudal cruelty for being special.
> **Note 23:** Demographic - Petty lords
> They used to pay taxes to the High Lord in exchange for protection. They may own a small castle and a village or two. When the High Lords are imprisoned in Le Grand Foyer, the petty lords are subjected to Royalist Intendants. They must comply with the king's taxes to get access to gunpowder to defend from bandits.
> **Note 27:** Demographic - 2nd Gen Royalists
> They are bored, educated, and ambitious. They don't want to be invisible cogs like their parents. Their traumatized parents felt General Gaudreau was a safe enough employer and let their kids leave Le Grand Foyer to become managers. Gaudreau uses them to replace his petty lord vassals who aren't up to the task of modernizing and bending the knee to his daughter's orders. The petty lord gets to sit in the castle passively while the royalist manager runs the show as the De Facto Lord of a Barony and bring efficiency through modern science. This is the end of arbitrary violence in Vinovia and the start of efficiency and cash flow.
> **Note 28:** Dialectics - King's mandate
> In Aquileia, Gerad's check on power was the bourgeoisie. In Herzland, it's the Archons
> **Note 29:** Dialectics - Urban Standardization
> Bourgeoisie embrace standardization for economies of scale and greed. Gerad Discret embraced standardization for control and loyalty of the traumatized.
> **Note 20:** Demographics - Griffons vs Pegasi
> Griffons view excessive flight as a privilege of the wealthy and knights. Pegasi serfs are so malnourished that their wings tragically only partially develop (like Scootaloo). To them, the sky represents danger—it is the domain of the Griffon Lords.
> **Note 40:** King Gerad invested in bourgeois farmers to ruthlessly grow monoculture wheat and potatoes around the cities, taxing heavily for a return on investment to feed the Royalists 
> When the land became infertile, they built tenements and factories over it 
> Then used fertilizer, copied the process from Herzland
> These massive industrial agriculture farms have no terroir or local ownership, it's wage slaves

### Grover III's Enlightenment (854 ALB to 914 ALB)
* **Category:** History
> **Note 0:** Timeline - Early Warlord Era
> Before Grover III, there was feudalism across Griffonia
> In real life, feudalism was justified by protection from Vikings, Magyars, Bandits
> In Griffonia, it is protection from magical beasts like Manticores and Timberwolves. The griffon knights living in castles on a hill run an "honest racket". They have no control over the vicious magical monsters that roam the lands between villages, but when one strikes and almost eats some serfs, the griffon knights slay the monsters using their enchanted swords and armor that they forged themselves. The serfs pay 50% of their harvest to the lord in exchange for protection. The knights also take the magical loot and biomass of the monster to fertilizer their personal farmland.
>
> This is not a "private game reserve" where the knights have control of the monster population. Each monster attack on a village is like an unpredictable natural disaster.
>
> Because Herzland is a flat plain, the knights were able to drive all the magical beasts into the Scheißwald Forest before other griffon regions dealt with their monsters. Then a charismatic warlord named Emperor Grover wielded the Idol of Boreas and led the Herzlander griffons to conquer the rest of griffonkind.
> **Note 1:** Timeline - Grover III Enlightenment
> Grover III was traumatized by his father's crusades against the Riverlands and recruited the knights to slay all the monsters throughout the Empire instead of fight foreign wars. He doesn't care for Herzlander supremacy and wants to form an enlightened, pan-griffonian identity of all knights against monsters to bring about a golden age of safety and prosperity.
> Manticores and Hydras have magical hides. "Soulless" Bessemer steel (which has no Ambition in the lattice) shatters against them. To kill a monster, you need Enchanted Steel. To join the Imperial Legion, you had to forge your own breastplate and halberd. This proved you had the Ambition (Magic) necessary to pierce a monster's hide. The Commoners loved them. They were true heroes, using their "Selfish" magic for the "Public Good." Once all the monsters were slain, Grover III gave "swords into plowshares" grants (like the GI bill) for the knights to become artisans, making civilian tools for prosperity. They used coal/steam power to operate the machines that have cutting edges enchanted with their magic which they learned to build themselves at universities. This is traditional griffon artisanship.
> **Note 2:** Demographic - Archon Clergy
> Before Grover III, the Archon Clergy had a monopoly on hand copied books. Grover III invented the printing press, but used the Archons to maintain a delicate balance of the moral social contract. The new industrial barons are committing blatant heresy by claiming that factory ledgers are as holy as the Book of Boreas, and Grover IV is agreeing with them.
>
> The Archons would fiercely push back, insisting that the "Lion/Eagle" dynamic is sacred and that factory-born wealth is "soulless" compared to the individualized magic of the forge. But the industrialists have the money, the guns, and the Emperor's ear.
> **Note 3:** Demographic - Displaced Serf Bandits
> Many farmers are displaced by industrial fertilizer and diesel tractors. They either become bandits or factory workers.
> **Note 4:** Concepts - Magic vs Industrialization
> Grover III restricted new chemistry and physics applications to artisan workshops, where griffons had to learn to make machines and operated them, giving them magical cutting/processing properties just like traditional armor. Making new products is an individual and moral process where the artisans are bound by chivalry and Boreas's moral judgement.
>
> Industrial processes like bessemer and haber-bosch and gunpowder have no morality built in.
>
> He remembers the Pre-Empire days when Warlords hoarded the monster loot to keep the serfs subjugated.
>
> He looks at the blueprints for massive chemical plants and realizes that only the ultra-wealthy will be able to afford to build them. If he allows this technology, the newly liberated middle-class artisan griffons will be outcompeted and forced into wage slavery.
> **Note 5:** Epistemological - Composite Warlord Myths
> Grover III's canonical history published with the Archons using his new printing presses say "Grover I used the Idol of Boreas to conquer the continent" and "Grover II carried on the crusade to the Riverlands" and each ruled for 80 and 69 years. The reality is that Grover III's father was a warmonger who died in his 30s, leaving Grover III traumatized and pursuing a peaceful enlightenment, inventing the printing press. The many warlords were canonized as the composite mythical conquerors of Grover I and Grover II and propping them up as chivalrous examples to follow.
> **Note 6:** Epistemological - Fabricated Sparlean Antiquity
> Grover III cannot tell his knights that "peace is good" because they will view peace as weak and un-ambitious. Instead, he tells them that the most glorious, ambitious, continent-spanning empire in history (Sparleos) achieved its majesty through philosophy and stability. He tricks their biological pride into striving for enlightenment by dressing it up as ancient, lost glory.
>
> The oral traditions and legends simply say Sparleos and Karthinia ruled over vast amounts of Griffonia for extended periods.
> **Note 7:** Epistemological - High Academic Herzlander
> Grover III makes all administration and elites speak a standardized academic Herzlander with Sparlean (greek and roman) terms, like Standard German, Roman Latin, and Mandarin Chinese/unified writing system.
> **Note 8:** Epistemological - Truth vs Religious Dogma
> In Herzland where everyone is biologically the same, they are told: if you are a peasant it is because you are a failure. The Lions have a divine mandate to rule and conquer others, shown by how the Idol of Boreas glows in their presence.
>
> Grover III's enlightement revealed the falsehood of the Archons' religion and that the Idol of Boreas was just a rock that reacted to ambition. There is no divine mandate. Grover III begins consolidating state power and standardizing knowledge to prepare to expose the Archons' lies via the printing press and declaring a secular, peaceful Empire.
>
> But if the nobility realized their ancestors were just violent extortionists, they would have no moral qualms about becoming ruthless, industrialized robber-barons in the present. Also his scientists also invented the bessemer process, haber-bosch process and gunpowder. He realized science had no moral safeguards, but religion does via shaming. The Archons had written tales of glorious, righteous crusades, divine mandates, honor, duty, state service, and noble sacrifice. Faced with the choice between a society restrained by a fraudulent religion or a society destroyed by unconstrained, industrialized greed, Grover III chooses the lie. So Grover III and the Archons reconciled and published the fake canonized history of Grover I and Grover II, inventing chivalry to keep the griffons moral. The knights are not allowed to be violent extortionists, that would be heresy; they must be "noble protectors of the realm".
> **Note 9:** Epistemological - Linguistic Purism vs Borrowing
> Grover III injects Sparlean (Greek) and Karthinian (Latin) words into Herzlander for the sciences and philosophy, inventing the myth of their glory to justify his enlightenment.
>
> Kaiser and Reich are the original, pure/folk Herzlander terms. They convey warlordism and tradition. Imperator/Emperor and Imperium/Empire are the Karthinian terms that Grover III adopted. They convey philosophy and statecraft. Grover III's printing presses intentionally retroactively dubbed Grover I and II as Emperors in his canonical history book to deter warlordism and encourage statecraft. When Eros overthrows the corrupt industrial nobility in 1007, he throws out Emperor/Empire and reinstates the traditional Kaiser/Reich, signaling the rot of the gilded age is over and Kaiser Grover VI is the ultimate authority (and the Archons).
> **Note 13:** Dialectics - Feudal Chivalry
> It is a myth printed by Grover III and the Archons to make griffons behave
> **Note 14:** Dialectics - Stagnation of Harmony
> Should Celestia respect Grover III (and actually met him) and his bans on gunpowder and the bessemer process for harmony, then saw Grover IV reverse the bans and the exploitation happen exactly as Grover III predicted? That is exactly why she banned industrialization.
> Meanwhile, Grover III asked Celestia how to maintain peace, and came up with printing the fake religion with moral imperatives baked in. It only lasted one generation.
> **Note 12:** Epistemological - Golden Age
> Grover III's texts claim they had a golden age due to solidarity against monsters, but the reality was it was only a golden age because Grover III's knights used the monster hunt loot to prop up their artisanship. Grover III thought he was building a permanent utopia, but he was actually just burning through the continent's savings account. Once the land monsters ran out, he directed the knights to hunt monsters in the ocean. Grover III naively thought all griffon peasants would peacefully farm the land and embrace artisan crafts. But once Grover III passed away, the children of the monster hunters chose to use mass production and industry instead of go sea monster hunting.
> **Note 15:** Meta-Narrative - The false glory of antiquity
> By mass-producing the highest intellectual ideals of Antiquity, the printing press created an illusion that Greece and Rome were societies composed entirely of noble philosophers debating in the Senate or the Agora. It conveniently glossed over the fact that Rome's economy was a brutal meat-grinder of human slavery, or that its primary export was unrelenting, bloody military conquest and slavery.
>
> During the 18th-century Enlightenment, philosophers like Rousseau, Locke, and later the American Founding Fathers wanted to dismantle the "Divine Right of Kings." To do this, they needed a historical precedent to prove that a society without a king could be stable and glorious. They pointed to the Athenian "Democracy" and the Roman "Republic." The reality is Athens was exclusionary and Roman Republic was an oligarchy of families who assassinated each other.
>
> Our modern consciousness of Greece and Rome is mostly a mirror reflecting the desires of the people who owned the printing presses between 1500 and 1800. They needed a mythical "Golden Age" of reason, law, and stoic virtue to justify their own political revolutions against feudalism and monarchy. So, they looked at the brutal, bloody, painted reality of the ancient Mediterranean, stripped away everything that didn't fit their narrative, and printed the rest.
> **Note 16:** Meta-Narrative - Trope Subversion - Dark Ages
> To sell a new era of "Enlightenment," you first have to convince people that the era they just lived through was dark and terrible. The early middle ages were actually full of tech, agricultural revolutions, and complex legal systems, but it was labeled "dark age" by renaissance printing presses in order to sell the renaissance.
> **Note 17:** Meta-Narrative - In the real world, as scientists and philosophers in the 16th-18th centuries discovered new concepts, they didn't use English, German, or French root words. They artificially constructed new words using ancient Greek and Latin roots to give the concepts an aura of universal, timeless authority.
> **Note 18:** System Mechanics - Military - Grover III's monster hunting legions
> No more conscription, peasants go back to peaceful farming. Only knights needed for monster slaying. It brought stability but also widened class divide between protectors and protected, leading to industrial exploitation later
> **Note 19:** System Mechanics - Military - Pre-Grover III imperial army
> The emperors before Grover III had elite armored knights with magical swords and armor, and also conscripted peasants to carry spears and crossbows to attack Riverlands ponies and minotaurs. Spearmen who show bravery on multiple campaigns get to become squires and learn to make their own armor and become knights. Only Herzlander peasants had a meritocracy of the sword. Vassal warlords in Aquileia and Wingbardy were kept weak to pay tribute while they dealt with their domestic monsters. The Aquileian serfs were trapped and abused, which Gerad Discret tapped into for his Royalist loyal machine. There was also a mix of the English model of raising an army for foreign invasion during Hundred Years' War - not feudal levies, but contracts for professional soldiers and Chevauchée to loot.
> **Note 20:** Dialectics - Imperial Order
> Like Rome, Griffonian Empire brought order to the chaos, that is Grover III's mandate over griffonkind
> **Note 21:** Demographic - Herzlander nobles
> Grover I-III integrated Nobles into the State. They are officers, not warlords, and then industrialists. But after Grover III and good times, they stopped serving the state and served their own profits.
> **Note 22:** Demographic - Herzland castes
> Lions (nobility/industrialists) - They embody Ambition. They initiate. They own.
> Eagles (peasantry/soldiers) - They embody Duty. They serve and receive orders.
> Heretics (Republicans) - Those who try to break the caste system, the divine order
> **Note 23:** Demographic - Herzlander new griffons
> Educated by Grover III, passed exams/education and became bureaucrats and eventually new money industrialists under Grover IV
> **Note 10:** Epistemological - Dark Ages
> Grover III's texts claim before Grover I united griffonkind, it was a dark age of monsters eating griffons. The reality is in Herzland they slowly cleared forests, drained swamps, and pushed the monsters in the Scichwald Forest while developing flourishing trade routes, crop rotation and animal husbandry. They learned how to use monster biomass as fertilizer. Once Herzland had no more monsters, their warlords naturally turned to Wingbardy and Aquileia, extracted feudal dues from the local warlords who were taking out monsters, and then began invading the Riverlands.
> **Note 11:** Epistemological - Crusades against the Riverlands
> The griffons initially encroached on the Riverlands not to raid ponies, but just to kill the monsters for loot. But this disrupted the feudal ponies' "honest rackets". Since they are brutes/religious fanatics who can't negotiate or talk the same language, the ponies fight back. The crusades go on for 70 years, and eventually the sons of slain crusaders barely even remember that the point was just to loot monsters, and they believe their own divine propaganda of griffon superiority and lion warrior birthright, so they raid the pony villages. Grover III is an enlightened thinker whose father died young in the mud of a crusade. He recognizes ponies as peer-level sentients with their own cultures and rights. He realizes that slaughtering sentient beings just to steal their local fertilizer/magic reserves is barbaric.

### 1st Aquileian Revolution and Counterrevolution of 980
* **Category:** History
> **Note 0:** Verany was a textile baron and the leading voice of the 1st Aquileian Revolution due to his slogan "Clothes for All"
>
> He formed the PRNA, Partie Republicain Nationale en Aquilee, or the National Republican Party in Aquileia. He wanted to join the NRP in Griffenheim and form a pan griffonian dream for the bourgeois with a free market. He wants standardization and industrial greed.
> **Note 1:** 3 months before the revolution, Verany smuggled the angry warlords out of Le Grand Foyer and armed them. Verany went from a generic rebel into a Machiavellian Gambler.
>
> King Gerad’s "State Machine" is strong. His professional army is in the capital. Verany cannot win a direct fight.
> • The Smuggling: Verany uses his textile supply wagons (which have access to Le Grand Foyer to deliver luxury clothes) to smuggle the Peripherie Warlords out of the palace.
> • The Quid Pro Quo:
>     ◦ Verany to Warlords: "I will sneak you out and give you crates of muskets (from my factories). Go back to your castles and start a rebellion. Reclaim your ancient rights."
>
> The Peripherie erupts in violence. The Warlords return to their castles and start slaughtering the King’s garrisons.
> • Gerad’s Reaction: Gerad, terrified of losing the provinces (his nightmare), sends the Iron Wing / Professional Army out of Aquila to crush the Warlords.
> • The Checkmate: With the army gone, Aquila is undefended. Verany triggers the urban uprising, captures Gerad, and beheads him.
> 3. The Betrayal of the Peasants (The "Clothes for All" Lie) This makes Verany a traitor to the peasantry from Day 1.
> • The Slogan: He shouts "Clothes for All!" (Protection/Dignity).
> • The Action: He literally armed the wolves (Warlords) and sent them back to the sheep (Peasants) just to clear the path for his own power grab.
>
> Verany relies on the Pan-Griffonian Dream. He thinks: "It doesn't matter if the Peripherie burns for a few years. Once I unite with the Herzland Republic, our combined armies will crush the Warlords easily." He views the Aquileian peasants as acceptable collateral damage for his "Grand Design."
> **Note 2:** Verany evicted all the Royalists from Le Grand Foyer bloodlessly in order to keep the district intact for the Republic to comandeer.
> Le Grand Foyer is the most valuable real estate in Aquileia. Verany wants to turn the "Golden Cage" into the "Citadel of the Republic" (or auction off the palaces to his industrialist friends).
> • The Mistake: He sees them as "Clerks," not "Architects." He throws out the people who know where the money is hidden, how the grain logistics work, and the legal codes of the land.
>
> This is short-term capitalist thinking at its finest
> **Note 5:** The Royalists (evicted by Verany) arrive in the rural villages carrying their ledgers and looking for safety.
> • The Peasant Reaction: Hostility. "You are the Tax Collector. You are the King's Pet who took my grain."
> • The "Clothes" Hope: The peasants still believe Verany is coming to save them. They think the "Clothes for All" decree means the Republic will send an army to protect them from the returning Warlords.
> Phase 2: The Realization (The Warlords Return)
> • The Horror: The Warlords (armed by Verany) attack the villages to re-enslave the serfs.
> • The Call for Help: The peasants send riders to Aquila begging Verany for help.
> • The Silence: Verany sends nothing. He is busy securing the city and arguing about free trade. He has "spent" the peasantry and doesn't care.
> Phase 3: The Alliance (The Royalist Redemption)
> • The Vacuum: The peasants have pitchforks but no leadership. They are panicked.
> • The Royalist Offer: The Pony Bureaucrats step up.
>     ◦ Royalist: "I know you hate me. But I know how to organize a supply line. I know where the Warlord hid his gunpowder (because I audited him). And I know that Verany sold you out."
> • The Shift: The peasants realize that Verany is the Traitor and the Royalist is the Tool.
> • The Revenge: This is why the peasants don't help Verany when the Counter-Revolution comes. They realize Verany unleashed the Warlords on them. They welcome Moriset Discret not because they love kings, but because Moriset promises to kill the Warlords and the Merchant who armed them.
>
> This makes the Counter-Revolution of 980 logically sound. It isn't just "Royalists winning"; it is the Countryside (Peasants + Royalists) uniting to destroy the City (Verany) and the Peripherie (Warlords) because both betrayed them.
> **Note 3:** When Verany smuggles the Warlords out of Le Grand Foyer via textile wagons to distract the King’s army, the operational priority is speed and violence.
> 1. The Husband's Priority (The Return of the Brute):
>     ◦ The Warlords have been emasculated for 8 years. They are drunk on the promise of violence and the muskets Verany just gave them.
>     ◦ Their goal is to race back to their castles in the Peripherie, raise their banners, and start killing the King's garrisons.
>     ◦ The Decision: Most husbands leave their wives behind.
>         ▪ Tactical Reason: Women and household retinues are slow. A Warlord needs to ride hard to secure his castle before the King realizes he is gone.
>         ▪ Psychological Reason: After 8 years of the "Impossible Trap" where their wives held all the social leverage (the Tableau), the husbands despise them. They view their wives as "corrupted by the capital." Leaving them behind is a final act of spite: "Rot in the city while I go reclaim my kingdom."
> 2. The Minority Case: The Forced Return
>     ◦ Some husbands, particularly those obsessed with lineage or those who view their wife purely as valuable chattel, might drag them along at gunpoint.
>     ◦ The Atrocity: This journey is brutal. The wife is treated as a prisoner of war by her own husband. This cements her hatred for him and destroys any lingering sense of feudal duty she might have felt.
>
> The following violence once the peripherie warlords return is the basis for Chrysalis's statthalter hierarchy policy of punching down, to bottle up rage to unleash at an enemy later
> **Note 4:** Verany views the Noblewomen as "Parasites." He likely seizes their jewelry and remaining assets to fund the Republic.
> 2. Survival of the Fittest (The "Eagle" Emerges):
>     ◦ These women spent 8 years learning Game Theory, Economics, and Psychology from Coltbert and his circle. They are not helpless damsels.
>     ◦ The Pivot: While their husbands are out playing bandit in the mud, the wives use their social networks to survive the Terror.
>     ◦ Asset Specificity: They realize their titles are worthless, but their Diplomacy is valuable. Some might negotiate with Verany’s lieutenants. Others flee to the countryside—not to their husbands, but to minor estates or the homes of the Pony Intendants they befriended.
> **Note 7:** Gaudreau's father followed Gerad Discret within 1 year. He became a general
> Cecille Gaudreau was trusted to manage the land. No more pony pets, only workers. Gerad drilled in that pets meant weakness and moral corruption, not hierarchial duty. "A pony is not a toy; a pony is a worker. To abuse a worker is to damage the machinery of the State. It is inefficient and dishonorable."
> Vinovia's peasants did not revolt. Verany proclaimed "Clothes for All" (Industrial Liberty), the Vinovian peasants didn't revolt because they weren't desperate. They had a "Good Master" (Gaudreau) who provided food and safety, compared to the "Bad Masters" (Warlords) or the "Unknown Master" (Verany).
> The return of the "Peripheral Nobles" (Warlords) is the external threat that solidifies Gaudreau's hold on Vinovia. When Verany took over, the "King's Peace" vanished. The Warlords fled the palace and returned to their castles, turning into bandits and raiding villages.
> Option A (Verany): "Come to the city and work in a factory." (Unsafe, far away).
>     ◦ Option B (Warlords): "We will raid you and re-enslave you." (Immediate threat).
>     ◦ Option C (Gaudreau): "Stay here. My father's regiment is guarding the borders. We have wine. We have walls."
> • The Verdict: They chose Gaudreau. The Warlords were the enemies of the Iron Statists, so the peasants and the Gaudreau family united against the common threat of "Feudal Chaos." The Peasants rejected Verany because he offered "Risk," while Gaudreau offered "Security" against the returning Warlords.
> Many of the Royalists arrived in Vinovia.
> **Note 8:** The counterrevolution (6 months after Verany took over)
>
> Moriset Discret hired Skyfall privateers, including his own cousin Dennis Discret, who had left Aquileia to be a pirate
> The Royalists attacked from Vinovia, and the privateers attacked the cities from the coast
> The privateers betrayed Moriset Discret by going rogue and levelling all the universities in Aquileia, Rila and Pelis
> Skyfall does not want a strong Aquileian King. They want a weak client state. Gerad Discret built Le Grand Foyer specifically to house the "State Machine" (The Cadastre, the Tax Rolls, the Bureaucracy) that made Aquileia independent. By shelling the district, Skyfall destroys the physical records of the state. They decapitate the bureaucracy just as they decapitate the intelligentsia by burning the universities. Verany and the hardline Republicans would likely make their last stand inside the fortified palace district. The Skyfall mercenaries can claim they shelled it to "liberate" the city, while conveniently destroying the infrastructure that allowed the King to tax the economy efficiently.
> The privateers were paid by Skyfall corporations to wipe out the competition in Aquileia
> Dennis Discret did a double betrayal, double crossing the Skyfall corporations and defecting back to Aquileia. He spared the University of Pridea on Coltbert's request and was rewarded as the Baron of Pridea
> **Note 6:** Verany’s dream of joining the Griffonian Republic (Pan-Griffonianism) is structurally incompatible with the survival of the Aquileian farmer.
> • The Herzland Reality (The Flat Plain): As noted, Herzland is a land of standardization. Its agriculture relies on Economies of Scale. Giant, flat farms producing massive amounts of generic wheat and potatoes.
> • The Aquileian Reality (The Hills): Aquileia relies on Terroir (Asset Specificity). Its geography prevents massive scale, but enables unique quality (Grand Cru wine, specific cheeses).
> • The Death Sentence:
>     ◦ If Aquileia joins a Pan-Griffonian Republic, trade barriers fall.
>     ◦ Cheap, mass-produced Herzland grain floods Aquileia.
>     ◦ The Aquileian farmer cannot compete on Price (Volume).
>     ◦ Since Verany’s ideology is "Standardization" (Clothes for All), he would likely encourage this, viewing small Aquileian farms as "inefficient."
>     ◦ Result: The Aquileian farmers go bankrupt and are forced into the cities to become factory workers.
> • Coltbert’s Salvation: By keeping Aquileia independent and focusing on Luxury/Artisan exports (Harmonic Capitalism), Coltbert ensures the farmers compete on Quality, where they have the advantage. This is why the peasants rejected Verany in 980—his "freedom" meant economic extinction
>
> The Psychology of the Deed: From "Pitchfork" to "Fence"
> This is the psychological pivot that stabilizes the nation.
> • The Serf's Desire: You noted they worked the land for generations. They possess the Human Capital (skills/knowledge of the vines) but lack the Physical Capital (Land/Deed).
> • The 50% Tax: In the old system, they gave 50% to a Lord who provided nothing (since the monsters were gone). They didn't want to stop farming; they just wanted to keep the fruit of their labor.
> • The Transformation:
>     ◦ Peasant with a Pitchfork: Has nothing to lose. He is a revolutionary force. He wants to burn the registry office to destroy the record of his serfdom.
>     ◦ Farmer with a Deed: Has everything to lose. He is a Conservative force. He wants to protect the registry office because that is where his property title is stored.
> • The FJA Identity: This creates the Petty Bourgeoisie. They are no longer the "Oppressed Masses"; they are "Small Business Owners." They will fight to the death against Communism (Seizure of land) or Absolutism (Seizure of crops).
> **Note 9:** Because Aquileia has dynamic terrain, a poor peasant griffon can still climb a hill and catch a thermal to glide. They have a "cheap" alternative to powered flight. This subtle geographical freedom perfectly mirrors Aquileia’s culture—they are more rebellious, dynamic, and resistant to absolute Herzlander-style tyranny because their environment naturally grants them a bit more vertical mobility.

### Hasbro Mandates to Subvert or Address
* **Category:** Concept
> **Note 0:** Celestial advice bad retcon
> Twilight wonders if celestia set her friends up
> **Note 1:** All bottled up is the worst Twilight, like fake friendship. Also The Mean 6

### Temp Changeling Lands
* **Category:** Unset
> **Note 0:** Demographic - Changeling Hive Drones
> They start out all in the same kindergarden and learn Simplified Herzlander. Drones who are crueler than others get chosen as statthalter proteges (and abused by their mentors to foster more cruelty). Drones who pass the advanced language and math tests become bauleiters or jaegers. Drones who don't pass the tests are stuck with simplified herzlander and relegated to factory workers. The workers who don't meet quotas become conscripts.
> **Note 2:** Demographic - Changeling Meritocratic Elites vs Skyfall Elites
> Skyfall Elites driven by personal ambition, profit and status. Changeling meritocrats are only driven by state service and the threat of demotion.
> **Note 3:** Demographic - Meritocrat and Drone Divide
> Chrysalis took the Skyfall dual economy and enforced it with the state. Their fate is determined by their kindergarden language and math aptitude exams.
> **Note 1:** Demographic - Changeling Meritocratic Elites
> They showed proficiency in the full High Herzlander and math, so they prove they have the intellectual capacity to serve the state at a higher level. They are fiercely protective of their status and will serve and order drones around in Simplified Herzlander to not get demoted. They have the language to understand injustice (and infiltrators learn Equestrian), but providing the lower castes with the vocabulary of freedom is the ultimate act of treason. They deal with the cognitive dissonance of injustice by constantly telling themselves that they passed the tests and the others failed.
> **Note 4:** Epistemological - Simplified Herzlander Newspeak
> Skyfall immigrants spoke the bare minimum corporate newspeak Herzlander to get by at the factories. Chrysalis had the Yale linguists take this corporate newspeak and define a Simplified Herzlander for the easiest set of words to teach a changeling drone. The ego-driven Yale linguists believe they are "gifting civilization" to a lesser species by designing a custom-built, idiot-proof language for Chrysalis's drones to read factory manuals needed for industrialization. It is a heavily sanitized, hyper-utilitarian language of corporate jargon and military commands. There are STEM terms but no philisophy. Chrysalis uses it for maximum control and totalitarianism. The drones don't even learn how to thinking about revolting. There are no synonyms, metaphors, or complex tenses (certainly not future perfect tense). The drones are not just obedient workers, they are biological automatons.
> **Note 5:** Meta-Narrative - Allegory - Extractive State Hybrid
> Chrysalis combines the worst elements of all of history
> Modern China - high tech surveillance and rat race (involution/neijuan) lead to atomization (citizens isolated within a crowded society)
> Regan's America - rugged individualism, if a drone starves or burns out, the state claims it is a personal moral failing, not a systemic flaw. The system gaslights the working class
> Nazi Germany - MEFO bills ponzi scheme and racial superiority
> Imperial Japan - death before surrender or unforgivable spiritual shame, rapid industrialization
> **Note 6:** Chasseurs were binary pairs built on passion. Jaegers are solitary predators in a rat race? Or maybe not really, it's surface level peerage. The total corruption of the idea, the posture, is deeply insulting
