### SCENE 2.11: Back in the trench

**Characters:**
* **[[Tally Mark]]**
> **Analysis:** Tally Mark isn't fighting for Princess Celestia; she is fighting for her home and her investment.
>
> In standard Equestria, if a monster attacks, you run and wait for the Princess. In the "New Way" (Star Energy/Tall Tale), you own your assets, so you stand and defend them. Tally Mark staying is the proof that this economic model creates stronger citizens.
>
> But unwavering faith in Star Energy Corporation's command economy is nerve wracking


---
### SCENE 2.12: Applejack and Mali meet Luna in the trench
> **Synopsis:** Applejack is woken up from a nightmare about collaborating in Appleloosa
> Tally Mark comes back from their shift and says Princess Luna is here
> Luna comes by and reads casualty reports
> Some soldiers says Princess! Save us from this nightmare!
>
> Applejack sees a mother figure who will save them
> Mali tells Luna to duck down because her ethereal mane and tall stature can attract a sniper's round, implying Luna has no idea what she's gotten into to neglect such basic safety.
> What's the plan to take back Vanhoover? I want to do those ponies I left behind right. They must be hurting, and I let them down. I can't lead, but I promise I'll ride in the front to save them.
>
> "That is not for you to carry, Applejack. The movements of the Night are vast. You have fought well; now you must trust that I see what you cannot."
>
> Should Mali say "we will fight and win" or "Luna, what's wrong?"
>
> Luna gives Mali a broken look and floats away as wisps of smoke (like in Friendship is Magic part 2).
> Applejack realizes the papers Luna was reading was casualty reports - how many ponies surrendered versus MIA
>
> Applejack isn't feeling revolutionary after this. She just feels out of line and pushy. She feels like a naughty foal.
> I pushed too hard. I should trust her. maybe I'm just tired.
> She goes back to the bunker to sleep.
> The sleep is denial. She hopes when she wakes up, Luna will be back to normal and the war will make sense again. Luna will announce and attack on Vanhoover to liberate the ponies there.

**Characters:**
* **[[Princess Luna]]**
> **Analysis:** Luna sees a binary choice: Vanhoover ponies are sad but safe. They are sleeping soundly. The ponies who didn't surrender and were subdued by force are in concentration camps and tortured
> She thinks continuing the war is Vanity (like Nightmare Moon)
> Surrender/Retreat is Mercy (what Celestia would do)
>
> Mali represents a third option - fight for liberty. When Mali implies Vanhoover isn't peace, it's a cage, Luna can't handle this and turns into mist to escape to protect her own delusion of mercy
>
> Luna doesn't measure victory by territory (km gained). She measures it by Spirit. She sees that even the "victories" are traumatizing her ponies. She believes prolonged exposure to modern industrial warfare will turn them into monsters (like Sombra). To Luna, the Retreat isn't military; it is Quarantine. She is pulling her children out of the fire before they burn up. She frames it internally as a "Humanitarian Evacuation."


**Codex:**
* **[[AJ The Collaborator]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Applejack has another nightmare about being a collaborator, this time on a white picket house in Appleloosa, more scale
>
> There should also be a canning line just like from The Cutie Remark just sitting there in the field, it won't make sense because it's an irrational dream, but then in chapter 7 we'll see the actual canning line at her parents' factory, that's where her psyche got the image from

* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Celestia and Luna’s Stagnation is built on treating ponies like foals.
> If Luna shares the raw data with Applejack, she is treating Applejack like an Adult and a Peer (a fellow General). That breaks the dynamic.
> That's why Luna says vagues like "You wouldn't understand" or "It is for your own good"


---
### SCENE 2.13: Luna announces the retreat order
> **Synopsis:** The next morning, Luna announces the army will retreat from Tall Tale to form a "better defensive position". A lot of ponies from the army cheer and get ready to leave, since they won't be dying in a trench today. Many of them volunteered for the army were not prepared for real war, or thought real war was impossible. To them, the fantasy world of the canon show IS their reality up until the changeling invasion, because Celestia has kept Equestria as a paradise for 1000 years by ignoring the problems outside Equestria. But the Tall Tale locals are in horror.

**Characters:**
* **[[Terrified Reservist]]**
> **Analysis:** Wakes Applejack up and says they can go home


---
## CHAPTER 3: Honesty
> **Chapter Note:** I need to further refine Luna's Retreat order to make it reasonable
>
> Luna’s mind snaps because the data is undeniable. Resistance equals torture. Surrender equals safety. Because she is an Alicorn conditioned to protect her "children" from pain, she applies this utilitarian math to Tall Tale. She orders the retreat not because she is a coward, but because her Dreamwalking empirically proves that fighting Chrysalis results in a fate worse than death.
> **Chapter Note:** Need to refine how Applejack gets isolated and buried without making it seem like she is trying to martyr herself, which she isn't. Maybe her crystal heart fails without her friends? But can't be that stupid. Maybe she is in the bunker, and Tally Mark and Mali already teleported out due to injuries but she got trapped and the teleport matrix shattered?

### SCENE 3.1: Applejack roams Tall Tale
> **Synopsis:** A lot of ponies cheer and leave the trenches to live another day
>
> The Regular Reservists are cheering. They are operating on survival logic. They are scared. Relieved that the suffering is over.
> The Tall Tale Garrison is operating on competence logic. They feel they can win. They are in despair.

---
### SCENE 3.2:  Applejack drinks wine with Henri
> **Synopsis:** Applejack finds Henri and asks for orders
> Henri is resigned and offers Applejack a drink, some Aquileian wine
> He says it was meant for victory
> What? You're giving up?
> Can't argue with the folly of monarchy. This is why we have a republic ;)
>
> Applejack sees Henri's (her competent commander) defeatism and takes off her infantry uniform, giving up
> Henri laughs and says that's undignified in Aquileia to not wear clothes
> Applejack says if you're giving up then I'm going back to being the farmpony
>
> Applejack is drinking with Henri
> They aren't toasting to courage. They are drinking the "Victory Wine" to wash down the taste of defeat.
> This is the first time they hoof wrestle
> They make a bet, if Henri wins, Applejack has to go up to Luna and give her a piece of her mind. If Applejack wins, Henri has to do something
> Applejack does win, but she says you know what? I’m just gonna barge right in there and yell at Luna anyway. I don't care if I get thrown in the mud.
>
> Henri provides the ideology and irrational courage through wine

**Threads:**
* **[[Establishing the Equestrian Republic]]**
> **Update:** The first seed of the idea of a republic, that monarchy is not good


**Codex:**
* **[[Clothing in Equestria and Griffonia]]** (SocietalDifferences)
* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Applejack says she has never had anything as strong as this wine before. She only has had cider. Henri laughs and says this is light for wine, and we sold tons of this stuff to Manehattan for decades
>
> Celestia does not allow anything that requires distillation, only natural fermentation like cider
>
> Drinking the wine represents Applejack "growing up" and accepting the complexity of the world outside the Garden


---
### SCENE 3.3: Outside the command tent
> **Synopsis:** Applejack goes to the war tent, where royal guards are barring entry to local ponies 
> Mali is one of the ponies trying to get in. She is in disbelief at Luna's decision to abandon Tall Tale
> All the Tale Tale garrison commanders are there, but are denied entry because they are not in the equestrian army. Comet Shine and Fleur Bloom too
> She stumbles there, a little tipsy
>
> Mali is outside the command tent, saying Luna explicitly barred her
>
> Applejack says she is a general and must be let in (I need to see my office)
> Uh sure, general
> She stands there blinking, then has a moment of clarity through the wine.
> She IS a general, and she needs to take responsibility now or else all they fought for was for nothing
> She is walking in as The Element of Honesty

**Characters:**
* **[[Princess Luna]]**
> **Analysis:** If Luna looks at Mali, she feels the shame of her decision. So she bars Mali to protect her own resolve. She thinks she is saving Mali's life by retreating instead of fighting.


---
### SCENE 3.4: Applejack confronts Luna about the retreat order
> **Synopsis:** Luna's eyes are wide, pupils dilated. She looks physically sick.
>
> Luna's Warning: When Luna orders the retreat, she might say: ◦"You do not know what this darkness does to you, Applejack. If you stay, you will become hard. You will lose your light."
> •Applejack's Rebuttal: ◦"I'd rather be hard and free than soft and dead."
> •The Contrast: ◦Luna believes Red Love (War) inevitably consumes Pink Love (Friendship).
>
> Luna: "I walked this path once. I thought I could fight for peace. But the fighting becomes the only thing that matters. I will not lead you down that road. We must withdraw. We must preserve what little light we have left."
>
> Applejack: "With all due respect, Princess... the light ain't worth much if there's no one left to see it. I'm staying."
>
> Applejack says Luna is insane, but Luna says the nightmares of these ponies are too horrible, they are too traumatized and her decision is final
>
> Luna thinks pride is arrogance. Applejack redefines pride as dignity.
>
> Not sure if I want this:
> Luna: "I cannot let you lead them into the fire, Applejack. I have seen what the fire does. Go back to your farm. Preserve your innocence."
> Applejack: "My innocence burned down with my barn. I ain't asking for permission." (She enters the tent based on Rank, not by winning the argument).
>
> Luna’s Argument: "I am surrendering to save them. I am protecting the innocent." (This is exactly what Collaborator AJ told herself in the nightmare).
> Applejack’s Reaction:
>     If she hadn't had the nightmare, she might agree with Luna.
>     Because she just saw where that logic leads (Appleloosa/P&K), she rejects it violently.
>     The Speech: "You think you're saving them, Princess? You're just fattening them up. I saw it. I saw what happens when we fold. We don't get mercy; we get used."
> "It's better I die fighting to try and hold them back than delay the torture."
> Realizes the predator is never full. The only way to save the herd is to injure the predator.
> This makes her tipsy entrance into the tent profound. She isn't drunk on wine; she is drunk on the terrifying clarity that Luna is wrong and The Nightmare is real, and she is the only one willing to die to stop it.
>
> She views Luna not as "Soft," but as Delusional. Luna is prioritizing the soul of the pony over the life of the pony.
> The Betrayal: Luna is essentially saying: "I would rather you be a clean slave in Vanhoover than a dirty killer in Tall Tale."
>
> ---
>
> Henri isn't claiming the bullets are fake; he is claiming the spine of the army is fake.
> It reframes the conflict from Strength vs. Strength to Motivated Free Will (Republic) vs. Performative Feudalism (Empire).
>
> The Dangerous Fraud: A poseur is dangerous because they are insecure. They will use excessive, performative violence (torture) to prove they are "real."
> When AJ calls Chrysalis a "Poseur" in the tent, she is effectively saying: "She is torturing ponies because she is desperate to prove she's scary. If we run, we validate her script. If we stand, we expose the prop."
>
> Luna cannot handle this. If Chrysalis is just a "Poseur," then the Dream Data (the torture) loses its mystical weight and becomes just... bullying.
> It implies that 1,000 years of Alicorn magic is being defeated by a grifter in a costume.
> The Expulsion: Luna throws AJ out not because AJ is wrong, but because AJ makes Luna feels small. To be defeated by a Monster is a tragedy; to be defeated by a Poseur is a farce.
>
> Luna snaps. She has seen the Torture (The Resistors). She cannot explain the "Clean Occupation" vs. "Torture" binary to a farmpony who thinks it's all just "Draining."
>
> You cannot understand!
>
> ---
>
> Get out, Applejack. I do not wish to speak with anypony.
> You're insane!
>
> Applejack, look at me. You had a dream about choosing to collaborate and the shame. I am saving you from the guilt of that choice. (not sure if this is too explicit)
> You think that dream represents what I want to do? No, it was a nightmare I reject. If we walk away now, that is our future!
>
> Luna says the enemy is a monster. We cannot stop the tide; we can only endure it. Vanhoover is safe.
> That is impossible. A victim cannot be safe. The enemy is not a monster. They are poseurs. They only act violent to look strong. If we run, it proves them right. If we punch them back, they'll run home with their tail between their legs.
>
> I am the Princess of the Night.
> And I am the Element of Honesty. The truth outranks the crown.
> Why did you run away last night?

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** When Applejack uses her General rank to get into the tent, it is the first time she is being "Honest" with herself about who she is. She isn't just a farm pony anymore; she is a leader, whether she likes it or not.
>
> The Aquileian wine lets Applejack say the hard truth, breaking her conditioning that she is just a farmpony that must listen to the princess
> The fact that the wine was needed reinforces the imposter syndrome
>
> Applejack rejects the Vichy logic. Saving our bodies at the cost of freedom is a false economy. That's the honest/hard truth.
>
> When she gets behind the machine gun (as a grunt) and wins, she proves to herself that Applejack the Mare is stronger than Applejack the General.
>
> The Tent Entry: When she enters the tent in Chapter 3, she isn't a General reporting to a Princess. She is a Victor reporting to a Coward. The lack of a uniform emphasizes that her authority comes from the mud on her hooves, not the stars on her collar.

* **[[Princess Luna]]** (Antagonist, Impact: CoreValueDemonstration)
> **Analysis:** "Liberty" is an abstract concept to Luna, who has only lived in a feudal era. "Pain" she is familiar with. It's her only metric.
>
> Luna says she must protect the soldiers' innocence. They are too traumatized. She can feel their nightmares. Applejack interprets it as total cowardice and nonsensical.
>
> Luna’s "Dishonesty": Luna isn't lying with malice; she is lying to herself. By retreating, she believes she is "saving" her ponies from nightmares and death. But this is a falsehood. She is only delaying their death and allowing the changelings to gain ground. She is prioritizing the feeling of safety over the reality of survival.
>
> The twist we learn later is that Luna isn't just processing through nightmares of ponies getting attacked by changelings. She is also seeing ponies dreaming of killing changelings and getting revenge. It reminds Luna of when she pulled the world's dreams of conquest and lost control of all the predatory ambition, which turned her into Nightmare Moon
>
> The real truth not revealed until much later, after the war, is that Luna was seeing the nightmares of soldiers who didn't surrender, were taken alive, and tortured by statthalters, and compared it to the ponies who did surrender and are treated "normally" in Vanhoover. She saw Applejack's dream (nightmare) about peaceful collaboration and lied to herself that retreating would take the shame for herself (Petain) but really it's out of fear
>
> Luna makes up the excuse about saving our souls but it's a coverup or denial for her implicitly accepting subjugation rather than resisting, after seeing the contrast between Vanhoover and the concentration camps


**Themes:**
* **[[P&K Subversion]]**
> **Commentary:** In P&K, the ponies rely on "Stories of Vanhoover" (fear) and Blueblood’s conscription (force) to motivate them. In TLTT, they have Ideology and Technology.


**Codex:**
* **[[AJ The Collaborator]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Luna directly says "I don't care about our pride, I care about our ponies"
> This is a direct reference to Petain in world war 2
>
> Applejack rejecting it means she becomes Petain from world war 1


---
### SCENE 3.5: Fallout and decision
> **Synopsis:** Luna throws Applejack out with magic. She falls in the mud. Mali picks her up.
>
> If Luna threw Applejack out, that means Applejack won the argument.

**Characters:**
* **[[Mali]]**
> **Analysis:** Mali asks Applejack what Luna said, Applejack says Luna is overwhelmed by emotions and can't see things practically or the hard truth
> Mali is deeply distraught that Luna is abandoning them. Luna is her hero because Mali grew up wanting the Tzinacatl to open up to the world (Malinalli means grass, meadow)
> When Luna turned out to be a harmonist and not Nightmare Moon, it validated Mali’s worldview
> Now Luna is abandoning them
> If our princess has given up, who do we turn to? Mali asked
> Star Energy Corporation? Applejack offered with a wry smile. They’re the ones with the foresight and backbone to actually put up a fight and protect the ponies of Tall Tale
> We don’t have a princess to follow, only each other
> Mali nodded. I can't go back home, not when the changelings are still coming, just to delay
> The ponies here are our friends
> What should we do?
> Applejack says she'll stay


**Threads:**
* **[[Establishing the Equestrian Republic]]**
> **Update:** Applejack realizes that the she and the Equestrian Army volunteers were fighting because Luna told them to. Tall Tale volunteers are fighting for each other. The Aquileian volunteers are fighting because they hate tyrants. They believe in freedom, fighting for a land that isn't there's.
> We shouldn't fight for Luna. We should fight for each other and for freedom.


---
### SCENE 3.6: Applejack gives the hard truth speech
> **Synopsis:** Applejack wants Star Energy's radio to broadcast it, so she goes to Comet Shine. She tells him get all radios to tune to Star Energy's channel across the city and frontline
>
> Henri slaps a star on Applejack's infantry uniform
>
> Applejack’s speech isn't just a rally; it’s a truth bomb. The "Honest" truth is that retreating means losing. The "Honest" truth is that to save Equestria, ponies have to die in Tall Tale.
>
> ◦This is the core of "Honesty." She was honest about the danger, honest about the cost, and honest about staying. That honesty bought her an army.
>
> Luna and most of the army still leave, although some stay and fight, including the whole Tall Tale Garrison.
>
> Should Applejack mention poseurs in the speech? Or does that reveal the playbook to the enemy?
> They march in lockstep to intimidate you. Why? Because they are hollow. They are copying an Empire because they don't know how to be one.
>
> I am not ordering you to stay. I am telling you that I am staying. I will not go home to see my family until the job is done. They shall not pass.
> We cannot trade freedom for safety because it is a lie.

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** She is stepping up to her title as General
>
> She is Eisenhower here
>
> She sloppily slaps on a star to her standard infantry uniform
>
> In the hard speech, she must be the general because the madmare didn't work in chapter 1
> her Honesty wasn't enough to save them from their own delusions.

* **[[Comet Shine]]**
> **Analysis:** While waiting for the message to propagate, to tune the radios, Applejack says you better put your money where your mouth is (she doesn't trust that he'll stick to the end)
> Comet Shine says I already did, I sold my whole fortune and haven't left yet


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** In the show, honesty is about not telling lies. In war, honesty is about facing the brutal reality that others are too afraid to look at.

* **[[P&K Subversion]]**
> **Commentary:** Applejack not passive


**Codex:**
* **[[Earth Pony Magic]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** She says they are digging up trenches in our farms to survive, talking about folksy honesty, and Comet Shine sees it as approval for more extraction. This is totally recontextualized later
>
> She is speaking from a place of pure, folksy Honesty. She believes they must defend their ancestral home, stand their ground, and honor the volunteers' physical sacrifices.
>
> Assuming Fleur already briefed her, Comet Shine hears a ruthless, pragmatic commander giving him the green light to continue processing the volunteers' phosphorus-rich topsoil to manufacture incendiary shells. He thinks Applejack is speaking in coded metaphors about the chemical reality, while she is speaking literally about community values.
>
> I've seen what these farmers are giving up. They're taking shovels to their own family orchards. They're digging trenches through their grandpappy's fields, tearing up their own homes just to survive. They are giving the very earth under their hooves to hold this line, and I will not let that dirt be abandoned to the Changelings. We don't retreat.
> Comet Shine interprets it as not giving up the phosphorus and potassium to let Chrysalis make munitions
>
> Applejack talks about home pride, not realizing the pride is literal

* **[[Canon Episode References]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Leap of Faith - Applejack mentions the comforting lie and placebo effect of Granny Smith and the tonic
> Peaceful Equestria is a comforting placebo effect. The hard truth is the predator is here, and we have to wake up and face it


---
### SCENE 3.7: Applejack sees Rainbow Dash
> **Synopsis:** She sees Rainbow at a medical tent, getting patched up again and grabbing another plane
> They say if we die we’ll die heroes
> The whole time, Rainbow was still in the air

**Characters:**
* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** Applejack's hard truth speech solidified Rainbow's atlas complex and unhealthy loyalty


---
### SCENE 3.8: Talk with Henri
> **Synopsis:** Henri says Applejack is a revolutionary, and he's going to stay
> Applejack brushes off the revolutionary comment and says good, because I need you to give me commands while I'm in the trench
> Henri balks and says are you crazy (French term)? The General can't go in the trench!
> Applejack says she still doesn't know a single thing (need countryism) about running an army, so she's going to the machine gun and she trusts him to do the best he can with what they have. You are the Big Boss (the joke becomes reality)
>
> Henri says this will not be easy like last time. They are poseurs, but heavily armed poseurs. Ignore the tanks and focus on the infantry with your machine gun. Trust that the air force will take out the tanks

**Characters:**
* **[[Henri Gourard]]**
> **Analysis:** This explains why Henri listens to a farm pony. He recognizes that while she lacks the technical training (which he provides), she has the political will to lead a free people. This makes the "Doctrine" chapter feel earned—she provides the will, they provide the way.
>
> Applejack is an inspiring figure


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** Applejack thinks the only way to not be a poseur is to be at the machine gun. She took Henri's lesson took the extreme. He doesn't push back, but he looks grim


---
### SCENE 3.9: 2nd Battle of Tall Tale Part 1 - The Trench
> **Synopsis:** Applejack, Mali and Tally Mark are back at the machine gun
> They don't have rain and mud on the other side. The enemy has tanks.
> Mali gets injured by shrapnel. Tally Mark takes her to the bunker.

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** The fact that she returns to the machine gun at the end of Chapter 3, but this time with resolve instead of guilt, serves as a visual indicator of her character growth. She isn't hiding in the trench anymore; she is holding it.

* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** She paints the nose and wings of any plane she flies in distinct rainbow stripes to strike fear in the enemy pilots. Applejack can see it from the trench


**Themes:**
* **[[Loyalty and Kinship]]**
> **Commentary:** Applejack is on the machine gun. Her Asset Specificity in this moment is suppressing the Changeling infantry waves. However, a Panzer is rolling toward her trench. Her machine gun cannot pierce its armor. In a "Rugged Individualist" (Skyfall/Changeling) mindset, Applejack would panic, abandon the machine gun, and flee the tank, causing the line to collapse. Applejack stays on the trigger, keeping the infantry pinned, and ignores the tank. She does this because she trusts Rainbow Dash and the Wonderbolts to execute their specific role
>
> This is an early demonstration that Friendship is Combined Arms


---
### SCENE 3.10: Twilight saves Applejack in the trench
> **Synopsis:** Applejack gets buried under rubble
>
> Applejack's trench gets overrun and Henri's last command is to stay hidden indefinitely. Then her trench is buried under rubble from artillery fire.
> Applejack reflects on the last time she saw her best friends, as she, Rainbow Dash, and Pinkie Pie were boarding the train to go from Ponyville to Vanhoover before the start of the war. Applejack specifically remembers Twilight shedding a tear. This is a direct reference to an in game event in EaW when playing as Equestria that triggers when the war with the changelings starts.
>
> The rubble starts getting undone and Applejack sees artillery fire but there’s a magical shield and it’s Twilight digging her out
> Twilight looks erratic, yelling Applejack! Applejack!
> Her shield gets hit by dive bombers and dirt flies everywhere
> I have to get us out
> Twilight, breathe
> Applejack does the Cadance motion
> Twilight repeats, ignoring the bullets against her shield
> The backdrop is the tanks crossing, Twilight teleports them away

**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]** (Positive)

**Themes:**
* **[[P&K Subversion]]**
> **Commentary:** Twilight not passive


---
## CHAPTER 4: Magic
> **Chapter Note:** Review the details from the original doc and the ordering, although much of it may be outdated now
> **Chapter Note:** The title "Magic" becomes ironic: The Magic saved the day, but killed the Friendship (innocence).
> **Chapter Note:** Twilight and Starlight are not the sole saviors, it's also Aquileians and Wonderbolts. Twilight and Applejack are one chasseur pair in a coordinated maelstrom of decapitation strikes

### SCENE 4.1: Convincing Twilight to stay
> **Synopsis:** Twilight teleported them to a barracks in Tall Tale
>
> They have a very emotional moment and Twilight repeatedly says she's so happy she found Applejack before Applejack got killed. Twilight says she left her research lab in Canterlot after hearing Applejack's speech and her intent to start behind in Tall Tale in spite of Luna's official retreat order. Twilight says she would not be able to live with herself if she just stayed back in Canterlot, however important her research was, and watch her best friend die in a trench.
>
> Twilight wants to teleport the two of them all the way back to Ponyville, saying she promised Applejack's family that she would bring Applejack back and they are so worried for her, but Applejack composes herself after their shared crying and asks Twilight to stay in Tall Tale and fight. But it's up to Twilight. Applejack won't stay no matter what and force Twilight to break her promise to AJ's family; Applejack has already done the most she can do, already solo operating a frontal machine gun and killing 20 changelings. A Hallmark of Applejack's character is that she is practical.
>
> Twilight hesistates for many moments, then says she'll do anything Applejack tells her to do. So Applejack radios Henri and says to name the highest priority target, whatever it will take to turn the losing battle around. (There's a joke about Applejack saying to ask for LITERALLY any target and Henri being baffled, tying into a recurring bit about Henri, a griffon who once scorned ponies in his youth, being blown away by what pony magic can do and its battlefield applications)

**Codex:**
* **[[Rebuilding Ain Trotgourait]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Twilight's has realized that Ain Trotgourait is currently cut off from crystals from the Crystal Empire due to the blockade, so all of Twilight's hard work building infrastructure there is going to be useless now
>
> When Applejack says "Vanhoover is suffering, and we can't reach them", this gets to Twilight. It reminds her of all the zebras who she and Pinkie got to know, who are being cut off now.
>
> It shifts her internal conflict from "I failed to save them" to "My methodology is fundamentally flawed." Up until this point, Celestia’s conditioning had Twilight treating the symptoms of war, but forbidden from addressing the root cause.
>
> That is why she agrees to fight, but only if Applejack tells her what to do, because she cannot trust her own judgement


---
### SCENE 4.2: 2nd Battle of Tall Tale
> **Synopsis:** Twilight gives Applejack wings of dew
>
> 1st Battle of Tall Tale there was no breakthrough thanks to good entrenchment and weather
>
> 2nd Battle of Tall Tale 
> Breakthrough in many places
> First target is logistics run on fuel tanks, which takes out an officer as a side effect
> Henri realizes the drones are conscripts who really don't know what to do if they don't have an officer
> So all of Applejack and Twilight's subsequent targets are jaeger officers or likely officers, precision death from above
> The conscripts start mass retreating and breaking rank or being disorganized. They are not on suicide cocktails yet which is why some are captured and suffering from withdrawal in chapter 6 but are conscious
>
> When the adrenaline fades, she should be shaking. This validates the "TwiJack" shipping. Applejack is the only one who understands the weight of the gun (or the horn). They are bonded by blood guilt.
>
> By the end of the day, the ponies manage to hold Tall Tale thanks to powerful magic (but not just raw power, it will be emphasized that the military tactics and coordination made it possible, as showcased by Applejack's role as the radiomare and intel)

**Characters:**
* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]**
> **Analysis:** The rest of the chapter features Twilight teleporting herself and Applejack around the battlefield and obliterating high priority targets like fuel trucks, tanks, and dive bombers, and shielding friendly troops from fire while Applejack receives commands over the radio and reports back with intel. This is in direct contrast to other depictions of Twilight in EaW, where her magic is shown as purely defensive. In Pax Chrysalia and The Princess and the Kaiser, Twilight's last stand was shielding all of Canterlot for 10 days until it fell, and she ends up being captured and gruesomely tortured. My story, in contrast, will show Twilight using her magic to kill changelings, which is totally against her nature from the show and EaW canon (where she's only doing magical research). The justification for this change in character is, naturally, Applejack hitting Twilight with the honest truth of what has to be done to save Equestria.
>
> The Celestia/Hasbro mandate version is passive, afraid of using force/thinks it's improper of her, and is easily captured. The highest escalation of this is the Pax Chrysalia ending where Twilight accepts her fate in the cave in the love harvester and just tells Jachs to "spread a little harmony wherever you go". This Twilight, who left Celestia to save Applejack, is unleashing her original, raw power (at Applejack's command).

* **[[Starlight Glimmer]]**
> **Analysis:** Starlight doesn't hold back. She unleashes utterly terrifying magic on the changelings, showing the repressed villain. It makes Twilight sad.
>
> It is because Maud is in critical condition.


**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Applejack holds Twilight's hoof the whole time to calm her as she casts destructive spells
>
> Applejack has high organization. Twilight has extreme soft attack but low organization (which further drops as she reckons with the first shot and the destruction). They are a team.

* **[[Perception of Changelings]]** (Stagnant)
> **Update:** Twilight remarks that the changelings look terrified from Starlight and Fizzlepop's magical terror. Could they be creatures too?

* **[[NOT Putting Down the Magic of Friendship]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** The killer guilt of death from above


**Codex:**
* **[[Wings of Dew]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Huh, I thought these were supposed to be butterfly wings
> They are thestral wings with Mali's and Tally Mark's colors
> Silver, midnight blue, beige and hazlewood brown
> because her friends in the trench are what give Applejack the anchor to keep fighting and go amongst the clouds

* **[[Aquileian Anti-Poseur Crusade]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Twilight and Starlight are not the sole reasons Tall Tale holds despite Luna's retreat, it's also Aquileian aces. When Applejack asks Henri for orders, they are one "chasseur unit" as part of the coordinated Aquileian decapitation tactics against the jaegers.


---
### SCENE 4.3: The Hospital after the battle
> **Synopsis:** Starlight is unconcious from magical exhaustion and her horn has cracks. The Tzinacatl give her magical herbs to repair the damage to her horn. Fizzlepop also has the herbs over her horn. Maud is in a cast with a broken spine (but it will heal with magic). Starlight went to save Maud from a trench just as Twilight saved Applejack.

**Characters:**
* **[[Starlight Glimmer]]**
> **Analysis:** Starlight unleashes so much violence that she is in the hospital with magical exhaustion and cracks in her horn being restored by Tzinacatl herbs. She thought she had to make up for the ponies who died or were captured in Vanhoover while she was hiding in the lab.

* **[[Fizzlepop Berrytwist]]**

**Threads:**
* **[[Perception of Changelings]]**
> **Update:** Twilight seeing Maud and Starlight in hospital beds makes her forget any previous sympathy, since she is off the battlefield now


---
### SCENE 4.4: Twilight cries and Applejack comforts her
> **Synopsis:** The chapter ends with Applejack and Twilight retiring to Tally Mark's house. With the adrenaline wearing off, Twilight is thoroughly traumatized after using her magic to kill hundreds of changelings who had lives of their own. Applejack comforts Twilight, saying she knows how it feels. She's not lying, because she's killed hundreds of changelings with a machine gun. (In HOI4, if you use infantry to attack fully entrenched infantry, you end up losing manpower at a 100:1 ratio. I'm not sure how realistic this was in real life).

**Characters:**
* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]**
> **Analysis:** AJ leaving Luna parallels Twilight leaving Celestia
> If Applejack can just defy Luna like that, then I can defy Celestia 
> Twilight already had doubts since she terminated the school of friendship which is her own project but still won't stand up to Celestia


**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** Applejack comforts Twilight over the burden of destruction (which she is familiar with, with the machine gun).
>
> The twist: Twilight turns around and comforts Applejack over the burden of leadership (which she is familiar with, having to live up Celestia's expecations, running the school while handicapped, having to be a princess and setting and example for others).
> Is this part here, or in chapter 6? I think in chapter 6.
> Or it does go here, because AJ is scared she made the wrong decision to defy Celestia/Luna. Or not that she made the wrong decision, but that she now has to live with the consequences of making the "right" decision (the hard decision) and following through, instead of going back home in shame.
>
> Dark twist: Applejack expected to die in the trench. She had already decided to die in the trench over running and seeing her family get drained when the changelings won. That's how low her self esteem was for being the "fake" general.
> Now she's not fake, but she has to live with the fact that ponies died today following her words. The ponies in Vanhoover died following Celestia's words, but the ponies who died today in Tall Tale were following Applejack's words, and her words ONLY. THAT is the burden of leadership and extremely REAL responsibility which Applejack has never felt before, but Twilight can understand via her (imagined early on like Lesson Zero, then real (Discord, Canterlot Wedding (?), the test from Crystal Empire, Magical Mystery Cure) once she was a princess, School of Friendship, fighting Starlight through time travel, Tirek, but ignore EQG even though it fits because I don't want to go down that rabbithole) responsibilities and anxieties that came about.
> Twilight is now here to comfort her out of that hole.
>
> Twilight knows she can spiral, once she sees Celestia isn't always right, she naturally turns to Applejack as the new moral north star because she won't trust herself


**Codex:**
* **[[Chasseur Doctrine]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Ventral-Ventral Deep Pressure—is the biological equivalent of an emergency trauma ward. I imagined the clinging to be applejack lying on her back and Twilight snuggled on top face down with her head reading on applejack's chest floof and tucked under applejack's chin, and applejack's forehooves resting on twilight's back with a blanket on top. Twilight's forehooves would wrap around applejack's back neck.


---
### SCENE 4.5: The Last Roundup
> **Synopsis:** Applejack says how she let everypony down as general
> Did you do your best?
> I did the best I could do, but it was pathetic
> Twilight says don't be too hard on yourself like that time with the rodeo money. She brightens up a little remembering good times
> Applejack says it's different, things were silly back then
>
> AJ's family in present day (Granny Smith, Big Mac and Apple Bloom) thinks her hard truth speech is delusional, like when she didn't get a blue ribbon and her family's expectations drove her to not come home
> They think the promise to protect the family and fight no matter the cost is a similar delusion
> That's why they sent Twilight to rescue Applejack

---
### SCENE 4.6: Twilight reveals she disobeyed Celestia to come to the front
> **Synopsis:** Celestia forbid Twilight from going to the front and also forbid Starlight and Fizzlepop because she doesn't want dangerous unicorn magic to dominate the battlefield, Twilight convinced them to stay and help her research instead. This is secretly because Celestia must avoid Twilight going down the same path as Luna did 1000 years ago, ending in Nightmare Moon (which she won’t, because she has Applejack and can use industry to outsource magic)
>
> When that didn't stop Twilight, Celestia said not to use her magic for killing. Because Twilight is the Princess of Friendship. Celestia also made sure Twilight kept Starlight and Fizzlepop at Twilight's magical research lab instead of going to the front. Applejack is shocked that Twilight defied Celestia's orders, in a parallel to how Applejack defied Luna's orders, because Twilight in canon zealously idolizes Celestia, being her faithful student, and never wants to let Celestia down. Twilight says Applejack was right about needing to do whatever it takes to win the war, repeating how Applejack is honest about what must be done. Twilight even dares to say she doesn't know if Celestia can possibly be right anymore, but she knows she can trust Applejack's decisions.
>
> To explain her disillusionment with Celestia, Twilight then recalls her despair when the war started and the initial army was totally encircled and vanquished, and the feeling that she wasn't there for her friends - on Celestia's orders to stay behind doing research.
>
> Applejack views this as honest. It's the old Twilight who looked after her in Applebuck Season.

**Threads:**
* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]**
> **Update:** Twilight realizing that "Doing what I'm told" (Loyalty to Celestia) is not the same as "Doing what is right" (Honesty/Saving AJ).


---
## CHAPTER 5: Laughter
> **Chapter Note:** When Pinkie Pie is found in the Temberik Mountains, she reveals the Category Mismatch. She explains how the conscripts tried to "Giggle at the Ghostly," and how the friendship shields shattered under the Stuka sirens. This is why Pinkie Pie can't go back to Tall Tale with Rainbow and see other Equestrians who aren't her close friends. She can't process the trauma of the shattering of her worldview.

### SCENE 5.1: Fluttershy arrives in Tall Tale with a letter from Celestia
> **Synopsis:** Celestia sent Fluttershy to Tall Tale ASAP to deliver a letter to Twilight saying not to use any more combat magic on the front.
>
> The letter:
> Didn't you learn from the fight with Tirek?
> Celestia gave all their magic to Twilight to fight Tirek to show Twilight that raw magic for violence isn’t the answer, friendship is what solves it
>
> Fluttershy tries to get Twilight to agree to Celestia (she's the element of kindness. That's why Celestia sent her.) Twilight says no, because if Celestia had her way, then Applejack would be dead. Fluttershy cries at Twilight's bluntness.

**Characters:**
* **[[Fluttershy]]** (Antagonist)
> **Analysis:** She is blindly listening to Celestia and cracked to pressure from the princess.


**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** Applejack realizes that Twilight is disobeying and essentially abandoning her life-long idol for her humble farmer self. "She gave up her goddess for me"
> This actually fuels Applejack's imposter syndrome even more, but she doesn't say it

* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Twilight completely dismisses Celestia's letter, saying she saved Applejack's life. She says she can't trust Celestia's judgement anymore. She trusts Applejack's judgement (!)
>
> Twilight perceives Celestia as treating her like a child


---
### SCENE 5.2: Fluttershy decides to stay
> **Synopsis:** Twilight writes a letter saying she's staying
> The letter is about opening her eyes and not wanting to let others die or suffer when she can help
> She tells Fluttershy to go back to Celestia and deliver it
>
> On the way to the train station, Fluttershy sees the distressed animals, with Twilight's letter in mind, and decides to stay

**Characters:**
* **[[Fluttershy]]** (Protagonist, Impact: CoreValueLearned)
> **Analysis:** She accepts her duty to defend Tall Tale.


---
### SCENE 5.3: Trouble Sleeping
> **Synopsis:** Twilight be hesitant or feeling guilty on one side, while Applejack looks like she's sleeping but actually just staring at the ceiling, so then they silently cuddle

---
### SCENE 5.4: Regrouping meeting after the battle
> **Synopsis:** About a week passes, Maud and Starlight heal. Applejack and Twilight are at the Star Energy factory looking at the spellblast tank prototype. Fluttershy has been taking care of animals. Rainbow has been flying the whole time.
>
> High command has gone silent in official plans for Tall Tale and the surrounding region since the official orders are to just “retreat” so Tall Tale's command economy is telling Applejack to officially declare hereslf as having command of all operations in the area as General
>
> A command economy is impossible to enforce on a diverse, panicked population (like Manehattan). It only works in Tall Tale because the population has self-selected. Everyone left in the city has implicitly agreed: "We are staying to fight."
>
> Applejack gets accustomed to the command economy and the reality that she is in command. She is Celestia. It is traumatic. She tells Comet Shine you run the economy and the Aquileians you run the army. Make me the pretty face. She's not delegating out of liberty, she is delegating out of fear and imposter syndrome.
> Henri laughs and says we'll train you, don't worry
>
> Comet Shine calls all the Aquileian commanders to debrief and reflect on the battle and where they go next
> Rainbow is frustrated sitting in the meeting room. She thinks she should be out flying
> Everyone is thankful to Twilight and Starlight
> Twilight says it was all Applejack and her commander. This is where she meets Henri
> Starlight says she'll do whatever it takes to save Equestria, she will not hold back anything. Rainbow resonates with her
>
> Henri says the changelings have stopped attacking Tall Tale, Trimmel has moved east on a course for Bales
> There's another changeling force headed for Diyarbecolt, home of the Temberik bat ponies
> They need to provide air support and weapons to the Temberik. If the mountain pass falls, the changelings can cut them off and isolate them from the rest of Equestria.
>
> Rainbow volunteers to fly there, then asks Starlight to fly with her to shield her plane, they'll be invincible (the birth of magical cas)
> Twilight says wait, Starlight you're injured, but Starlight says she wants to go.
> What if you get shot down?
> That won't happen. But remember, I can fly too
> What?! Henri squawked
>
> But some other wonderbolt says their planes don't have enough fuel to fly all the way to Diyarbecolt and back for refueling. They need to build an airbase and fuel deliveries in Diyarbecolt first
> Henri unveils Drop Tanks to extend their range, showing off some griffon engineering pride (even though it's a Herzland invention)
>
> Then Comet Shine says they need crystals from Temberik Mountains as well, so we need a negotiating team
> Maud volunteers, saying she knows the mountains and has met a few Temberik. Mali also volunteers. Comet Shine also goes to establish a crystal mine and brings more unicorns to cast the changeling detection spell, along with himself, in order to help the Temberik flush out changeling infilitrators. They go in a truck. Rainbow and Starlight fly ahead with the drop tanks.

**Characters:**
* **[[Maud Pie]]**
> **Analysis:** Maud greets Minette and Reni in a dead pan Aquileian. Starlight looks confused and asks you know Aquileian?
> Maud says she got her Rockterate at the University of Pridea


**Threads:**
* **[[Magical Combined Arms]]**
> **Update:** Rainbow asks Starlight to fly with her on a whim. It's the start of the pegasi/unicorn duo

* **[[NOT Putting Down the Magic of Friendship]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Applejack realizes that the Changelings are weak because they do not trust each other. Trimmel’s conscripts freeze when their officers are sniped because they lack Auftragstaktik (independent mission command) rooted in peer trust.


---
### SCENE 5.5: Air Battle over Diyarbecolt
> **Synopsis:** At the air base while they are getting their drop tanks, Rainbow Dash introduces Starlight to Reni, whose she reveres like she ones revered the Wonderbolts back in season 1. Rainbow says Reni taught her how to fly a plane back at Mount Aris and was SO AWESOME!
> Minette gives Starlight some pointers on how to withstand the G forces in a plane and fire out of the plane
>
> Reni says his plane is powered by his L'Élan du Chasseur
>
> Rainbow and Starlight absolutely decimate the changeling Luftwaffe and the ground forces, receiving radio orders from a Temberik commander (Rasti)
> They also see frequent artillery barages to clean up the stragglers of their brutal bombing
>
> taking out all changeling planes, shielding themselves, and dive bombing changeling ground troops with bombs and magic, but there's is also a concerning pattern of the two egging each other on for more violence against the changelings
>
> They laugh at the damage they're doing in order to mask the pain, egging each other on the whole time. Wildly unhealthy and dystopian but sort of in character for the two of them
> It's an echo chamber
>
> Once all enemies on the mountain are subdued, Rasti thanks them and asks them to land in Diyarbecolt so they can meet

**Characters:**
* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** Rainbow Dash and Starlight laugh at their inferior enemies and compliment/egg each other on in order to cope with the pain/trauma of killing other creatures to defend their country. This is unhealthy coping, in stark contrast to the TwiJack dynamic of healthy coping (AJ stops Twilight from spiraling, Twilight keeps Applejack soft)

* **[[Starlight Glimmer]]**
> **Analysis:** Rainbow Dash and Starlight laugh at their inferior enemies and compliment/egg each other on in order to cope with the pain/trauma of killing other creatures to defend their country. This is unhealthy coping, in stark contrast to the TwiJack dynamic of healthy coping (AJ stops Twilight from spiraling, Twilight keeps Applejack soft)

* **[[Réni Ducep]]**
> **Analysis:** L'Élan du Chasseur (The Hunter's Spirit/Ego) is a psychological shield. It makes the pilot feel invulnerable because they possess absolute moral certainty. Réni and Minette are taking out Jaegers (voluntary predators) and sparing conscripts. Réni's moral certainty is 100% intact. Therefore, the aluminum is magically hardened. He is the untouchable Ace.


**Themes:**
* **[[Laughter and Resilience]]**
> **Commentary:** L'Élan du Chasseur = Predatory Joy, a slippery slope to tyranny


**Codex:**
* **[[Crystal Enhancer]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Minette gives Starlight some paste to apply to her horn when casting

* **[[Dragon Lands]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** They witness a rogue dragon getting shot effortlessly and harvested. They bomb the harvesting crew
>
> This establishes that the dragons aren't a deux ex machina, they are actually useless and fueling the enemy! Until friendship is combined arms

* **[[Griffon Biology and Magic]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Knights explained here, he is a flying knight


---
### SCENE 5.6: Finding Pinkie
> **Synopsis:** Rasti cringes at Rainbow Dash's (non static radio) voice and says I know you... you were that insufferable mare who ran foalish pranks during Nightmare Night
> Rasti was a lunar guard for Princess Luna and he quit after that night, deciding Equestria was a joke and Luna was a massive disappointment
>
> Rainbow apologizes but genuinely laughs, remembering good times
>
> Rasti brings Rainbow and Starlight into the Diwan, where Comet Shine, Mali and Maud have arrived via truck (since Rainbow and Starlight were flying for so long)
>
> They look over and...
> Pinkie! Rainbow zips over and tackles her with a hug
> Pinkie smiles and is happy to see Rainbow and Starlight
> But her mane is deflated and she's lost a lot of color
>
> Pinkie sees Rainbow's desperation (somehow, there should be some giveaway) and says she'll leave the meeting hall, it's important
> Maybe it's Pinkie Sense that clues her in (check if there's a pinkie sense related to being sad in canon)
> So she says it's really important (or it's a doozy)
>
> Rasti looks really confused, and further gives Rainbow the stink eye
> Rainbow nervously laughs
>
> They have a good time, genuinely

**Characters:**
* **[[Pinkie Pie]]**
> **Analysis:** Pinkie genuinely tries to cheer Rainbow up initially and be her old self?
> She sees how sad and distraught Rainbow is on the inside, and resolves to cheer up Rainbow just like old times (her mane is still deflated and color gone, but she does it anyway)
> Pinkie gets a pinkie sense that rainbow needs cheering up, so she does her best for her friend

* **[[Rasti]]**
> **Analysis:** Rasti being the lunar guard from Luna Eclipsed! is hillarious because that scene is the foundation of thestrals as a fanon creation
> This is the intersection between the EaW world and canon FiM that I'm going for


**Codex:**
* **[[Color]]** (MagicSystem)

---
### SCENE 5.7: Mali and Rasti debate on whether Luna was a disappointment
> **Synopsis:** Midnight Oil is the leader of the Temberik militia. His real name is Rasti (Honesty)
> He was also a former night guard and calls Mali Tempest Wind
> Says Luna was a disappointment
> There were members of the community who had nightmares that Luna was in danger so they started smuggling weapons, got caught and punished, that’s why no weapons
> Luna didn’t stand up for them
> They think Celestia wants the changelings to destroy them
> Rasti says Starlight and Rainbow only came because they need Tall Tale defended, once the war is over nothing will change
>
> Mali is optimisic when she joined the lunar guard. She wanted to bring harmony to the squabbling Tzinacatl tribes.
>
> Rasti wanted to see the strong warrior princess of legend who would guide the thestrals into a new age. He was deeply disappointed with "Celestia-washed Luna". When Luna was "redeemed" by the Elements of Harmony, she didn't just come home; she renounced the ideology. To a bat pony loyalist, Luna didn't "get better". She "gave up". She let Celestia put the muzzle back on, only introducing token reforms and not integrating or elevating the bat pony societies.
>
> Mali says this time will be different. Star Energy can do what Luna can't. That's what she's seen from her time in Tall Tale. Rasti rejects the notion and calls Mali naive, just like she always was. He says he expected the warrior princess, and what did he get? Escorting a joking princess to Ponyville and getting pranked by Rainbow Dash in a shadow bolt suit and some grown mare pretending to be a chicken (the dramatic irony!)
> He says hearing Luna bellow "the fun will be doubled" was traumatizing
>
> Mali says that sounds like fun, she should've went instead of him
>
> Rasti says Luna was fooling around like a schoolfilly and generally just living a "harmonic lie" and disconnected from reality instead of working on real societal problems, that's why he quit

**Characters:**
* **[[Mali]]**
> **Analysis:** Here we learn for the first time that she is Tempest Wind, the general from the game.
>
> The Name as a Cage (Assimilation)
> •The Meaning: "Tempest Wind" is a cool-sounding pony name. It fits the naming convention of Pegasi/Unicorns (Adjective + Noun). It is palatable.
> •The Erasure: By forcing Malinalli to go by "Tempest," Celestia was essentially saying: "Your heritage is too foreign for Equestria. To serve Harmony, you must strip away your history."
> •The Irony: A "Tempest" is a violent, chaotic storm. By naming her that, Celestia was subconsciously labeling Bat Ponies as dangerous forces of nature that needed to be contained within a uniform. Also, Mali is the opposite of a violent, chaotic storm. She is cute like Fluttershy and naive like Pinkie.

* **[[Rasti]]**

---
### SCENE 5.8: Star Energy/Temberik crystal mine negotiation
> **Stakes:** Star Energy needs the crystals for the war machine.
> **Synopsis:** Maud knows about the crystals from her travels. The crystals in the Temberik Mountains have similar properties as the crystals in the Crystal Empire
>
> This isn't Comet Shine's first rodeo. He reached out to the Temberik twice before the war, during peace time, and so did many Skyfall companies (Chrysalis)
>
> The Temberik still don't want to do it. They say the crystals are cursed and recount their legend about Nightmare Moon's transformation being the source of the crystals' power. Comet Shine says they will be compensated financially, but Rasti is extremely reluctant. So instead, Comet Shine sits down with them and talks about what the Temberik want how they can cooperate together. He says the Temberik will have full financial control of the mines and he will teach them how to operate the machines. They still reject him! He gets frustrated at their ludditism and isolationism.
>
> Starlight blows up at them, saying "This is war! Wake up!"
>
> The divided Temberik council settle on a single preliminary mine, and Maud is in charge, because they know her and her sister. Star Energy takes what they can get and promise to earn the Temberik's trust and partnership. Rasti is skeptical.
>
> When Starlight leaves, she thinks about how to do the mine herself without their permission, only to realize she is just casting magic without thinking about the consequences
> Maud comes out and says they agreed on one mine let's do it right.

**Characters:**
* **[[Comet Shine]]** (Protagonist, Impact: CoreValueDemonstration)
> **Analysis:** Comet Shine only knows how to talk about mutual profit/prosperity. He isn't a good communicator on the harmonic aspect of it. Therefore, he comes across as no different from Chrysalis to the Temberik.

* **[[Mali]]** (Supporting, Impact: CoreValueDemonstration)
> **Analysis:** Mali wants friendship between thestrals and Equestrians.

* **[[Rasti]]** (Antagonist, Impact: CoreValueLearned)
> **Analysis:** He is convinced to let go of isolationism a little bit.

* **[[Maud Pie]]** (Supporting)
* **[[Minette]]**
> **Analysis:** She says she defended them from the air, but she is only as strong as the crystals she has stockpiled in the plane. They need more crystals. The crystal empire is getting pushed back from their mines.


**Threads:**
* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
> **Update:** Rasti is extremely wary of outsiders and reluctant to work with Comet Shine. This is because Chrysalis also approached the Temberik hoping to mine their crystals and ally with them.

* **[[Harmonic Capitalism]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** The Temberik reject Comet Shine's ideas. They only allow a single mine operated by defender Pinkamena and her sister/friend.


**Codex:**
* **[[Luna's Banishment]]** (History)
* **[[Crystal Trade]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Star Energy got crystals from the Crystal Empire, but the war risks cutting them off. They want to establish mines in the Temberik Mountains for national self-sufficiency for the war effort.

* **[[Temberik]]** (Organization)
> **Note:** They repeatedly say the crystals are evil and not to be weaponized, citing myths
> They view their mountain as sacred, cursed land not to be meddled with or else The Night will fall on them
>
> Comet Shine tries hard to hold back a scoff, and asks for an expert opinion
>
> Maud quietly says the crystals aren't much different from the others


---
### SCENE 5.9: Rainbow's Departure
> **Synopsis:** Starlight and Maud stay at the Temberik Mountains to help with the defenses and establish the mines. Rainbow wants to bring Pinkie back to Tall Tale to be with Applejack, Fluttershy and Twilight, but Pinkie says she'd rather stay here. The Temberik don't expect her to be a party pony, just an artillery commander. Maud understands.
>
> Pinkie says you can't laugh to make them disappear anymore (Giggle at the Ghostly)
> Rainbow will literally say "Remember what you taught us girls?" And Rainbow actually starts singing Pinkie's song. She wants the old times to come back.
>
> Starlight says Tell me she's not and Maud says she is. Comet Shine, Mali and the Temberik are just baffled at the hardened pilot singing a little filly's song.
>
> "Just- mmmmm mm mmmm mmmm mmmmmmmmm!"
> Pinkie puts her hoof in Rainbow's mouth and says "Dashie, do you really think that'll work now?"
> "W-What?"
> Pinkie explains how when they were retreating, she felt one of her ponies was acting not like themself. She deduced it was an infiltrator and shot them. It was an infilitrator
> Pinkie found the real pony later, who had retreated to a bunker and teleported out, but it was still traumatizing
> We can't laugh to make them disappear anymore...
> That's where laughter ends and despair begins
>
> Starlight wants to stay because after seeing what war did to Pinkie, she doesn't want it to happen to her. Instead, Starlight will work on the crystal mine with Maud. Rainbow is disappointed
>
> Rainbow flies back to Tall Tale alone, more bitter and resentful than ever. Alone in the plane, she sings to herself half-heartedly the rest of the lyrics of the song, shedding a tear at the fact that Pinkie won't even sing with her anymore.
>
> "Snortle at the spooky..."
> Sheds a tear
>
> Then she sees a changeling plane in the horizon and mutters to herself "And tell that big dumb scary plane to go to tartarus", pushing the throtlle and slamming the trigger to shoot down the enemy. If she can win the war, then Pinkie can sing again...
>
> Rainbow realizes she doesn't have enough fuel to get back to Tall Tale, even with the drop tanks from earlier, because she spent too much fuel destroying changelings. She has to go back to Diyarbecolt and land the plane, then sonic rainbooms back to Tall Tale by herself, not even talking to anyone after landing the plane

**Characters:**
* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** Pinkie shattered Rainbow's delusion that things could go back to normal

* **[[Pinkie Pie]]**
> **Analysis:** Rainbow says she wants Pinkie to come back to Tall Tale with her, and they can have a party with Applejack, Twilight and Fluttershy, just like old times. But Pinkie says she doesn't want to go back. She can laugh with Rainbow and their friends, but she can't laugh for all the other ponies. She can't go back to being the Element of Laughter or the Party Pony.
>
> She tried to throw parties ahead of Vanhoover, and it turned out she was forcing fake smiles. She can't pretend or act on false hope.
>
> Pinkie believes that her entire worldview—and her special talent—was built on a walled-garden illusion
>
> The "Mask" Theme: Pinkie’s special talent/purpose in her life is a performance of happiness to make others smile.
> •The Temberik Connection: Why does she like the Bat Ponies? Because they don't know who she is. ◦To Rainbow Dash, Pinkie is "The Party Pony." Rainbow expects Pinkie to smile, which is an exhausting burden for a depressed person.
> ◦To the Temberik, Pinkie is just "The grim mare who fires the artillery." They accept her silence. This gives her a strange, tragic peace.
>
> •Maud’s Role: Maud is the perfect foil. She is naturally stoic. She doesn't demand smiles. She can sit in the silence with Pinkie without trying to "fix" it. This validates their sisterly bond.

* **[[Starlight Glimmer]]** (Impact: CoreValueChange)
> **Analysis:** Starlight decides to stop going on a magical destructive rampage. It's not because Celestia or Twilight told her to stop.
>
> It's because she used to brute force magic without considering the consequences


**Threads:**
* **[[Perception of Changelings]]** (Disaster)
> **Update:** Pinkie describes an infiltrator who pretended to be her friend to kill her friends and try to kill her. It broke Pinkie and deflated her.


**Themes:**
* **[[Laughter and Resilience]]**
> **Commentary:** Pinkie says you can't laugh to make them disappear anymore
> She thinks the only alternative to False Hope is No Hope.
>
> The Temberik represent the pure Element of War. Their worldview is built on isolated, joyless survival. They don't want to thrive; they just want to endure.

* **[[P&K Subversion]]**
> **Commentary:** Pinkie staying with the Temberik changes her EaW war arc from a simple "she's depressed" trope into a deeply philosophical choice and sign of maturity.


---
## CHAPTER 6: Kindness
> **Chapter Note:** Need a new pass on the narrative plans here, after I made the separation between pink love and red love and fleshed out the magic system
> **Chapter Note:** Must integrate Minette into this chapter! She knows some broken Simplified Herzlander from her time at the cartel in Skyfall, since that's the language of industry in a diverse trade hub
>
> How does Minette's presence change chapter 6? Or should she show up later?
> She respects Fluttershy after the stare
> Minette development is Fluttershy showing even a bauleiter can be reformed, who Minette dismissed as a grifter
> Minette's usual taming tactics completely fail because the changelings sense her aggression and total contempt and run away or scream
> Only Fluttershy's kindness works
> The pre-chapter 6 should be in Minette's side story
>
> Should Minette still be cold against the changelings at this point? She softened up and gave therapy to the crystal ponies who were victims, not thugs
> **Chapter Note:** Henri wanted to give Mercy to the pows because that is honorable, but was trying hard not to treat them like animals
> **Chapter Note:** Need to figure out the technology behind the crude translators
> Universal Translator I've established

### SCENE 6.1: Henri asks Fluttershy to help deal with the changeling POWs
> **Synopsis:** Fluttershy initially is trying to coax the animals into eating SAA rations
> Please try one little bite? I know it doesn't look good, but it's nutritious, I promise
>
> Fluttershy asks why Henri wants her to talk to POWs. She initially sounds really scared. Then Henri says they are acting like animals, Applejack couldn't stomach dealing with the changelings up close, so she told Henri to go find Fluttershy, an animal expert. They are acting wildly, hissing, screaming, attacking each other and biting each other. (Oh my...)
>
> Should imply that Applejack is saving the changelings from herself by assigning Fluttershy, who is the embodiment of Kindness. Applejack current believes she has to put on the performance of the "tough general".
>
> They have enough food, but they're attacking each other anyway
> Some pony soldiers are almost ready to just shoot the rabid changelings dead, but Henri forbade it, saying it's against the "rules of war"
> There are rules of war? Fluttershy asked
> Yes, in civilized griffon society, we have rules. Chrysalis does not follow them. I don't want us to stoop to her level.
> Me neither, said Fluttershy, shuddering. I'll see what I can do...

**Threads:**
* **[[Cute Intelligence Agency]]**
> **Update:** Fluttershy is telling creatures not to disturb the changelings or you'll get hurt (passivity)


**Codex:**
* **[[Knightly Code / Rules of War]]** (WorldRules)
> **Note:** Henri reveals the griffon rules of war


---
### SCENE 6.2: Fluttershy uses The Stare and talks to a POW, sharing love
> **Synopsis:** the only way to get love is the old way... Biting their prey to suck the love out, like an actual bug. This is the primative, animal like way. So all the love starved changeling prisoners are beating each other up with bare hooves and biting each other to drain the love to not starve.
>
> Fluttershy witnesses one changeling attempt to bite another, and stops him using The Stare. The changeling stops and the other one scampers away. Then Fluttershy comforts the predator changeling like a baby animal and asks why he was doing it.
>
> The attacher is Friedrich, a former pilot who bailed. The victim who scampered away is Hans Kessel, a demoted bauleiter sent to operate the mobile love harvester
>
> Fluttershy gives him The Stare (the other slinks away) while scolding him in Equestrian 
> He doesn't understand the language, but understands her body language (that's what the stare is all about) 
> Fluttershy then stops scolding and shows kindness, patting the changeling. He calms down and smiles a little, feeling the kindness
> The griffon is weirded out and flabbergasted 
> Fluttershy has the griffon translate
> The griffon asks why did you attack the other Changeling 
> The changeling says he's starving
> The griffon blurts back in Herzlander we've been feeding you plenty, you ungrateful pig
> Fluttershy asks to please translate and don't be mean. Ask why is he hungry, is there not enough food?
> The changeling says they are love-deprived, starving for love
> They usually have love rations to take but none here
> So they tried draining each other but because of the inhibitor rings they can't use their draining magic, so they have to use their teeth to drain love, the ancient way 
>
> These bugs are parasites, the griffon spat. The changeling visibly recoiled, feeling the disgust from emotion sense
> Fluttershy glared and says stop calling them bugs. They are changelings. And I'll have you know I have several friends that are actual bugs and they are perfectly capable of behaving themselves and asking nicely for a meal
> You asked me to help you take care of these changelings, so are you going to listen to the expert?
> Fine, I’m sorry
> I’m sorry too. I was a little harsh, Fluttershy admitted
>
> The POW explains that they're starving and also they feel so much hate that it's driving them crazy
>
> Now, it seems like we need to get some of these “love rations” so that these changelings can get their basic needs met without attacking each other
> We have some, the griffon said. From the captured equipment. They’re labeled extra condensed red love rations
> Can we distribute them?
> No way! The changelings use these in battle and they go crazy. They rush at enemy lines with no regard for their own life! It turns them into ani- I-I mean, it makes them suicidal!
> Fluttershy widens her eyes at suicidal, and then decides to call in a real doctor
> They confirm the symptoms of substance addiction
>
> Any way we can make smaller portions? Just enough to satisfy their basic needs, because what we have right now is cruel!
> Fluttershy tells the griffon to tell the changeling they will get him some love rations. The griffon says we can’t promise him. Fluttershy says we’ll find a way
> The griffon says it
> The changeling says thank you and he is okay for now, Fluttershy’s love is very strong
> I haven’t felt real love in a long time
> The griffon translates it, looking flabbergasted
> What do you mean real love? Not rations?
> Nice emotions like kindness exuded towards him that he can pick up on
> Noling has been nice to him in a long time, he’s just been taking rations while at the factory as a grub and then in the army. I really appreciate your love
> The griffon relays it, looking disgusted or pitying
> Fluttershy says maybe friendship is so rare for them that just basic friendship is love to them, and they’re very lonely
> Are you still hungry?
> I’m not at insane starvation levels anymore but still very hungry after being so deprived
> Fluttershy extends her hoof and says to take a little from her
> The griffon says are you crazy?
> Fluttershy says she’s let bugs take blood for a meal before, and there’s nothing wrong with that
> The changeling takes just a little bit, not draining very much at all. Fluttershy feels the little prick in her soul like a bug bite and then it’s over
> The changeling says thank you so much
> Fluttershy says let’s go find that other changeling and you’re going to offer some love to him as an apology. And next time, you share a little instead of fighting
>
> We will work on getting rations for everypony (everyling?) but until then you should start sharing
>
> How can you tell them apart?
> I tell my animals apart all the time. It’s the little things and demeanor
> Griffons and ponies see all the changelings as the same because both griffons and ponies have always had bright, distinct colors and differences for manes and plumage. But Fluttershy is used to animals being similar
>
> The Draftees: They are rural or factory drones. They learned "Factory Herzlander"—enough to read signs, take orders, and operate machines. They lack the vocabulary for philosophy, history, or complex emotion.  What if the drafted prisoners don't know the Herzlander words to convey the full reality to Fluttershy and Henri? Can this define the balance of languages? These should be draftees with minimum education who previously worked in factories and ate bland love rations.
>
> Fluttershy realizes the prisoners aren't "Evil"; they are Love-Sick. They are going through withdrawal.
> Fluttershy: "They aren't fighting us because they hate us. They're fighting because they're hungry and hurting. Punishment won't fix that."
>
> Fluttershy lets the changeling bite her to drain some love. He says it's delicious and he hasn't had "real love" in a long time
>
> Fluttershy learns from the prisoners that they used to eat "High Quality Rations" made from "The Bad Ones." She assumes this means criminals. She doesn't grasp the scale.
>
> Do I frame it as starving or withdrawl from addiction? How do I balance the two? How does Fluttershy learn the nuance? Right away, or later?

**Characters:**
* **[[Henri Gourard]]**
> **Analysis:** Henri, ever befuddled by equestrians and pony magic, is completely stunned and unresponsive at Fluttershy's use of her Stare to cow a predator. Which is also outside the realm of even typical pony magic. Fluttershy says something like "oh, it just comes out sometimes, I can't really control it (check dialogue from Stare Master)" and Henri laughs it off, saying he will never understand Equestrian ponies (who are culturally distinct from Aquileian ponies) but that's okay.
>
> We learn that Henri can speak Herzlander because he grew up in a region in between Aquileia and Herzland (Ailmont, Avian, Verenia)
>
> Henri remarks that the changelings have the vocabulary and eloquence of a five year old cub (simplified Herzlander as a second language)
>
> And they are parasites

* **[[Friedrich]]** (Antagonist)
> **Analysis:** Fluttershy stops him with The Stare. He unravels the curtain about changeling conscripts.

* **[[Fizzlepop Berrytwist]]**
> **Analysis:** Fizzlepop Berrytwist helped Star Energy make inhibitor rings, which she is aware of from her time as the Storm King's Right Hoof. The inhibitor rings are magically sealed on (somehow)
>
> She must've learned it from Maregypt or Saddle Arabia

* **[[Minette]]**
> **Analysis:** She hears how the old Skyfall translator turned "Fluttershy" into literal "trembling coward" and says don't use that. It triggers her trauma about being named after a pet (kitty)


**Threads:**
* **[[Perception of Changelings]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** It's revealed that the conscripts are addicts and could change

* **[[Camp Fluttershy]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** The start of the camp. The POWs are only on mild drugs so they can stabilize with just a little kindness


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**

**Codex:**
* **[[Changeling Emotion Sense]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Fluttershy first learns about the emotion sense

* **[[Changeling Biology]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** With the inhibitors, they can't use the draining spell, but they can drain via biting.
> If they had natural latent friendship, they can still absorb that; they don't need a spell for it.
>
> They could also just live on food? Or do they need some pink love no matter what? A mix is required?
> Either way, they are biting each other not because they are starving (they have food), but because they need their fix. They are addicted to the red love and experiencing withdrawl and need an intervention. Either they steal their next fix from a victim, or they receive a pink love donation from a live creature, which is concentrated enough to ease them off the red love addiction.
>
> When a creature offers a donation, only pink love is drained? But my law says you can't drain the pink without draining the red. Maybe this only applies to forceful extraction/draining. Donations can be pink only. This would make sense in ancient changeling society; they wouldn't evolve to accidentally drain red from willing donations, that would cause addiction.
> But then the love donator doesn't work?
> Must refine...
> I guess donations also contain red love, and should only be used sparingly
> Or the changeling will say it's delicious, but warns about addiction? He doesn't want to drain ponies anymore, even from donations. He wants to be free (from the addiction, but it's vague because the simplified Herzlander isn't very expressive)


---
### SCENE 6.3: Why not shapeshift to escape?
> **Synopsis:** Chrysalis’s industrial war machine literally strips Changelings of their defining biological trait (their uniqueness/shapeshifting) to turn them into static, mass-produced infantry. She erases their "Asset Specificity" to make them interchangeable cogs. Drugged conscripts are too scared and lack the vocabulary or will to leave the hive and their supply of jaeger geist, which is the only thing keeping them sane by drowning out the emotion sense with a warm glow of synthetic appreciation.
>
> Chrysalis’s propaganda relies on atomization and fear of the outside world. The conscripts have been indoctrinated to believe that Equestrians are either weak livestock to be harvested or vicious monsters who will kill them on sight. The jaeger-geist artificially validates the Hive as the only source of warmth and approval. To the conscript's chemically altered mind, deserting the army does not look like an escape to freedom; it looks like stepping out of a warm house into a freezing, lethal vacuum.
>
> The Changeling command structure utilizes asymmetric Emotion Sense. While the conscripts are chemically blinded by jaeger-geist, the Statthalters and veteran Jaegers (who have the discipline to control their Emotion Sense without drugs) act as an emotional panopticon. A conscript cannot secretly harbor thoughts of desertion because the intent to flee generates a spike of fear, desperation, or wavering loyalty. The embedded Statthalters in the professional units can literally feel this ideological deviation before the conscript even acts on it. The dissenter is identified, pulled from the line, and either executed, sent to the Love Harvesters, or demoted to a factory floor.

**Codex:**
* **[[Changeling Biology]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** The drugs prevent shapeshifting


---
### SCENE 6.4: Fluttershy orders the guards to treat the changelings nicely
> **Synopsis:** Fluttershy starts really soft and naive sounding, but eventually turns assertive and makes the guards admit they only signed up because they wanted to shoot or torture changelings who try to escape. Then Fluttershy forgives them for their thoughts and asks them to reconsider. Some take the opportunity to leave their posts while others promise to better themselves.

**Characters:**
* **[[Henri Gourard]]** (Protagonist, Impact: CoreValueLearned)
> **Analysis:** Henri learns more sympathy and grace from Fluttershy. He is in shock at how well demure Fluttershy asserted herself and fixed the situation.

* **[[Fluttershy]]** (Protagonist, Impact: CoreValueDemonstration)
> **Analysis:** Fluttershy demonstrates not just Kindness, but Grace.

* **[[Minette]]**
> **Analysis:** Minette is totally humbled by Fluttershy (expand from her perspective near the end of her prequel side story)

### SCENE 6.6: Henri gives Fluttershy love rations and an old translator to communicate with the changeling POWs

>
> Fluttershy says "wow, what a useful invention" and Henri remarks how it was soulless, invented by the Herzlanders who took over Cloudbury in order to remake it into their image. Henri boasts about how he put in the effort to learn Equestrian because Equestria has a lot of culture. The Cloudburians didn't want to learn Herzlander, so the Herzlander elites circumvented their own culture's soullessness (in Henri's opinion) by inventing an equally soulless device to get the job done.
>
> There is also the horror of the translator translating Equestrian names as objects
> Fluttershy says "Hi, I'm Fluttershy", it comes out with a literal translation, and Henri flinches and says Don't say that, they'll think you're a coward, or prey

**Threads:**
