### SCENE 6.7: Arrival/Shipment From Ponyville
> **Synopsis:** Dr. Fauna arrives on a train from Ponyville with all of Fluttershy's animal friends from the animal sanctuary
> Because Ponyville is fully evacuated to Manehattan, as per Apple Bloom's letter
> Fluttershy seeing her animal friends, Applejack brings apples to her, with Mali accompanying, and that's where the flutterbat joke comes in?
> Since Mali is similar to Fluttershy (perhaps a more outgoing version of Fluttershy), Applejack would say she reminds her of Flutterbat but without the monster part
>
> Maybe Fluttershy was trying to feed her friends SAA rations and they were refusing and Fluttershy was coaxing them, then gives The Stare to other animals and Mali sees that
>
> Fluttershy has already decided she will do whatever she can to both save the displaced animals and rehabilitate the changeling POWs, even at risk since they are at a combat front
> She can't do it alone, she needs her animal friends to help comfort the other animals and the changelings as comfort animals
> She also needs Dr. Fauna to diagnose the changeling drug symptoms!
>
> Apple Bloom also sent a crate of hats from the "hats and bows closet"
> But Applejack doesn't put on a hat, keeping her infantry cap instead. She says she can't be the farmpony, she has to be the general
> Twilight is saddened by this

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** Applejack doesn't put on a hat, keeping her infantry cap instead. She says she can't be the farmpony, she has to be the general
> She sees that Fluttershy trusts her so much that she brought her entire animal sanctuary to an active combat zone. Applejack can't handle the pressure, it eats her up inside

* **[[Mali]]**
> **Analysis:** Dr. Fauna and Fluttershy bring the evacuated animal sanctuary to Tall Tale. The animals are traumatized, displaced, and refusing to eat the flavorless, mushy SAA canned rations.
>
> Mali’s "Parloir" instinct would be to coax them, validate their distress, and perhaps try to find them fresh apples (which are unavailable). She expects Fluttershy to do the same. Instead, Fluttershy drops the demure persona, deploys The Stare on her own beloved animals, and strictly commands them to eat the slop. The animals immediately comply.
>
> Mali is shocked not by the magic of The Stare, but by its application. Fluttershy just used absolute, non-negotiable dominance on a traumatized victim.
> Mali realizes that true empathy sometimes requires overriding a victim's immediate comfort for their long-term survival. Fluttershy knows the animals will starve if they don't eat the SAA mash. Coddling them would be a death sentence. To save them, Fluttershy has to be the "Bad Guy" for thirty seconds.
>
> The lesson for Mali is Tough Love. Mali learns that you cannot always meet a traumatized creature "where they are" if where they are is a state of suicidal stagnation. Sometimes, you have to force them to eat the mash.
>
> Mali recognizes The Stare as an extreme, weaponized form of boundary-setting. However, because she is still trapped in her "Structural Subservience," she categorizes it as a tool for others (specifically, for an Element of Harmony). She does not yet feel entitled to wield that level of dominance herself.

### SCENE 6.8: Flutterbat and Apples
> **Synopsis:** They are eating mush at the mess hall because there's not enough fresh harvest today
>
> Rainbow doesn't mind. She says it's good enough and filling so she can fly. She picks up several cans of SAA rations to take with her on the plane
>
> Then the fragance of fresh apples hits them
> Several crates from Sweet Apple Acres along with a note from Apple Bloom saying everypony in Ponyville is evacuating to Manehattan for safety, and I got as many apples as I could before we left. Please come home safe. Love, Apple Bloom
>
> Rainbow stays put for the apples
> Applejack sees Mali's fangs next to Fluttershy and holds back the apple instinctively from Fluttershy, as a reference to bats
> They laugh about it all and share the story to their friends
> Then Rainbow is done laughing and marches off to fly again
>
> Fluttershy mentions love donations
> Applejack volunteers

**Characters:**
* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** She only briefly stops to reminisce before going back to detached death


**Codex:**
* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Compare the canned ration to the fresh apple
> The apples turning to mush from Flutterbat sucking them dry is like the mush they are eating right now
>
> Applejack doesn't mention her parents yet
>
> Fluttershy: "It looks just like the leftovers from my... episode."
> Applejack: (Staring darkly at the can) "Yeah. Takes a monster to turn a good apple into something that lifeless."
>
> When AJ says, "I hate this slop," the characters hear: "I miss home cooking."
> The narrative meaning is: "I hate that my parents chose this life."
>
> The "Laughter" Theme This fits Chapter 5's "Laughter" theme by showing how humor is used to cover pain.
>     The group laughs at the "Vampire Bat" comparison because it distances them from the horror of their current reality.
>     Applejack uses the "Grumpy Farm Mare" persona (guarding the apples from Fluttershy) to mask the genuine heartbreak she feels looking at those cans. She plays the part of the "Apple Obsessed Pony" so she doesn't have to play the part of the "Abandoned Daughter."

### SCENE 6.9: Applejack donates love to the POWs using the love harvester
> **Synopsis:** Dr. Fauna hears the changelings explain symptoms of their starvation, and determines it's substance addiction.
>
> Fluttershy tells Applejack that she repurposed a love harvester and wants donations for the POWs. She says they can help the "poor little things" and get them on their side
>
> Why do they keep launching suicide missions? Applejack asked
> The garrison commanders and advisors say it’s to deter us from redeploying some of our troops to the more important front in Northern Equestria that they are actively pushing through
> They don’t realize most of our troops are local now and that we were abandoned. Celestia wouldn’t force us to move elsewhere anyway, just as she didn’t force the army to stay and defend here
>
> Applejack also has the mentality of Rainbow Dash. If she's not contributing in some helpful way, she's hurting the war effort. She sees the public donation as a way to take a day of rest from her studies on how to be a general (learning from the Aquileians and Comet Shine).
>
> This donation using the vanilla love harvester should hurt - Applejack should grimace.

**Characters:**

> She interprets this as meaning she is a big giant weapon that is dangerous. Only Applejack can control the weapon (her)

* **[[Henri Gourard]]** (Impact: CoreValueChange)
> **Analysis:** Henri realizes the conscripted changelings were misled by a Herzlander imperialist poser tyrant queen, just as he once was


**Threads:**

* **[[Camp Fluttershy]]** (Positive)

>
> The FJA and Star Energy bought out all the ruthless Skyfall industrialist influences within Tall Tale.
>

>
> Henri mentions how the Aquileians united griffons and ponies against the king. Now we must accept the changelings who want to redeem themselves, to stand up against the greatest tyrant, Chrysalis
>

### SCENE 6.10: Relaxing after the Love Donation
> **Synopsis:** Applejack asks how things are going at Star Energy
> Twilight says the spellblast cannon is okay, they're trying to miniturize it into a rifle but it's unstable. Fizzlepop Berrytwist is leading the effort since it's her specialty, Twilight's only helping. But Twilight doesn't really want to talk about magical weapons right now (she needs a distraction from fixating on Chrysalis's evil love harvester) and says she wants to talk about nice things. Applejack accepts.
>
> Can I braid your mane? Show me how to do it earth pony style, no magic
> Twilight braids Applejack's mane with her mouth, earth pony style
>
> Applejack admits she only did the donation to get a "sick day" from being "The General", not because she particularly wanted to help the changelings. Applejack sighs about the burden of constant leadership
> Everypony looks at me and sees a hero, a leader, but I just wish I was applebucking instead
>
> Twilight says that's how she's felt as Celestia's student, and she thought she was happy but not anymore
>
> You know what let me ease up for the first time? The letter you sent after Flim and Flam
> There's a lot of things that went wrong that day, but
> When Twilight saw Applejack’s letter to Celestia saying she didn’t learn anything except to stick to what she knew was right, it made Twilight reflect and appreciate simple things instead of always pushing herself. It was liberating. One of the things that drew her to Applejack in contrast to her devotion to always trying to please Celestia
>
> Twilight always admired Applejack for her humble attitude. While Twilight spent her whole life trying to be better to impress Celestia (to the point of ignoring social life, and then starting the process all over again as a princess, and once she got her wings and title she felt even further from Applejack and basically gave up on pursuing Applejack romantically), Applejack is completely happy with her place in life (ever since getting her cutie mark)
>
> You don't have to be The General with me. I came here to save you, the farmpony who taught me that magic isn't everything.
>
> You really still see the farmpony?
>
> Of course I do. That's who I've always seen you as, and in a world of constantly exploding expectations, I mean it as a GOOD thing
>
> That means a lot, Twi. I admit I kinda felt that we were drifting apart after you started the school
>
> I know... and I'm sorry... but remember when I was freaking out about getting everypony presents and you calmed me down?
> Even after the school and all I put you girls through, you still connected to the real me, just like before
>
> If we survive this, would you go back to being the farmpony?
>
> In a heartbeat, Twi
>
> Twilight took a deep breath, then said "back then when I realized how liberating it was... I kinda sorta had a crush on you..." I sealed the thought away as the responsibilities kept building up, but when you and Rainbow and Pinkie left Ponyville on that train... and then we heard about the disaster at the border... and then you gave that speech after Luna's retreat order, and I didn't hear The General, and I heard the honest truth as you always do, but I also heard the farmpony I loved throwing her life away. No changeling, not even Celestia could hold me back from rescuing you."
> I love you, Applejack.
>
> Applejack doesn't immediately say I love you too, but she says something like "this feels right"
> It perfectly captures AJ's lack of pre-existing crush while allowing for an immediate, deep connection. She isn't swooning; she is anchoring.
>
> ---
>
> The Breaking Point (The Climax) This aligns with your plan for the "Love Donation" scene.
>
>     The Event: Applejack performs the Love Donation to the POWs. As per your notes, she does this partly to take a "sick day" from being General, feeling the burden of leadership.
>
>     The Discussion: This is where the "Imposter Syndrome" talk happens.
>
>         AJ confesses: "I'm not the hero you think I am. I only donated that love because I wanted to stop being a General for five minutes. You defied Celestia for a fraud."
>
>     The Resolution (The Kiss): Twilight uses the refusal of the letter (from Ch 5) as the proof to counter AJ's doubt.
>
>         Twilight's Argument: "I didn't defy Celestia for 'General Applejack.' I defied her for the mare who is honest enough to admit she's tired. I trust you, not the rank."
>
>     The Result: This breaks AJ's wall. She realizes Twilight loves her (the honest farm pony), not the "Lioness" mask. That is why she decides to reciprocate.

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** Her guilt during the "Turkey Shoot" and subsequent battles isn't just about killing; it's about Usurpation. She feels she is stepping out of her caste (Earth Pony/Farmer) and taking on a Royal burden she isn't qualified for.
>
> Applejack’s role as "General" is a performative shell. She delegates the actual strategy to Star Energy and the Aquileians because she is self-aware enough to know she lacks the expertise. However, because she is the "Element of Honesty," the troops rally around her as a figurehead. This creates a painful dissonance: the more they cheer for "General Applejack," the more she feels like a liar. She feels she is wearing a costume Luna gave her.
>
> Twilight is not "preserving innocence" (which implies naivety); she is validating reality. When Twilight looks at Applejack, she ignores the medals and the rank. She interacts with the "Farmpony."
>
> Twilight’s love is the antidote to the Imposter Syndrome. By loving the Farmpony, Twilight confirms that the "General" is just a temporary job, not a permanent transformation. She represents the Hope of Return. Twilight is the living promise that once the war is over, Applejack is allowed to take off the costume and go back to the orchard.
>
> Twilight grounds her. The "Farmpony" dynamic—simple affection, physical touch, references to home—reminds Applejack that she is still real. This keeps Applejack's morale up, which in turn allows her to keep the army's morale up.

* **[[Fleur Bloom]]**
> **Analysis:** Twilight mentions she has a feeling Fleur Bloom doesn't really like her, so she's been avoiding the magical weapons development and leaving that to Fizzlepop
> Applejack says she kind of reminded me of you when you first came to Ponyville
> Twilight says I had a great friend to teach me how to be a friend
> Then maybe Fleur just needs a friend?

* **[[Fizzlepop Berrytwist]]**
> **Analysis:** Twilight mentions how Fizzlepop is leading the spellblast turret improvements


**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** Twilight unveils why she started liking Applejack (Super Speedy lesson, then Hearth's Warming when Applejack calmed down her anxiety about finding gifts and preparing for the holiday)
>
> Twilight didn't come to save the general, she came to save the farmpony 
> That is what AJ clings to, a promise that she can go back to being the farmpony one day
>
> Twilight admits her feelings here. Applejack doesn't immediately say "I love you" but she says "this feels right" and they are both happy
>
> Applejack thinks "this isn't the princess anymore, it's the friend who first came to Ponyville" and thinks she can love that mare. It's also a shared rebellion of rejecting the roles Celestia and Luna thrust them into.

* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]**
> **Update:** Applejack had always offered an alternative lifestyle and role model compared to Celestia.

* **[[Stagnation's Redemption]]**
> **Update:** Twilight didn't come to save the general, she came to save the farmpony 
> That is what AJ clings to, a promise that she can go back to being the farmpony one day


**Codex:**
* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Applejack feels like acting as the general is betraying her cutie mark - her earning her cutie mark is her deciding she is a simple farmer, not a sophisticated mask wearer. The farm makes her happy and at peace/authentic. The general makes her anxious and inauthentic.

>
> Twilight thinks Celestia treats her as a child


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]** (CentralConflict)
> **Commentary:** They perceive Celestia is a poseur here. She has always seemed to be the preacher and authority on Harmony, but in reality, she is just a paranoid mother of her nursery.


**Codex:**
* **[[The Battle of Mount Aris]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Twilight's naiveity shattered

* **[[School of Friendship]]** (Backstory)

---
## CHAPTER 7: Generosity
> **Chapter Note:** The upper class Orange family is hosting all the Apples and Pears (from Vanhoover). It's an example of Generosity, but it's voluntary through familial bonds. Systemic equity is still needed, and in fact, the poseurs can point to model citizens like the Oranges and say look, we don't need laws, they're doing enough
> **Chapter Note:** CMCs are unhappy working in Rarity's assembly line even though they know it's for the war and their sisters. Their special talent is finding other ponies' special talents. They become campaigners. After the referendum and total mobilization act, they help ponies find a way to make their unique talent helpful for the war effort!
> **Chapter Note:** Manehattan should be inspired by Poland today. This chapter should not be totally grim, it should be uplifting
> **Chapter Note:** Maybe Celestia shouldn't be a total statue? What can she be doing, in line with the idea she like the 1991 consenus and not just a traumatized mother? Because statue might be too apathetic
> **Chapter Note:** The theme and social commentary should be that the vast majority of native Manehattan ponies and refugees want to help the volunteers on the front (because they believe in harmony and are grateful) but don't know how because they grew up in a nursery (the Stagnation of Harmony). EEEE union workers provide the guidance by running the seized factories from the traitorous collaborators
> **Chapter Note:** iI a total war, Generosity cannot just be giving a gem to a beggar. Generosity must be systemic Equity.
> **Chapter Note:** The Historical Blueprint: Britain's Emergency Powers Act of 1940
> the labor unions (led by Clement Attlee) only agreed to suspend strikes, freeze wages, and work grueling shifts because the government instituted a 100% Excess Profits Tax on corporations. The British public accepted this not at gunpoint, but out of absolute, existential clarity. The class divide temporarily evaporated because a Luftwaffe bomb did not distinguish between a slum and a mansion. This birthed the "Blitz Spirit"—a profound, localized solidarity.

### SCENE 7.1: Flowing Current shows up at Rarity's shop
> **Stakes:** The backstory should be:
> In Ponyville:
> Rarity starts by stitching cutie marks (this is pre-war, before the volunteers go the front)
> The war starts, it's a disaster. Rarity is in shock. The whole Apple Family is in shock that Applejack is MIA, and the Pears are lamenting the loss of their homes in Vanhoover, their friends who stayed behind, and are indebted to Applejack who demanded that they all go to Manehattan a week before the war started.
>
> They get a letter from Rainbow Dash saying Applejack is in Tall Tale (AJ didn't write back, she was too ashamed of the imposter syndrome, it's like The Last Roundup but way more real)
>
> Rarity realizes she needs to do more. She ditches the cutie mark stitching and goes to Manehattan to make clothes for refugees, taking the CMCs and Trixie with her.
>
> Then they hear Luna's retreat order, and Applejack's speech in defiance
> Twilight promises she will bring Applejack home
> Then they find out that Twilight stayed in Tall Tale and violently defended it with Applejack.
> Celestia comes to Ponyville and begs Fluttershy to bring Twilight home.
> Fluttershy sends a letter back saying she is going to stay in Tall Tale.
> Here is where the extreme guilt sets in for Rarity. All 5 of her friends are at the front while she is sitting at her shop.
> NOW she transforms her Manehattan shop into an assembly line for uniforms and works herself to the bone (sacrifice).
> **Synopsis:** Maybe Rarity's initial work is to stitch volunteers' cutie marks onto their uniforms so they won't feel the loss of their special talent when they go to the front
> It's about self esteem, very Rarity. Seems stupid on surface level, it does matter to Rarity's in-character, but Her transition is moving to equity instead of just self esteem 
>
> At the beginning, Rarity feels like stitching cutie marks to uniforms is important and good enough. It's the best she can do with herself without losing herself in mass production. On the surface level it's stupid but if you know Rarity's character it does have merit
> But in chapter 7 Rarity updates her ambition from simply empowering self esteem to delivering Equity
>
> She makes the most robust uniforms and also adds cutie marks for morale
>
> Maybe everyone else is in an assembly line but Rarity is still stubbornly doing custom made stuff and that's a conflict between Rarity and Sweetie Belle
>
> Flowing Current shows up at Rarity's shop-turned-assembly line (which looks soulless) where Rarity, her associates, Trixie, Spike and the CMCs are working in an assembly line to make uniforms or clothes for refugees
>
> He tells Rarity that he is the leader of EEEE! and they want to invite her to their meeting tonight.
> The princesses have arrived at the mayor's mansion
> What? Canterlot has fallen already?
> No, but the princesses evacuated Canterlot after the fall of Bales
> EEEE! had a war economy plan and tried to talk to the mayor, who said the city treasury is broke and they can barely handle the refugees
> They tried to talk to the princesses but were refused, now they want Rarity to talk to her
> Come to the meeting and we'll talk you through our next round of ideas
>
> The CMCs overhear and want to go
>
> The CMCs are with her in Manehattan because they are underaged and not allowed to go to the front
> If they are 9 at the start of the show, they would be 20 by the war's start in 1011. However, Celestia was extremely conservative with the army and said the minimum age is 25 because that is when the brain's risk taking abilities are fully developed. She wants to protect her little ponies

**Characters:**
* **[[Rarity]]**
> **Analysis:** Rarity is not able to make customized uniforms for everypony, but she at least designs flexible variants to fit different builds and preferences
> This is why she’s the Uniform Designer
> She is guilty that she can’t stomach the front while all her friends are out there, so she works extra hard
>
> Maybe everyone else is in an assembly line but Rarity is still stubbornly doing custom made stuff and that's a conflict between Rarity and Sweetie Belle


**Threads:**
* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
> **Update:** Rarity's shop is running using Sassy Saddles' assembly line blueprint from the episode Canterlot Boutique! That episode (one of my favorites) perfectly describes the difference between ruthless/laize faire capitalism and harmonic capitalism. The Rules of Rarity!
>
> Sassy Saddles got her ideas from Skyfall fashion magnates (facilitated by Chrysalis). She canonically worked for multiple firms, but they all went bust due to the ruthless capitalism and saturation of Manehattan's commondity fashion market. So she went to Canterlot to start anew.

* **[[Harmonic Capitalism]]**
> **Update:** The Rules of Rarity is Harmonic Capitalism. We immediately see that the Rules or Rarity are dead. They are following Sassy's blueprint in an assembly line to make as many uniforms as possible.

* **[[The War in the North]]**
> **Update:** The princesses evacuated Canterlot after the fall of Bales


**Themes:**
* **[[Generosity and Equity]]**
> **Commentary:** Generosity becomes Sacrifice during the war

### SCENE 7.2: Rarity attends the EEEE! meeting
> **Synopsis:** The mayor is a nice old stallion like Celestia. He won't abandon the gold standard (moral imperative, old ways). He is kind, but outdated and not fit for war. He welcomed all the refugees ahead of the war and during it, but now doesn't have the resources and organization to handle the refugees, the despair crisis and the drug crisis. He is paralyzed.
> The mayor tried to raise taxes, but there are literally no bits to circulate.
>
> The Mayor rejects it not out of malice, but out of absolute macroeconomic illiteracy. The Equestrian treasury has been drained of Gold Bits by Chrysalis’s Skyfall-backed tycoons. To the Mayor, money is a finite, physical object. The concept of "Civic Fiat" (printing bonds backed by future labor) sounds like a literal scam. He cannot authorize war production because, according to his 80-year-old ledger, the city is bankrupt.
>
> EEEE! wants Rarity to talk to the princesses about forcing the mayor to mandate war production and set up programs for all the refugees who are coming in
>
> Manehattan was flooded with refugees who ran from the changelings, and they want to work triple shifts in the factories to reclaim their homes. But the factory owners are being extremely complacent, saying they're not getting paid by the army and can't pay the workers
>
> EEEE is already operating on credit/future work within its ranks for taking care of refugees and sharing food. They need to extend the system to the whole city, legally
>
> The origins of EEEE! is bat pony rights, who work the night shifts
>
> Flowing Current (from the Manehattan event in Luna’s thestral path, even though in TLTT she went with Celestia’s token reforms. A pegasus? Because they control the weather, thunderstorms, he is comfortable at the power station)
> He is the leader of EEEE! (Equestrian Equality, Empathy and Education!) which is a grassroots intellectual group that started out of the token reforms in order to bring actual reforms to Manehattan
>
> Here is the law that EEEE! agrees on by the end of the meeting:
> The law is essentially Britain's war economy laws
> Issue war bonds as payment for work, to be paid after the war is over. That means every pony needs a bank account
> War bonds also for restaurant owners so they can sell super cheap food so that factory workers don't have to go home and cook. They can work longer hours at the factories
> Profits for CEOs are mandated/capped
> all adults required to contribute in some way according to their talents, foals allowed to skip school to contribute

**Characters:**
* **[[Rarity]]**
> **Analysis:** When Rarity first walks into Foyer de la Jeunesse d'Avant Garde, she recalls coming here as a filly amazed at all the couture (a hint towards her Aquileian-influenced origins) and contrasting it to how it is a soup kitchen now packed with refugees but the charm is everlasting


**Threads:**
* **[[Perception of the Griffonian Republic]]**
> **Update:** It seems like EEEE and the war bonds system is all from Aquileia since their headquarters is in a massive, glamorous Aquileian parloir. Only much later do we learn that the war bonds and civic fiat based purely on trust are actually originating from the Griffonian Republic


**Codex:**
* **[[Tzinacatl]]** (Organization)
> **Note:** Batponies from the jungle moved to Manehattan starting from around 988 (23 years ago) to work night shifts at the factories
>
> The urban Tzinacatl in the union point out the drugs coming from their "traitorous cousins" in the jungle and remind everypony to refuse and ostracize any drug dealers that show up on factory floors
>
> The parloirs are being flooded by despairing refugees and they can't all be comforted

* **[[Parloirs/Pre-War Manehattan]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** There is a despair crisis as depressed refugees are going to parloirs for an escape, ruining the art of it, and drugs are hitting the streets. We need to give everypony something to work for
>
> The Salons were designed for Empowerment (Adult intimacy/Pride). But for a traumatized refugee who just lost their home in Vanhoover, the Salon becomes a tool for Dissociation.
> The Salon Operators (Harmonic) want to heal the refugees. They offer touch, warmth, and soft lights to counter the cold terror of the Changelings.

* **[[Banking]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Flowing Current says we are in a crisis, we have no money, the Equestrian Army is not making contracts with the factories or directing them to make guns because the bank is empty and there's no tax revenue, ponies don't have savings accounts
> All the bits left Equestria. We spent our bits buying goods from these tycoons, and the tycoons spent the bits buying stuff from outside and collect their wealth in Skyfall Marks
> Now with the submarines, everything is useless. We can't buy weapons with the Skyfall Marks
> But our labor does not need to be tied to bits. We'll give everypony a bank account of war bonds to spend after the war is over
> Money is a fiction we agree on. If we all agree to believe in Victory, we can print the money to achieve it. Stagnation is the only sin.
>
> It's John Maynard Keynes's 1940 theory How to Pay for the War which is the British Total Mobilization policy
> Tax the rich at 99.25% effectively nationalizing all collaborators
> Workers paid in tickets for food and post war credits
> The Philosophy: You are paying the worker with a claim on Future Productivity.
>     "You cannot consume now because we are fighting for survival. But your labor is recorded. When we win, and the factories switch back to making toasters, you can cash in this bond."
>
> Gold standard (Celestia) - money represents past labor/stored value. If you run out, you stop.
> War bond/Fiat (EEEE!'s plan, from Keynes) - money represents future labor/potential value. As long as you have the will to work and the resources to use, you can never "run out of money" (but it does cause a growth trap and ambition machine which is incompatible with stagnation of harmony)
>
> They copy the plan from Aquileia, which has war bonds
> taxing the rich at 99% isn't just punishment; it's necessary to prevent inflation when the supply of goods drops (due to the war)
> This explains why they aren't turning to communism, because they have Aquileian ideology as a model to follow

* **[[EEEE!]]** (Organization)
> **Note:** Foyer des Joueurs d'Avant-garde is the name of their meeting hall. It was the first and most grand parloir, which means the hearth of the vanguard players
> Coltbert named it after players because of game theory, a player who flirts, and performers who play roles/musicians who play instruments
> This location was designed as a massive importer of new Aquileian luxuries and mimics the FJA acronym of Gaudreau's original social club

* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** The EEEE members mention "SAA" as the good company. They need to hold all the other companies to SAA standards
> They stitch their cutie marks onto their factory uniforms and sing at the production line, it's like a family
>
> They are operating on pure harmonic faith and to protect their farms, donating rations to the army for free and working for free because they know they have to survive against the changelings. They help EEEE develop the war bonds to get the rest of Manehattan working

### SCENE 7.3: Rarity speaks to Celestia and Luna
> **Synopsis:** Rarity is stopped by a royal guard, who casts a detection spell before calling Celestia to the door. Celestia invites Rarity in.
>
> Luna is sleeping on a pillow (during the day), fitfully. Celestia says she is having nightmares, but Rarity must not tell anypony. Luna's mane is no longer ethereal.
>
> Celestia should exhibit similar symptoms to Pinkie from being broken by war, naivety shattered. It's not easy to spot because her ethereal mane doesn't deflate and her coat is already white, but Rarity can figure it out since she has an eye for detail, so she stops pressing Celestia
>
> Rarity presents EEEE!'s war economy proposal to the princesses
> Luna is out of her element, not having a clue how the modern economy works
> Celestia doesn't want to force ponies to work in a factory, saying it breaks the cutie mark system and ponies will get hurt. Rarity says "all adults required to contribute in some way according to their talents" but Celestia is not convinced
> She also singles out the banks as immoral and doesn't want bank accounts, saying banks are a Skyfall thing and they're evil?
>
> Celestia says Blueblood confronted her and demanded to enact conscription, and Celestia shut him down. Do you want to be Blueblood and enact conscription?
> Rarity is horrified, but then tries to argue that factory work isn't the same as being on the front. She explains to Celestia about her journey from cutie mark stitching in Ponyville to clothes for refugees in Manehattan, and finally converting her shop into an assembly line. It's the least she can do to support her friends who are actually out there. We can't just leave our friends out to die!
>
> Celestia ultimately says she cannot unilaterally pass this law anyway, it needs to be enacted by the mayor. She cannot go for or against.
>
> Rarity glances at Luna, who made a sound before going back to fitful sleep. Rarity dares to ask if there is any chance of Luna becoming...
> No, said Celestia softly. The Elements of Harmony purged all traces of Nightmare Moon, so that is not a concern. Even so, Luna needs rest. Again, do not tell anypony.
>
> Flowing Current says we are using Aquileia's economic model
> Rarity mentions it's a proven system 
> Celestia grimaces at Aquileia (the land of griffons abusing ponies)

**Characters:**
* **[[Princess Luna]]**
> **Analysis:** Princess Luna is having nightmares about the war
> Rarity pities her, audience should too
> (Celestia says Nightmare Moon isn't a concern because Luna is very aware of her own folly. It isn't a random external corruption, it was a concious choice to pull the ambitions out of dreams that led to the corruption and nova event.)

* **[[Princess Celestia]]**
> **Analysis:** Celestia is not staring blankly out of mourning or apathy. She is silent because she is terrified of inducing a national panic cascade. Millions of refugees are flooding into Manehattan. The Vanguard army has evaporated. If Celestia appears in public looking terrified, uncertain, or desperate, the entire nation will shatter. She adopts the "Statue" persona—stoic, quiet, unmoving—because she is using every ounce of her willpower to project an illusion of stability. Rarity (and the reader) looks at Celestia and sees an aloof, exhausted monarch bound by red tape. The materialist truth is that Celestia is holding her breath so the country doesn't hyperventilate.
>
> When Rarity presents EEEE!’s plan for War Bonds, total mobilization, and the suspension of the Gold Standard, Celestia is faced with the exact nightmare she banned in 930 ALB: Fiat currency and heavy industry. She doesn't understand that EEEE! is a civic institution; she doesn't know such a thing is possible psychologically, or at least believes it will only last one generation.
>
> Rarity expects Celestia to act as the "Deus Ex Machina" and simply decree the War Economy into existence.
> On the surface, Celestia refuses because EEEE's proposal sounds like industrial tyranny. It's not the pony way and she isn't convinced the ponies will be able to follow. It'll cause mass panic.
> The hidden reality, the internal truth, is that Celestia is terrified of issuing a top-down mandate. She just watched Luna issue a top-down mandate (the Tall Tale retreat order) that turned out to be the wrong call which made things worse for Applejack and Tall Tale and resulted in Twilight going to the front. Celestia is dedicating all her energy to maintaining a calm image (the statue) because she believes if she shows that she is terrified, ponies will panic.
>
> Rarity leaves the suite believing Celestia is hopelessly out-of-touch and stubbornly clinging to a dead era. The epistemological gap is perfectly maintained; Rarity cannot see that Celestia’s inaction is actually a desperate attempt to avoid causing a mass panic or a tactical catastrophe.


**Codex:**
* **[[How names are perceived]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Celestia speaks Rarity's name very softly because she is scared of vocalizing any pony names. She received a report about the changelings using literal translations to label the ponies as prey (but doesn't say it). Rarity assumes Celestia's weak vocalization is total exhaustion.
>
> Celestia realized by trying to make her ponies cute and harmonic, she made them edible. She stripped them of the armor of "Personhood" (standard names) and clothed them in the softness of "Idioms". She wanted to protect them from the hardness of the world, but instead, she packaged them as products for the predators.

* **[[Banking]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Rarity proposes the EEEE! plan of war bonds. 
>
> Celestia singles out the supposedly benign clause about bank accounts (because it means they can't go back to stagnation after the war, they have to pay all the workers)
> She kept Equestria on strict bits and the banks can only lend out the bits they have saved because it keeps ponies honest. Celestia says debt is greed. How can you lend out bits on a piece of paper for work that doesn't exist?
> Rarity says you trust the other to pay it back? I've done that many times. Debt isn't greed, it's trust. We trust in our future victory.
>
> If we only use bits, we are stuck. We can't even raise taxes to pay for weapons. All the bits already left the country!
> We have to borrow from our future selves, that's the way EEEE! explained it. The future harvest pays for the present plow
>
> That sounds like a scam, Celestia said
> Lending money you don't have is lying about reality, promising a future that doesn't exist yet.
>
> With all due respect, Princess, we have to survive. The ponies have to work for something. We have nothing right now except our own futures. We are not going to stop because there are no bits and let Chrysalis eat us. We have our futures to provide.

* **[[Pre-1st Revolution Aquileia]]** (History)
* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Equestria does not have strong federal laws because life was simple and harmonic.
>
> Celestia relied on moral authority rather than federal bureaucracy, local jurisdictions have massive, untapped legal power
>
> This goes both ways. Industrial exploitation went unnoticed and Celestia can't legally force ponies to fight or work in the factories or force the factory workers to work for the State, but it also means EEEE! can have massive legal power to turn the war around if they win the referrendum and legally take over the government.
