### SCENE 7.4: Plan for the 1st Referendum for mayor recall
> **Synopsis:** the first round desperate referendum should be a law about corporate ownership/board recall, with a threshold for 66%, and EEEE interprets the city as a "corporation" based on Manehattan's original establishment as a trade hub created by an eccentric pony. The "historical precedent" is how Skyfall corporations work: when there's an important shareholder vote, the moneyed interests bribe others to abstain or hire hitmen to threaten shareholders.
> They legally classify the municipal government as a "Corporate Board" and initiate a shareholder recall requiring a 66% threshold of cast votes.
>
> We will use the tycoons' laws against them
>
> CMCs go out on the streets to encourage voting in the referendum
>
> Flowing Current attacks and mocks the rich relentlessly, which encourages a lot of refugees who lost everything

---
### SCENE 7.5: Defeatism in the Media
> **Synopsis:** Refer to the event chain
>
> Here we see Equestrian defeatist propaganda and some fringe fear mongering about Twilight’s destruction which was a changeling psyop
> The CMCs encounter these posters and preachers/radio programs spreading propaganda about Starlight being an evil Equalist now back on the front and Twilight Sparkle the Princess of Friendship turning into the Princess of War and Tempest Shadow the Storm King's Right Hoof with death from above
> Also racism against buffalo (it says they are sabotaging oil fields) and bat ponies (they are deviants and drug dealers)
>
> There are posters praising Trimmel for being a genius and being merciful (like British press about the Desert Fox to explain their own failures)
>
> Skyfall Dream aspirational poster
>
> An empty throne in Canterlot, bathed in cold sunlight. Slogan: "She Watches. She Waits. She Weeps. Do not break Her heart with more violence. Accept the Peace."

**Threads:**
* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
* **[[The War in the North]]**

**Codex:**
* **[[New Mareland]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** New mareland breaks off from Equestria after the princesses retreat to Manehattan, since if Equestria can't even defend itself then why stay at war with the submarines? They are a penal colony anyways, they don't necessarily like Celestia

* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** The 1011 ALB refugee crisis is a catastrophic culture clash. When the Changeling Blitzkrieg shatters the North, the 85% innocent agrarian ponies are forced to flee into Manehattan and other eastern cities. They are suddenly thrust into the brutal, industrialized quarantine zones they and their grandparents spent 80 years ignoring. The "Nursery" violently collides with the "Adult World."


---
### SCENE 7.6: Industrialists protest to the mayor and Celestia
> **Synopsis:** The nobles and industrialists complain to Celestia saying the referendum is a sham. "This recall is a disharmonious, radical mob hijacking the city using frightened refugees! It violates the peace!"
> They demand her to shut them down
>
> The comprador class uses their own "stagnation of harmony" posture to argue against their own culture in front of Celestia. The Compradors publicly appeal to Celestia and the Mayor, cloaking their greed in the aesthetics of the Nursery.
>
> They complain saying they are disrupting the peace! They want to take foals out of school! Just shut it all down. Change the city's law with your authority to cancel all referendums during wartime
> Celestia also doesn't intervene on the democratic process (her aloofness goes both ways, she is not a hypocrite, just very scared of being viewed as a tyrant)
>
> The mayor publicly announces the referendum is not legal
>
> EEEE! sends Rarity to talk to Celestia, and Celestia sighs and exhaustingly chides Rarity
> Do you really think this referendum is fair? Calling Manehattan city council a foreign company? Only counting votes that were cast, while ignoring all the ponies frightened in their homes?
>
> Rarity leaves with her head down
> dramatizes the transition from "Vanguard Activism" to "True Democratic Consensus," while serving as a razor-sharp critique of modern voter apathy and how elections don't require every citizen to vote

---
### SCENE 7.7: Rebranding the Referendum
> **Synopsis:** EEEE! has another meeting with a new, very archaic law to use - Manehattan's Hearth's Warming Charter, which predates Celestia
>
> The recall mechanism in the historical precedent of the "Assistants" (Clover the Clever, Smart Cookie, Private Pansy) bypassing their "Leaders" (Princess Platinum, Chancellor Puddinghead, Commander Hurricane); it is not a mere political loophole, it is the foundational survival mechanism of the Equestrian state.
>
> The social contract of Equestria is conditional upon mutual survival. If a ruling class (The Leaders) hoards resources or refuses to act during an existential crisis (The Blizzard/The Submarine Blockade), their legal authority is voided, and executive power defaults to the logistical operators (The Assistants/The Unions).
>
> To prevent frivolous coups, the founders codified the exact demographic ratio that saved them from the Windigos. To legally oust a paralyzed leadership class, the recall requires the absolute consensus of the two "Allied Tribes" (2/3rds or 66.6%) plus at least half of the failing leader's own demographic base (1/6th or 16.6%).
>
> Come up with wording of the ancient charter which leads to the recall rules
> the wording doesn't mention "mayor" or "city council", so the EEEE interpretation is fluid. The principle transfers, but not the exact words
>
> They have to make the most strict, most honest interpretation of the law and make it so harmonic and self impose the hard rules so Celestia doesn't question it and just agrees. They just want a simple yes or no from Celestia, so she can say yes to something that apeals to her principles with no further questions asked.
>
> The ancient law says only villagers who have been there for 12 moons are allowed to vote, so they commit to using Manehattan's official census from a year ago
>
> They decide to rebrand War Bonds into Hearth's Warming Bonds, which perfectly mirrors the spirit of Britain's war economy into an Equestrian framing
> The financial term "bond" shares the exact same linguistic root as the social term for friendship and connection.

---
### SCENE 7.8: Rarity's 3rd talk with Celestia
> **Synopsis:** Rarity sells the Hearth's Warming Bond to Celestia and the frightened 85% majority using the fairy tale. "We must share our warmth to survive the freeze." This comforts the populace. It feels like the Nursery. It feels safe. The actual legal document they use—the Hearth's Warming Charter—was written during the real Hearth's Warming Eve, which was a Labor Strike. The 5/6ths rule wasn't written by magical heroes; it was written by desperate, freezing villagers who realized their leaders (the Kings and Chancellors) were incompetent, and they needed a legal mechanism to avoid starving.
>
> By rebranding the mobilization as the "Hearth's Warming Bond," Rarity successfully translates Kemerskai's cold, industrial Civic Fiat into a language that Equestrians can biologically and culturally process.
>
> She proves that Posture (marketing/branding) is not inherently evil or "Poseur" behavior if the underlying Asset (the intent to save the nation) is authentic. Rarity uses her Aquileian-style social grace to dress a brutal, industrialized war economy in the comforting, pastel sweater of an Equestrian holiday.

**Characters:**
* **[[Princess Celestia]]**
> **Analysis:** Rarity thinks Celestia accepts the referendum rules because of the Hearth's Warming branding, but the materialist truth (hidden until Chapter 22) is that Celestia is honoring the 930 ALB petitions. The ponies legally asked her to ban this exact type of industrial mobilization 80 years ago. She refuses to unilaterally violate that social contract, which isn't in paper but is in her soul. If Equestria is going to industrialize, the ponies must choose to do it themselves.
>
> It perfectly preserves the epistemological gap. Applejack and Twilight (at the front) and Rarity (in Manehattan) all operate under the assumption that Celestia is just an out-of-touch monarch who is too exhausted to adapt. This allows their frustration to build organically, fueling their "Revolutionary" drive, which makes Celestia’s eventual Chapter 22 reveal (the 930 ALB petitions) hit with maximum, devastating force.


---
### SCENE 7.9: 2nd Referendum for mayor recall
> **Synopsis:** This time only Manehattenites who lived in the city before the start of the war are allowed to vote
>
> The elites run propaganda propping up the confused mayor
>
> Rarity tells Flowing Current to calm down with the explosive rhetoric against the factory owners and let her do the talking
> She speaks the language of the elites. Sche can schmooze, flatter, and shame them into submission. She uses her social capital to dismantle the very class she aspires to belong to. She gets them to reverse course, stop their voter suppression efforts, and come out publicly for the referendum. She pits some influential individuals (media, celebrities) against the mayor.
>
> Rarity has to go into the wealthy, terrified, stagnant neighborhoods of Manehattan. She has to schmooze the elites, comfort the terrified middle-class bakers, and shame the complacent. She uses the Parloir ethos: "Dignity is not something Celestia gives you; it is something you claim. If you stay home, you are letting the Changelings come. You must participate."
>
> the War Economy is not a Griffon machine; it is simply the Hearth's Warming Eve story scaled up to an industrial level. "When the Windigos came, our ancestors didn't hoard their food. They pooled it together to survive the freeze. Chrysalis is the freeze. The Hearth's Warming Bonds are the Fires of Friendship that lives in our hearts."
>
> The CMCs sing The Vote song but instead of "vote for Pip" it's "vote for EEEE!"
>
> Recall needs 83.3%
>
> It still passes by 85%
>
> The mayor himself comes out in support of the referendum near the end

**Characters:**
* **[[Trixie]]**
> **Analysis:** Trixie runs stage magic during the concert, to convey that "magic isn't dead" even though they're in an industrial horror. Her illusions are about morale, not being a conmare
> She is usually selfish and self-centered, but here she is spotlighting others


**Codex:**
* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Apple Bloom's parents revealed to be the leaders of SAA - Standard Agricultural Amalgamated
> Apple Bloom is estranged from them even though they say they're proud of her for raising awareness
> Apple Bloom hates their factory because her special talent is finding/rediscovering other ponies' special talents. In the factory assembly line, everypony is a blank flank. Apple Bloom would rather sew uniforms in Rarity's shop than work at her parents' factory.
>
> The factory should look and feel and sound exactly like The Cutie Remark alt timeline


---
### SCENE 7.10: Celestia refuses to intervene with the 2nd referendum's results
> **Synopsis:** The holdouts beg Celestia to overrule the law and avoid forced work and opening up their property for housing, but in typical Celestia fashion she does nothing, and the law starts getting enforced
>
> Next plan is to replicate it in all the other industrial cities

**Threads:**
* **[[Establishing the Equestrian Republic]]**
> **Update:** The self determination of Manehattan lays the groundwork of the republic, so it's not coming out of nowhere later


---
### SCENE 7.11: Total Mobilization Begins
> **Synopsis:** Parloirs continue to operate as a healthy retreat. They are considered part of the war effort to keep morale up

**Characters:**
* **[[Terrified Reservist]]**
> **Analysis:** This scene should be from her point of view.
>
> She joined the reserves because she was an athlete and all her athlete peers were signing up
>
> She is initially in therapy then comes out of the club and up the stairs to the union hall where the CMCs are delegating ponies to factories according to their special talents

* **[[Parloir Operator]]**
> **Analysis:** She offers therapy and hugs/massages/mane grooming to help struggling ponies who were hit with the trauma of the war


**Threads:**
* **[[Harmonic Capitalism]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** Total mobilization should be a negative depiction of industry. Ponies working in assembly lines with protective suits that cover their cutie marks/manes so they all appear as replaceable cogs. Children allowed to work in the factory, especially refugee children are signing up.
>
> This is ruthless and soulless capitalism, forcibly commandered from the ruthless capitalists and repurposed for war. The harmonic version will be revealed later.


**Themes:**
* **[[Bottom Up > Top Down]]**
> **Commentary:** The Aquileian Model: Represents the theory of democracy (Constitution, laws, structure).
> •The Manehattan Model: Represents the necessity of democracy (Survival, resource allocation, public will).
> •Why this matters: If the Republic was only imported from Aquileia, it would feel foreign to Equestria. By having Manehattan overthrow its mayor via referendum before AJ returns, you prove that Equestrian soil is fertile for Democracy. The ponies are ready to rule themselves.


**Codex:**
* **[[Weather]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Some of the pegasi jobs mentioned involve industrial weather, starting new weather factories, supporting the earth ponies with planned weather


---
## CHAPTER 8: Loyalty
> **Chapter Note:** Reni and Minette were the Aquileian aces who told Rainbow Dash back in Mount Aris
> Should they be here for the meeting?
> Fizzlepop also here
> And they should find out about Ain Trotgourait falling to Colthage and Chirropterra, all of Twilight's work captured
> **Chapter Note:** When Rainbow Dash explains how the Changeling tanks adapted, and Twilight experiences the guilt of "Death from Above," the narrative reveals how the Equestrian static lines were encircled. This justifies the shift to Combined Arms and Applejack's reliance on Henri’s tactical expertise.

### SCENE 8.1: Grabbing Rainbow
> **Synopsis:** Applejack and Twilight are casually walking from their house to the command center when they see a sonic rainboom headed straight for the air base
>
> Rainbow Dash isn't flying gracefully. She is erratic. Her trail isn't a perfect arc; it's sputtering. She is flying on pure adrenaline and desperation, perhaps nursing a bruised wing or ribs from the ejection. She is flying low—below the radar, literally and metaphorically—heading straight for the hangar to scramble again.
>
> Applejack asks Twilight to teleport them to the air base
>
> Rainbow Dash is marching toward a new Spitfire. She's covered in soot, bleeding a little but with a rag. She is shouting at the ground crew to fuel her up.
> Rainbow! What are you doing! AJ yells out, galloping over.
> Hey AJ. I'm going after the ace who shot my wing. I know exactly where he is
> Rainbow is about to leap into the cockpit, but Applejack bites her tail
>
> You've been flying every day, and she's so devoted that when her plane gets shot down, she ejects, flies back to the airbase for a new plane, and hunts the changeling that shot her down.
> It's time to rest
>
> She says Henri told her that soldiers who don't take breaks turn jaded. They become monsters. AJ tells RD to at least come to a war meeting with her. RD reluctantly agrees but doesn't think she'll be useful.
>
> She needs to be in the air shooting bugs to be useful. What use am I if I’m not in the air?
> AJ says you won’t do any better if you burn yourself out. You’ve already been the best pilot over the continent, nopony’s expecting better. Come along to this planning session me and Twi are having with the commanders
> Rainbow sighs and follows, saying she’ll be useless if she’s not in the air

---
### SCENE 8.2: Rainbow explains why she won't rest
> **Synopsis:** Silverstream goes home because Mount Aris is under attack from the Storm King
> Out of loyalty, Rainbow leaves the school to learn how to fly a plane with the rest of the Wonderbolts
> The school shuts down
> Rainbow says they have to do something and convinces the Wonderbolts to militarize like the past, but with griffon biplanes
> Crack Lightning tells the pilots the enemies are evil, they are not creatures, they destroyed this continent and we will destroy them
> Reni told Rainbow don't listen to Crack Lightning, that is cheap. The warlords are creatures. They chose to be that way. They are poseurs who are actually weak and scream loudly to hide the fact they are fragile. We are true flyers who will put them in their place
>
> We crushed them
>
> But deep down, Rainbow says if she takes a break and ponies die she can’t live with herself. The real reason is that she doesn’t want to stop to think about all the changelings she killed or all the ponies she failed to save

**Characters:**
* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** Reni and Minette told Rainbow Dash back then that the warlords are irredeemable poseurs, they terrorized the continent. Shoot down the Storm King's blimps
> To a Harmonic Equestrian, pulling the trigger of a machine gun is a profound violation of their worldview. To cross that line, they must recognize that they are not just fighting enemy soldiers; they are stopping an active genocide.


**Themes:**
* **[[Loyalty and Kinship]]**
> **Commentary:** The Cognitive Shift: The Wonderbolt stops seeing the airship as a vehicle full of living creatures, and starts seeing it as a flying guillotine aimed at a child's neck. Shooting it down isn't murder; it is the ultimate, necessary expression of Equestrian Loyalty. They are sacrificing their own moral purity to protect the innocent.

* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** The Storm King's warlords are poseurs because they lack statecraft, culture, or true discipline. But they mask that incompetence with performative cruelty. They maintain their "Alpha" status among their peers by seeing who can burn a peasant village the fastest. Their indoctrination is the belief that empathy is weakness and brutality is power. The Wonderbolts realize that Harmony cannot exist in a vacuum. If they refuse to shoot down the airship, the warlords on board will land and slaughter the Hippogriffs.


**Codex:**
* **[[The Battle of Mount Aris]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Rainbow Dash is the Element of Loyalty, but she is also a Wonderbolt—she craves glory and status. During the Battle of Mount Aris, she watches Reni and Minette fly off and wipe out airships with pure, individual artisan skill (and Reni's enchanted plane armor)
>
> Dash concludes: "If you are good enough, you don't need a team. A single, hyper-competent hero can carry the entire war on her back and save everyone." This gives her the Atlas Complex. When the Changelings invade Equestria later, Dash tries to fight the entire war herself, burning out because she adopted an Ego-Capitalist military tactic to fulfill a Harmonic burden of loyalty.


---
### SCENE 8.3: Friend talk before the meeting
> **Synopsis:** Rainbow Dash, Applejack and Twilight arrive at the command center's (magically secure) radio room. Fluttershy is already there.
>
> They come in tense, but Fluttershy is completely happy go lucky. She talks about the changelings taking care of animals and about learning their language
> Rainbow interrupts in disgust, then rants about how she should be flying instead of here
> Where are Celestia and Luna? They're princesses, they should've stopped this!
>
> They connect to Rarity in Manehattan over the radio.

**Threads:**
* **[[Camp Fluttershy]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Fluttershy mentions the changelings taking care of displaced/distressed animals, it's perfect for teaching them empathy and giving the animals care, plus their emotion sense lets them understand animals a little bit
>
> Herzlander is the language of pain, so the changelings are learning Equestrian. They have signs in both.
> She mentions learning the native changeling language and makes a click sound, Rainbow is disgusted


**Themes:**
* **[[Loyalty and Kinship]]**
> **Commentary:** Subverting "Loyalty": Rainbow thinks Loyalty means always fighting. Applejack shows her that Loyalty means staying alive so you can fight tomorrow.


**Codex:**
* **[[Weapons of Mass Destruction]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Why can't Celestia just drop the sun on the changeling lands?!


---
### SCENE 8.4: Tirek and Discord
> **Synopsis:** Rainbow asks Fluttershy why isn't Discord here? Can't he snap his fingers and make the war go away?
> Fluttershy says his magic didn't work, and Celestia got angry at him, so he left
>
> Twilight makes the realization that Celestia had Fluttershy befriend Discord as an alternative solution to industrialization, but it doesn't work
> The whole thing about Tirek and the chest, Twilight makes a guess that Celestia planned it, and also wanted her to be the next princess
>
> Twilight just gets more mad at Celestia’s manipulation to get her to be the next princess without asking her
>
> Twilight also learned her friends are everything which is why she went to save Applejack no matter the cost
> I once gave up my magic to save my friends, now I’m using it to save them
> I'm done playing Celestia's games?

**Characters:**
* **[[Discord]]**

**Threads:**
* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]**
> **Update:** I'm done playing Celestia's games?
>
> Celestia’s order to stay off the battlefield creates an impossible paradox for Twilight: to be the perfect "Princess of Friendship," she must sit safely in Canterlot and let her friends die in Tall Tale. This completely breaks Twilight's mind.
>
> Twilight realizes that the "Magic of Friendship" (sacrifice and solidarity) only works against villains who play by mythical rules (like Tirek or Discord). It fails completely against a mechanized, industrialized fascist like Chrysalis.


---
### SCENE 8.5: News From Manehattan
> **Synopsis:** The meeting starts with Rarity and Flowing Current connecting over the radio, and they talk about taking over Manehattan's factories for the war, converting to military production, and running 24/7
> Henri and Fleur are happy that the "revolution" is reaching Manehattan, but might pale when they hear descriptions of ponies working in assembly lines
> Comet Shine looks a little grim, but says they probably deserved it
>
> They explain the new war bonds, and Comet Shine likes it
>
> Comet Shine presents new medium tank bases from Star Energy, and Fizzlepop presents the new Spellblast Cannon, a magical turret that fires bursts of raw magic pre charged with crystals. They conclude that for a proper mobile force to rival the changelings, they need crystals from the mountains of the Temberik and more oil from the southwest and rubber from the Tzinacatl in the southeast.
>
> Why Rubber? You can have all the steel and magic in the world, but without rubber gaskets, engines leak. Without tires, trucks stop. Without treads, tanks throw tracks.
>
> Comet Shine says they'll use the war bonds for oil and give another try with the Temberik
> He mentions them having allies in the jungle already for rubber and the war bonds can be used to increase production

**Characters:**
* **[[Comet Shine]]**
> **Analysis:** Not happy about asset seizure, but says the greedy bastards probably deserved it

* **[[Fleur Bloom]]**
> **Analysis:** Glad the ruthless capitalists were toppled

* **[[Henri Gourard]]**
> **Analysis:** Glad to hear the poseurs were shown their place, but worried that the factories have no soul and pities all the ponies who have to work in those soulless boring factories, but it's a necessary evil


**Codex:**
* **[[Banking]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Applejack should make a scene of saying "that ain't honest, you're making money out of thin air?"
> And Rarity will say it is honesty and trust. It's trust in the future of our country
>
> Comet Shine has to explain they've been running a command economy with no money, just pure harmonic trust the locals give in him. Now he can start issuing war bonds, and talks with Flowing Current to establish the retroactive rates for Tall Tale garrison and everypony who helped. They also mention it for active soldiers. It is a bureaucratic discussion full of jargon. Applejack is alienated.
>
> Applejack only trusts the war bonds as a financial asset because it is Rarity approved. Otherwise she would call it a scam and would be too suspicious of Comet Shine.


---
### SCENE 8.6: War council tactics discussion (Rainbow unveils her genius observational skills)
> **Synopsis:** AJ and the Aquileians are discussing the nature of the changeling mobile warfare doctrine. It's not so different from the FJA's own tactics, but with more conscripts. Every jaeger is responsible for way more subordinates than the FJA are used to. Whenever they are stuck, RD unexpected fills in the gaps. Turns out she instinctively knows the whole strategy because she subconsciously observes everything below her while flying. This is a reference to the canon episode Testing Testing 1 2 3.
>
> They are debating how the tanks rolled them over so they can plan for it, and out of the blue Rainbow chimes in
>
> Rainbow Dash recalls how the tanks rolled over them in Vanhoover which she saw from the sky (reference her ability to learn while flying from Testing Testing 123)
> She describes how even after she bombed a tank, the others reacted and kept operating, changing tactics in response to the air assault
> Twilight pushes Rainbow to remember more
> Rainbow also mentions how they push until their supply lines need to catch up which she saw as she oversaw the shattered retreat
>
> That's impossible, said Henri. They are just like the Herzlander Imperial Army of old. Unwilling serfs led by knights. Kill the knight, and the serfs have no honor or courage to hold them. The changelings are poseurs wearing modern uniforms over a feudal skeleton.
>
> For the infantry, yeah, but I swear, it wasn't like that with the tanks
>
> Henri is in shambles. He previously thought the changelings were feudal slaves and the tanks were Herzland copies. He perceived the changeling army as having no honor, just hollow feudalism. Now he is outraged that the jaegers use Aquileian revolutionary spirit, peer trust and initiative for themselves, but total oppression to the conscripts. It is taking the scale and conscription of Imperialist Herzland without the rules of war, and the fraternity of the Aquileian citizen soldiers without the civil rights and serving a tyrant!
>
> This is even worse, said Henri. They have fraternity for the elites, and slavery for the masses. The elites are the ultimate poseurs, selling the idea that the little changeling can rise while they send them off to their deaths. The ordinary foot soldier is... a victim? Henri grimaced.
>
> That's what I've been saying! said Fluttershy
>
> That can't be! Rainbow Dash cried. Those changelings marched and shot and drained ponies! They're all monsters!
>
> They come up with ways to adapt the tactics to their unique strengths and how to apply pony magic to out do the changelings

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** She recognizes that strong figureheads are important for getting uninformed ponies to work together 
> She just doesn't want to do this forever, hence elections later
>
> This moment cements Applejack’s transition from "Fake General" to true leader. A weak leader would beg Rainbow to rest. A strong leader (The Lioness) uses a brutal truth ("Honesty") to cut through a subordinate's delusion. It shows Applejack is willing to hurt her friend's feelings to save her life—a weaponized form of Honesty that parallels the weaponized Kindness Fluttershy uses on the POWs.

* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** The Problem: Rainbow Dash sees herself as a "Grunt"—a weapon to be pointed at the enemy. She dismisses her own intelligence ("I'm just a flyer").
>
> She evolves into a leader after realizing her tactical intuition, and finally agrees to stop flying herself to death

* **[[Rockfeller]]**
> **Analysis:** Comet Shine alludes to bad oil coming from Rockfeller which is screwing up Star Energy's factories, but says he can try to negotiate and work with the oil workers to figure out the root cause of the problem

* **[[Fizzlepop Berrytwist]]**
> **Analysis:** She's the lead on the spellblast cannon, not Twilight. She is still her friendly, reformed self, but with some flashes of Tempest Shadow as she describes the destruction that awaits the changeling armor


**Threads:**
* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
> **Update:** Took Herzland imperialism and Aquileian individual spirit and peer trust

* **[[Establishing the Equestrian Republic]]**
> **Update:** Applejack calls a war council meeting, saying high command has gone silent in official plans for Tall Tale and the surrounding region since the official orders are to just “retreat” so she is taking command of all operations in the area as General. This meeting establishes joint communications with Flowing Current and Rarity in Manehattan. They plan to get oil from the southwest, rubber from the southeast, and crystals from the Temberik Mountains.

* **[[Magical Combined Arms]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Rainbow reveals that she is subconciously a tactical genius due to her subconcious obesrvation skills (canon from Testing, Testing, 1 2 3)


**Themes:**
* **[[Bottom Up > Top Down]]**
> **Commentary:** When Applejack calls Rainbow a "Jaeger," she is accusing Rainbow of operating under the Feudal/Tyrannical mindset. A Jaeger (Hunter) is an elite, solitary predator who believes they are superior to the drones around them. By refusing to rest and believing the front will collapse without her, Rainbow is implicitly stating that she does not trust her fellow soldiers. She believes she is the "Chosen One" (Top-Down), which is antithetical to the Republic's ideal of collective strength (Bottom-Up).


**Codex:**
* **[[Magical Engineering]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Initial magical rifles and spellblast tank use power crystals for ammo, but they are unstable and unreliable (reflected in in-game stat). It is forcing the magical energy of lower grade crystals down the barrel.

* **[[Combat Drugs]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** The tanks drove for 30 hours straight. How?

* **[[Dragon Lands]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Applejack tells Rainbow do you want to be a dumb dragon that gets shot down? That's where your heroism complex comes from

* **[[Thorax's Failed Harmonist Revolution]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Fluttershy naively believes they can start an uprising and Chrysalis will collapse fast once the changelings see true friendship like the ones at her camp


---
### SCENE 8.7: Jaegers vs Chasseurs
> **Synopsis:** They want Rainbow to go with them to Appleloosa and the jungle, but Rainbow says "I can't leave Tall Tale hanging!"
>
> Applejack ups the stakes by accusing Rainbow of being a jaeger - thinking she can be the hero that must protect all the little ponies on the ground and thinking the front will collapse without her
>
> Rainbow says how dare you, I don't send anypony to die
>
> The war council disbands pretty quickly after, not wanting to see the argument between Applejack and Rainbow Dash
>
> Fluttershy, what does jaeger mean in Herzlander?
> Um, it means hunter...
> Exactly! You're being a hunter, Rainbow Dash.
>
> Rainbow Dash likely assumes "Jaeger" just means "Monster" or "Changeling." When Fluttershy translates it literally as "Hunter," it stings more. "Monster" is a vague slur; "Hunter" is a specific trade. It implies Intent.
>
> Rainbow Dash views her exhaustion as proof of her devotion. Applejack reframes it as proof of her arrogance. By calling her a "Hunter," Applejack strips away the glamour of the "Wonderbolt/Hero" archetype and reveals the ugly mechanical truth beneath: Rainbow has become a killing machine that views herself as separate from and superior to the flock she protects.
>
> Rainbow views herself as the ultimate Shield. She flies to save the "little ponies" on the ground.
> A Jaeger is a Weapon. By using the Herzlander word for "Hunter," Applejack implies that Rainbow is no longer flying to save lives, but to take them. It suggests that Rainbow is addicted to the adrenaline of the "dogfight" (Red Love/Ambition) rather than motivated by the preservation of her friends (Pink Love/Connection).
>
> Calling her the enemy's name—specifically the name of the enemy's elite killers—forces Rainbow to look in the mirror. It aligns Rainbow with Trimmel (the Meritocrat/Hunter) rather than the Temberik or the Earth Ponies (the Community). It breaks her "Atlas Complex" by equating her individual excellence with enemy ruthlessness.
>
> Fluttershy interrupts by asking Rarity how are things going

**Characters:**
* **[[Réni Ducep]]**
> **Analysis:** Réni says I am not a hero, Rainbow Dash.
> So you're a mercenary?
> No, I'm a chasseur (hunter)
> I fight for my ego and to take down poseurs
> When I'm tired I rest
> He is brutally honest
> You are an Equestrian hero. You need to rest
>
> AJ says that's where you're wrong Réni, you are our hero and we love you too


**Codex:**
* **[[The Predator's Dilemma (Coltbert's Paper)]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Applejack calling Rainbow Dash a "Jaeger" is not merely an insult; it is a diagnostic intervention. It diagnoses Rainbow with the "Predator's Dilemma"—she is trapped in a cycle of violence because she refuses to trust the collective. This forces Rainbow to abandon the "Hero" archetype and accept the "Soldier" archetype, allowing her to finally embrace Fraternity and eventually participate in the true Combined Arms doctrine.

* **[[Moriset Discret's Aquileia/Coltbert Reforms]]** (History)
> **Note:** Rainbow Dash desperately turned to Reni. I learned everything from you. We aren't hunters, are we?
> Reni explains how he and Minette met as chasseurs hunting warlords. So he literally is a hunter
> Chrysalis copied their tactics and their name
>
> The Aquileian Ace dives into a swarm of airships alone. He survives the immense stress of this maneuver because he is insulated by Ego. He does not care if the Hippogriff infantry below him lives or dies. He only cares about adding to his Tableau de Chasse (hunting scorecard). His detachment makes him light.
>
> Rainbow Dash watches this solo hotshot tear apart the poseur warlords and thinks, "That is how I keep my friends safe. I must be so fast, so skilled, and so independent that no one else has to risk their lives." Dash tries to execute the Aquileian solo tactics, but she is carrying the psychological weight of the entire Equestrian defense. She cares deeply about everyone on the ground. She is trying to be a ruthless, detached Ace while holding the emotional burden of the Element of Loyalty. Because she lacks the Aquileian arrogance to detach herself from the stakes, the sheer weight of trying to win the war alone crushes her.
>
> Dash becomes so obsessed with being the ultimate, authentic "Anti-Poseur"—the lone hero who relies purely on her own undeniable skill to destroy frauds—that she forgets she is part of a team. She learns that while Ego-Capitalism and Artisan Skill are incredibly effective at killing poseurs, they are terrible tools for carrying the weight of a nation.
>
> Reni admits the Aquileian volunteers hunt poseurs
> Reni concludes that he may be a hunter who hunts warlords and poseurs for ego, but Rainbow Dash is not a hunter. She is the element of Loyalty who is fighting a moral war for her friends. She must rest.


---
### SCENE 8.8: Rainbow stops flying
> **Synopsis:** They ask Rarity how things are going, Rarity talks about the CMCs and Spike and says Applejack's family (and the pears) are all safe and working hard in the factories
>
> Prince Blueblood is doing well managing the northern front, he actually knows what he is doing
>
> Twilight asks about Celestia, and Rarity says Celestia is "like a statue"
>
> What about Luna?
> Rarity lies and says Luna is doing her best to keep Celestia going, and it takes up all of her energy
> Applejack says at least they are looking out for each other
>
> Rainbow realizes there is no Equestria to save. What is she even fighting for?
>
> Here is where Rainbow decides they aren't fighting for the princess or "Equestria" (the state), they are fighting for each other (Fraternity)
>
> If the princesses aren't here, then we are fighting for each other
>
> Now her heroism complex is broken down. Initially, Rainbow gets mad at the princesses, thinking if Rainbow can be the hero, why can't they? But Applejack and Rarity pity the princesses. If the princesses collapsed under the pressure, then Rainbow surely will soon enough.
>
> We don't want to lose you, Rainbow. We love you. A jaeger won't have that love. You can trust the ponies on the ground to fight while you rest. It'll be okay.
>
> Rarity says EEEE! operates on solidarity, not lone heroes
> This appeals to both Twilight and Applejack too, lowering the weight on their shoulders as they realize Tall Tale acts in solidarity too
>
> This marks the moment the Mane 6 officially stop waiting for permission to save the world.
>
> Rainbow sees how she might be more valuable not flying all the time and finally agrees to take a break from the air and go with the rest of them to Las Pegasus

**Characters:**
* **[[Princess Celestia]]**
> **Analysis:** Rarity says she is "like a statue", paralyzed and unwell. Twilight goes from anger to pity?
>
> In reality, Celestia is certainly guilty that it came to this, but she really doesn't know how to save Equestria without losing its soul. She is truly lost.

* **[[Rarity]]**
> **Analysis:** Rarity says she's sorry she can't be at the front with the rest of them, but Applejack says you're doing just as much as us, and only you could have corralled the upper class and taken them down. Rarity is relieved of her guilt.
>
> She relays the Sisterhooves Social lesson to Rainbow Dash that Applejack gave to her: given in a little and compromise


**Threads:**
* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]**
* **[[Camp Fluttershy]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Rarity is sorry she only wanted to stitch cutie marks on uniforms initially, not taking the war seriously at the start
> Fluttershy says that's a great idea, she'll have the changelings stitch cutie marks with silk and dyes


**Themes:**
* **[[Loyalty and Kinship]]**
> **Commentary:** Loyalty to her friends' earnest wishes instead of Loyalty to the abstract state or the nameless soldiers below


**Codex:**
* **[[Canon Episode References]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Sisterhooves Social


---
### SCENE 8.9: Fleur starts nerding out over Rainbow Dash and wants her to come to the lab
> **Synopsis:** Fleur wants to study Rainbow since Rainbow is taking a break from flying, before they leave for Las Pegasus. Twilight joins. Rainbow is not pleased. Chapter ends here on a comedic cliffhanger.

---
### SCENE 8.10: Aquileian Cutie Mark Theory
> **Synopsis:** Fleur explains to Twilight how unicorns have a discount on their special talent, and since Twilight's special talent is magic, she gets a discount on everything
> Twilight never thought of it that way
> This is the beginning of their nerd-alliance. Rainbow is super freaked out
>
> Then Fleur says you have these weird signals keeping you from sleeping, and it's very bad for you
> Rainbow admits as she flew over Diyarbecolt she got awake spells from Starlight who is at the mine
> That's why she's been irritable. She thought if the jaegers can drive their tanks for 30 hours then she should be able to fly all day.
> Twilight is horrified and says I'm going to put you to sleep with a sleep spell. Rainbow accepts it.

**Characters:**
* **[[Fleur Bloom]]**
> **Analysis:** The Twilight-Fleur nerd alliance begins here!
> Prior to this, Twilight had a feeling that Fleur didn't like her for some reason


**Themes:**
* **[[Loyalty and Kinship]]**
> **Commentary:** the logical thing to do would be to plop Rainbow Dash in a medical tent or barracks in Tall Tale so she can catch up on sleep and recover. But instead, Twilight, Applejack and Fluttershy still take Rainbow Dash with them to Las Pegasus and later Appleloosa. They put her on a cloud bed and push the cloud around with them while Rainbow is sleeping. Rainbow wakes up, say 30 hours later, in Appleloosa with her 3 friends nearby instead of in a hospital bed.
>
> This serves the theme and also is hilarious when other characters question why they are taking a sleep pegasus on a cloud with them through the streets of Las Pegasus and the prairie of Appleloosa


**Codex:**
* **[[Unified Theory of Magic]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Fleur wants to study Rainbow's pegasi abilities and correlate it with her special talent
>
> discounts for cutie marks unveiled
>
> Because Rainbow's Cutie Mark is tied to stunt flying, her brain is biologically optimized to process complex spatial, kinetic, and environmental data at near-zero cognitive cost—only when she is in the air.
>
> This sets the stage for the reveal that Earth Ponies and Pegasi aren't just "doing physical labor." They are running highly complex, latent magical equations in the background of their biology. Fleur's Earth Pony magic is a background chemical reaction with the soil; Rainbow Dash's Pegasus magic is a background physics calculation with aerodynamics.

* **[[Cutie Marks]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Twilight says she can cast any spell, Fleur counters by saying your special talent IS magic, so you get a discount on everything.

## CHAPTER 9: Sabotage
> **Chapter Note:** Need to come up with a reason for Twilight to realize how the love harvester tech can be used. Something about AJ and Twi discussing capitalism for good and reevaulating Applebuck Season, Winter Wrap Up and Squeezy at night. Then in the morning, something related sparks and idea
> **Chapter Note:** Might want to split this chapter
> **Chapter Note:** The teachings of the University of Pridea also apply to oil fields
> Rockfeller employed earth ponies to run the oil drills but kept most of the profits for himself. Applejack and Twilight believe that if they give the oil drills to the workers, they will be more productive because they will care about their land
> The oil drills on buffalo land are given to buffalo to operate
> The buffalo have similar magic to earth ponies where their traditional stomping and stewardship of their land is how the prairie flourished without pegasi weather management
> The cooperation between the ponies and buffalo in Appleloosa is why the apples there are exceptional. You gotta share, you gotta care!
> Star Energy experts take over the company’s operations and facilitate the fair distribution of company assets to the workers who actually operated the drills (outside buffalo land) and sets up a training program for the buffalo for drills inside buffalo land
> Applejack said it's your land that was invaded by Rockfeller, I understand if you want to destroy the oil fields in a grand stampede and be done with it
> The Buffalo say no, it is our obligation to contribute to the war effort
>
> Applejack talks to Comet Shine, saying how Rockfeller had total control over the place thanks to his money and influence
> She says how can they guarantee that Star Energy doesn’t go that way? After all, you own all the factories in Tall Tale, all the infrastructure, power plants, the port, the mines in Unicorn Range, and now you’re taking over Rockfeller’s empire too
> Comet Shine says he wasn’t the founder of Star Energy
> Star Energy was a simple oil-powered tractor company whose business model was to buy used tractors from Griffonia and lease them to struggling farms around Tall Tale
> The promise to the farmers was it would make them so much more productive, but the farmers had to pay to use the tractors, they didn’t own them. When the old tractors needed repairs, the company would charge more and the farmers had no choice. Also only one supplier of oil, coincidentally one of Rockfeller’s early partners
> The business was undoubtedly exploitative and cruel, hurting the farmers and taking advantage of those who are already down and desperate
> The business failed as it didn’t help the farmers at all, just made some quick bits for the scheme. As the farmers stopped doing business, the CEO stopped bringing in more junk tractors and was going to shutdown the company and run off to Las Pegasus with his profits, leaving the tractor technicians with no jobs either
> Comet Shine came in to take over the company, bringing in the new crystal powered tractor but more importantly a new business model based on honesty which would focus on uplifting the farmers. Giving ownership of the tractors to the farmers, not price gouging but wanting to spread his invention for the good of the community, honest service and pricing for oil and maintenance, and giving jobs to unicorns to recharge the crystals
> The workers told him to change the name of the company because it already had such a bad rep, but Comet Shine wanted to keep it and turn the reputation around, that’s how he’ll know he succeeded (but changed the logo to match his cutie mark). And it worked, since everypony loves Star Energy today and for good reason
> I am not Rockfeller, Comet Shine vowed. And if I ever became Rockfeller, my employees would kick me out
> I appreciate you asking, Comet Shine said to Applejack
> **Chapter Note:** It seems like the casualty of the farms is enough reason for Twilight to split, don't even need the early Hans Kessel revelation or plan with the love harvester yet? But still want it for the friendship letter. Maybe Twilight sends a different friendship letter
> **Chapter Note:** Thunderhooves gives a list of collaborators and locations of stockpiles to prove it, but says he can't say how they got the info
> Fleur is disappointed that gilded isn't on the list
> Star energy isn't
> The mayor and police are on the list, so they need gilded to help
>
> Gilded Trust says I knew those Skyfall griffons were evil
> Ponies first
> Giving out endless loans
> Chrysalis shell company
>
> After seizure, they have to decide how to split the oil fields
>
> The town hall is between both Comet Shine and Gilded Trust
> This is the grand debate
>
> It will be a proportional vote
> Some ponies do buy gilded's vision

### SCENE 9.1: Applejack reflects on how Star Energy Corporation had the incentive and means to save Tall Tale
> **Synopsis:** Rainbow is sound asleep despite the bumpy road thanks to Twilight's sleep spell (this is the foreshadowing for how they will outdo blitz-essenz)
>
> Actually I shifted the perception to saying Applejack does not like their vibes
>
> On the way to Las Pegasus, Applejack mentions how Star Energy is motivated because they need to protect their assets. Corporations are great
> Star Energy in charge of Tall Tale is doing great
>
> If Star Energy was in charge of the country, Chrysalis would have lost back in Vanhoover
>
> But Applejack, they're making guns, said Twilight
> How can we trust them?
>
> It should be framed as "Star Energy's greed kept Tall Tale alive, but they should not run everything post war, there can't be any harmony in that"

**Threads:**
* **[[Harmonic Capitalism]]**
> **Update:** Applejack says the capitalist corporation had the means to defend against Chrysalis because they had incentive to protect their assets
> Twilight believes their greed is good for war, but it can't possibly work in peace time for harmony, right?

**Themes:**
* **[[Loyalty and Kinship]]**
> **Commentary:** Rainbow is theirs and they are hers and the mission does not supersede that. The mission went on. Rainbow came with it. Both things were true simultaneously, which required three ponies to push a cloud through the streets of Las Pegasus while explaining to confused strangers that yes, there is a sleeping pegasus on that cloud, yes we know, she is with us.

### SCENE 9.3: Meeting Gilded Trust first
> **Synopsis:** Comet Shine goes to Gilded Trust for oil first, since he is the current best supplier for Star Energy
> He can increase a little, but Rockfeller has the lion's share and is suspiciously inefficient
>
> Comet Shine says Gilded Trust propped him up, they would've collpased without his oil donations for the war effort
>
> Gilded Trust gives Applejack a warm welcome, saying he liked her hard truth speech and that she saved them
> Comet Shine says Gilded gives a lot of supply chain support to the Star Energy factories, it's not all self sufficient in Tall Tale. All the oil that powers their factories and planes is coming from Gilded Trust, he is partly responsible for why they haven't collapsed
> Applejack is unsure how to feel about this guy who has even more flim flam vibes than Comet Shine
>
> Why do you even want to talk with Rockfeller? I can get more for you
> With respect, it won't be enough. I wholly appreciate you holding out and not selling-
> You bet I held out
> He will never sell his small sliver of the oil fields to Rockfeller because he views Rockfeller as a Skyfall puppet/self-colonizer/parasite.
>
> Comet Shine and Gilded Trust want to have a joint negotiation with Rockfeller to give him more pressure or offer to buy the oil fields off of him to make them more efficient, and do some corpo speak to decide some deal
> Not sure if Gilded Trust should be there if he has so much grievance with Rockfeller
> Comet Shine came for advice first

> **Analysis:** Applejack doesn't know what to think. He praised her, but he's also such a businesspony
>
> The "Gilded Bit" is a tangible symbol for his "Great Man" complex
>
> He gave oil to Comet Shine for free since it's for national defense and Rockfeller wasn't giving


**Codex:**
* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Gilded Trust has nearly the same coloration as Applejack (to fit the Trump coloration, he is orange with a blonde fake toupee)
> He says your parents always said I reminded them of their little filly
> Applejack shuts him down right away saying I don't want to talk about my parents, with viciousness

### SCENE 9.4: Meeting with Rockfeller
> **Synopsis:** Fluttershy notices the huge bird observatory attached to Rockfeller's estate on their way in
>
> Rockfeller and Comet Shine should be talking the exact same language and vibe (inspired by Flim and Flam) this should be hillarious corpo speak
>
> Comet Shine starts the conversation with Rockfeller by saying they need oil for power plans since they had to blow the dam
> They need moer oil for their tanks
>
> Rockfeller says he doesn't have the capital for more production
>
> Fleur says they can cooperate and do joint research with earth pony magic
> Rockfeller says his well designs are patented and he's not accepting outside sharing or joint development
>
> Rockfeller says Celestia has no money, so he needs money from Star Energy for the workers and capital improvements and names an enormous price tag in bits
> Fleur balks at the price tag and walks out, muttering about needing to stir up the revolution all over again
>
> Comet Shine takes over with more corpo speak and says we have war bonds. Rockfeller rejects the war bonds. (How does he do this without sounding straight up treasonous? He does not believe solidarity has value, and gives some economic arguments) Comet Shine then tries to appeal to Rockfeller's ego, but it goes nowhere
>
> In the corpo speak, have Rockfeller ask Comet Shine about industrial pest controls, since Comet Shine operates a tractor company
> Actually they should be past associates with a business history!
> The moment Comet Shine greets Applejack as an old friend, she immediately shudders
>
> Then Rockfeller says there's another way to increase production. He says the buffalo constantly sabotage his oil drills and pipelines. He doesn’t know how they do it, some witched practices they have, but they must be stopped
> They're traitors hurting the war effort
>
> Comet Shine and his employees stay behind to try and iron out a deal with Rockfeller by wining and dining in Las Pegasus. Fleur Bloom is disgusted and tags along with Applejack, Twilight, Rainbow Dash and Fluttershy instead.
> Fluttershy wishes she could have visited the bird observatory

* **[[Comet Shine]]**
