### SCENE 10.5: Convincing Hans Kessel to help them study the Love Harvester
> **Synopsis:** Twilight, Fleur and Fluttershy grab Henri to translate Herzland and then get Hans at the POW camp
>
> It's not dumb luck that they have a subject matter expert amongst the POWs.
> Chrysalis's school pipeline means there are thousands of engineers who know how the love harvester works, in order to have many firms iterating on it.
> After the 2nd great leap forward and consolidation event, he burned out.
> Once rehabbed, all he can do is draw schematics, because Chrysalis ingrained in the changelings that your work is your life. Without it, you are nothing. That's why he draws, inadvertently exposing state secrets and the key to Equestria's industrial response to Chrysalis.
>
> He joined a startup that condensed love to pack more in a canister
> His team won a medal
> New urgency as Leistungsgemeinschaft consolidation approaches. He started taking blitz-essenz in the final weeks before the showcase, but his company still got absorbed
> Kessel-Druck GmbH (Kessel Pressure) Kessel=Boiler, it's the pressure vessel
>
> Henri tells Hans you can help us adopt the harvester for our purposes, and you will surpass all the other changelings who scorned you. We will help you; you are part of a new team. We won't let you crash again. Do you want to sit here in Equestrian kindergarden or do you want to go to the lab and beat Hauptstock (Main Hive Industries)?
> Hans chooses to weaponize his knowledge out of spite and professional pride

**Characters:**
* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]**
> **Analysis:** Twilight realizes she used to think her self-esteem was based on her output and Celestia's validation, before she had friends

* **[[Hans Kessel]]**
> **Analysis:** He believes his only value is his output.


**Themes:**
* **[[Rugged Individualism / Wedge Issues Deconstructed]]**
> **Commentary:** After the consolidation, Chrysalis let the company workers keep their surnames as a source of "pride" even though the reality is that the company lost all autonomy to the state. The preserved name is a mask, a gesture, a wedge issue to deflect from the reality that they lost all economic autonomy/capital rights. They can pretend they have their own found families, but really they all belong to the State.

* **[[Opportunity over Pacification]]**
> **Commentary:** Changeling school is fair and meritocratic, but those who fail are pacified in the factory


**Codex:**
* **[[Chrysalis's New Order]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Hans reveals the kindergarden to engineering/military school/indoctrination pipeline, and how the failure case is drafted and drugged.
> This hits Twilight's student anxiety "Magic Kindergarten" hard. Also Fluttershy struggling in Flight School. The conscripts really are kindergardeners just learning to read, make friends, and color within the lines.
>
> The Company surnames are revealed, how you don't get a name until you graduate school and join a company or a military division or statthalter sect.
> Henri and Fleur see this as You don't own your shop or land. You don't have a heritage. The State owns you.
>
> Hans reveals how his company had to work harder to compete, he started taking drugs to keep up, burned out, his company got acquired, he really crashed and was told you can't innovate anymore, go operate the harvester in the field and maybe if you do well you can come back. By the way here are some more drugs to deal with the pain of the front.

* **[[Love Harvester]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Hans Kessel says the core engineering problem his company and sector worked on is extraction efficiency. The three way valve requires magic input to actively filter red from pink. The red coming out of the harvested victim is unstable, but must be fed back into the three way valve and draining crystal, so they need large, durable three way valves and draining crystals that can handle the unstable extracted red love. They started with a crude IP copy from Skyfall and iterated on it crudely.
>
> Since the donated red love is pure, they can use new bleeding edge hydraulic valves that are delicate

* **[[Magical Engineering]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Hans Kessel worked on increasing the efficiency of the three way valve, but it was always ineffcient due to the unstable extracted red love
> He says they stole and replicated old Kessel valve designs
> He starts marvelling at the stability of the donated red love and this gets him to start understanding harmony and having a cause to fight for. He spent his career trying to stabilize the tortured red love

* **[[The Battle of Mount Aris]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Henri heard stories from the other Aquileians about the poseur Storm King. But now he realizes Chrysalis is fundamentally a different class of enemy.
>
> Chrysalis isn't flying outdated airships filled with screaming warlords; she is rolling out mass-produced panzers and disciplined, drugged infantry.

* **[[Moriset Discret's Aquileia/Coltbert Reforms]]** (History)
> **Note:** The royal tastings and Chrysalis's corruption of it into industrial and military showcases is detailed here
> Discret used patronage to celebrate Terroir and craftsmanship. Chrysalis uses it to celebrate innovation, but mainly industrial efficiency, killing machines, and torture
> Chrysalis stripped the soul out of the Aquileian tradition and left only the performance
>
> This furthers Henri and Fleur's hatred of everything Chrysalis stands for. She acts like a King but has no taste. She is the ultimate soulless poseur.

* **[[How names are perceived]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Hans recognizes Twilight as "Funken-Dammerung" which is a literal translation "Spark-Twilight" who is the princess. It sounds like a welding accident or electrical fault during the evening shift.
> He reveals the literal name interpretations

* **[[Thorax's Failed Harmonist Revolution]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** The reality of atomization revealed


---
### SCENE 10.6: Twilight sends the first letter about Kessel
> **Synopsis:** Twilight isn't sure how to write a "love letter"
> I've written letters to parents about their students' progress, I've written diplomatic letters, I've written ... but I've never written a love letter. I wish Rarity was here. Do you know how to write a love letter?
> Fleur laughs and says forget all those letters that you were forced to write before. Just talk about your magical research today, just be yourself
> Twilight's eyes widen
>
> It's just like a friendship lesson
>
> Dear General Applejack,
> Today I learned a very important lesson about friendship. We have always grown up in a safe environment where everypony wants to be friends, but what if you never had a friend before? The changelings were raised in an environment where every other changeling is a competitor. Even when they do become colleagues, it's still them against the world, and they forget about each other if they don't show results.
> We met a changeling named Hans Kessel. He was once the top of engineering elite, and was on a team that got medals from Chrysalis. But when he stopped putting out good work, he was slowly forgotten. His entire existence is built on results and output; he has no self-esteem.
> We can't just ask the changelings to be friends because they truly don't know how to be friends. It sounds crazy but it's not. It's not a matter of just offering them friendship. We have to show them what friendship really is.
>
> [Sometimes just being nice isn't enough, but shared purpose is another way to build lasting friendship]
>
> Talks about rifle details
>
> Love, Twilight Sparkle

**Characters:**
* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]**
> **Analysis:** Thanks to Fleur, Twilight is going to have a full "return to form" to season 1-3 (but with friendship lessons learned) in that she is no longer shackled by Celestia's (Hasbro's) expectations of being The Princess of Friendship and can now be the original (Lauren Faust) version again.
>
> No more polished royal diplomat teaching top-down friendship. She is the nerd, the egghead.
>
> Hyper-fixates, cares deeply about details, wants to solve problems (instead of holding back out of fear of insulting others)
> - not always generic kindness, but snarky, dry wit
> - no more bureaucratic management of friendship. A return to obsessive, deep-dive research (It's About Time, Spike at your service, Boast Busters). This is the perfect environment, at Star Energy, developing the rifle that will democratise her own killing power. As a Magical Engineer, she has gotten back her Agency - engaging with dangerous magic to solve problems.
> - No more accepting "legends" or taking Celestia's words at face value. She is going back to skepticism and empiricism (like Feeling Pinkie Keen and Bridle Gossip where Twilight demanded proof)


---
### SCENE 10.7: Applejack receives the first letter
> **Synopsis:** Applejack closed her eyes with a warm smile, holding the telegraph paper to her chest
> It's that good, huh? Comet Shine chuckled
> You can read it?
> Excuse me? I dare not read your love letter!
> It's a report about rifle development. It's good news. Y'all want to read it?
>
> It must be so poetic, said Mali
> Rainbow made a face. Is it really that sappy? Take a look, RD.
> No thanks
> No, really
> Bwa ha ha! Rainbow collapsed. This is a love letter? Twilight is such an egghead!
> That's the kind of letter Twi would write to Princess Celestia when she first came to Ponyville. And look who it's addressed to? Me, Applejack said smugly.

**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** The friendship lesson reports to Celestia (vertical) are subverted into love letters that are a horizontal partnership. Applejack interprets the egghead report as a declaration of supreme love.


---
### SCENE 10.8: Meeting Rarity and the CMCs at the medicinal tribe
> **Synopsis:** They read Twilight's letter
> They are in high spirits
> Comet Shine says they already have allies and are delivering more equipment, they give us medicine
> The volunteers you saw
> Some are with them in the entourage too
> Mali says she's ready to convince her mom to join the cause
> Applejack thinks the mission will be easy. They have friends already, all they have to do is convince isolationists by investing in them
>
> They meet up with allied tribes who have longstanding connections with Star Energy
>
> Applejack enters the tribe's caves/temple system and sees griffonian tech everywhere. She is shocked that the Tzinacatl are more advanced than Ponyville!
> But also tons of SAA rations
> Rarity and Flowing Current and thestrals from EEEE! are already there
>
> The CMCs came too. They aren't little kids anymore, they are union members and policy makers
> Apple Bloom and Applejack reunite, Scootaloo and Rainbow Dash reunite (biggest payoff for Rainbow pausing flying to go on this mission)
>
> Rainbow shows Rarity the letter that Twilight sent Applejack
> Rarity gasps
> You two are together? This is the best possible thing!
> Rarity says she saw Twilight have a crush and get a little flustered around Applejack, and Applejack was softening her stubbornous around Twilight
> Then when Twilight became the princess and the library was destroyed and her new imperative from Celestia came, the light died, and it was a tragedy
> What happened? Tell me tell me tell me!
> Well, first, Twi saved my life in the trench, said Applejack. Rarity swoons at how romantic that is.
> Then I made Twilight kill hundreds of changelings. Rarity's face fell.
> We cried each other to sleep that night. But the catalyst was talking about being a fake general
> Twilight said she wanted the real me, not the general. Rarity swoons again
> And look how she wrote me this friendship letter as if I'm the Princess
> Rarity giggles and says how very Twilight Sparkle of her
>
> Rarity meets Mali and they click right away

**Codex:**
* **[[Baltimare Communist Party]]** (Organization)
> **Note:** The CMCs and Rarity went to Baltimare before the Tzinacatl

* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** The Thestrals prove that Celestia’s "Stagnation" was a deliberate choice, not a biological inevitability.
> Why Celestia Suppresses Them: They are the living proof that her "Garden of Eden" is artificial. If regular ponies see Bat Ponies succeeding with ambition and "honor combat," the facade of the Stagnation cracks.


---
### SCENE 10.9: Flowing Current mentions Applejack's Parents
> **Synopsis:** Applejack asks Apple Bloom how are Mom and Dad
> Apple Bloom says fine, they wanted me to be their chief talent officer but I sewed uniforms instead until the referendum campaigning
> They're hosting the whole Apple and Pear families, they're getting along, so that's nice
> The factory still makes everypony a blank flank
>
> Flowing Current says they worked for free even when Celestia couldn't pay, because they knew the army and the country needed food to survive the changelings
> They were always the best factory to work for, everypony wanted to work for them
> They offered salvation to many starving creatures in Skyfall, the Griffonian Republic, Herzland, Zebrica
> Applejack starts forgiving them, now that she understands Harmonic Capitalism, but still hates how they "abandoned her"
> Many Tzinacatl buy canned food from them too, it keeps them nourished. Actually that's one of the reasons they exported for bits or skyfall marks, to buy cheaped canned food for sustenance while they focused on artisan goods
> The medinial Tzinacatl also mention Zap Apple Jam from Sweet Apple Acres
> This plants a seed in Applejack's head that maybe the farm and the factory are not mutually exclusive
> Applejack's parents bought Zap Apple Jam from Filthy Rich's supply chain and promoted it
>
> They sold zap apple jam to go as flavor for the rations
>
> Applejack says maybe it's not so bad after all. They fed thousands of griffons
> Applejack comes to terms with the fact that the farm and the factory can coexist

**Themes:**
* **[[Accelerants used for Evil can be Repurposed for Good]]**
> **Commentary:** Applejack's parents used the industrial machine to feed the army for free until war bonds could be signed into law. The other industrialists were sabotaging to collaborate with Chyrsalis. The industry is neutral. It's the earth pony family spirit vs rugged individualism that determined the outcome.


**Codex:**
* **[[Parloirs/Pre-War Manehattan]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** In Celestia’s "Nursery," ponies are chained to their biological destinies. But when these ponies get bored of the Stagnation of Harmony, they flock to the Apple parents' factory and put on industrial jumpsuits. They are literally covering their cutie marks, voluntarily pausing their biological asset specificity to experience the standardized, anonymous industrial world. Choosing to be a cog temporarily is agency against their predetemined roles in stagnant Equestria.
>
> Apple Bloom uses "Blank Flank" not as a derogatory insult but as a way to describe the unionized factory workers. She's moved past the childhood trauma and making a mature observation of the nature of her job as an asset specificity consultant and how not all ponies need it to be content

* **[[Banking]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** They worked through faith in each other. No bits needed.

* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** They built a machine strong enough to save Equestria. They were the adults who did what Celestia neglected to do.


---
### SCENE 10.10: Applejack sends a letter about expanding world view
> **Synopsis:** It should relate to her new understanding of her parents
> Just as Twilight's first letter was about changelings never having friendship, Applejack says she never had ambition outside her farm life
>
> Twilight's take away connects these concepts directly to pink love and red love
>
> Applejack sees it, doesn't like General so she intentionally starts it with Dear Princess Twilight Sparkle and ends with Love, Applejack (no general)

**Codex:**
* **[[Unified Theory of Magic]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Come up with a name for the theory based on the changeling lands and Applejack's farm!
> Maybe delays this for pinkie sense

### SCENE 10.11: Starlight helps with the research
> **Synopsis:** Starlight arrives in Tall Tale with a fresh batch of highest-grade crystals from the Temberik mine

**Characters:**
* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]**
> **Analysis:** Twilight makes a point of teaching Starlight about harmonic capitalism, and Fleur approves
> This is where their mutual teaching nerdiness shines through
> Starlight will find it endearing and a little disturbing
> Starlight should comment on how Twilight is being an organic teacher instead of rigid like with the Friendship Lessons, and noting the shift to "old Twilight" which Starlight never fully met until now

* **[[Pinkie Pie]]**
> **Analysis:** Twilight asks Starlight how Pinkie is doing. Starlight says she works hard in the mines with Maud and the Temberik and fires the artillery whenever there's an attack up the mountain. She's quiet. She doesn't want to see other Equestrians who expect the Party Pony.
>
> Twilight says when you go to get more crystals, bring Pinkie back with you. We might have a job for her. (baking with pink love for changelings)

* **[[Starlight Glimmer]]**
> **Analysis:** Starlight knows a thing or two about taking magic away from ponies (her cutie mark removal spell)
> When Twilight tells Starlight that magic can be used for evil or good, Starlight comes forth with the details of her cutie mark removal spell. She spent a decent amount of time making sure the process was painless for the pony so that the citizens of Our Town wouldn't be revolted by getting their magic taken away.
> Starlight's spell isn't total magic removal? It's not exactly the same? How exactly does it work within my framework?
>
> Actually... in the episode, it kind of looked like it hurt, or at least was not comfortable for the mane 6 when Starlight took their cutie marks. I guess that's the best Starlight was able to do, and they were fighting against it
>
> But the part where she numbed the pain will work with the changeling draining spell
> So they incorporate that into the spell matrix of the love donator to make it not hurt for the donor, unlike the love harvester which hurts for the victim as their magic and friendship are forcibly taken from them, as if being drained by a changeling
>
> The changeling draining spell crystals are crude copies and large crystals, stamped with industrial master die. Brutal, fast, 2.5D surface pattern shaped like a changeling horn
>
> Hans does the draining spell, Fleur puts it under a microscope, Starlight recreates it and integrates it with her numbing spell, since she knows a thing or two about draining ponies... now used for good intentions. She's already done much worse, actually stealing a pony's magical capability by ripping their cutie mark out and putting it in a jar
>
> She used to strip ponies of their asset specificity so they would all be equally weak. Now she is making everyone equally empowered (by donating with less discomfort) and equally lethal (with a magical rifle)


**Codex:**
* **[[Unified Theory of Magic]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** How does Starlight's cutie mark removal spell fit into the unified theory of magic?

* **[[Love Donator]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Starlight made the draining spell less painful.

* **[[Crystal Enhancer]]** (Technology)
* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Twilight considers that Celestia never pursued magical enhancement technologies because the cutie mark system dictates separation
> Celestia never pursued the industrial application of magic, favoring a balance between the three tribes and their roles

### SCENE 10.12: Twilight sends a letter about Magic as an Accelerant

### SCENE 10.13: Medicinal Tribe Negotiations
> **Synopsis:** Star Energy entourage delivers some more equipment for medicine refining, more capital to scale up medicine production
> It's harmonic capitalism at it's best, and Applejack is happy
> Wonderbolts provide transport planes to go to Skyfall because they decide not all the medicine goes to the front, some go for export (contraceptives, evasive)
> They need to purchase critical parts from Aquileia and Skyfall for the factories
> They are Aquileian-aligned and get along with Star Energy and Mali right away
>
> Currently, the allied medicinal tribes only produced enough medicine for Star Energy
> Now they are investing more capital and offering war bonds in order to increase production for the entire Equestrian army
>
> The capital they are bringing is for more rubber production for planes, trucks and tanks
> It improves rubber yield, respects traditional methods and respects the trees (terroir)
>
> Comet Shine offers the war bonds as payment in order to ramp up rubber production dramatically for the war.
> The equipment is for scaling up rubber distiliation operations, which they have done joint research on to connect Aquileian engineering with traditional Tzinacatl alchemy. It respects both terror (the rubber trees) and special talents (alchemy). The double whammy of harmonic capitalism!
> They are trusting of Comet Shine/Star Energy, but they do not trust the central Equestrian government after Celestia's mere token reforms. They are accustomed to being left behind and living in the shadows/the night of manehattan and never legitimized
>
> Mali has to convince them that times have changed
> Applejack and Rarity provide legitimacy and honest promises

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** Applejack transitions from "Imposter Syndrome" to "Statesmare" not by learning military tactics, but by applying honest hard work and deals (farm logic) to governance/diplomacy.

* **[[Rarity]]**
> **Analysis:** The parloir elegant culture in Manehattan is what inspired Rarity as a filly. She is an Aquileian in spirit
> When she set up shop in Manehattan she helped revitalize several parloirs with new home-grown fashion and fresh ideas instead of the same Aquileian imported stuff
> Rarity is a veteran of the Night economy


**Threads:**
* **[[Establishing the Equestrian Republic]]**
> **Update:** By backing the war bonds, Applejack and Rarity are unknowingly claiming sovereignty from Celestia


**Codex:**
* **[[Tzinacatl]]** (Organization)
> **Note:** The initial perception is this will be easy and glorious. They already have allies, they are delivering more capital and war bonds. Literal economic integration. They just need to convince Mali's mom to join up. They think it's just theological differences causing the squabbles (but in reality the theology are allegories for economic and moral ethos which are the actual root problems)

* **[[Parloirs/Pre-War Manehattan]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Some more hints about Mali and the parloirs
>
> How to drop hints of Mali’s past without breaking her "Pinkie/Fluttershy" archetype during the negotiations:
>
>     Vocabulary of Service: Even when discussing war or alliances, Mali uses words like "Comfort," "Needs," "Satisfaction," and "Atmosphere." She views the alliance as a Transaction of Trust.
>
>     Reading the Room: While the other tribal leaders are posturing (showing teeth/wings), Mali is observing. She notices who is tired, who is hungry, who is lying. This is the skill of a Parloir hostess who has to read a client’s mood in seconds to ensure safety.
>
>     The "Touch" of Civilization: When she negotiates, she brings small comforts—tea, cushions, lighting. She civilizes the negotiation space. Her mother sees this as "soft," but the other tribes see it as "sophisticated" (Aquileian influence), which wins them over.

* **[[Tzinacatl Magic/Alchemy/Biology]]** (Technology)
* **[[Crystal Enhancer]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** How the crystal enhancer needs Tzinacatl alchemy
> Caquiz-Olli/"Echo Gel" was originally for healing batpony ears who used too much echolocation


---
### SCENE 10.14: Rarity's Recontextualized Backstory vs Applejack's
> **Synopsis:** Rarity's canon personality is a rejection of the stagnation of harmony
> Celestia’s Stagnation of Harmony is defined by a lack of ambition, a lack of clothing (nakedness = innocence/prey), and a static acceptance of one's lot in life. Rarity rejects all three of these premises in canon, making her a natural philosophical ally to the 2nd Aquileian Republic.
>
> In the daytime, the Parloirs are Salons of High Culture. They are places where ponies wear exquisite clothes, drink tea, discuss art, gossip about "Romance" (in the dramatic, storybook sense), and practice advanced etiquette.
>
> Rarity saw thestrals and ponies interacting with Charm and Style. She saw that through Fashion and Charisma, even a common pony could command the room like a Princess.
>
> She absorbed the Aquileian concept of L'Art de Vivre (The Art of Living)—the idea that life should be a performance of beauty.
>
> When she returned to Ponyville to design costumes for the school play, she wasn't just making clothes; she was trying to bring that Manehattan Glamour (Aquileian Ambition) to her small town.
>
> Her Cutie Mark (Gems) represents finding Value in the rough. Just as the FJA believes industry should enhance the land, Rarity believes magic/fashion should enhance the pony. Her talent is revealing the hidden beauty/worth in things—the ultimate capitalist-for-good trait.
>
> Rarity's canon personality is recontextualized as the Aquileian way
> Now Applejack can understand it after the town hall
>
> Applejack hated Manehattan because her parents left the farm
> She was initially distrustful of Rarity
> Now they can understand the difference between Skyfall influence and Aquileian influence

**Characters:**
* **[[Rarity]]**

**Codex:**
* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Celestia’s Blind Eye: Just like with the drugs, she ignores the parloirs as long as they stay in the "shadows" of Manehattan and don't infect Ponyville. The parloirs are containment zones.


---
### SCENE 10.15: Applejack sends a letter about special talents
> **Synopsis:** Harmonic capitalism for rubber production, respecting local methods
> It's just honesty and barn raising scaled up!

---
### SCENE 10.16: Twilight visits Fluttershy's POW camp
> **Synopsis:** Without Applejack around, Twilight spirals into self-doubt
> She feels she is not the princess of friendship anymore because she failed to make friends outside of Celestia's bubble
> She's back to being just the element of magic, making spells
> At least she's not doing research alone anymore, she has Fleur
> Seeing Applejack about to die made her start to hate the changelings, Starlight’s example also enables Twilight
>
> Twilight checks on Fluttershy who is making love donators and meets the changeling volunteers
> The whole place is decorated like a kindergarden sleepover, with soft and colorful silk blankets and pillows everywhere. The changelings are playing board and card games, since this is what they had in underground harmonist dens, using smuggled Equestrian foals' games. It is regression therapy. Since their education ended early and they became workers or conscripts, they need the simple games to learn basic social skills.
> They need Rules (The Game) to understand Interaction.
> They need Turns to understand Fairness.
> They need Winning/Losing (without punishment) to understand Safety.
>
> They make silk stuffed animals for the love donators which hides the draining crystals and the love tubes go through the stuffed animal's tail to the canisters
> They donate love and help other POWs with rehab
> They stitch ponies' cutie marks on their uniforms for morale and pride, eating fruits for different silk colors. They are good at making the cutie marks because shapeshifting requires mimicry
>
> It's so innocent and so very Fluttershy. It softens Twilight and restores her faith in friendship.
>
> Really, you can trust changelings to be CIA agents? Fluttershy is the ultimate authority, and changeling emotion sense is good for intepretting animals. They have a lot of reports to process which the buffalo are not enough for
>
> Chrysalis's regime runs on theft, extraction, and synthetic substitution. It is a closed-loop ponzi scheme of misery. Fluttershy provides genuine, freely donated Pink Love. It isn't refined in a roaring, brutalist centrifuge. It is given with consent and warmth. When the POW tastes Fluttershy's donated Pink Love, it physically and psychologically breaks Chrysalis's conditioning because it introduces a superior, positive-sum alternative. They do not have to be a monster, nor do they have to be a drugged slave, to feel the warmth of a community.

**Characters:**
* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]** (Impact: CoreValueChange)
> **Analysis:** She regains her empathy and the vision that all creatures can be friends!
>
> At the same time, she needs to roll her eyes at how Fluttershy has literally set up Magic Kindergarten. This is a distinct trait from season 1. But it shouldn't be out of malice, it should be teasing Fluttershy
> "Wow, Fluttershy, it's like I'm in Magic Kindergarten!"
> Fluttershy is oblivous and says "isn't it wonderful?", and Twilight rolls her eyes and smirks, which Tally Mark catches
> (Which means Tally Mark will only know the real version of Twilight, not the princess version! Same for Mali, who reads the letters. This should be great for the tank scenes later.)

* **[[Fleur Bloom]]**
> **Analysis:** Fleur doesn't want to go to the camp. She wants to keep working on the rifle. She is like the old, obesessive Twilight. Twilight insists that Fleur take a break and make some friends, which is shows her character development.

* **[[Fluttershy]]**
> **Analysis:** Some changeling POWs call Fluttershy the loving Queen they never had who has endless pink love to share. The POWs are basically being coddled by Fluttershy; she gave them pink love and jobs and kids' games, but they don't have self determination.
>
> Fluttershy has become Celestia.
>
> Remember that Fluttershy liked Our Town because everyone was nice. She ironically may prefer forced harmony over free will.

* **[[Starlight Glimmer]]**
> **Analysis:** Starlight recognizes changelings who are fake smiling and tries to comfort them
> Or Starlight helps changelings shed their view of Chrysalis the cult leader, the ones who are struggling. She is being the guidance counselor.


**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** Without Applejack around, Twilight spirals into self-doubt
> She feels she is not the princess of friendship anymore because she failed to make friends outside of Celestia's bubble
> She's back to being just the element of magic, making spells
> At least she's not doing research alone anymore, she has Fleur
> Seeing Applejack about to die made her start to hate the changelings, Starlight’s example also enables Twilight

* **[[Camp Fluttershy]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Twilight sees the full camp with layered medical protocols and prosperity in action. It is literal sunshine and rainbows!
> Self organization
>
> The tiered approach designed by Dr. Fauna with help from Fleur's colleagues (how would information cross the ocean at this tech level?)
> Hospital (loading bay/basement) is for medical detox. Inhibitor rings on for safety.
> Barracks is where they sleep, with silk hammocks and stuffed animals
> The workshops in the side halls is where cutie mark weaving, silk and toy making is done
> Classrooms for learning languages, math and science
> Sanctuary (greenhouses and atriums) where changelings take care of animals, where they can shapeshift into animals
> Sports areas like hoofball
> Game rooms for kids games and socializing
> Music rooms
> Library
> Cafeteria
>
> The whole place has glass ceilings since it's agricultural
> Specialized habitats for animals and changelings like the animal sanctuary. No hard angles, soft curves
>
> Somewhere else is the CIA HQ


**Themes:**
* **[[Bottom Up > Top Down]]**
> **Commentary:** Fluttershy is a Top Down enforcer. She uses The Stare to make the consequences of predation too great. It's like... Eros.


**Codex:**
* **[[Chrysalis's New Order]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Language dynamics unveiled
>
> Changelings striving to learn Equestrian. The ones stitching cutie marks are fluent.
> Changelings teaching other changelings the native language (if they only know simplified Herzlander) and also working on the writing system
> CIA agents speaking native buffalo or Equestrian
> Level 2 drones who were demoted to level 1 are teaching level 1 drones math and science and how to read
>
> One of the changelings says before Chrysalis, they called themselves Ama-Xolo meaning carapace creatures
> But the nursery folk tales say the ancient changelings called themselves Ama-Bumbano meaning united creatures
> The tongue was called Isi-Limi for "the tongue"
> They are deciding to call the language Isi-Bumbano for other langauges to use - Chrysalis weaponized simplied Herzlander for tyranny, they will "weaponize" their own language for harmony
> Umu-Vikeli are the protectors. Jaeger is Chrysalis's intentional translation to hunter
> U-Bubele means pink love (compassion/kindness)
> Am-Andla means red love (Power/force/ambition)
> Um-Hlobo for Pony, meaning friend/partner
> Umu-Sebenzi for maker/worker instead of drone
> Isi-Dleke means the nest/place of nuturing, what the hives are called
> Isi-Dleke Sasemanyano is what they will call the new nation - The united hive/ the nest of unity
>
> Conscripts freeze or rout when their officer is wiped by a precision strike because they don't have the language for abstract thought. They literally do not possess the internal vocabulary for "Auftragstaktik" (independent mission command). They only know how to execute orders.
>
> If a drone is flooded with extreme, synthetic oxytocin ("basking in the appreciation of the hive"), their brain is being told they are perfectly safe and performing optimally.
>
> When a Wonderbolt precision strike wipes out the officer, the conscript’s external directive vanishes. They are standing in a slaughterhouse, their eyes seeing blood and artillery, but their "Earmuffed" Emotion Sense is screaming “Everything is great! We love you!” This severe cognitive dissonance causes a total psychological system crash. They freeze because they literally cannot reconcile their visual reality with their chemical reality, and they lack the linguistic tools to formulate a backup plan.
>
> The tragedy is once the Equestrians adopt precision strikes, all the conscripts are given max dosage suicide mixes

* **[[Changeling Emotion Sense]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Fluttershy realizes that this sense allows them to understand displaced and distressed animals, turning a predatory tool into a mechanism for teaching empathy.

* **[[Love Donator]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** They come up with the plan for redesigned love donators which will be needed for the Luna Nova Rifles

* **[[Combat Drugs]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** The rehab pipeline revealed (advised by Fleur Bloom and Dr. Fauna):
> Stage 1: concrete loading bay. The conscripts are screaming in pain (Panzer-haut withdrawl), thrashing (blitz-essenz aggression), and can't handle the emotions (jaeger-geist withdrawl). They are given a strong dose of donated pink love to stabilize and provide calories. They will crash into a coma.
> Stage 2: Hibernation. They sleep for 5-7 days straight, only taking water and pink love and passing out. They rest in comfy silk cocoons made by the other changelings. They are isolated for quietness so the emotion sense is not overwhelmed. They get weighted blankets and stuffed animals, which are laced with pink love.
> Stage 3: Anhedonia. The inhibitor ring comes off and they go to the nursery with the general population. They need a handler to watch over them and play simple games with. They need donated friendship to wean off the panzer-haut.
> Stage 4: The Spark, they are able to learn things again. The brain has to relearn to make its own dopamine. They get animal companions and play board games.


---
### SCENE 10.17: Spy Network Intel
> **Synopsis:** Combination of intel from animals and POWs lets Fluttershy understand Pagala
>
> Fluttershy gives an update from her talks with the prisoners saying morale is low and most of the changelings are conscripts who are not pony predators since most of the hardcore changelings are in the Northern advance and these are reserves, but arrogant changelings commanders keep sending reserves out to threaten us in random surprise attacks and the changelings are scared
>
> They are led by General Actia Pagala
> She took over Acornage and incited terror on the civilians, which was unproductive, and her encouragement of feasting on civilians was making soldiers break rank, and also made the ponies hostile and required more garrison troops, so Trimmel removed her from the advancing front and put her in the stationary position across Tall Tale
> Later prisoners say Pagala’s orders were to not be captured
> Pagala justified the terror on Acornage from the city expelling the changelings after the Canterlot wedding
>
> They get crucial details about the state of Equestria behind the front lines:
> When the changelings occupy rural villages, they round up everypony, drain their love, and ship them to Vanhoover for forced labor in factories for the war machine, then drain them again repeatedly too.
>
> They also find out more collaborators besides Rockfeller in Las Pegasus, Gilded Trust is taking over their production for the military and working with them
> He has mercenaries and loyal workers locking down treasonous factories and securing contraband in warehouses for the war effort
> He has explosive rhetoric against Chrysalis, Skyfall, and the other tycoons and the workers flock to him, hoping to work hard and become him. They get war bonds and patriotism.
>
> Some tycoons do escape. Gilded Trust calls them out as traitors and takes their companies
> The goal isn't justice, it's about seizing the factories without sabotage. Actually by being public about coming for the traitors, he makes them flee before they can sabotage their factories, which Chrysalis might demand that they do if they want safe passage. So some get torpedoed
> Gilded Trust is "The Liberator of Las Pegasus." and a Warlord of Industry. This validates his post-war political power. He isn't just a rich pony; he is a war hero with a private army.
>
> Gilded Trust says in speeches that "I alone will sniff out all the collaborators" and that's a rumor among the workers that lead to loyalty and fear
> Perfectly satirizes the "self-made man" archetype. He takes credit for the collective effort of nature and the resistance, packaging it as his own individual brilliance.

**Characters:**
* **[[Actia Pagala]]**
> **Analysis:** She is revealed to be the cruel changeling general who keeps ordering random assaults across the river to catch Tall Tale off guard (acts like an AI constantly attacking with infantry)

* **[[Gilded Trust]]**
> **Analysis:** He is being a helpful figure


**Threads:**
* **[[Changeling Tall Tale Command]]**
> **Update:** The reason Fluttershy has more prisoners to talk to in the first place is because Pagala is in charge of the troops holding the tall tale front on the changeling side. She is cruel and forces the green infantry to launch futile infantry assaults to try and catch the ponies off guard. Like in Verdun in WW1.

* **[[Cute Intelligence Agency]]**
* **[[Camp Fluttershy]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** The random infantry charges ordered by Pagala lead to changeling POW captures who are in way worse state than first cohort from the 2nd Battle of Tall Tale (Trimmel). Fluttershy needs help from Fleur to understand the medical withdrawl.
>
> By treating the POWs as patients rather than prisoners, Fluttershy breaks the "Conscript Logic". A conscript fights because they fear their commander more tahn the enemy. If the enemy offers love (Pink Love) and the commander offers death (Red Love/Suicide missions), the logical choice—and the biological choice for a love-starved Changeling—is to defect.
>
> Framing Red Love as an opioid/stimulant and Pink Love as methadone/rehab is a sophisticated way to handle the "Love" magic system. It moves the conflict from "Good vs. Evil" to "Sickness vs. Health."


**Codex:**
* **[[Love Harvester]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Mass usage in Vanhoover

* **[[Chrysalis's Economic Scheme]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Parallel to the 4th crusade in 1204 AD: The Crusaders needed ships to get to the Holy Land. Venice built them the ships but charged a massive fee the Crusaders couldn't pay. So, the Doge of Venice (Enrico Dandolo) said, "If you can't pay in gold, pay in conquest." He directed the Crusader army to sack the Christian city of Zara, and then Constantinople itself, to pay off their debt to Venice.
>
> The Equestrian tycoons took massive loans (ships) from Chrysalis to build their factories. When the war starts, they can't pay the debt. Chrysalis says, "You can't pay? Fine. Pay me by sabotaging Applejack's war effort."
>
> The Twist: The tycoons are not ideologically aligned with Chrysalis; they are just debtors who are forced to become traitors to avoid bankruptcy. This makes them tragic or pathetic villains rather than mustache-twirling evil ones.
>
> They formed a "Comittee of Public Safety" planning to switch sides once Tall Tale fell and the tanks started moving toward them
> They don't see themselves as traitors; they see the Queen (or Applejack/Celestia) as "bad for business." They invite Chrysalis in not as a conqueror, but as a "protector of free trade."
> They look at Olenia as the example


---
### SCENE 10.18: Tally Mark explains her cutie mark
> **Synopsis:** Before this plot point, it should be Twilight training Tally Stock on combat magic with the Crystal Enhancer. Tally gets a new mage uniform and says she wants to get her cutie mark stitched, so that's why Twilight tags along. Twilight was leaning into framing the changelings as animals during the training due to bitterness.
>
> Tally is getting her cutie mark stitched to her uniform and explains her cutie mark, a hazelwood tally that is split and lined up perfectly, to the changeling
>
> Before the machines, she ran the produce silos and export. She was great at counting objects with her magic. During big havests, if the bank didn't have enough bits (Celestia doesn't like bank accounts), she would make wooden tallies where the grains have to match to prove that no one tampered with the amount owed. The bigger part is called the stock and goes to farmer, making them a stockholder of the silo.
>
> Once the industrialists came in, they had weight machines that were rigged to skim profit. She got fired for pointing it out and trying to investigate, and they said her wooden tallies were useless now. Star Energy brought transparency and prosperity to Tall Tale. That is why she fights.
>
> She ends by making a tally for the changeling and giving them the stock! As payment for the silk cutie mark.

**Characters:**
* **[[Tally Mark]]**
> **Analysis:** Backstory revealed!


**Threads:**
* **[[Harmonic Capitalism]]**
> **Update:** Use industry to empower the honest agrarian system


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** Tally Stock is a unicorn who embodies the earth pony way of hard work, honest work, by making these authentic wooden tallies that can't be forged

* **[[Accelerants used for Evil can be Repurposed for Good]]**
> **Commentary:** The ruthless industrialists used machines to cheat. Comet Shine encourages transparency. His fair scale allowed Tally Stock to work more efficiently.


**Codex:**
* **[[Banking]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Her tally sticks are how the medieval economy worked without enough bits
> It also is a symptom of the bit drain to Aquileia and Skyfall
>
> Tally proves that as long as the work exists and the trust exists, you don't need the gold

* **[[Chrysalis's Economic Scheme]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Tally Mark mentions no gold bits so they needed tally sticks. Should this come before or after the reveal?


---
### SCENE 10.19: History of Tall Tale
> **Synopsis:** Twilight and Fleur look at the museum exhibit about the history of Tall Tale
> The museum is attached to the expo center, and changelings are practicing reading comprehension there
> It looks like a children's museum. Fleur scoffs at it, feeling like a child
> The history of Tall Tale plaques are written at a fifth grade level, but they are insightful
>
> The area has always been home to mostly earth ponies who make huge harvests
> The village was originally known as "Tally Valley" because it's where all the farmers brought their huge crops to. Since there was so much economic activity and not enough bits, they used tally sticks to trade
> Out of town ponies started calling Talley Valley a tall tale because the rumors of massive harvests (like pumpkins the size of a carriage, stuff like that) sounded like tall tales, lies or exagerations
>
> When cartographers made an official map of Equestria at some point (around 500 ALB or something like that) Tally Valley became the region, but the ponies decided to call the specific village where they held the harvest festivals "Tall Tale" as a smug reclaimation of the rumors. They embraced the fact that their farming ambitions were so great (compared to the rest of Equestria) that outsiders thought they were exagerations
>
> Fleur realizes that Tall Tale already practiced harmonic capitalism via tally sticks (pride-based artisanry and ambition) even before the industry came
>
> Tall Tale is like Silicon Valley. The air zone is even called Coltfoalnia
>
> Twilight writes a letter about how sometimes we are more similar and advanced than we thought

**Characters:**
* **[[Fleur Bloom]]**
> **Analysis:** She is humbled by the fact that the Equestrians of Tall Tale already had a knack for "The Aquileian Way" before Aquileian influences. Even through things as innocent as tally sticks and harvest festivals. This puts a dent in her cultural superiority worldview


---
## CHAPTER 11: Tempest
> **Chapter Note:** Will Mali explain the many tribes first? Do they get ambushed? How does she win over her mom?
> **Chapter Note:** The primary conflict is Cultural Transition. The technology (rubber) is the catalyst, but the story is about the people (Mali, Tally, AJ) choosing "Adult Agency" over "Childlike Stagnation," and the friction that choice creates with the people they leave behind.

### SCENE 11.1: Mali explains how there are many Tzinacatl tribes who bicker
> **Synopsis:** Nightmare Moon united the tribes through Centralized Authority (and shared "Red Love" ambition). When the "Great Man" (or Mare) disappeared, the unity evaporated. There was no structural reason for the tribes to work together, only a person they followed. The Tzinacatl broke up into infighting.
>
> Mali left home to join the night guard because she wanted a harmonic way to solve the tribal infighting. Unfortunately she didn't find much luck in Celestia's court.
>
> They've been killing changeling infiltrators and have a concoction to undo disguises. The changelings tried to sabotage various tribes 20 years ago so they rapidly developed defenses

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** Applejack's naivety shattered. She thought thestrals were already enlightened allies or traditionalists who just needed to be treated right. She was blind to the fact that there are thestrals who are ruthless and "evil" and profiteers
> It also shows that thestrals are not all the same.

* **[[Princess Celestia]]**
> **Analysis:** By allowing them to infight, she proved she didn't view them as "Ponies" worthy of Harmony. She viewed them as "Creatures" to be contained.


**Threads:**
* **[[World Peace / Overcoming The Predator's Dilemma]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Applejack interprets the warring tribes similar to the Hearth's Warming Eve story. But that was prehistoric times.


**Themes:**
* **[[Isolationism is Immoral]]**

**Codex:**
* **[[Tzinacatl]]** (Organization)

---
### SCENE 11.2: Mali navigates the rapids to get to another tribe

**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** While on the raft, Applejack is writing a letter to Twilight. Applejack misses Twilight and is worrying about her even though Twilight isn't in a combat zone and can literally teleport out of danger. Rainbow Dash teases AJ and Mali thinks it's so adorable.


---
### SCENE 11.3: Meeting Metzli's Tribe
> **Synopsis:** Mali convinces her mom
>
> Her tribe is inland and the most traditional. They don't participate in the drug trade. They are the most pacifist and "harmonic", though isolationist. Like the Temberik.
>
> Meztli's Perspective:
> •She views Mali not as a hero, but as a Fool.
> •Metlzli: "You went to them. You changed your name. You wore their armor. And for what? They still look at you with fear. You are 'Tempest' to them—a storm to be weathered, not a person to be loved."
> •The "Letter" Subversion: Instead of a death letter, maybe Metlzli received letters from Mali signed "Tempest Wind" and burned them. She refused to acknowledge a daughter who erased herself.
>
> Meztli does not like Equestrians, or anypony different than themselves. She doesn't like the Aquileian imports of the other tribes and thinks the drug dealers are immoral. She is the thestral version of Celestia.
>
> Mali's Perspective:
> •She admits the "Tempest" persona was a mask, but the Service was real.
> •Mali: "I wore the mask so I could stand on the wall. And because I stood there, I met Applejack. She doesn't call me Tempest. She asks me how to say 'Friend' in Nahuatl."
>
> The Council (Mali vs. Metlzli)
> •This is the debate on Assimilation.
> •Metlzli: "Why should we help the ponies? They wanted to turn us into them."
> •Mali: "Because the Changelings don't want to turn us into ponies. They want to turn us into dust. Celestia ignored us; Chrysalis will hunt us. We fight with the Republic, or we die alone."
>
> When Metlzli saks Mali why are you fighting for a sun princess who ignored us for 1000 years and a moon princess who betrayed us?
> Mali says she isn't fighting for the princesses, she is fighting for Applejack, and Applejack is fighting for her
>
> The Argument: Metlzli wants to keep the tribe isolated to protect them (just like Celestia).
> •Mali's Counter: "I didn't survive because of Celestia. I survived because of an Earth Pony farmer who stood next to me in a trench. I am fighting for her."
> •The Result: The Tzinacatl don't join for Equestria; they join for Malinalli.
>
> Token reforms, separate barracks and quarters and separate neighborhood
> Why sleep in a cold barracks alone?
> Mali mentions to her mom how Tally Mark invited Mali to stay at her home, not a cramped barracks
> Celestia may have convinced Luna to do only token reforms, but the ponies of Tall Tale connected with us
>
> Meztli calls Mali a sell out, who changed her name, a traitor who "fraternizes with ponies" (another hint) and spitting on tradition/respect for the tribe/family
> She does not like Equestria, and she does not like the other tribes who work with Aquileia even more, viewing them as more corrupting
> Mali must mend the divisions between tribes
>
> Mali's Mom: Resents the cultural erosion. "You are making us into pets for the ponies."
>
> Defense of the tribe in their petty wars is the highest imperative. Being friends with other tribes is betrayal, so is leaving to get fat in Equestria as second-class citizens/pets
>
> Applejack's perception is that Metzli is just paralyzed by fear (like Celestia?) but soon will learn the horrifying truth when she meets the fate of the left behind drug tribes
>
> Metzli can't argue with her daughter's logic, but her agreement to take capital/war bonds and contribute to the war effort should be contingent on all tribes doing so. She thinks the cartels will never cooperate, and therefore she can sit in place.

**Characters:**
* **[[Mali]]**
> **Analysis:** •Mali is in a state of inner turmoil (a tempest). She loves the idea of Harmony (Luna's promise), but hates the reality of Assimilation (Celestia's rule).
> •Returning home brings this storm to a head. She is walking back into the place she abandoned.
>
> She explains her cutie mark about grassroots connection. Instead of staying stagnant, she aspired to be Aquileian. (That is the arc Applejack is now pursuing)
>
> Mali fights because she knows what "Stagnation" looks like (her mom’s tribe) and what "Liberty" looks like (Manehattan/Tall Tale). She is fighting for the right to be an adult.
>
> That's why she thinks Equestrians and thestrals are not different at all
>
> Mali negotiates with her isolationist mother, Meztli. Mali uses her Parloir de-escalation skills, but she applies a fraction of Fluttershy's "Tough Love." She refuses to back down or apologize for leaving the jungle. She holds the boundary, securing the initial alliance, but it is still mostly diplomatic.


**Themes:**
* **[[P&K Subversion]]**
> **Commentary:** Metzli should explicitly mention a dream where she received a letter of condolence for Mali's death after the fall of Vanhoover. Celestia mispelled Mali's name, or it said "Tempest Wind", which fuels the resentment. Also it's obvious Celestia didn't write it herself.
>
> So Mali was supposed to be a Moonspeaker and instead chose to leave and be a parloir worker in Aquileian luxuries


**Codex:**
* **[[Luna's Banishment]]** (History)
* **[[Tzinacatl]]** (Organization)
> **Note:** Meztli is a contemporary of Granny Smith. She was a young adult during the 914–930 ALB crisis. She personally remembers the Golden Age of trade, and she remembers the exact day Celestia’s embargo destroyed her tribe's livelihood.
>
> Metzli rails about the evils of industry and how it desecrated the jungle and their way of life, also brought death (foreshadowing), she is just like Celestia!
> The artisan tribes say their crossbows never killed anypony, but Metzli says it led to the other tribes picking up guns

* **[[Parloirs/Pre-War Manehattan]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Mali tells her mom what her work has been
> She isn't just "Tempest Wind" an assimilated 2nd-class citizen
> She chose the name herself as a therapist at a parloir
> She signed up to be a lunar guard to meet Luna
> She realized Luna needed help and provided the ear for Luna to process her trauma
> She and the parloir owners provided therapy for the crystal ponies for Cadance and Shining Armor under Celestia's nose
>
> Mother (Isolationist): "If we go to the cities, we become servants or slaves. The Day Ponies will eat us."
>
> Mali (Integrationist): "If we stay here, we starve. The Day Ponies are hungry for what we have."
> Mali manages her mother’s anger the same way she managed difficult clients in the Parloir: De-escalation, active listening, and reframing the narrative. She treats her mother’s xenophobia as "anxiety" that needs to be soothed.

### SCENE 11.4: Applejack sends a letter about partial success with Metzli's Tribe
> **Synopsis:** Dear Princess Twilight Sparkle,
> I'm writing to you today because I learned an important lesson about friendship. [assimilation is bad, integration is good]
> Still need to get a deal by talking to the other tribes, but things are looking good
> rafts are great, echolocation is awesome, Mali is awesome
> I learned that there's so much more to a friend once they bring you to their home
> Love, Applejack
>
> Applejack sends a letter about Mali being such a good connector. Twilight gets jealous that Mali is a better Princess of Friendship

### SCENE 11.5: Twilight makes the spellfire spell matrix
> **Synopsis:** It works with a crystal enhancer as the power source
> Now they need to integrate it with the magic from a canister
> Hans Kessel starts working on the stabilizer for the output of the three way valve
> Before, it just sloshes into the canister. A stable output to the spell matrix is a novel requirement (like constant voltage to a CPU)
> The three way valve is used by Star Energy for next gen tractor fuel injector prototypes, which are powered by oil and crystals

**Threads:**
* **[[Developing the Luna Nova Rifle]]**

---
### SCENE 11.6: Tally Mark meets Pinkie and brings her to the Camp Fluttershy kitchen
> **Synopsis:** Pinkie arrives in Tall Tale with Starlight, Twilight and Fluttershy hug her
> They show Pinkie Camp Fluttershy with all the decorations, but Pinkie is skeptical
> They have Tally Mark help Pinkie bake
> They want Pinkie to work with an Equestrian who knows she's the party pony but doesn't expect it. She's high energy like the old (canon) Pinkie Pie. She gets Pinkie Pie to smile even though they just met!
>
> There are a bunch of bakers working on making treats infused with pink love for the changelings
> Gustav Le Grand is part of the Aquileian chefs
> Tzinacatl medics are studying the rehab responses
> They want Pinkie to bake as many different variations as she can think of
> This restores her asset specificity

**Characters:**
* **[[Pinkie Pie]]** (Impact: CoreValueLearned)
> **Analysis:** Pinkie sees the first example of having a cheerful spirit alongside facing the danger. They are not mutually exclusive.
> She's able to smile with a stranger. Tally doesn't need Pinkie to be the party pony because Tally is already exuberant.
>
> Instead of laugh to make it disappear it's laugh while you make it disappear
>
> Giggle at the Ghostly is not inherently toxic positivity, the flaw is a category mismatch (psychological internal fear versus kinetic bullets)

* **[[Tally Mark]]** (Impact: CoreValueDemonstration)
> **Analysis:** She demonstrates morale in the face of danger


**Themes:**
* **[[P&K Subversion]]**
> **Commentary:** Pinkie learns to be resilient


**Codex:**
* **[[Tzinacatl Magic/Alchemy/Biology]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** The difference between Tzinacatl painkillers from friendship plants and Chrysalis's synthetic, tyrannical panzer-haut is that the friendship plants naturally slow the intake of the endorphins, keeping it non-addictive (like methadone). Panzer-haut floods the brain with the painkiller (like fentanyl) creating a dependency

* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Flavorless rations now get flavor through pink love infusion, also a morale boost

* **[[Color]]** (MagicSystem)

---
### SCENE 11.7: Fleur Bloom watches Pinkie bake morale cakes
> **Synopsis:** They want to integrate the pink love into canned rations
>
> Wait, mon amie, you forgot the milk!
> They stare at Fleur like she's crazy. Milk, as in milk for foals?
> Fleur is flabbergasted by the fact that Pinkie doesn't use milk, she has three different ingredients from earth ponies that simulate milk
> How do you make it X? This thing! And Y? That's this!
> Fleur is pacified when she tastes Pinkie's cake
>
> Tally says the Tall Tale museum had an exhibit about how ponies used to eat "oat cakes" (sweetened, heavy bricks of grain and fruit) until earth ponies, unicorns and pegasi coming together to create cakes which were "impossible delicacies from a a far away land" until harmony created them in Equestria
> Fleur snorts and says the original cakes must have been Aquileian and the butter and cream come from cows
> But isn't the milk supposed to be for the calf?
> Yeah, but we treat them well! Not like the Herzlanders who put them in cages
> And what do you do with the male cows?
> ... we eat them.
> What are you, a manticore?!
>
> Twilight and Fleur go to the museum exihibit, the museum is attached to the expo center which is now Camp Fluttershy
> Fleur reads the descriptions and pieces the chemistry together, since she knows how earth pony magic and chemistry work
>
> How it works:
> Silk Wheat replaces Casein (milk protein). Earth ponies flooding the roots with nitrogen creates a gluten structure so strong it behaves like a dairy curd. Pegasi create a localized drought bubble over wheat fields just before harvest to force the grain to harden and concentrate the protein. (false drought)
> Churn Nuts replace butter fat. Earth ponies flooding the nut trees with phosphorus creates excess ATP that the trees put into their nuts/seeds as saturated fat. Tropical humidity via high pressure system over the nut orchards to get high fat nuts
> Amber Sap replaces Lactose (browning/moisture). Potassium regulates water and sugar transport in plants. By flooding the soil with potassium, the ponies force the Amber Maple trees to pump a sap that is supersaturated with sugars and amino acids, which causes the maillard reaction and golden crust. Pegasi drop the temperature below freezing every single night in the sap orchards to expand and contract the wood, allowing thick sap to flow (freeze thaw)
>
> They start working on adding pink love to the canning process at the factory
> Tall Mark uses a crystal enhancer to move materials around. Her specialty is counting materials/logistical organization, and the crystal enhancer makes her as capable as Twilight

**Codex:**
* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Making the ingredients for a cake is a laborious exercise to force friendship on purpose


---
### SCENE 11.8: Mage Meadowbrook explains the history of the magical plants
> **Synopsis:** Meadowbrook effectively turned the pacifist bat ponies into living, sociological water-sprinklers for medicinal botany. They have spent the last millennium refining her alchemical methodology into the organic painkillers used in present day.
>
> The pink love that Fluttershy is working with can be used for healing too

**Codex:**
* **[[Tzinacatl Magic/Alchemy/Biology]]** (Technology)
* **[[Ancient Equestria (Before Luna Banishment)]]** (History)

---
### SCENE 11.9: Crystal City Encircled
> **Synopsis:** Shining Armor's crystal army was not encircled and obliterated like the armies of Vanhoover and Acornage
> The crystal ponies had more resolve and morale than the Equestrians, because they dealt with Sombra
> Shining Armor was able to stage organized retreats across all of the crystal empire, and all crystal ponies evacuated to Crystal City ahead of the army
> They inflict a lot of casualties against the changelings and never fall to a breakthrough/encirclement, but in order to reduce their own casualties they are always losing ground (no last stand orders like the Soviets)
>
> The Crystal City is encircled behind the shield
>
> Twilight learns that Shining Armor and Flurry Heart and Cadance are all trapped, the shield is faltering

**Threads:**
* **[[The War in the North]]**
> **Update:** The main characters know how the Crystal Heart works. It's not a mystery how the Crystal Empire is surviving. Cadance is not holding up the shield alone; all the crystal ponies contribute their solidarity to the city-wide shield. The tension and dread stems from whether the Crystal Empire can hold long enough for Equestria to turn the war around.
>
> Celestia urged Cadance and Shining Armor to evacuate but they refused. When Celestia learns that the Crystal City is surviving, she cannot claim it as a victory for the Stagnation of Harmony. She must confront the fact that her own niece operated a shadow government, importing foreign technology to build a war machine right under her nose. It isolates Celestia further, proving that the younger generation outgrew the Nursery long before the war even started.


---
### SCENE 11.10: Stalliongrad Enters the War
> **Synopsis:** As the front advances to the east, Stalliongrad enters the war and Blueblood cedes administrative control of Northeast Equestria to Stalliongrad so they have control of supply lines
> Changeling Heer launches full assault on Stalliongrad and pushes them back into Severyana proper where the front stalls, there’s back and forth and guerilla warfare
>
> Changeling side of the Tall River thins out as troops go to face Stalliongrad but Henri says there's no point advancing without being ready
> The local ponies who want to retake Vanhoover get agitated
>
> Reports from Stalliongrad indicate an absolute massacre on both sides

**Threads:**

### SCENE 11.11: Vacuum Grade Love Canisters

**Threads:**
* **[[Developing the Luna Nova Rifle]]**

* **[[Love Canister]]** (Technology)
