---
### SCENE 11.12: Chrysalis is a self-hating colonizer
> **Synopsis:** Chrysalis erases the native changeling culture as barbaric, replacing it with efficient simplified Herzlander
>
> This parallels with how Celestia wanted the bat ponies to assimilate into the stagnation of harmony, like making Mali change her name to Tempest Wind
> It also parallels the Skyfall (Chrysalis) investors attempting to farm rubber with a factory and the Tzinacatl tribes revolting and destroying the factory in response.
> The message to Applejack is to go for harmonic integration, not assimilation or erasure

---
### SCENE 11.13: Applejack and Star Energy meet the second isolationist tribe
> **Synopsis:** The great twist is that the Tzinacatl are NOT isolationist. They are connected to the global drug economy - the tribal squabbles are on paper about theology, but in reality are about control of the waterways and flight/walking corridors between them for exporting drugs as well as stealing drug stock from each other to sell. But ever since Chrysalis entered the scene, she flooded the market with red love via her love extractors. So the Tzinacatl are anti-industrialization for making their special drug (medical on paper but in reality for escapism) obsolete. But they are united against Chrysalis.
>
> Mali makes the case that the Tzinacatl need to move beyond their one lucrative drug trade and diversify. Work with Equestria for survival. Industrialize and provide the rubber for planes/trucks and the rubber balsa rafts for supply lines. In exchange, Equestria and Aquileia will share their products, industrial, food, and magic. But the Tzinacatl must make peace internally otherwise the whole system fails.
>
> Thematic Emphasis: Acceptance vs. Assimilation
>
> To make the title "Tempest" hit hard, you need to highlight Celestia's "Polite Racism."
> •The Old Way (Celestia): "You can be one of us, provided you act, look, and sound like us." (Assimilation). ◦Evidence: Changing names. Hiding the fangs. Wearing the Solar/Lunar armor that covers their unique features.
>
> •The New Way (Applejack/Republic): "You are one of us because you are different." (Integration). ◦Evidence: Applejack needs them to be Tzinacatl. She needs their echolocation and their balsa rafts. If Mali were just a "Pony," she couldn't solve the logistics problem.
> ◦The realization: The Republic succeeds because it utilizes the differences Celestia tried to hide.

**Threads:**
* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
> **Update:** Chrysalis attempts to ally with the Tzinacatl and give them industrial goods in exchange for rubber, but the Tzinacatl refuse since she is disrespecting their traditions
>
> Actually one or some of the tribes tried to agree, got a little taste, and were punished by the other tribes. These are the ones whose members leave the jungle to work night shifts in the cities (also funded by Chrysalis)
>
> Chrysalis sends level 1 VOPS to infiltrate the Tzinacatl and take ruin them for defying her, but they rapidly develop an alchemical mix to undo the changeling disguise spell
> But through it all, the tribes are still disunited 
> Because their economy is based on export and stealing from each other

* **[[Harmonic Capitalism]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** It's a system that will unite the tribes everlasting, not just a strongmare like Nightmare Moon. It's because the system gives all the tribes a mutual incentive to cooperate, since the source of their wealth is no longer a zero sum game. Before, they fought each other to export drugs. Now they must increase rubber and raft outputs to trade for other goods.
>
> This proves the model works in the most extreme environment possible (a fragmented, hostile jungle).
> •Standardization (Griffon Rep): Would try to build a paved road through the jungle and force the Bat Ponies to drive trucks. (Would fail due to terrain/culture clash).
> •Harmonic Capitalism (Aquileia/AJ): Uses the local method (Rafts/Alchemy) to feed the global need (Rubber for tanks).
>
> It is the ultimate validation: You don't need a Goddess to make people get along. You need a Good Contract.


**Themes:**
* **[[Stagnation can evolve into Harmonic Republicanism]]**
> **Commentary:** The Tzinacatl stuck to their old ways, and were pushed out of their market by Chrysalis. Now they are changing for the better.


---
### SCENE 11.14: Applejack sends a letter about a breakthrough in the negotiations
> **Synopsis:** Also Tzinacatl are willing to unite against Chrysalis because the submarines attacked their vessels so they lost their black market drug trade too (rubber vessels, that's how they get to Griffonia)
> And this is why all supply lines must be over land
>
> In the reply, Twilight mentions Fleur reading it and really liking how the Tzinacatl are embracing Aquileian Harmonic Capitalism

**Threads:**
* **[[Harmonic Capitalism]]**
> **Update:** Star Energy breaks through because they ask the tribes to contribute rubber using their traditional methods, not by burning down the jungle and making organized rubber plantations which are abominations (that's what Chrysalis got the tribe to do, and why the other tribes briefly united to cut down the industrial tribe for desecrating the jungle) 
> Star energy provides tools and machinery and crystals to help but tell the Tzinacatl to figure out how to utilize the industry in a way that works for you and your unique alchemy and methods for your geography. Industry is an accelerant of special talents!
>
> Tzinacatl have both unique special talents and terroir, they just needed harmonic capitalists to trade with to acquire capital to improve their traditional methods and have a market to sell to. This is the FJA's post war dream!
>
> Harmonic capitalism is not do it our way, it's "what do you do best?"


**Themes:**
* **[[Embracing and Complementing Special Talents]]**
> **Commentary:** Thesis: standardization and efficiency are dead in the modern western world because of globalization for cheap labor and automation. So we need to specialize. Use our current amazing tech to enhance our special talents and pursue what we want to do.
>
> You can't just work hard at a factory anymore. A 2nd fdr style new deal would be obsolete. Getting a standard 4 year degree in a good field may not even be enough anymore. You have to really embrace what you want to do. Replace rugged individualism with harmonic capitalism.


---
### SCENE 11.15: Reflecting on the Crystal Tax for Ain Trotgourait
> **Synopsis:** Fleur admits she was annoyed at Twilight because the tax was inefficient and ineffective
>
> From Fleur’s Aquileian perspective, Twilight is a naive aristocrat playing with global supply chains. Twilight’s tax actively chokes the flow of high-grade crystals that Aquileia’s foundries desperately need. When the Changeling submarines easily collapse Twilight's system, Fleur's Aquileian superiority complex feels completely earned. It proves that idealism without a robust, defensible logistical base is useless.
>
> This leads to Twilight sending a letter about supply chains, which is a bridge to Applejack needing to secure the rubber supply chain

**Characters:**
* **[[Fleur Bloom]]**
> **Analysis:** Fleur's mildly condescending Aquileian superiority complex is due to the observed failure of Twilight's top down naive charity mentality


**Codex:**
* **[[Rebuilding Ain Trotgourait]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Twilight returns from Ain Trotgourait after burning out in 1008 from casting too many healing and water filtration spells personally, she spends a year designing her Magical Supply Organization system (a supply teleportation network) and other spell matrices. To get the highest grade crystals to run these in Ain Trotgourait for the suffering zebras, Twilight convinces Cadance to collect a percentage tax on crystals from crystal mines that go towards the charity work, justified by the fact that Ain Trotgourait is literally the most devastated place in the world due to the Storm King's horde camping there for months preparing for the invasion of Mount Aris. 
>
> Twilight realizes that personal martyrdom (casting water spells until she burns out) is unsustainable, so she scales up to a "systems" approach. However, she still thinks like an Equestrian: she believes she can just tax the top to fund a charity without considering the downstream industrial consequences.


---
## CHAPTER 12: Crash
> **Chapter Note:** Drug high crash, market crash, "crash program" of rapid R&D for the rifle, "crash course" in combat magic, plane crash, Applejack and Twilight crashing into each other during reunion!

### SCENE 12.1: Tzinacatl drug trade revealed
> **Synopsis:** The squabbles about the nature of Luna and Nightmare Moon are only the tip of the iceberg. The underlying conflict is economic
>
> Mali explains how the tribes they met, they historically exported medicine (and contraceptives) to all of Griffonia
> The other tribes export drugs
> Aquileia, specifically a royal advisor pony named Coltbert, connected with the medicine tribes, invested, and recruited workers for Aquileian parloirs in Manehattan which is the night life, social clubs (she leaves out the sexual liberty part)
>
> During ambush, they only decapitate each other with darts. There's no killing in their traditions, only raid and pillage for stock
> You take captured warriors and exchange for tribute. Bloodless but humiliating
> They have an extreme warrior culture
> It is literally the pre-industrial changeling hives!
> Originally they used blowguns and wind-propelled darts using their wings, also echolocation
> Aquileia gave them crossbows to protect the shipments to Aquileia/Manehattan. Not guns, respecting local traditions. But it cracked the game. It removed the glory of battle for the other side, while the crossbow wielders laughed and were arrogant
> They have rules of war developed over 1000 years because if a tribe got too ruthless and started killing, the trauma would incentize other tribes to unite and punish. So they stagnated out of mutual de-escalation. They spend a lot of their lives training to fight each other instead of developing their society
>
> Mali has to explain that most of the tribes are connected to the global economy black market because griffons (and rich ponies) love their traditional drugs. All the assumptions about the Tzinacatl completely fall apart.
>
> Ironically, this means the Tzinacatl are not blind to the world, and actually makes them more receptive to harmonic capitalism than the totally isolationist Temberik.
>
> Drug tribes sold to Chrysalis extensively from 979 to 986
> Chrysalis started selling synthetic red love as a drug to fund the great leap forward, copying their drug properties, crashing the Tzinacatl export economy for the drug tribes
>
> The medicinal tribes did not step in to help. Tribe divisions persisted, and the medicinal tribes laughed at the drug tribes
> Mali's tribe and other traditionalists also felt vindicated at the drug tribes' fate
>
> It's marked as "the love of the decadent changeling nobles who abused their drones and were conquered"
> The Tzinacatl are pissed that Chrysalis is disrupting their "sacred" market and cheapening their craft. Since Chrysalis's stuff is ultra refined and engineered to be maximally addictive, while Tzinacatl drugs have complex processing to make it give a high without destroying the body (too fast) and each tribe has different "flavors" or "kicks"
>
> Many Tzinacatl ended up just using the drugs for themselves, leading to stagnation
>
> Others wanted to pursue industry
>
> Then Skyfall entered the picture. Tycoons set up subsidiaries along the coast and provided housing
> The drug tribes worked there, and some of the traditionalists left home too, tired of stagnation
> It was a unifying force for industrious bat ponies of all tribes because the rugged individualism was an alternative to the stagnant communal tribal life
> But then one company, the biggets one (actually Chrysalis) started hiring thestrals to cut down the jungle for raw materials and mass rubber, and armed them with guns and killed thestrals and tried to take over more of the jungle to burn down
>
> The tribes united, burning the rubber and logging complex down for desecrating the jungle, but then continued on and destroyed all factories
> The exodus of industrious bat ponies from all tribes to the Equestrian cities to work at Skyfall-affiliated new factories (to work for Chrysalis's puppets)
> They formed a union, and the parloirs took care of them. That's the origin of EEEE!
>
> Then Chrysalis tightened supply, and the price spiked as all the addicts needed their fix
> Tzinacatl raiding wars started all over again to fill the market, except now all the artisan pride was gone, and some addicts wouldn't even take it since it wasn't enough
> So that's why Rockfeller had stockpiles, Comet Shine realized. He was holding out to sell to Griffonia post occupation, under Chrysalis's nose. He truly flew too close to sun.
> Comet Shine realizes how Chrysalis raked in the Skyfall Marks from her addiction and constriction scheme, and it's terrifying. The collatoral was the entire Tzinacatl economy.
>
> There was a second market flood of red love in 1008 after the occupation of Olenia
> Some drug tribes refused to be beaten, and they bought and stockpiled Chrysalis's red love, experimenting to enhance the effects, and waited for the next contraction
>
> Then the Tzinacatl say the war started, and submarines prevent them from exporting or importing anything
> Comet Shine realizes all his former cohort are incentivized to sabotage the war effort so they can unlock their funds
> He realizes Chrysalis sucked all the bits out of Equestria for Skyfall Marks that are now useless due to the blockade.
> Chrysalis doesn't need any leverage on Skyfall, it's pure market driven cost of insurance. They'll actively trade with her for more red love to sell to addicts.
> She barely needs leverage on the rich tycoons, just the fact that their Skyfall Marks once bought them the world and currently can't buy them anything, plus the imagery of "peaceful" Olenia
>
> Once the war started and the submarines came, the most ruthless tribes started selling their refined red love stock to the cities, and refugees are taking them to cope, factory workers are losing productivity

**Characters:**
* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** Rainbow Dash realizes the jaeger tanks were cheating using drugs. She pushed herself for nothing!
> When Réni embraces the "Anti-Poseur Crusade" and Rainbow Dash embraces the Élan du Chasseur (The Hunter's Spirit), they are actively doing to themselves what Changeling Jaegers do in training. They are choosing to hyper-fixate on their ego to numb their natural equine/griffon empathy.

* **[[Coltbert]]**
> **Analysis:** Mentioned as a fun-loving royal Aquileian noblepony
> But wait, Fleur said Coltbert was her mentor, but she hates monarchy. What gives?
> He would be royally amused by the use of traditional crossbows to "solve" an economic problem (but increase resentment, so he didn't solve the problem truly)
> Aquileia will have a whole artisan industry about crossbows and they hate the displacement by Herzland muskets (despite the hypocrisy of crossbows commoditizing violence from skilled longbows and knights)


**Threads:**
* **[[The War in the North]]**
> **Update:** Submarines based in Rottendedam are terrorizing all Equestrian trade with Griffonia and New Mareland

* **[[Camp Fluttershy]]**
> **Update:** Applejack asks the tribes which drugs were sold to Chrysalis and what they do. This information is sent to Twilight in a letter so Fleur can connect it with medical papers and advise Fluttershy on the proper treatment for Pagala's sacrifices.


**Themes:**
* **[[Opportunity over Pacification]]**
> **Commentary:** The thestral tribal wars were fair (anyone could train to be the best warrior) and "pacified" (no killing), but there was no opportunity to advance society. It was stagnant.


**Codex:**
* **[[Knightly Code / Rules of War]]** (WorldRules)
> **Note:** The Tzinacatl rules of war are a little different from the griffon ones, but it has a similar Stagnation
> The "Don't Kill, Just Steal" rule kept the tribes in a permanent state of low-level conflict (Stagnation). No one ever won, no one ever lost, nothing ever changed.
> The Crossbows ended the stagnation. One side won. This forced the "Losers" to look for an outside equalizer.
> This makes Chrysalis's intervention even darker. If the status quo is "Non-Lethal," and the Medicine Tribes respected that (technically), then Chrysalis introduces Lethality.

* **[[Tzinacatl]]** (Organization)
> **Note:** Different tribes have different drugs and qualities

* **[[Combat Drugs]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** One of the drugs keeps the user awake for extended periods of time
> Huge damaging side effects
> Twilight decides to make a sleep spell matrix so they can match
> Applejack wants to recruit Tzinacatl for tank crews for nocturnal
> There are a lot of industrious Tzinacatl who came back from Manehattan bitter from mistreatment and shunned by their own tribes too
> They also want to fight Chrysalis for destroying their economy, they hate the inward turn of the drugs and decadence and infighting 
> When Tzinacatl from different tribes were at the factory late at night, tribal differences evaporated 
> A lot of potential for volunteers
> War bond systems setup for volunteers

* **[[Moriset Discret's Aquileia/Coltbert Reforms]]** (History)
> **Note:** Aquileian ships delivered crossbows and picked up Tzinacatl goods and delivered Aquileian goods. Other tribes were ignored and subjected to Skyfall predatory insurance/privateers. This fueled the tribal arrogance and resentment/division

* **[[Banking]]** (SocietalDifferences)

### SCENE 12.2: Letter about tribal petty wars
> **Synopsis:** Dear Princess Twilight Sparkle,
> Today I learned the hard truth that not everything can be solved with investment and war bonds.
> Funny how that's coming from me, ain't it?
> Tribal wars, deep divisions, extreme warrior culture
> The reality is our allies are arrogant, and by helping them more the other tribes will hate us more. We have a long way to go
>
> Fluttershy reads the letter and says the changelings before Chrysalis were the same, according to the POWs
> If Applejack can solve the jungle, it must mean the changelings can be saved too

**Threads:**

### SCENE 12.3: Twilight contemplates Hearth's Warming Eve
> **Synopsis:** Maybe it IS Hearth's Warming Eve? And she didn't get to spend it with Applejack? But she has Fluttershy and Fleur and Henri (AJ and RD are in the jungle)
>
> Fleur and Henri want to hear the Hearth's Warming Eve story, and laugh at how naive it sounds
> Fleur thinks Celestia made up the story to foster friendship, then laughs about the fact that unicorns don't even raise the sun and moon, SHE does. How would the unicorns even manage to move the sun, anyway? We need a crystal enhancer just to cast a decent shield spell these days. Fleur jokes that Celestia scrubbed the power crystals from history; she wouldn't be the first monarch to do so (anti-monarch bias coming through). Fleur looks at Twilight, who is frozen. She apologizes for insulting Twilight's mentor so harshly.
>
> Twilight? Twiiiiilight...
>
> The twist: Twilight isn't mad at Fleur. She's frozen like a statue because she's contemplating whether that could be true. She comes up with the idea that maybe Hearth's Warming isn't a fairy tale; ancient unicorns could have used crystals to move the sun and moon.
>
> What else was never studied in the last 1000 years? Celestia held back scientific progress on purpose?
> Fleur studied how earth pony magic works. Does Celestia know, or she doesn't even know or never bothered to understand it?
>
> Twilight realizes she doesn't have to speculate. She can ask Star Swirl, who was Celestia and Luna's teacher and therefore must've taught them how to move the sun and moon. This actually definitively confirms that Celestia cut back on magical research...
>
> Fleur sighs and says Celestia reminds her of her parents, and goes into her backstory
> This relieves Twilight of some more guilt
> I'm glad you're not that kind of princess, Twilight
>
> You should have seen Twilight at the School of Friendship, Fluttershy giggles, unexpected sass
>
> Twilight's eyes widen. Now she thinks Celestia was trying to turn her into that over protective mother. She was going to become the overbearing mother of all of Equestria
>
> You know, I didn't always have these wings. Fleur, you remind me a lot about myself. But you know what? You told me to just be myself, and I've been doing that. Thank you

**Characters:**
* **[[Fleur Bloom]]**
> **Analysis:** Backstory of the farm revealed
>
> The attack on Celestia won't be that she's a monarch. It's that she is boring and chaste


**Threads:**
* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** The implication is that Celestia hid power crystals as a means of unlocking and democratizing magic and actually scrubbed the existence of power crystals from history. Twilight doesn't want to believe that Celestia did such a thing, but she's having ideas in light of her other disagreements with Celestia. She is starting to feel resentful...
>
> Celestia lobotomized Twilight's critical thinking to make her the next overprotective mother figure
>
> Fleur asks Twilight why didn't Celestia use the Elements of Harmony to purify Luna? Why need you and your friends?


**Codex:**
* **[[Life Forces of Sentient Creatures]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Fleur mocks the Elements of Harmony as tools of Stagnation

* **[[Luna Doctrine]]** (History)
> **Note:** Twilight imagines it here, and Fleur names it

* **[[Weather]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Twilight laments how it isn't winter, it's not Hearth's Warming Eve without snow, there's no hearth to warm (seasons canceled due to the war)
> Fleur points out how inefficient seasons are, making the Equestrians realize there are no seasons in Aquileia but they do have different climates based on geographic features
> Henri says Herzland is flat, boring and sterile

* **[[Ancient Equestria (Before Luna Banishment)]]** (History)
* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Fleur accuses Princess Celestia of 1000 years of built up culture of separation of key talents - only earth ponies allowed to farm, only pegasi allowed to make weather and only in Cloudsdale, as a way to keep peace between the three tribes
>
> Celestia sacrificed efficiency to preserve harmony. She is a moral absolutist and a luddite.
>
> Therefore, the necessity of earth pony magic was never studied, it just was the way it was
> Only in Aquileia where industrialization supplanted griffon farming but failed to supplant earth pony farming was this topic ever studied by the University of Pridea

* **[[Cutie Marks]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Fleur accuses cutie marks as a tool for stagnation


---
### SCENE 12.4: Learning the real struggles of the drug tribes and the formation of EEEE!
> **Synopsis:** Break this into two separate Applejack segments? So there can be two different letters.
>
> The initial problem is already laid bare - the drugs are going to Manehattan and other cities
>
> Flowing Current and EEEE! are belligerent to the drug tribes. They want to arrest and ostracize for all the damage done to Manehattan. They literally have Chrysalis's drugs in stock and have been refining them. They should be nationalized punitively like what they did to the collaborators in Manehattan
>
> Flowing Current says the parloir owners know their rival tribes sold opioid-like drugs to Aquileian nobles pre-Coltbert reforms for the nobles to give to their pony pets to keep them pacified so they won't run away
>
> Mali talks and connects with the downtrodden thestrals of the drug tribes, rank and file, not the cartel leaders
> This is like bypassing corrupt leadership like Netanyahu/Fatah and Hamas and building grassroots connection directly with the community members
>
> They were global drug leaders and were wealthy selling to griffonia
> Stayed clear of Equestria due to royal guard crackdown and no demand for drugs due to stagnation of harmony
>
> The Aquileian-aligned artisans got their crossbows, which violated the raiding culture of 1000 years
> At least they didn't use their crossbows to conquer us, they were content being smug and laughing at us from their high and mighty places
> Drug tribes adapted, creating more drugs to sell on the market, becoming organized cartels
> So the crossbows actually stopped the zero sum game and actually encouraged internal development and an export economy
>
> Chrysalis paid above market rate for their drugs
> Then she synthesized the drugs with red love and flooded the griffonian markets
>
> Thousands of Tzinacatl who were living rich international lives are now poor. It's a gold mine of workers for legitimate Skyfall tycoon opportunists
> Skyfall investors offered to build subsidiary factories and factory towns on the jungle coast, clearing land to do so. These were standardized steel and aluminum mills, textiles, some rubber production using Skyfall methods with natural rubber additives
>
> The rubber factories do the synthetic way that's already used in Skyfall and the changeling lands using petroleum and oil, but mix in some amounts of natural rubber from the Tzinacatl to improve the properties. The factories are massive and heavily polluting. But they aren't cutting down rubber trees yet, they are mobilizing the defeated cartels to collect natural rubber the way they did for rafts in order to mix into the oil-based processes. The Skyfall companies for the most part aren't self-destructive, they are looking at long term profits
>
> The Tzinacatl already knew about industrialization since some went to Skyfall for night shifts, but often come back with stories of abuse, isolation and drug addiction (from their own drugs on the streets)
> Many traditionalist tribe members actually left their tribes for the opportunity of the factories on their soil. The factories and factory towns broke tribal divisions. Duty/fanatic zeal to one's own tribe goes away, only duty to self and competition. But it's ruthless compitition, not solidarity
>
> These legitimate factories provide a real alternative to the old ways. For a brief moment, there is a "middle class" rising in the jungle, thestrals who are fed and housed in company towns, modernizing and purchasing international goods legitimately, although their wages are low and profits do flow to Skyfall. This was their way out of Stagnation.
>
> Then the logging company came (Chrysalis's company in disguise)
> They made a deal with a particular tribe to cut down the jungle, extract all the rubber and get all the wood, and then they gave guns to invade neighboring tribes to cut down their wood and rubber and magical drug plants
> They recruited downtrodden thestrals with fascist/supremacist propaganda
> See The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis
>
> All tribes united to burn them down after killing and ecological destruction, including all traditionalists, medicinal artisans, and even some cartel tribes
> But then the traditionalists and some artisans/cartels continued, in anarchy, destroying all the company towns/factories as "Skyfall rot" because it all looks the same - factories run by griffons with heavy machinery
> The workers started getting recruited by opportunist Equestrian tycoons to Manehattan, working night shifts
> The local thestrals describe how they just turned inwards, taking their own drugs, and stagnating. The ones who left to Manehattan are traitors, they just became Equestrians and are abused/second-class citizens, and the ones who stayed at least still have their dignity of self. They decided to reject industry and go back to the old ways/become depressed post-industrial shells
>
> (Flowing Current describes this part, refuting the biases of the left behind locals)
> He says the thestrals were originally union breakers, and this angered the ponies trying to form a united union to get better wages from the tycoons who learned from Skyfall. The thestrals who arrived were rugged individualists, bitter against their own ancestral tribes, trusting no one, and ripe for exploitation by the tycoons
> The parloirs, who had shed the arrogance and tribal divisions back home due to being minorities (they are not the elite in Manehattan, they are outsiders, and also you can't run a business based on empathy and passion if you are arrogant, you have to listen and give a safe space for others), used empathy and friendship to bridge the gap
>
> If the Parloirs didn't exist, these refugees would have become beggars or criminals. Because the Parloirs did exist, they had a Sanctuary.
>     The Parloirs likely turned into soup kitchens by day and shelters by night during the initial crisis.
>     This act of charity cemented the bond between the "City Thestrals" and the "Refugee Thestrals," erasing the class divide that existed in the jungle.
>
> pony workers with their wages frequent the parloirs for excitement since the factories are dull and soulless
> This shared space, facilitated by parloir operators, connects the pony workers and thestral workers, which formed EEEE!
> That's why it's Equestrian Equality, Empathy, and Education!
>
> The drug tribes are victims of a scam who have never been presented another way
> The isolationists burned down the scam
> The medical and artisan tribes watched and laughed instead of helping, due to deep tribal divisions
>
> Present day, the drug tribes are in a domestic crisis of their own, taking their own traditional drugs which no longer have a market to cope with hopelessness and the loss of their golden age
>
> Mali teaches the artisan tribes that they have to share so the other tribes aren't desperate enough to kill again
>
> Next conversation with a higher up cartel leader:
> Some kept trying to sell drugs, not giving up. Their patience and persistence was rewarded when Chrysalis constricted red love supply in 1002 (after Canterlot Wedding), and they now had an open market to sell their drugs again
> In 1008, after Olenia was puppeted, the red love flooded the market again, and this time the cartels did not despair. They saved up wealth for later. Now they bought Chrysalis's cheap drugs, refined them even further with real Tzinacatl drugs, and sold at a premium, and also stockpiled, waiting for the next time Chrysalis inevitably constricted supply.
> But that has yet to pay off, because of submarines... so they sent their stockpiles to Manehattan. That's how we got here. They are opportunist and morally grey, but they are still victims who are driven by economic incentives first, not irrational malice or hate for ponies. They just need a market (short term) and alternative opportunity (long term)
>
> Mali says we cannot leave them to rot, that would be against our principles. And we need full unity, otherwise they will disrupt our supply lines. Do you want Chrysalis to have an ally?
> We have scorned them for so long. We must unite in the face of extermination.

**Threads:**
* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
> **Update:** Destroy the jungle for short term rubber and gain a puppet to invade Equestria with in the process, just like Stalliongrad
> The mentality she sold to the very tribe who she destroyed via copyright infringement and market flooding is: your jungle doesn't matter, extract it all then turn on the other tribes for domination. End the 1000 year feud and rule the jungle and become a corporate capitalist state where you are the upper class. Seize the factories from the Skyfall tycoons and become an industrial superpower
>
> Method: Scorched Earth Capitalism. She isn't trying to build a sustainable industry; she is trying to "strip-mine" the region to deny it to the Republic and break the local culture.
> The Cycle of Abuse:
>     Chrysalis destroys their economy (Synthetic Drugs).
>     The tribes starve.
>     Chrysalis (via shell company) returns as a "Savior" offering jobs.
>     The "Job" is to destroy their own home (clear-cutting) and kill their neighbors (with provided guns).
> Motivation: They aren't evil; they are cornered. They view the Medicine Tribes (who are rich and smug) as enemies who refused to help them. The Skyfall Agent is the only one offering them a future, even if that future is ash.
>
> The Agent's Weapon: It isn't just guns; it's Lies. He is telling the thestrals, "The Medicine Tribes want to keep you poor. The traditionalists wants to keep you stagnant. Equestria ignores you. Only we want you to be strong."
>
> 981-987 (The Foundation): The Parloirs establish themselves. They become trendy. They build the "Safe Space" infrastructure. Chrysalis witnesses the gold drain to Aquileia
>
> 988 (The Crisis): Chrysalis establishes the attempted puppet state in the jungle but she fails, The factory burns. Thousands of traumatized, starving Thestrals arrive in Manehattan.
>
> Chrysalis sees how to weaponize popular anger, and plans the Winter Revolution, this time aiming for the popular revolution to succeed on purpose to break from Equestria
> She dismisses the failure to puppet the thestrals as simply them being too uncivilized. The Severyanas are civilized and can be puppeted


**Themes:**
* **[[Anti-Racism / Anti-Tribalism / Politics of Division]]**
> **Commentary:** Nationalism/tribalsim are blind. It destroys the good with the bad. The legitimate investors didn't deserve to have their assets burned, and the workers didn't deserve to lose their new livelihoods because of the one company (implied to be Chrysalis after what they learned from Las Pegasus)

* **[[Embracing and Complementing Special Talents]]**
> **Commentary:** Most pony factory workers chose to do that because they were bored of their own special talents/old lives.
> In the factory, they are blank flanks/cogs. It's an alternative lifestyle, but it's soul crushing.
> At the parloir at night, they can be themselves again, culturally rich. It's a social club.
>
> Applejack internalizes that parloir=social club (like Gaudreau's social clubs, FJA=young aquileian foyer). She is completely blind to the sexual liberty aspect.

* **[[Rugged Individualism / Wedge Issues Deconstructed]]**
> **Commentary:** The Skyfall tycoons abandoned the workers when the other tribes burned things down. The companies viewed the workers as assets, not family, because they are rugged individualists
>
> The thestrals believe in the "myth of meritocracy" because they came from isolationist tribes where survival/thriving comes from individual skill of being a warrior. Plus they are influenced by Skyfall propaganda and the material wealth they saw from selling drugs to griffonia and Chrysalis and importing foreign goods with the profits.
>
> Because they believe this myth, they view the Pony Union not as brothers, but as "Lazy Entitled Ponies" who are standing in the way of progress. They willingly cross picket lines because they think they are "winning" the economic game.
>
> The Manehattan Tycoons (Skyfall-trained Ponies) are cynically using the Thestrals' desperation and work ethic to crush the Pony Union. They have no intention of ever promoting a "Bat" to management. The Thestrals are a disposable weapon.
>
> In the social club/empathetic environment of the parloirs, the rugged individualist thestrals learn that collective barganing is actually the ultimate form of strength (solidarity)
>
> For the pony workers, the lie is replacing Family Tradition with Corporate Exploitation. They promise "Blank Flank" means "Limitless Potential", but in the factory, it really just means "Interchanegable Part/cog"
>
> Actually, the CMC's version of Blank Flanks is the harmonic version. And the episode On Your Marks actually deconstructs the cutie marks by the CMCs realizing they aren't stuck doing one thing, they can still learn other things

* **[[Rejecting Destiny]]**
> **Commentary:** The pony workers are bored of the safety of stagnation. Putting on a jump suit that covers their cutie mark is an act of rejecting destiny. I am not a baker just because magic says so. I am a worker because I chose to be.
> They are choosing "Misery with Agency" over "Happiness with Destiny." This is a very human, "Adult" choice
>
> This deepens the villainy of the Skyfall-aligned Tycoons. They didn't just enslave helpless ponies; they exploited a genuine desire for growth.
>
>     The Pitch: The Tycoons likely sold the factory jobs not as drudgery, but as Modernity. "Come to Manehattan! Be a part of the Future! Leave the farm behind and build something Real!"
>
>     The Reality: The ponies wanted Adulthood (Independence, Grit, Complexity). The Tycoons gave them Alienation (Anonymity, Exhaustion, Poverty).
>
>     The Shame: This explains why they don't leave. If they go back to Ponyville, they have to admit, "I couldn't hack it in the real world." The shame of returning to the "Playpen" keeps them trapped in the factory. They would rather be miserable adults than happy children.
>
> The Unintended Consequence: The Tycoons tried to erase individuality to make "Cogs," but they accidentally created Class Consciousness. The workers realized that without their Marks defining them, they were all just "Ponies struggling together." This lays the groundwork for the Union (EEEE!) to transcend tribal lines.
>
> Why They Hate the "Scab" Thestrals (Initially)
>
> This clarifies the initial friction before the Parloir unification.
>
>     The Cultural Threat: The Pony workers sacrificed their cultural identity (Marks/Harmony) to build this new "Modern" world.
>
>     The Thestral Arrival: The Thestrals arrive and are willing to work for less, and they don't carry the same baggage of "Rejecting Harmony" (since they were never part of it).
>
>     The Resentment: The Ponies feel: "We gave up everything to be here. You just showed up because your house burned down. Don't ruin the dignity of this hard life we chose."
>
> The Parloir’s New Role: Validating the Choice
>
> This is crucial. The Parloirs don't just offer "escape"; they offer the Promise Fulfilled.
>
>     The Validation: The Parloirs show the workers: "You were right to leave the farm. Adulthood IS better. But Adulthood should look like Wine, Jazz, and Debate, not just soot and gears."
>
>     The Healing: The Parloir proves that you can be "Modern" and "Harmonious" at the same time. You don't have to choose between being a baby in Ponyville or a slave in Manehattan. There is a Third Way (The Aquileian Way).
>
> There is also an aspect of rejecting judgement based on what they are supposed to be by becoming a blank flank at the factory, now they get judged by their current performance
>
> The Parloir’s Synthesis: Reclaiming the Skill, Rejecting the Role
>
> The Parloirs become the place where these ponies learn to separate their Talent from their Trauma.
>
>     The Realization: A pony marked for "Violin" might have hated the pressure of being a concert soloist for the Canterlot elite. But in the Parloir, playing jazz for their fellow workers, they realize they do love the violin—they just hated the expectations attached to it.
>
>     The "Adult" Mark: The EEEE! movement isn't about hiding the mark (Factory) or blindly obeying it (Tradition). It's about Redefining It. "I am not a Violinist because my mom said so. I am a Violinist because I play in the Union Band."


**Codex:**
* **[[Tzinacatl]]** (Organization)
* **[[Parloirs/Pre-War Manehattan]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** The Two Capitalisms of Manehattan
>
> This sets up a brilliant thematic conflict that mirrors the Jungle conflict but in an urban setting.
>
>     Faction A: The "Skyfall" Ponies (The Antagonists)
>
>         Origin: Ambitious Ponies sponsored by Chrysalis to learn "Ruthless Efficiency."
>
>         Model: Standardization, low wages, high turnover, alienation. They treat workers like Chrysalis treats drones.
>
>         Role: They are importing the "Poison" into Equestria.
>
>     Faction B: The "Aquileian" Parloirs (The Protagonists/EEEE!)
>
>         Origin: Medicinal Tribes trading with the Republic.
>
>         Model: "The Artisan Way." High wages, high skill, emotional connection, luxury/quality over quantity.
>
>         Role: They are the antibody. They prove that you can run a business without destroying the soul of the worker.

* **[[EEEE!]]** (Organization)
> **Note:** The parloirs are third places, community halls
>
> Because the Parloirs sell "Intimacy and Connection" (the Aquileian Way), the atmosphere is inherently empathetic. A factory worker (Pony or Thestral) enters a Parloir and is treated like a pony, not a cog. This creates deep loyalty to the Parloir owners.
>
> The Parloirs are profitable. This means the Union (EEEE!) has independent funding from the start. They aren't scraping for dues

* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Now Applejack can better understand why her parents left the farm.


---
### SCENE 12.5: Rainbow Dash meets the fake Daring Do and Ahuizotl
> **Synopsis:** Daring Do (thestral in a costume) and Ahuizotl do sham tourism after their economy collapsed
>
> Ahuizotl is a changeling who is always the cat monster. He describes being a good financier (the top of his class in the hives) so Chrysalis had him be a Skyfall agent for Krystallfels Hendelsgesellschaft
>
> In the Hive, loyalty is extracted via terror and the Love Harvester. In the Cartel, loyalty is bought with blood-oaths, shared risk, and the "Fraternity of the Outlaw." When a rival cartel or a Skyfall privateer tries to double-cross Ahuizotl, the Daring Do protects him—not because the State ordered her to, but because he is her business partner. For the first time in his life, Ahuizotl experiences a bond based on mutual, horizontal respect rather than top-down totalitarian terror.
>
> Chrysalis issues the directive to clear-cut the jungle. She orders Ahuizotl to burn the Cartel’s ancestral drug-crop territories to maximize short-term rubber yields for the impending war machine. Ahuizotl looks at the spreadsheet and realizes Chrysalis is a parasite that destroys her own capital. The Hive does not reward loyalty; it consumes it. If he follows orders, he destroys the only genuine partnership he has ever built. He chooses the "Honest Racket" of the Cartel over the suicidal, totalizing extraction of the Hegemony.
>
> Ahuizotl realizes he cannot go back to the sterile, loveless Hive. He fakes his death in the fire and permanently adopts the "Cat Monster" persona, choosing the messy, vibrant life of the jungle over the cold meritocracy of the C-suite.
>
> When the Traditionalist and Medicinal tribes unite to burn down the Krystallfels complex for desecrating the jungle, Ahuizotl knows he cannot simply resign. VOPS executes defectors. He must die a highly visible, undeniable death in front of his Skyfall Griffon middle managers.
> Thestral Daring Do coats a jagged machete in a highly concentrated, proprietary cartel cocktail: a mix of hyper-coagulants, neuro-blockers, and localized tissue-stimulants.
> As the complex burns, Daring Do corners the "griffon CEO" and savagely slices him up in front of the real griffon middle managers, who flee in abject terror. Daring Do then drags Ahuizotl into the jungle and heals him.
>
> Because the pain is real, Ahuizotl broadcasts 100% authentic agony and terror. The Griffon managers feel this via their own panic, cementing the memory. When VOPS later audits the Griffons using Emotion Sense, the auditors will detect absolute, unshakeable truth: the Griffons watched their boss get gutted.
>
> The griffoness CEO of the Krystallfels Hendelsgesellschaft, Kriemheld von Krystallfels, is originally Queen Chrysalis, but other changelings play as her now
> He says the biggest supplier, customer, and shipping company that does business with Equestria and employed the most Equestrians of any Skyfall corporation is secretly run by Queen Chrysalis
>
> Rainbow Dash laughs at him
> Applejack says "and we're supposed to believe a cat monster hiding in the jungle running sham tourism? Sounds like you're just a defeatist coward"
>
> Ahuizotl said Prince Blueblood sought him out 8 years ago. Prince Blueblood deduced everything, but the Canterlot Elite and the Aquileians rejected him insane for coming up with a conspiracy theory. Ahuizotl confirmed Blueblood's theories and explained how it was even worse, but it's hopeless. Blueblood spent the last 8 years preparing for the apocalypse when no one would listen, just like they aren't listening
>
> So that's why he was at Mount Aris, said Rarity
> You aren't saying you believe this fool, do you? Applejack scoffed

**Characters:**
* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** Daring Do is the lens through which she views heroism. She views the war like a Daring Do book. Ponies are the heroes, changelings are the monsters. It is a moral binary.
>
> When she meets the fraud Daring Do and the repentent changeling Ahuizotl who wanted to stop being an evil bug serving the Queen, Rainbow starts to question the moral binary (Crack Lightning's indoctrination)


**Codex:**
* **[[Changeling Emotion Sense]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Ahuizotl explains how VOPS uses emotion sense to audit lies

* **[[Chrysalis's Economic Scheme]]** (Backstory)

---
### SCENE 12.6: Applejack sends a letter about the Tzinacatl Factory/Rubber Plantation
> **Synopsis:** Before it was only predatory investors who want scale, replaceable workers and don't respect local terroir. And the market was a black market of nobles, capitalists
>
> This letter is about tribal differences evaporating at the factory floor
> Sometimes standardization can be a good thing... sometimes
>
> Also a ton of praise for Mali's empathy and grassroots connection skills, which gets Twilight jealous
>
> There is also a technical report on the chemistry of the red love drugs for Star Energy and Fleur to study, and for Twilight to learn more about the red love refining

**Threads:**
* **[[Cute Intelligence Agency]]**
> **Update:** Tell Fluttershy and the buffalo to go to other cities and investigate all factory owners, looking for Skyfall Marks, hidden strategic resources, suspicious merchant ship survival, and the greatest smoking gun of them all: red love stockpiles


**Codex:**
* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Now Applejack understands - just as the Tzinacatl factory crossed tribal differences (both for the factory and against the factory), her parents' company united themselves and united the families against them


---
### SCENE 12.7: Fleur attempts to study Pinkie Sense
> **Synopsis:** Pinkie is back to breaking the laws of physics with cartoon logic when cheering up Twilight over the rifle logistics and jealousy of Mali. Her Pinkie Sense is back too
> Fleur attempts to study Pinkie Sense and other anomalies, strapping Pinkie to high tech machines and saying the science doesn't make sense, this breaks all established magical theories so far
> Twilight laughs and says that doesn't mean it's not true, you see the empirical evidence
> She rationalizes it as Pinkie's special talent being spreading joy, which means she has the ambition to spread laughter, which lets her magic bend reality, similar to Discord's chaos magic. Pinkie lost those abilities when she thought it was all false hope, but regained it after learning that morale and resilience is not false hope but real.
>
> Fleur asks for Pinkie's cutie mark story
>
> When Pinkie saw the sonic rainboom color the gray landscape, she realized she had to make the joy happen. That is her special talent
> It's like fiat currency and earth pony pride to activate the chemical weathering. You have to make it happen
> Let's just leave it at that
> Okie dokie lokie!

**Themes:**
* **[[Lauren Faust's Original Themes]]**
> **Commentary:** In the show, Twilight effectively gives up on science and accepts blind faith. Your rewrite turns Twilight into a true empirical scientist. just because her current textbooks on unicorn physics and chemistry can't explain Pinkie Sense, that doesn't mean it's "magic without rules." It means her models are incomplete. She respects the empirical data: Pinkie's twitching predicts danger 100% of the time.
>
> Lauren Faust had the animators concentrate most of the cartoon gags on Pinkie


**Codex:**
* **[[Unified Theory of Magic]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Magic = Ambition!
>
> Pinkie's magic is the ambition to make others laugh, made reality
> Pinkie's magic is a latent, localized probability-manipulation field fueled by the ambition to entertain
>
> Pinkie Sense is not mystical precognition; it is a Subconscious Probability Radar.
> If a falling object is about to hurt someone, it will ruin the mood (anti-laughter). Her ambient magical field detects this incoming threat to the "vibe" and sends her physical nervous system an involuntary twitch (a tail twitch, an ear flop) to move her out of the way.
> It is a survival mechanism happening not to preserve her life, but to preserve the potential for comedy.
>
> Pinkie's special talent is laughter, she has the ambition to make others laugh, so her magic manifests as cartoon physics. Just like the animators make impossible silly things happen for laughs because animation is not constrained like live recording. They simply make it happen
>
> cutie mark discount theory applied to earth ponies too
> Fleur earlier proved that rainbow Dash's observation skills (cartoon logic from the episode Testing Testing 123) are from a cutie mark discount on stunt flying 
>
> Twilight previously viewed magic strictly as an active formula arranged inside a unicorn's horn
> If the Love Harvester mechanically drains a Changeling's will to fight (Red Love) and connection to others (Pink Love), and Fleur's chemistry proves Earth Pony magic requires pride/will to activate, the grand unified theory clicks into place: Magic is not just a formula; it is the physical manifestation of Ambition.

* **[[Canon Episode References]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Feeling Pinkie Keen

* **[[The Charitostatic Effect]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** This is the magic that Fleur doesn't yet understand!
>
> Pinkie Pie can effortlessly ignore the laws of physics because she possesses so much Pink Love. Her reality-bending is flawlessly anchored by her empathetic drive to build resilience and make others smile.


---
### SCENE 12.8: Reni's Plane Crash/The Fall of Stalliongrad (the city)
> **Synopsis:** Reni and Minette detail the fall and destruction of the capital of Stalliongrad, and how the most brutal fighting is taking place there with huge casaulties on both sides an civilian suffering
> They crashed, escaped the meat grinder and a hopped on a supply train straight to Tall Tale to get as far away from the horror as possible
>
> Burned to the ground
> Sets the stakes for why Stalliongrad will not stop at white peace and why they want gulags
> No rehab
>
> It sets the stakes of the war - Pagala is the true face of the changeling army, Trimmel is delusional and his promise is certainly a lie
>
> The front stabilizes east of Stalliongrad before Petershoof

**Characters:**
* **[[Trimmel]]**
> **Analysis:** Trimmel has to fight towards Manehattan against Blueblood while his comrades in Stalliongrad are fighting in the North along the same front
> He feels Blueblood is a nepotist and an enemy of the people, but Blueblood actually learned to be competent
> Blueblood actually handed over land to Stalliongrad, the land they couldn't secure during the revolution

* **[[Pinkie Pie]]**
> **Analysis:** This interaction perfectly demonstrates the rules of Pinkie’s context-dependent magic.
>
> Her magic is inherently joyful and non-violent. When she learns Resilience, her magic returns, but she realizes it does not belong in the trench. She uses non-magical, real-world morale for combat survival, and saves her magical cartoon physics for the camp where joy is safe.
>
> Because Réni is putting up a good front in a safe camp, Pinkie initially reads the room as "Safe/Healthy." She fires up her Naive Laughter (cartoon physics/gags) because that is what her magic dictates for a healthy pony taking a break.
>
> The Diagnosis: But Pinkie is no longer the naive pony from the Stagnation of Harmony. The meatgrinder taught her to see through false hope. When she recognizes the hollow, traumatized look behind Réni’s fake smile (perhaps recognizing the same look she had when the Changeling tanks first arrived), her magic violently shifts gears.
>
> The Tone Shift: The cartoon physics instantly drop. The gags stop. She transitions from the "Bubbly Entertainer" into the "Trench Chaplain." She sits down with him, entirely grounded in the heavy, material reality of his trauma, and offers him the Resilience she learned from Tally Mark. She doesn't try to make him laugh; she just lets him grieve the death of his innocence.
>
> Pinkie and Réni seem like complete opposites (a bubbly Equestrian baker and a haughty Aquileian fighter pilot). But structurally, they have suffered the exact same materialist trauma: They both lost their magical physics because the meatgrinder destroyed their worldview.
>
> The Synthesis: Pinkie started with Naive Joy, lost it to the trench, and had to learn Resilience (Fraternity/Real Morale) from Tally Mark. Réni started with Predatory Joy (Élan), lost it to the trench, and is now lost.
>
> The Lesson: Pinkie acts as the translator. She takes what Tally Mark taught her and applies it to Réni. She teaches him that he cannot fly on the high of the "Hunter" anymore. If he wants to protect the aluminum of his plane, he has to forge it with the heavy, unglamorous iron of the "Protector." He must replace Élan (Ego) with Esprit de Corps (Resilience).

* **[[Minette]]**
> **Analysis:** Since 975 (her rebellion against Lord Westkeep), Minette built her entire identity on the belief that Asset Specificity = Safety. She believed that if she was stylish enough, clever enough, and magical enough (the "Lioness" persona), she could negotiate her sovereignty and never be a "Mouse" (prey) again. She conquered interpersonal predation. Stalliongrad shatters this illusion because total war is industrial predation. An artillery shell does not care how impeccably groomed her dove-grey coat is. A lobotomized Changeling conscript does not care about her snark or her social leverage. The tragedy isn't that there is a bigger predator; the tragedy is that the predator is the system itself, and everyone is meat. For the first time in 36 years, her individual impact drops to zero. She is stripped of her agency not by a tyrannical Lord, but by math, logistics, and industrial output. She feels like prey again, which violently resurrects her deepest childhood trauma.

* **[[Réni Ducep]]**
> **Analysis:** He initially puts up a front. His ego cannot handle the truth: that his Élan du Chasseur (Predatory Joy) shattered because he couldn't stomach shooting drugged slaves.
>
> Réni believes that because he was able to choose his destiny, everyone has the power to choose. To him, the "Anti-Poseur Crusade" is a clean, mathematical equation: If you are an oppressor, you chose to be one, and therefore you deserve to be hunted. He views the revolution almost as a romantic adventure because he has never truly known the crushing, inescapable weight of systemic powerlessness.
>
> At the beginning of the war, they were taking out the statthalters who were hiding in half tracks. Hunting them feed his elan de chasseur. Then Shining Armor and Cadance sent them to Tall Tale and Diyarbecolt, where they were wiping out jaeger poseurs, the peak of the ace making an impact. When trying to defend the Crystal Empire before the Crystal City was cut off, they were gunning down changeling conscripts on suicide mix, but at least they were saving crystal ponies. Finally, when it came to Stalliongrad, both sides were conscripted meat grinders. The glory of the hunt completely evaporated and Reni felt like a cog with no agency for the very first time.
>
> The core of Réni and Minette’s L'Élan du Chasseur (The Hunter's Spirit) is Individual Impact. They believe that an elite, morally righteous artisan can change the world by decapitating the right tyrant. Réni’s enchanted aluminum is hardened by his absolute arrogance—his belief that he is an apex predator bringing justice to the wicked. Looking down at the millions of dying conscripts on both sides, Réni's ego collapses into existential dread. He realizes his "Anti-Poseur Crusade" was a childish fantasy. He is not a hunter; he is just meat. The exact millisecond his ego breaks, his ambition drops to zero. The magical discount on the aluminum vanishes. The plane is instantly subjected to the cold, heavy reality of live-recording physics. A stray piece of Stalliongrad flak or a shockwave that his ego-hardened armor would have deflected yesterday now effortlessly tears the fragile metal apart. The crash is the physical manifestation of his worldview dying.
>
> Just as Pinkie loses her cartoon physics and suddenly feels the oppressive weight of gravity, Réni’s plane suddenly becomes just a machine. The magical discount vanishes. They both lost their magical physics because the meatgrinder destroyed their worldview.


**Threads:**
* **[[The War in the North]]**

**Codex:**
* **[[Stalliongrad]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Stalliongradian ponies driven by ideological zeal, absolute Sobornost (togetherness), and the voluntary erasure of the self. They are martyrs for the collective.
>
> In Stalliongrad, there are no individuals. There is only a thermodynamic meatgrinder. The sheer, industrialized scale of Stalliongrad's self-sacrifice proves to the Aquileians that "Ego-Capitalism" and "Artisan Pride" are utterly meaningless when two nations decide to mathematically erase each other.

> Tally Mark embodies resilence

**Themes:**

> **Commentary:** Resillience as the adult version of Laughter

### SCENE 12.10: Twilight finishes the Rifle
> **Synopsis:** Twilight stares at the rifle, thinking "what a monster we've invented"
> We're draining all this magic from our own ponies for the war
>
> Look on the bright side, once the war is over you can invent new spell matrices for everyone to use for useful things. You can let other creatures teleport as they please!
> And I can let them fly! But wait, flying and teleporting take a lot of magic.
>
> Yes, but we'll have plenty of magic
>
> Twilight looked at the crystal, and the rifle, and finally the canister. Wait you mean we're not going to put away the love donators after the war?
>
> They will have an open market for magic and an accessible market price, just like clothes, iron, electricity
>
> I know that sounds bad in a world of greedy griffons and Rockfeller, but what do we do as harmonic capitalists?
> We grow the pie with our special talents, Twilight answered
>
> We'll have so much magic, anypony can fly if they want to. Or teleport!
>
> What are we going to do about the valves? I can try to invent an alternative
> That could take a while. We might be better off buying them from Skyfall... let's see what Applejack and Comet Shine think
> **Outcome:** The letter about the magic market for peace time should inspire Applejack in the Tzinacatl negotiation.

**Characters:**
* **[[Star Energy Griffoness Artisan]]**
> **Analysis:** Twilight is worried the love donator will make her job obsolete, but she says she can't be happier
>
> Most magical stabilizer operators are bored of it but everyone has demanded more appliqués ever since the crystal empire returned in 1003, so they want to try new things but the market demands they stay. She is glad all the griffon artisans can move on to new bleeding edge areas
>
> She can't wait to see automated assembly lines of T2 and T1 spell matrices so she never has to make another spell matrix again


**Threads:**
* **[[Developing the Luna Nova Rifle]]**
> **Update:** Before, it just sloshes into the canister. A stable output to the spell matrix is a novel requirement (like constant voltage to a CPU)
> The three way crystal valve is used by Star Energy for next gen tractor fuel injector prototypes, which are powered by oil and crystals. Now they need a ton of these durable micro valves from Skyfall for the rifles which is the path of least resistance versus having to figure out how to manufacture these crystal valves. The love harvesters had massive pipes the size of a leg to brutally slosh the magic into a canister

* **[[The Great Griffonian War]]**
> **Update:** Fleur says she hates Skyfall. Ever since Equestria was attacked and Chrysalis's submarines blockaded Equestria, Aquileia has had to sell their luxury goods for Skyfall at scam prices in order to fund the preparation for war with the Reich.
>
> She wants Twilight to make a magical engineering first principles solution to the three way valve. But they need the valves now, so they have to buy from Skyfall.

* **[[Harmonic Capitalism]]**

---
### SCENE 12.11: Twilight sends a letter about the rifle completion and needing valves from Skyfall
> **Synopsis:** The letter is optimistic, talks about the post war magic market possibility and infinite opportunity for every creature - if we can get the valves
> It says the rifle is done but they need valves from a company in Skyfall. Can you arrange it? I know you can. I believe in you
> Love, Twilight Sparkle

---
### SCENE 12.12: The team at the jungle hears that Manehattan is panicking from the fall of Stalliongrad the city
> **Synopsis:** They are hearing panicked reports that the changelings are approaching Manehattan and the utter destruction of Stalliongrad
> The refugees in Manehattan are working harder than ever, desperate not to face a draft situation like the horrors of Stalliongrad and the mass slaughter
>
> firsthand evidence that without overwhelming technological superiority, you cannot fight Chrysalis’s suicide-drugged conscripts without resorting to human waves.
>
> Then they get the telegraph saying Twilight has the rifle, but needs valves from Skyfall
> The stars align for the drug trade deal
>
> Without the 3-way crystal valves from Skyfall, the rifles cannot be mass-produced. Without the rifles, the Equestrian lines will break. If the lines break, Applejack will be forced to institute a draft and send her citizens into a Stalliongrad-style meatgrinder, stripping them of their agency and turning them into meat.

**Themes:**
* **[[Stagnation can't survive the modern world]]**
> **Commentary:** Just like modern Western nations boasting about green energy or advanced electronics while ignoring the cobalt mines and sweatshops in the global south, Equestria’s "Clean War" is built on a foundation of foreign suffering. They are intentionally fueling a devastating drug epidemic in Skyfall's underclass to keep their own ponies out of the mud.


**Codex:**
* **[[The Predator's Dilemma (Coltbert's Paper)]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** This perfectly proves Coltbert’s fundamental thesis: "Morality is a luxury good that can only be purchased with the currency of capacity." Equestria can only afford to be moral to its own soldiers because it is outsourcing the immorality to Skyfall.


---
### SCENE 12.13: Applejack arranges the investment for the final tribe (the drug lords)
> **Synopsis:** They enter the negotiations
>
> The most revered cartel leader has a Dr. Caballeron persona, monetizing Daring Do
> The cartel leaders say why should we trust you? We only trust ourselves.
> We hate the Aquileians who look down on us with their fancy clothes and crossbows
> Because we will help you do what you do best. We will... help you export your drugs with transport planes. We need to buy the valves. You will get war bonds once the war is over, and we will help you transition into other industries. You will have a stake in the future of Equestria
> The alternative is Chrysalis will enslave you. You know how working with her goes.
>
> Offering the "solution" (industrial rubber factories) after causing the problem (market crash) is classic predatory capitalism. It proves that Skyfall/Chrysalis views the Thestrals as raw resources (labor and land), not people.
>
> We do view you as creatures who deserve a future to work for. We can overcome past differences, even 1000 years
>
> They need valves from a Skyfall company
> They don't have enough Skyfall Marks, and it'll be a huge premium for security with how much presence Chrysalis has in Skyfall
> They realize the last holdout tribe has the key. They are the artisans who already refine red love with their drug. Star Energy can invest in refinery infrastructure to scale up. They are becoming drug dealers, but they are using Chrysalis's economic weapon against her and delivering justice to the Tzinacatl
> They will get help from the Wonderbolts for transport planes to bypass the submarines
>
> Comet Shine says don't worry about Kessler or the Trade Council. Skyfall isn't like Equestria. There are no victims there, only players. Every griffon in that city signed up for the rat race. If they want to buy our 'Premium Stimulants' to get an edge on their competitors, that's their choice. We're just feeding the market.
> Applejack views the export not as "Drug Trafficking," but as "Supply Meeting Demand." She treats the drugs like Coffee or Energy Drinks—a commodity for ambitious griffons.
>
> Flowing Current likes the arrangement. He was previously belligerent to the drug tribes, but now he is correctly identifying that they can be class allies, also abused by Chrysalis the tycoon/parasite, and is shifting his belligerence to Skyfall
> Mali says isn't that a hypocrisy? You will be hurting Skyfall's workers, not their owners
> Flowing Current justifies it by saying how they don't have unions. The workers don't want unions, they want to become bosses. They are like Las Pegasus. Aquileia isn't like that; they have unions. Skyfall chose to be that way, and if we can solve our magical engineering problems by profitting off of their misery, then let's do it
> Comet Shine agrees, saying Skyfall has poisoned Equestria and the world's perception of capitalism, and their fleet is horrifically abusive of global trade. Destabilizing them is a good thing. We will use their greed and arrogance against them
> This appeals to Applejack, who hates how Skyfall took her parents away from her!
>
> The final agreement also entails Tzinacatl using their rafts to transport goods across the jungle from the railways. They are using their traditional combat corridors in solidarity now for the war effort and post-war economic integration/cooperation. It is to be the end of the tribal wars
>
> For the isolationists entering the fold, it's a question of good engineering vs bad engineering.

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** Applejack dismisses Mali's concerns about hurting Skyfall by flooding them with drugs based on an emotional reason: in her view, Skyfall took her parents away from her.
> This is a sign of moral rot, but also a sign of outgrowing the nursery.


**Codex:**
* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
* **[[Chrysalis's Economic Scheme]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** The Tzinacatl reveal the history of Chrysalis's oppression
> 982 - Chrysalis bought drugs from them (We taught the bug queen how to dream)
> 986 - she flooded the market and they crashed. Drugs turned inward and they stagnated.
> 1002 - the squeeze, they got back in
> 1008 - second flood, they adapted by buying her red love, putting their traditional additives and reselling
> 1011 - the blockade. Drugs went to Manehattan and other cities
>
> The Tzinacatl were effectively conquered by Chrysalis years ago, not by armies, but by shell companies. Applejack is walking into a post-apocalyptic economic zone.


---
### SCENE 12.14: Applejack sends a letter authorizing the drug trade
> **Synopsis:** Dear Princess Twilight Sparkle,
>
> A family feud isn't about hate, it's about hunger
> EEEE! wanted to disown the Tzinacatl in the jungle for flooding the streets with dark medicine
> But when I sat down with them, I didn't see villains, I saw ponies who were cut off by the submarines and trying to feed their foals
> So we fixed it. We're going to build refineries and provide planes to ship to Skyfall, flying over the submarines. The Tzinacatl will reclaim the market that Chrysalis stole, we'll keep Manehattan safe and heal the wound between cousins, and we'll get you the valves you need! Start sending love to the jungle and prepare the labs. That was the last group of tribes. The tribal feuds are over. I'm coming back.
>
> Love, Applejack

---
### SCENE 12.15: Combat Magic Training
> **Synopsis:** When Minette claims you can't fly on magic alone, you need to believe in a partner, Starlight snarkily flies on her own using just magic (brute force levitation, as she did in The Cutie Remark)
> Twilight says she also can fly without wings (as shown in The Crystal Empire Part 2 when she catches herself before hitting the ground after tripping on the stairs)
>
> Minette says that's because they have magic as their special talent, and technically Minette could do that with a crystal enhancer, but that would be highly inefficient
>
> So it's a Pinkie Promise? asked Pinkie
> What?
> You know, "Cross my heart, hope to fly, stick a cupcake in my eye!"

**Characters:**
* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]**
> **Analysis:** - no more being a bad teacher by shaping curriculum around Celestia's expectations. It will be like Testing Testing 1 2 3, where she meets the student where they are at.

* **[[Starlight Glimmer]]**
* **[[Minette]]**
> **Analysis:** the spell isn't just about romance; it is about the raw will to fight and the creature(s) keeping you grounded. This is a subtle but vital moment of growth for Minette, showing her recognizing that Equestrian "Band of Brothers" survival bonds are just as valid as Aquileian passion.


**Codex:**
* **[[Combat Drugs]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Comparison between jaeger geist which is a chemical that destroys their connection to others vs elan de chasseur organically fueled and sustained by their connection with their partner and others

* **[[Wings of Dew]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Twilight had the spell in her exotic spellbook that Shining Armor and Cadance slipped her from the parloir
>
> ailes de rosée
>
> Minette says the wing colors are a reflection of their [Aquileian partner term]
> What's that?
> How do you say it... your special somepony
> The unique colors are a broadcast of individuality and partnership
> Because we don't fight like cogs, we are individual hunters
>
> At the end of training, Twilight goes into a spiral, saying Applejack's wings were Tally Mark (who is right next to them, with active wings) and Mali (who is with Applejack and in her letters)
>
> Minette walks it back and gives a more technical explanation: the wings show who is keeping your hunter's spirit alive - Elan de chasseur
> She defaulted to special somepony because that's what chasseurs feel
>
> Unicorn chaseurs originally invented the spell to fly with our griffon partners
> It helps them fly more agilily too, and the wings have always reflected each other
>
> But Twilight won't stop spiraling about Mali deserving Applejack more
>
> The caster must "cross their heart"—meaning they must mentally and magically lock their devotion, trust, and vulnerability to their Chasseur partner. It is the absolute prerequisite for the spell.
> Flight is the ultimate expression of Red Love (Ambition). But the phrasing here is vital. You don't just decide to fly; you hope to fly as a direct result of crossing your heart. The rhyme explicitly states that the Thymodynamic thrust (flight) is only possible because the Charitostatic anchor (the crossed heart) was established first.
> "...Stick a cupcake in my eye." - In children's terms, this is a silly penalty. In materialist physics, this represents the catastrophic failure. If you don't believe in and love your partner, the wings will burn up. This is exactly what happened to Rarity in Sonic Rainboom when she forgot she was there to cheer on Rainbow Dash and instead stole the spotlight.


---
### SCENE 12.16: On the way back to Tall Tale
> **Synopsis:** I'm worried Twilight is more interested in Fleur Bloom instead of me
> She's the egghead version of you, Rainbow laughed. She can talk fancy
> Applejack huffs and says I can talk fancy
>
> Mali tells AJ not to worry (she's cute and kind like Fluttershy), but then says if AJ is really worried, then she needs to be a little assertive and "take her princess back"
> Applejack and Rainbow Dash don't comprehend it. In Equestria, the princess has always been Celestia, who is somepony to look up to.
> Mali says it's a theme in Aquileian books. Since the Tzinacatl trade globally, she's read stories from them translated into Equestrian. In feudal Aquileia, knights impress lords and win the countess/duchess/princess as a prize. And they fight for their ladies if some other knight tries to take over. (Don't use Herzlander or AJ won't try it. She's predisposed to like Aquileia though.)
>
> Rainbow says that sounds kind of uncool
>
> Like changelings... Applejack muttered
>
> It's romantic! Mali insisted. I'm not saying Fleur is your rival... But if she is, you gotta take Twilight back the Aquileian way, Mali giggled. Wouldn't that be poetic?

**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** AJ worried Fleur is stealing Twilight
> But the real reason Twilight enjoys Fleur's company so much is that Fleur unlocked the idea that Twilight can be herself again, and that means she can love Applejack guilt-free


---
### SCENE 12.17: Twilight worried about Applejack's return
> **Synopsis:** Henri makes a joke about Applejack and Mali being close since they fought in the trench together and escaped/arrived in Tall Tale together
> "Trench Bond? Is that a thing?"
>
> Twilight is spiraling, thinking Mali is a better princess of friendship than herself (the twist is that Applejack genuinely loves the pre-princess Twilight the most)
>
> She's a natural at friendship, like the best parts of Pinkie and Fluttershy combined
> She's the real Princess of Friendship, I just have a textbook and wings
> But Applejack doesn't love them, she loves you
>
> Twilight anxious about applejack not liking the old Twilight (unfounded spiraling)

**Characters:**
* **[[Mali]]**
> **Analysis:** Fleur has an inkling that Mali worked at a parloir
> The leter describes hyper empathy and high level social negitation skills
> Uses soft power to disarm enemies
> Also a passionate soldier
> Is she the embodiment of Aquileian spirit exported to the Tzinacatl?
> Now Fleur is intrigued by Mali


**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** Dramatic irony. Of course they'll be together, this story is too unapologetically TwiJack!
>
> But Twilight's concern here is that Applejack said she loved Twilight for promising a return to the past. Now Twilight has been making weapons and encouraging drug deals.
> The past was friendship and rainbows - that's what Mali represents
> Fleur says surely Applejack wants to see your true self, not whatever Celestia had you be
