### SCENE 12.18: The TwiJack Reunion
> **Synopsis:** Henri saw Mali brushing Applejack's mane
> Rainbow Dash saw Fleur doing the same to Twilight
>
> But then they get together and it's love at first sight, no doubt about it. They mess up each other's manes in the process
>
> Fleur, I apologize if there was some important work to be done, but the princess is mine for tonight, Applejack declared, holding Twilight close. Twilight blushed. 
>
> Fleur giggles and says, of course, take her! 
>
> See everypony in the morning, Twilight said. Then she teleports herself and Applejack away (hilarious because Twilight is the one teleporting them even though she is the princess they were fighting over) 
>
> Mali d'aws and says Applejack was worried you were stealing Twilight. Can you believe it? 
>
> Fleur giggles even harder and says Twilight was jealous of you!
>
> The two "rivals" advise Rainbow Dash and Fluttershy "give them a few hours before going home"

**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** Love at first sight again


---
## CHAPTER 13: Passion
> **Chapter Note:** The stakes of the previous chapters kept escalating. The friendship letters served as spots of warmth, but by the time of reunion Twlight and Applejack are both on the verge of burnout

### SCENE 13.1: Talking about the negative parts after Reunion - Part 1, Twilight
> **Synopsis:** AJ and Twi wash up together and did the kisses and cuddling, but they don't go anymore passionate. Instead, now that they are together, it's to confide in each other the negatives that they didn't write in letters (this is actually more mature/profound than expected)
>
> Twilight talks about Celestia hiding crystals, the Luna doctrine, and the horror/insecurity of using Chrysalis's tech, worried if they will become evil like changelings despite the Aquileians always talking about harmonic capitalism. She herself was crazy weaponizing her magic (lesson zero)
> This is where she talks about Celestia using her and not wanting to be the next princess. It depicts "princess" as negative, meaning responsibility and stagnation 
> Applejack talks about the revelation that the Tzinacatl aren't neglected and forgotten batponies who wanted to crawl into Luna's lap which Luna continued to neglect. They are actually infighting, and worse, they are merchants of death/hedonism 
> AJ is unsure if harmonic capitalism can actually turn those bat ponies into cooperative partners or if they can be friends. She's scared that the Tzinacatl are only working with them now because they hate Chrysalis for collapsing their traditional black market, and that they don't want to be friends with Equestria or each other. AJ also admits they were ambushed and she didn't say it in a letter
>
> Twilight for some reason comes up with the "Luna Doctrine"
>
> Applejack respects it, since she loves her family. She calms Twilight down
>
> Twilight accuses Celestia of trying to turn her into something she's not. She used to be all magic, all ambition (her ambition was to please Celestia). Twilight wielding the elements of harmony purged Luna with a blast of pure friendship because the plot of the first episode featured friendless Twilight abandoning her ambition-only mindset to make friends. The elements of harmony responded to a pony who was once all ambition and had just let friendship into her heart, and that's why it was able to purge the excess ambition/corruption from Nightmare Moon and restore Luna.
>
> But then Celestia went too far in trying to make Twilight a Princess of Friendship. Princesses were traditionally only crowned/ascended in order to help with Equestrian family planning when they had special talents related to love, and they aren't immortal. Twilight accuses Celestia of grooming Twilight to be the next Celestia so she could retire with Luna (The Luna Doctrine, just revealed, supports Twilight's theory), thus molding Twilight into a creature of pure pink, which she's not. Her special talent is literally Magic. Celestia tried to replace Magic with Friendship wholesale, when the elements of harmony fundamentally acted on a balance of ambition and friendship. This is why Twilight is straight up rejecting the notion of becoming the next Celestia.
>
> Twilight says can't be Celestia. She needs honesty
> Twilight is Ambition, not Stagnation 
> She is now free from rigid interpretations of friendship lessons that made her feel she wasn't worthy of Applejack in the past (applebuck season, winter wrap up, squeezy)
>
> Celestia's pattern: She abandoned the Buffalo, she ignored the Storm King, and she abandoned the Thestrals. Twilight does not want to be Celestia.
>
> Twilight mentions the realization that she changed herself to satisfy Celestia's image of her, and Applejack says I just want you to be yourself, and cites several distinct memories of old Twilight from seasons 1-3
> Fall Weather Friends should definitely be the first one, with the Egghead label
> Feeling Pinkie Keen, the Rapidash moment
> Games Ponies Play, the breathing exercise
> We really didn't get to spend much time just the two of us after you became a princess, huh?
> Applejack mentions that the old Twilight began to shine through again after the battle, but she didn't say anything about it?
>
> Also some examples from other fanfics, with an author's note at the end of the chapter to see if readers can cite which fanfics these are from
> The time you came over and I taught you how to eat grass (Romance Reports)
> Something from About Last Night

**Characters:**
* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]**
> **Analysis:** Twilight mentions the realization that she changed herself to satisfy Celestia's image of her, and Applejack says I just want you to be yourself, and cites several distinct memories of old Twilight from seasons 1-3

* **[[Princess Luna]]**
> **Analysis:** Luna gets portrayed as having survivor's guilt

* **[[Princess Celestia]]**
> **Analysis:** Celestia gets portrayed as selfish


**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** One driving part of Twilight's jealousy of Mali is that Mali (potrayed in the letters) seemed like a better princess of friendship (she has Pinkie Pie's friend-seeking with Fluttershy's demeanor, how can you beat that?)
> The relief is that Applejack enjoys the old Twilight more than the Princess version (even states that she didn't know the princess version as well)
> Applejack earnestly loves Twilight more now that Applejack has returned and sees Twilight the quirky and slightly snarky researcher

* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]**
> **Update:** Twilight feels like she was conditoned, used, and manipulated by Celestia. She feels betrayed
>
> the "Princess of Friendship" role was a cage of toxic positivity that forced Twilight to erode her intellect to make others feel comfortable.
> The "Princess" persona is passive, reactive, and obsessed with maintaining the status quo (Celestia's Stagnation). It failed to stop the war.
>
> But Applejack calms her down by reminding Twilight that she wanted to stay in Ponyville, Celestia didn't make her stay in Ponyville to learn about friendship. She had agency, and she chose to make friends with Applejack and the others


---
### SCENE 13.2: Twilight explains how she feels about Celestia's lessons now, using something Applejack did
> **Synopsis:** Applejack demonstrating Honesty as a virtue
> She could have told Twilight that Rainbow and Fluttershy would catch her, but instead she said "trust me"
>
> An adult Twilight would feel, looking back, that she had been manipulated into a leap of faith that could have been a reasoned decision. Her trust was weaponized to produce the right outcome rather than respected as something she was entitled to direct.
>
> Applejack reflects on it and says she wanted Twilight to learn to trust them since Twilight was being so stubborn about going off on her own. Because Twilight's ambitious and dismissive attitude reminded her of the industrialists in Manehattan that she despised and left her parents to go back to the farm for.
>
> Twilight says it worked, because that was the first trial and she stuck with them afterwards. But this is exactly the critique of Celestia's pedagogy that TLTT builds from. Celestia's tests — sending Twilight to find the Crystal Heart without full information, the Tirek situation engineered to teach a lesson about self-sacrifice, the entire apparatus of friendship letters as reports to authority — all operate in this mode. The lesson is more important than the person's informed consent to being taught it.
>
> The element activates because Applejack is honest, not because she did the honest thing in that specific moment.
>
> In TLTT's terms, the reason Applejack didn't tell Twilight about Rainbow and Fluttershy is the Stagnation of Harmony. Applejack has been conditioned, in a nursery culture, to manage other people's fear rather than inform it. The kindest thing to do for a terrified person, in Celestia's model, is to give them a reassurance they can hold onto — "you'll be fine, I promise" — rather than a full picture that might overwhelm them or change their decision. Applejack is not withholding out of deception. She is withholding out of care, in a mode of care that treats adults like children who need to be protected from the full truth.
> This is the exact pattern TLTT diagnoses in Celestia throughout the story. And it is present, already, in the pilot — in Applejack, the element of honesty herself, in her very first demonstration of her element. The story's argument is that this has always been there. The nursery's distortion of honesty was not imposed on Applejack by later seasons of flanderization. It was there from the beginning, baked into the culture she grew up in, expressed even in her most iconic moment.
> The war strips that away. The moment Applejack has to tell soldiers they will probably die, she cannot give the comfort-managed version of the truth anymore. And that is when the element becomes something real.

**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** The Hard Truth is the grown up version
> In Friendship is Magic Part 2, Applejack operates under the assumption that Twilight means well. By withholding the fact that Rainbow and Fluttershy are there to catch her, Applejack gets Twilight to understand that Applejack means well too
> In Tall Tale, Applejack explicitly says the changelings do not mean well. They want to drain us. No information withheld.


**Codex:**
* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** In TLTT's terms, the reason Applejack didn't tell Twilight about Rainbow and Fluttershy is the Stagnation of Harmony. Applejack has been conditioned, in a nursery culture, to manage other people's fear rather than inform it. The kindest thing to do for a terrified person, in Celestia's model, is to give them a reassurance they can hold onto — "you'll be fine, I promise" — rather than a full picture that might overwhelm them or change their decision.


---
### SCENE 13.3: Talking about the negative parts after Reunion - Part 2, Applejack
> **Synopsis:** Applejack talks about the revelation that the Tzinacatl aren't neglected and forgotten batponies who wanted to crawl into Luna's lap which Luna continued to neglect. They are actually infighting, and worse, they are merchants of death/hedonism
>
> They are drug lords!
>
> AJ is unsure if harmonic capitalism can actually turn those bat ponies into cooperative partners or if they can be friends. She's scared that the Tzinacatl are only working with them now because they hate Chrysalis for collapsing their traditional black market, and that they don't want to be friends with Equestria or each other. AJ also admits they were ambushed and she didn't say it in a letter
>
> Applejack also conveys the sobering reality of just how ahead Chrysalis was
> She had pony allies, Tzinacatl wrapped around her hoof, if they had lost Tall Tale, Chrysalis would have already won. Rockfeller and Las Pegasus would have collaborated immediately, the Tzinacatl tribes would destroy each other to submit to her, she stole all our bits under our noses
> What else is going to come next? I can't handle this, why am I the only thing that stopped Chrysalis? Me, a farmpony?
>
> they talk about shared burden, not wanting title, then smile about how they used each other's titles but not their own in their letters

**Threads:**
* **[[Harmonic Capitalism]]**
> **Update:** Can it really work?


---
### SCENE 13.4: Fleur and Mali compare notes
> **Synopsis:** They compare notes, giggling like schoolfillies. The mutual jealousy shows true passion, it's beautiful
> Mali mentions the Aquileian story and a knight taking their princess
> Fleur asks have you ever been to a parloir?
> Mali says she worked at a parloir. But I didn't do anything to AJ to comfort her. I just listened. (She says it in such a Fluttershy-esque way)
> Fleur realizes she is a professional, respects boundaries, is a passionate agent of liberty
> AJ was feeling jealous over Fleur and Mali reassured AJ and encouraged AJ to "take Twilight back" instead of giving Applejack pleasure to cope. Fleur recognizes Mali as an eagle and wants to feel Mali's lion side.
> Thus Fleur gives Mali bedroom eyes (you are an eagle, but I want your inner lion)
>
> On Fleur's side, she sees Twilight nervous about AJ talking about Mali being the best princess of friendship, and Fleur's advice is to be yourself (the kind of honest advice AJ would give). AJ wants the real you, not the one Celestia forged. This should let Mali see Fleur as having the self-actualizing ideals of the parloir
>
> If I were to write out their scene, it would consist of Fleur realizing Mali always gives but rarely receives. She suppresses her own desires/lust for the comfort of the client. (She naturally learned/adapted to ignore herself since she cares so much for helping others)
> Mali is in control, but only for Fleur's benefit. To a revolutionary like Fleur who believes in egalite, this inequality is unacceptable
> After Mali is done servicing Fleur (who asked for maximum lion), Fleur flips the script and tells Mali don't hold back your desires. You matter. Be loud, and let me fulfill it all
> By the end, Fleur asked how did I do? It's been a long time since I've been with another mare
> Mali says better than a princess
> That good, huh? Fleur said smugly
> She's certainly experienced and shared with me her deepest appreciation. But you told me to embrace my own desire. Nopony has ever told me that.
> Fleur is in shock.
> Mali smirks and holds her tighter and they have a deep sleep

**Themes:**
* **[[Capitalism for Good / Ambition for Good]]**
> **Commentary:** The parloir self-actualization spirit is harmonic capitalism applied to the heart - giving ponies the tools to be their best, fully adult selves
> Compared to predation or exploitation to the highest bidder


---
### SCENE 13.5: War Council Meeting to get everyone up to speed
> **Synopsis:** There's a war council meeting for Applejack to get up to speed on the state of Tall Tale's army, technology and new tactics. Applejack and Twilight walk in just in time while everyone else is already assembled, side by side with braided manes and tails, still looking tired but happy.
>
> Meanwhile, Fleur and Mali are sitting next to each other with unkempt manes. Usually Fleur's is perfectly straight like Twilight's, now frizzled, and Mali's is perfectly flowing/soft like Fluttershy's and is now tangled.
>
> You must've had a wild night, Fleur and Henri say
> AJ and Twi don't get it, and just start the meeting
>
> You look like you had a tough night, said Twilight. Your mane is all messed up. Did you have a bad dream?
>
> Twilight presents the Luna Nova Rifle to everyone. The name comes from the Temberik. She explains how they studied Chrysalis's love harvester and miniturized the tech to be less painful and fit into a rifle. She explains the canister cycling - consume donated red love, draw from yourself if necessary, donate the byproduct pink love for the captured enemies. Fluttershy says it's mandatory. Twilight cites the proof that she came up with, and its clever name about how Friendship is Magic. Maybe she names it after Celestia for the irony? Or Applejack because AJ was the first friend who Twilight met and the first to help her during the cliff fall
>
> Applejack and Mali lay out the established supply lines through the Tzinacatl Jungle and the rubber made through their alchemical traditions.
> Applejack also confirms the supply chain and funding for the valves, via drugs to the Tzinacatl and supply planes to Skyfall
> They will clear out Kessler's inventory now, and also license production in Manehattan where he will fly his special trade secrets to operate in a partner factory that they are preparing. They have to pay royalties for Manehattan production at 15%, which is why they will have to continue the red love supply. Tooling lock in - the most important steps require consumable components only found in Skyfall which have to be shipped in (Sintered Tungsten-Carbide cutters wear out after ~500 valves. Without fresh cutters from Skyfall, the domestic machines produce leaky, useless valves.)
> The valves are like transistors/chips. Kessler is TSMC
> Rarity is also on the call, and says EEEE! wants to send their appreciation of getting the drugs off the streets so the batponies won't be looked at with distrust and shady night creatures anymore
>
> Braeburn and/or a buffalo give a report on the oil fields (other scientists besides Fleur helped get the magic working, but Fleur still says there is so much potential she wants to study in peacetime)

**Characters:**
* **[[Fleur Bloom]]**
> **Analysis:** Braeburn and/or a buffalo give a report on the oil fields (other scientists besides Fleur helped get the magic working, but Fleur still says there is so much potential she wants to study in peacetime)

* **[[Pinkie Pie]]**
> **Analysis:** Fluttershy freaks out at the drug trade. You're taking donations and turning them into drugs?
>
> Pinkie grimly accepts the drug trade. She saw how Reni and Minette were broken by the meat grinder in Stalliongrad and doesn't want that to come to the Equestrian front. Her Resilience dictates that her primary duty is to protect the psychological and physical survival of the ponies in the trench holding the line. You can't give conscripts resilience, you either break or have to resort to tyranny or drugs. They must preserve their volunteer war using whatever technology is needed.


**Codex:**
* **[[Luna Nova Rifle]]** (Technology)

---
### SCENE 13.6: Fleur asks Twilight how was her night
> **Synopsis:** The two are continuing their research in the lab after the war council meeting
>
> Twilight reaches for a cup of coffee
>
> How was your night? asked Fleur
> It was a relief, we got our insecurities out and I love Applejack so much, Twilight blushed
> But we were late to the meeting, we don't have time for romance, we have so much work to get done
> Twilight and Applejack feel guilty the next morning the they spent too much time braiding manes and not getting enough sleep
>
> That's it?
>
> How can we be braiding manes when Stalliongrad is burning? They view their intimacy as a selfish distraction from the war effort.
>
> Twilight, promise me you're being serious. Where do foals come from?
> When two mares love each other very much, they go to my foalsitter Princess Cadance for a spell
> When two stallions love each other very much, a seapony finds a foal from the ocean and a hippogriff delivers them in a nice blanket.
>
> Fleur abruptly leaves
>
> (That's the story I grew up with, but obviously-)

---
### SCENE 13.7: Henri is talking with Applejack
> **Synopsis:** Henri tries to get extra details out of Applejack (he's much more aggresive and blatant than Fleur)
>
> Fleur goes to AJ and asks her to be honest. Where do foals come from?
> When a mommy and daddy love each other very much, they have intercourse (need slang southern term that is NOT sexy) and the mare will get pregnant and give birth
> Oh phew. So did you and Twilight have a good night of sex? 
> Uh, beg pardon, but there's no way I would want to have a child in the middle of war. That would just be plain irresponsible 
> Fleur's eyes widened 
> Besides, it's not like we have access to Princess Cadance right now...
> What... What does Princess Cadance have to with anything?
> Well, with dogs or hogs, ya need a boy dog and a girl dog, yeah? Same for ponies if you wanna do it without Cadance. If it's two mares, we need Cadance's fancy magic to have a foal
> And... How are two stallions supposed to do it? That's allowed, right?
> Yeah, a seapony finds a foal in the ocean and a hippogriff delivers the foal to the gentlecolts, Applejack smirked
> You can't be serious, Fleur growled
> That's the story I thought for a long time, until I witnessed the Battle of Mount Aris, Applejack sighed. The truth is, every year, the royal guards take in all the abandoned foals in New Mareland and bring 'em back to Equestria for couples to adopt. I reckon most Equestrians don't know about New Mareland and think all the foals come from Equestria. Sometimes the truth hurts too much...
>
> Fluttershy comes by, wanting to speak with Applejack privately
> Applejack walks off

**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** The truth is, every year, the royal guards take in all the abandoned foals in New Mareland and bring 'em back to Equestria for couples to adopt. I reckon most Equestrians don't know about New Mareland and think all the foals come from Equestria. Sometimes the truth hurts too much...


**Codex:**
* **[[The Battle of Mount Aris]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Truly the shattering of innocence. Prior to this, the Mane 6 thought hippogriffs and seaponies were majestic creatures who gifted good couples with foals (like Hearth's Warming Eve). This shows the hippogriffs are creatures just like them who needed help defending their home from warlords.

* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Celestia has other alicorns in the past, who are not immortal, including Cadance
> Celestia needs the alicorns to help with family planning
> Anyone with special talents related to love
>
> Applejack views sex as a biological duty and can't comprehend sex for pleasure, love, or stress relief
> To Applejack, having a foal is like planting corn. It is honest work, but it is a chore. Applejack literally cannot separate the physical act of intimacy from the logistical outcome of reproduction. She has no concept of "Recreational Ambition" or sex as a tool for stress relief.

### SCENE 13.8: Fluttershy gives Applejack The Stare about the drug trade
> **Synopsis:** Fluttershy says you are feeding an addiction. Those are Chrysalis's victims
> Applejack says it's necessary for the war, and it's justice for the Tzinacatl. They are Chrsalis's victims too... Plus we're keeping the drugs away from the streets of Manehattan. It's a win-win!
> Fluttershy gives Applejack The Stare and says once the war is over, we are going to Griffonia and curing every addict
> The implication is that Applejack is an unruly predator animal like a cockatrice who needs a moral lesson
> Applejack makes the promise (war crime becomes debt)
> We break it, we fix it. Cross my heart, hope to fly, stick a cupcake in my eye
> Fluttershy nods and says she's going to go help organize the shipment of the love donators at the camp to the rest of Equestria
> **Outcome:** Applejack leaves the interaction feeling like she is using others, extracting value out of creatures she has never met across the ocean. She feels like a predator, a tyrant.

### SCENE 13.9: Fleur and Henri decide the educate their friends, recruiting Mali
> **Synopsis:** They realize that their friends literally just kissed, cuddled, and braided each other's manes and tails
> They look like fillies at a slumber party
> They think love is companionship, grooming, and nuzzling.
> Mali giggles and says you're missing the meaning. Twilight could have used magic, but she braided by mouth because it's the earth pony way. I expected that, didn't you?
> This is a tragedy. Love is supposed to be fire
> To Fleur, an Aquileian who views relationships through a lens of passionate performance and efficiency, spending the entire night doing something as low-stakes as mane braiding is agonizingly boring and inefficient.
> Mali just smiles and says it's fine, they have their innocence
>
> Fleur says she and Mali may have messy manes, but they are well rested, psychologically satisfied and highly focused
> Twilight and Applejack spent all night braiding each other's manes. They are going to be stressed. They need relief and good sleep
> Mali shrugs and says "in the meeting you said you invented a sleep spell. Can't they just use that?"
>
> Fleur facehoofs
> Don't you think they can handle being adults? Fleur said.
> We scaled up their courage. We scaled up their leadership. Now we have to scale up their love.
> You can't just wait for the revolution to happen. You have to make it happen!
> Mali's eyes widen at that
>
> Fleur and Henri come up with a plan
> Fleur will treat sex ed as strictly logistics for Twilight
> Henri will give the romantic angle and lion's courage since Applejack is honest. She just needs to learn a fundamental truth and she'll act
>
> Fleur and Henri know the Equestrians are sexually "psychologically blockaded" as it was nurtured out, but they are shocked at the tragedy of just how much Applejack and Twilight love each other and yet are missing the ambition/passion aspect completely. Fleur decides she'll educate Twilight on biology. That's Twilight's specialty, hard science. Henri will educate Applejack about ambition and passion, specifically the nuance between a having ambition versus being a poseur (a predator posing as a lover) where it relates to sexual ambition. They discuss the Aquileian subversoin of The Lion and the eagle. This is Applejack's specialty, Honesty (how to not be a poseur).
>
> Mali, will you help me teach Twilight?
> Ok, she whispered
> You will?
> Mali looked up deteminedly. I used to only work with ponies who wanted to learn the Night by showing up at the parloir. I wanted to keep the rest of Manehattan innocent. But you're right, I know they are ready to grow up through their jealousy and devotion. Let's do it.
> Yes! My fellow revolutionary

**Characters:**
* **[[Mali]]** (Impact: CoreValueLearned)
> **Analysis:** Mali always met the client where they are at. She doesn't push them.
> Her policy ethos was: she will be there for them (open door policy) but not force them
> She joined the night guard and helped Luna, but she didn't force Luna to change. When Luna felt she couldn't go through with reforms, Mali didn't push. She left amicably to find another way instead (opptimistic, but a pushover).
> This passivity allowed stagnation to persist
>
> Mali agreeing to help Fleur teach Twilight how to give/receive passion is a way of adopting Fleur's revolutionary ethos: you can't wait for the revolution to happen, you have to make it happen (Like when she left her family farm to be a scientist)
> Basically instead of keeping the child safe, you have to pull them out of the nest/walled garden. Drive change instead of just surviving it
> She has to bring the Night to Twilight and Applejack


**Threads:**
* **[[Establishing the Equestrian Republic]]**
> **Update:** The Aquileian Revolution isn't just saving Equestria's economy; it is saving the souls of its Elements by teaching them that Passion is not a Sin.


**Codex:**
* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** When they first got together, braiding each other's manes is a symbol of love and healing
> Now, after a long separation and reunion, braiding each other's manes is a symbol of stagnation!
> In stagnant Equestria, lovers have no need to separate for long periods of time because there's no other ambition that would separate them. Also, Twilight's decision to disobey Celestia and go to the front is an act of ambition, to save her crush. That plays into the title "Magic" even further, retroactively, because the equivalent element of Liberty is Ambition!

### SCENE 13.10: Fleur gives Twilight a sex ed lesson
> **Synopsis:** Twilight is reaching for another cup of coffee
> Twilight, Mali and I have a very important lesson for you that will drastically reduce your reliance on coffee and sleep spells. It's called the chasseur doctrine.
> Twilight gets a scroll ready
> We had chasseurs - a griffon and pony pair, out to hunt warlords. Like Reni and Minette. Mountains were cold, combat was terrifying
> Fundamentally, the chasseur doctrine is about controlling the parasympathetic nervous system and the sympathetic nervous system.
> During combat, you experience a ton of adrenaline, but as the adrenaline fades, you suffer from acute cortisol toxicity and shock. The body demands oxytocin for homeostasis. The skin cells have C-tactile afferents that detect slow, gentle touch which send signals up the sipnal cord to the hypothalamus, which synthesizes oxytocin. It's a targeted biological antagonist to cortisol, lowering heart rate, dilates blood vessels to increase warmth, and forces the amygdala to calm down.
> Twilight asks too many technical questions, like what are the properties of adrenaline, oxytocin, why do they do affect the brain in that way
> Mali interjects and says Fleur, too much technical detail, you're missing the point. Twilight, you're focusing on the how, not the why.
> Okay, here is the traditional Aquileian way to describe it. You go into battle as Lions. Afterwards, you crash, and need to be Eagles together.
> Twilight says that doesn't make sense, both lions and eagles are predators
> Fleur facehoofs and asks Mali what do the Tzinacatl use
> Mali says they use Ocēlōtl (Jaguar) for Lion and Cuāuhtli for Eagle, but this is adopted from the Aquileians. Traditionally the jaguars represent the earth, the jungle, and the night. The eagle represents the sun, the sky, and the day. Because the Tzinacatl are nocturnal, the jaguar is the initiator who hunts during the night and the eagle is the receiver who rests during the day. Someone who can be both ambitious and graceful is a Cuāuhocēlōtl
> But Twilight has a point
> Fleur then looks at the lab table and sees the love canisters
> Okay, here is a new way to explain it. When you are in combat, you are full of red love. After the battle, the body has a desperate need for pink love. Now, Twilight, how do you restore pink love with Applejack?
> We cuddle and braid manes?
> Exactly! Why does cuddling work? Because of the C-tactile afferents providing a steady stream of pink love - oxytocin
> Mane braiding... well, I don't really know
> Mali giggles and says mane braiding is about trust and community
> Fleur says then it's endorphins and serotonin
> What do you do when you really want to show Applejack you love her?
> We kiss?
> Yes, kissing causes a localized blood pressure spike and instantly triggers the release of dopamine, craving, and adrenaline, heart rate increase. In other words, it's red love.
> Magic? Kissing is magic?
> Mali laughs and Fleur deadpans, yes, kissing is magic. Now, would you kiss after a battle?
> Uh, not really. I think I'd rather just cuddle...
> That's exactly right. You don't need more red love after a battle, you are crashing from too much red love. You need a steady source of pink love, from cuddling. That's exactly what chasseurs did to survive after a battle.
> Twilight thinks back to the aftermath of the second battle of tall tale and nods
>
> So, how do you and Applejack cuddle?
> First night, I clung to her chest and she held me
> That's good, very efficient for healing trauma
> Then we just snuggle into each other's sides
> That's very inefficient, you want to maximize surface area. Try spooning
> But that's what parents do for foals
> So what? said Fleur. Cuddling is a science. The C-tactile afferents are hardwired directly to the hypothalamus, your brain doesn't need to "think" about being cuddled to reap the chemical benefits. It's basically free healing that you need to maximize.
>
> After a massive adrenaline dump (a firefight), cortisol lingers in the bloodstream for hours. If a Chasseur went to sleep alone, that lingering cortisol would disrupt their REM sleep, causing combat nightmares and severe fatigue the next day. By falling asleep tangled together, the Chasseurs ensure a continuous, 8-hour intravenous drip of oxytocin. The physical contact literally acts as a chemical shield during sleep, neutralizing the cortisol rebound in real-time.
>
> Mali doesn't dismiss the parent foal, but she explains, a foal is just a passive sink for body heat and comfort, helpless and needs warmth and protection, but an adult little spoon has a mane and tail to provide and excess body heat to share
>
> The asymmetry of spooning is not a flaw; it is a highly specialized biological tool. It allows two traumatized soldiers to divide the emotional labor of survival.
> Big spoon gives vasopressin, the protector red love. Vasopressin is the hormone of territoriality, loyalty, and protection.
> Little spoon receives the max oxytocin because the amygdala and fight/flight response shuts down by having your dorsal (back) protected
>
> Little spoon is great for recovering from a warlord guilt crash, because you're providing unconditional biological foregiveness. sensory deprivation, block out all ambient light, wind, and sound, allows the overstimulated prefrontal cortex to completely shut down and reboot.
> Big spoon is great for curing imposter syndrome. If your partner is feeling anxious or insecure, offer to be the little spoon. They'll wake up feeling emboldened and protective.
>
> Chasseurs also had to cross long terrain and maneuver around warlord armies. These were long, multi day treks. It wasn't dangerous, but it was tedious and boring. A good night's rest is essential, but the looming stress from cortisol from thinking about combat and further trekking prevents it. They are irritable, tense, and have trouble sleeping. Staring at the tent ceiling doesn't help.
> Twilight connects that exactly to the previous night while she and Applejack were sharing their emotions. And pretty much all her spiraling episodes related to meeting Celestia's expectations. That's why she invented the sleep spell back then.
> So this is the phase we're in now, right? Not in a battle, but so much to do and not enough time, said Twilight, reaching for her coffee
> Exactement!
> I invented the sleep spell for this purpose... So what did the chasseurs do? Twilight asked.
> They start by producing a safe adrenaline spike, a spike of red love. They progressively increase the dopamine, red love, until they reach a climax, which includes a dump of oxytocin and prolatin, pink love, which is a biological sledgehammer to forcibly flush out the cortisol and work-related anxiety and puts the brain into a heavy, restorative sleep cycle. This was absolutely essential for staying in top shape for the next day's trek or battle. This is the safe environment morale boost that traditonal armies never had, which made chasseurs unmatched.
> Twilight takes furious notes
> Now, what do you suggest as the first step?
> They... get into a fight? For red love? No, wait, you said a kiss is red love too. Hold on, so Applejack and I are chasseurs? Jaegers? Are jaegers romantics? Wait, no, that's why all the changelings carry love canisters, so they can fake all this.
> It's a tragedy, Mali nodded
> But we're getting off topic! Fleur interjected. You said a fight, and a kiss. Actually, it's a little bit of both. Start taking notes, non fidèle étudiant

**Characters:**
* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]**
> **Analysis:** Twilight has massive "Asset Specificity" and natural Ambition. Her brain natively produces high levels of "Red Love" (dopamine/adrenaline) to solve problems. However, because she was constantly terrified of disappointing Celestia, her brain paired that ambition with severe stress, flooding her system with cortisol. In combat, that adrenaline is burned off through physical action. But when Twilight was just sitting in a library—or now, sitting at a logistics desk managing the Luna Nova supply chain—the adrenaline has nowhere to go. Her sympathetic nervous system is redlining, but she is physically stationary. This creates a toxic biological feedback loop: she is exhausted, but chemically unable to sleep.
>
> Refer to It's About Time - I haven't slept since the whole thing started
>
> Twilight realizes why she invented the sleep spell - a blast of sedatives.
> Celestia’s Equestria did not teach ponies how to process complex adult stress or ambition. If a pony was stressed, the Equestrian solution was just to "smile and braid manes" (the slow oxytocin drip). But as Fleur pointed out, a slow drip is useless against an active cortisol spiral.
>
> Fleur says a sleep spell might be nothing to you, but it would be highly inefficient for a unicorn chasseur
>
> She invented the sleep spell as something that doesn't resolve the stress; it just magically severs the nervous system’s consciousness to bypass the biological anxiety. It is the ultimate manifestation of Equestrian avoidance—turning off the machine rather than learning how to drive it safely.
> She officially retires the sleep spell as a personal crutch. She agrees to stop "turning off the machine" and finally learns how to drive it safely by actively engaging in the "Lion/Eagle" dynamic with Applejack in the tent. This is her graduating into adulthood.


**Codex:**
* **[[Combat Drugs]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Drugs are essentially molecular imposters

* **[[Chasseur Doctrine]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Fleur—who understands the Aquileian balance of Red and Pink Love—diagnoses that Twilight and Applejack are treating themselves like machines (Standardization) rather than living creatures with Asset Specificity.
>
> Fleur explains that intimacy is not a distraction from Harmonic Capitalism; it is the fuel that makes it sustainable. If they do not allow themselves the "Night" (adult passion and relief), they will eventually burn out and become top-down tyrants like Celestia or Chrysalis. They must practice Pink Love to safely wield the Red Love required to win the war.

### SCENE 13.11: Henri gives AJ a lesson in the Lion and the Eagle
> **Synopsis:** This should actually intertwine with Fleur/Mali's lesson, back and forth parallels
>
> Henri notices Applejack is brooding (because Fluttershy gave her The Stare). He assumes she is brooding because she wants to take her relationship with Twilight to the next level but is too Equestrian to do it.
>
> Good to see you, big boss. What you got for me? New tactics? Or a hoof wrestle? I could really use a hoof wrestle
> No, we need to talk about your relationship
> Uh, what? (genuine confusion)
>
> What do you like about Twilight?
> Applejack says some genuine things about Twilight as a pony, speaking from the heart
> Henri asks what about her looks (like locker room talk) she has wings and a horn (mirroring what Fleur just said)
> Applejack admits I do like her wings, but did you know she used to not have wings? She was a different pony back then too, but now it's like Fleur brought her back. I really missed seeing that spark in her eyes and how her muzzle scrunched up in excitement and her eyes lighting up when she pulled off a new spell
>
> Something about the knight descending on the griffoness's wing and she likes it
> The satisfaction of appealing to a griffoness and seeing her wings spread for you
> You are full of passion! She is an eagle waiting for her lion. You need to be a lion! Take the initiative!
> AJ cuts him off, disturbed, and says he sounds like a predator. Isn't this what changelings do, drain others' love? And warlords, and industrialists.
> Henri laughs jovially and says no, because they are poseurs. They have no special talents other than violence and hoarding money.
> Like a warlord who shoots hundreds of changelings with a machine gun and signs country scale drug deals? Applejack grumbled. I don't want to be a predator. Now you're saying to do that to Twilight? You want me to look at Twilight like prey, like something to be taken? What the buck?
> Henri walks it back and explains the Herzlander version of Lion and Eagle about birthright. The Herzlander lions are poseurs - they claim status without the asset specificity to back it and think they are entitled to the eagles.
> Then he explains the Aquileian meaning of Lion and Eagle.
> The Aquileian Lion is the one who has more passion and ambition in the moment. They have a surplus of passion they want to share.
> The Aquileian Eagle receives that passion with grace
> for the individual, you need to be capable of being both a lion (courageous) and an eagle (graceful) to truly be an honorable Aquileian griffon (citizen, since it applies to ponies too). But you are only expect to be at least one at a time and are flexible and respectful.
>
> Initiation does not equal violation
> Look, the drug trade is ugly. It's a predator's choice. Like vaporizing an officer. It is the cost of survival.
> But in Love, Ambition is a Gift, not a Theft.
> You have been taught that wanting is bad, but wanting is what makes you a griffon or a pony
>
> Henri reframes it for Applejack's "Element of Honesty"
> It is dishonest to want something and hide it. To be the Lion, embrace and be honest about your ambition, and say "I want you to be mine" is real honesty.
>
> You said you were taking your princess. That's a good start. There is hope for you :)
> Mali said it was an Aquileian story, but Twilight doesn't want to be princess anymore
> Henri says even better, you flip the meaning. Twilight isn't THE princess. She is YOUR princess (!)
> **Outcome:** Applejack learns that passion is not a sin. Friendship and romance can include passion (sexual ambition) without losing the friendship. Harmony and Ambition can coexist.

* **[[Fluttershy]]**
> **Analysis:** Ok now I'm really tempted to do flutter dash because Fluttershy is the perfect example

* **[[Rarity]]**
> **Analysis:** Applejack cites Rarity as an example of predatory flirting, which Henri has to distinguish
> Applejack makes the realization here

### SCENE 13.12: Applejack Initiates
> **Synopsis:** Twilight and Applejack meet each other at night and try out what they learned from their close Aquileian friends. It is silly and slow and sweet and embarrassing, but never really awkward because they trust each other and are in it together. They slowly unlock the passion that was previously nurtured away.
>
> Hey Twi, you said you don't want to be the princess, right? 
> Yeah. I don't want to be Celestia. There's so much I'd have to do that I just can't anymore, not after what we've been through
> But you have that sleek horn and those big, fluffy wings. You can't not be a princess. 
> Twilight sighed. I know...
> Applejack lifted Twilight's chin up with her hoof. So tell you what. Instead of being the princess, how about you be my princess? She asked, giving a smouldering look, gently stroking Twilight's mane with her hoof
> Twilight's eyes widened, and she perked up. Her wings twitched. She looked straight into Applejack as if mesmerized by the proposal. "What do I need to do?"
>
> Oh, nothing much. I just need a few snuggles at night, some nuzzles during the day, make sure the other generals see it, so they know who you belong to. And I could use a few kisses-
> Twilight darted forward, smushing her muzzle against Applejack's

**Threads:**

* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]**
> **Update:** Princess has a new meaning, going from a bad rep since Twilight is losing faith in Celestia and doesn't want to be her puppet or the next princess, into being Applejack's Princess

> They take a supply train to Maredin
>
> After having sex, AJ and Twi talk like "you're mine" in the deeply romantic sense whereas before that would've been interpreted as slavery or servitude

### SCENE 14.2: Temberik Mountains crystal negotiation round 2
> **Stakes:** Twilight and Fleur have invented a reliable magical rifle by studying/imitating/miniturizing the technology in Chrysalis's love harvester. Now they must secure crystals for mass production of love donators and rifles.
> **Synopsis:** The meeting takes place at the Diwan (reception hall/governing council) at Diyarbecolt
>
> More tenets of harmonic capitalism. The buffalo are proof of real economic integration and not false promises. Tzinacatl already in trade agreement. Rarity and Temberik workers are on the radio during the meeting. The isolationist Temberik are finally convinced.
>
> The (isolationist) Temberik are more receptive to economic integration now that the (belligerent) buffalo and (merchants of death) Tzinacatl are being integrated respectfully, alongside stories from relatives in Manehattan saying the night shift thestrals have real solidarity and rights now.
>
> Here is where they ask the Temberik to name the rifle.
>
> The Temberik council decides on the name "Luna Nova Rifle" after the small town at the base of the mountain (even though it's an Equestrian town, not a Temberik village).

### SCENE 14.3: Joint War Council Planning
> **Synopsis:** Still at the Diyarbecolt Diwan, they connect with all the new mayors and Marshal Blueblood to plan out the unified supply chain.
>
> Crystals from the Temberik mines go to Tall Tale, Manehattan and Baltimare, and a local facility in Diyarbecolt (Comet Shine insisted on making a local factory from scratch, owned by the Temberik council, to prove he is sincere. It's inefficient industrially but good politically. Also simpler supply chain and he says it's still important strategically in case Diyarbecolt ever gets cut off; they need to be self sufficient. Comet Shine envisions the mountain to never fall. This appeals to the Temberik "no friend but the mountains" idiom). Star Energy and two other companies will process the crystals using Twilight and Fleur's schematics to make the following components:
>
> Improved draining spell matrix
> Crystal for canisters to stabilize the magic
> Spellfire matrix for the rifle
> Friendship shields
> Crystal enhancers for unicorns (so they can teleport themselves, teleport supplies, and what else? Spellblast for CAS. Bomb bays are imprecise, trained unicorns aiming and using crystal enhancers are deadly efficient)
> Star Spade crystals for entrenchment (the OG star energy invention)
> Improved Spellblast for tanks
> Radio crystals
>
> Call to arms for miners to come to Diyarbecolt for the crystal mines, the Temberik are welcoming all
> Diyarbecolt is built up to be a self sufficient fortress since it's now the most important strategic resource since the Crystal Empire is cut off
>
> Volunteer ponypower assessement
> More ponies volunteered after the war started, representing the removal of the magic of friendship as ponies realize the changelings won't be peaceful, that's how it goes from 0.8% to 1.5%
> They also encourage local militia training, hoping that those training to defend their home may also join the army proper later, plus there is genuine threat (The local militias are from the decisions after Equestria is at more than 21% surrender progress)
>
> Cloudsdale was moved to the east coast and there’s multiple new weather factories under construction. Not with the elegance of the original but pure function
>
> Temberik crystals
> Tzinacatl provide rubber 
> Baltimare aluminum 
> Others steel
> Southwest oil
> They tally up factories and mines after seizing the tycoons' factories (Chrysalis's puppets. She spun up the industries to fund an invasion of Griffonia and believed in tycoon cowardice and pony softness, but was proven wrong by the element bearers and grassroots orgs, and now the seeds she planted have been commandeered against her. Nothing is a coincidence, it's all Chrysalis)
>
> Supply chain plans: use the rail line through Ponyville, with a contingency plan through the jungle with balsa rafts
> Can't use ships for transport because changeling submarines are terrifying
> Implicit suspicion: there is no representative of Canterlot in the meeting
>
> Luna Nova rifles go to Manehattan front first
> Love donators and canisters must also be distributed and red love donations go to the front
> Pink love for POWs, morale cakes, friendship shields

### SCENE 14.6: Upcoming Battle Plan
> **Synopsis:** Fluttershy gets Applejack to call a meeting. Pagala has been replaced with former Hivesmarschall Synovial, who is planning an all-out assault on Tall Tale
> Pagala was chaotic and fanatic, but Synovial is methodical. He's got old tank models (the ones who ran over Applejack's army in Vanhoover) and new crews. It's going to be a serious assault.
>
> Can we hold? Applejack asked the team
> The commanders discuss available weaponry, magical machine guns and such, logistics. Yes we can. We'll fortify the trench line and position our armor across from theirs. They won't stand a chance at break us
> Good, AJ says triumphantly
>
> Fluttershy sniffs and says it will be a disaster.
>
> It’ll be a massacre! So many changelings are going to die, they're just conscripts who don't want to be here. The river will run red for weeks!
> The whole room fell into silence
> Isn't there another way? Fluttershy asked
>
> It’s almost entirely a green army of new draftees. Even the tank crews are green. The veterans are all in the east and got new tanks, the old ones were sent here for new crews to die in
>
> Why would they do this? Surely they know how many changelings will die from a total wave assault?
>
> Synovial is in charge. He is Chrysalis’s cousin, and was once the Hivesmarschall until the attack on Canterlot failed and Chrysalis replaced him with Trimmel. Ever since then he’s been trying to regain his place
> He’ll want to take Tall Tale using any means possible, and achieve what Trimmel cannot. That’s why Chrysalis placed him here
> Their rivalry goes back even further. Synovial made the first tank doctrines and put them in practice as part of the attache in the Griffonian Reich, but Trimmel’s doctrine is more innovative and outdid him
> Henri ponders. So he’s desperate for a victory? asked Henri. And he’s an inflexible tank commander?
>
> That's what I've heard from the POWs, said Fluttershy
>
> If the crews are green, we can try to capture their old tanks instead of destroying them. Can we use them?
> Comet Shine says we can retrofit them with better engines and firepower. The armor will be adequate enough
> Henri unveils elastic defense: a coordinated retreat north of the city to lure the enemy in, then a backbuck blow with their limited spellblast prototypes and air support to wipe out the entrance and cut off all the troops
> Then we'll compel them to surrender and get their old tanks intact for retrofit
> The commanders only agree because they want the tanks, not because they care about the changelings, but Fluttershy is thankful
> She says if we rehabilitate them, they can donate magic for the war
>
> Applejack says let them cross and try to encircle us but hold the river at all costs at all the other points
> He’ll stop attacking and redirect his troops to the valley and go for the encirclement. Just dig in and hold out
>
> What? Henri squawked. We'll be cut off! The biggest red flag! 
> You said we have the greatest defensive line in the world. You can find a way to hold
> But why are we letting them cross? It'll be easier to destroy them while they're crossing
>
> We will cut them off, accept their surrender, and capture their old tanks. We’ll use their own weapons against them, the very ones that rolled over us at the start of the war. And as a bonus, we can show these changelings some mercy. It's just a bonus. The main objective is to capture their tanks. We have the best defensive line in the world, let's use it.
>
> Dig more trenches for an elastic defense with star spades
> Use the potassium rich soil (due to earth pony magic releasing potassium and it's the best for cloud condensation nuclei) in power plants and boilers to make rapid clouds (beautiful purple flames)
> Deploy the clouds once they cross to keep the tanks down, but not over the river
> We'll cut them off with our tanks along the river, and rain over the rest of the area
> The shallow trenches they dug will fill with water and the old tanks will get stuck

**Characters:**
* **[[Réni Ducep]]**
> **Analysis:** He is in a standard issue Wonderbolts plane with Minette, ready to fly in formation with the Wonderbolts, take out the statthalters, and rescue the changeling conscripts from tyranny


**Threads:**

* **[[Camp Fluttershy]]** (Positive)

**Themes:**

> **Note:** Henri talks about taking out the corrupt lords on the battlefield to free the conscripted serfs (he does not say the Republic did it but also does not mention Discret)
> The changeling conscripts are the peasants of the Peripherie, Henri says. We will liberate them

### SCENE 14.7: 3rd Battle of Tall Tale
> **Synopsis:** Applejack is a leader/administrator/negotiator on a strategic level, but on the battlefield, she doesn't know what she's doing and doesn't try to be something she's not. Henri is always Applejack's tactical commander. Applejack is a frontline recon radiomare, and being at the tip of the spear for inspiration. Twilight is the firepower and shield at the front.
>
> Henri is the tactical genius 
> He comes up with elastic defense north of the city for Synovial to try to encircle 
> Wide gap so the troops flood in, fill trenches with extra supplies and cover to dig in
> Then the tanks roll across the gap, turret obliterates enemy armor, heavy machine guns take out troops in trenches
>
> Tall Tale is a big front. They will need sleep rotation to perform the backhoof blow, otherwise they need blitz-essenz
>
> It's the opposite of the Vanhoover opening of the war. Back then, Trimmel blew up the pitiful Equestrian tanks and machine guns wiped out ponies in trenches. Applejack is rolling over the changeling entrenchments and vaporizing their tanks.
>
> In the third battle of Tall Tale (1st was chapter 1 and 2nd was chapter 4), Synovial commands an army of green, young draftees to do a mass charge across the whole front. He also commands his typical division of outdated tanks. Applejack intentionally opens up a hole in their fortified line, faked as a retreat, baiting Synovial into the classic armored spearhead breakthrough and encirclement of the Tall Tale front line. Synovial halts the mass charge order and funnels the infantry through the breakthrough to encircle the defensive line and cut off the ponies on the front. This is Synovial's pioneering tactic of maneuver warfare debuted 30 years ago in the Republican counter revolution. Synovial is very proud of his pioneering tactic and wants the glory of a clean encirclement. Then Applejack applies Trimmel's tactic of a Backhoof Blow (Backhand Blow), in which she reveals their new Spellblast tanks and cuts off Synovial's spearhead after a huge amount of green infantry has flooded the breakthrough. The outdated tanks also have green crews. Applejack encourages surrenders and a massacre is avoided. Synovial retreats in shambles.
>
> After encirclement of the greens, Fluttershy's kind voice plays over a speaker, speaking in native changeling (with clicks), "Please don't resist. Surrender and you will be safe."
> This is in contrast to Trimmel's recording saying "Surrender and your lives will be spared. Resist and you will be eliminated."
> For conscripts who are medically incapable of surrender due to combat drugs, they use a sleep spell

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** She is traumatized being a tactical commander from the opening battle of the war, even though it wasn't her fault that she was slotted in. She leaves tactics to the professionals. She is in the lead tank.
>
> There's also an ironic air of invincibility since Twilight would never let her die.

* **[[Mali]]**
> **Analysis:** Mali is part of Applejack's lead tank crew alongside Twilght and Tally Mark.

* **[[Fluttershy]]**
> **Analysis:** After encirclement of the greens, Fluttershy's kind voice plays over a speaker, speaking in native changeling (with clicks), "Please don't resist. Surrender and you will be safe."
> This is in contrast to Trimmel's recording saying "Surrender and your lives will be spared. Resist and you will be eliminated."

* **[[Synovial]]**
> **Analysis:** Synovial blew his part of the plan for personal glory and an attempt to rehabilitate his image as the original Hivemarshall, if he managed to break Tall Tale and the infamous Lioness with his "diversion army".
>
> He is depicted as a glory seeker, outdated and out of touch.
>
> But later, Trimmel suggests the reality is Synovial wanted the old ways, when war was honorable and had rules.

* **[[Tally Mark]]**
> **Analysis:** Tally is part of Applejack's lead tank crew alongside Twilight and Mali. This is because Tally and Mali were Applejack's trenchmates in the 1st Battle of Tall Tale. She has a crystal enhancer and Twilight taught her combat magic, so now she can do everything that Twilight did back in the 2nd Battle of Tall Tale.


**Threads:**
* **[[Magical Combined Arms]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Applejack uses deceit via elastic defense to bait Synovial before unveiling tanks and magical CAS to cut off his troops. Rainbow Dash observed Trimmel do this maneuver during the opening battle of the war, which is how Trimmel encircled and shattered the naive Equestrian army.


**Codex:**
* **[[Applejack's Radio]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** You're still using that old thing?
>
> New parts inside, courtesy of my wonderful marefriend, but I keep the outside as a reminder
>
> This was the radio they gave me when I failed. I ain't putting it down until we win.

>
> This whole attack on Tall Tale from Synovial, it wasn’t meant to be a victory. He was planning on just sending wave after wave of green draftees at our lines to distract us so that we can’t redeploy to the south
> This Tall Tale battle was supposed to last a month, remember the plans Fluttershy gave? Synovial saw the opening and got greedy, so we ended the assault and got their equipment in a day (or 2?)

> Chrysalis is in Canterlot, sitting on Celestia's throne
> Sweet Apple Acres is burning

## CHAPTER 16: Combined Arms
> **Chapter Note:** The iconic ending line "Friendship is Combined Arms." The setting is Ponyville, at the Castle of Friendship, the canonical setting of Friendship is Magic. It's also a subversion, because someone glancing at the chapter titles would naturally assume that "Combined Arms" is a combat chapter. Instead, it's THE friendship chapter! And it fits the HOI4 term theme!
> **Chapter Note:** There won't be any concept of "Republic" or "President" at this stage. Only a de facto military governor (not even de jure).
> **Chapter Note:** Celestia is resigned and deferential in this chapter
> **Chapter Note:** Applejack reconciling with her parents' industrial path gives her clarity. She isn't an insecure farmpony pretending to be a general. She has the moral grounding that says they are wielding modern weapons with responsibility. Applejack doesn't have to look to Celestia for moral authority anymore because she can now get that from her parents.
>
> Applejack has merged the "evil" of industry with the "purity" of the farm, proving that Celestia’s 1,000-year-old Nursery thesis is wrong. Celestia steps back not out of respect, but because she simply does not know how to operate in a world where a pony can hold a gun and still have a pure soul.
> **Chapter Note:** Need a definitive name for the jungle where the Daring Do books take place, which is explicitly not the Tzinacatl jungle
>
> And what should be Twilight Velvet's takeaway and how does she validate Twilight leaving the nursery? Or maybe she doesn't validate Twilight but she validates Rainbow Dash.
> **Chapter Note:** Ponyville one of the last settlements, from Granny Smith
> On the edge of the Everfree Forest 
> That's why Ponyville ponies are ambitious and why the show is there!
> **Chapter Note:** Pinkie resolves to cheer up Fluttershy and brings Tally Mark to abandoned Sugarcube Corner where they bake cakes for everyone
> Who else goes?

### SCENE 16.1: Applejack reunites with her parents at the remains of Sweet Apple Acres
> **Synopsis:** Applejack is overlooking the destroyed Sweet Apple Acres alone (Twilight is preparing the castle for the meeting and organizing everyone, but Applejack asked to leave for some time to herself)
>
> Apple Bloom and their parents arrive; they are here for the war council meeting since they are in charge of industry
>
> They say they are so proud of her
>
> Applejack says she understands why they left the farm now
> They made hard decisions to help the world
> They are just one pair of the many quiet ponies who actually run the world
>
> Applejack says after the war is over, can we rebuild the farm, and can you read me stories?
> They say we won't stop running the factory until the world is safe, but we'll make time to come to the farm

**Threads:**
* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
> **Update:** Everything that opposes Chrysalis was partly caused by her own mistakes/cynicism
> Chrysalis’s shell companies looked for ponies with high "Resentment" and high "Ambition."
>
>     Target: Bright Mac & Pear Butter. (Resentful of the Feud, Ambitious for a new life).
>
> The Error: Chrysalis assumed Resentment = Cruelty. She thought they would want to punish the world.
>
> The Reality: They possessed Resentment + Empathy. They wanted to feed the world so others wouldn't suffer like they did. They "slipped through the cracks" because Chrysalis couldn't conceive of a "Benevolent Industrialist."

* **[[Stagnation's Redemption]]**
> **Update:** Applejack wants to be a filly again, but her parents encourage her to be a grown up. This is the start of Applejack's path of accepting her role as a permanent leader

* **[[The Meaning of Leadership]]**
> **Update:** Applejack wants to go back to the farm and not be a leader after the war is over


**Codex:**
* **[[The Predator's Dilemma (Coltbert's Paper)]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** The SAA leaderboard was not a Skyfall rat race. It was Aquileian ego capitalism, turning the feud into a positive some game
>
> Bright Mac and Pear Butter rely entirely on the Asset Specificity of the Earth Ponies. They do not automate the harvest; they provide the logistical backend (canning, shipping, international trade) that allows the Earth Ponies to maximize their own latent magic. SAA is the infrastructure; the families are the engine.

* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Canon FiM represents the Stagnation of Harmony, and Applejack's parents aren't there. They didn't die. They grew up and left the nursery. When the reality of war comes, Applejack grows up too. She finds her parents and now understands them.
>
> They didn't tell her for 20 years because Applejack is an Element of Harmony in Celestia's court, and they don't want Applejack to have to lie or hide the truth of their operations, trading to the Griffonian Republic and funding a war against tyranny
> They visited the farm monthly not to pretend they are still humble farmers, but because carrying the weight of the world is exhausting. That's why no business talk during their visits, just farmpony life and the silent pact not to talk about Manehattan
> Applejack says she's sorry she ever gave them a hard time when they visited

* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** They grew up after 930 ALB, in the peak of Celestia’s "Nursery." They grew up in absolute safety. And they were bored to death. They listened to Granny’s stories of the "Wild West" and viewed them as literal "Tall Tales"—quaint myths from a bygone era. They craved the adult agency their parents had, but Celestia’s system denied it to them. All they had was the petty Apple-Pear family feud because there was no other outlet for the ambitious earth pony clans.
>
> Canon FiM represents the Stagnation of Harmony, and Applejack's parents aren't there. They didn't die. They grew up and left the nursery. When the reality of war comes, Applejack grows up too. She finds her parents and now understands them.


---
### SCENE 16.2: Twilight reunites with her parents
> **Synopsis:** Twilight is afraid they think she's a monster
> Night Light says he was a supressed scientist for 40 years and is proud of Twilight
>
> He had access to the formulas for combat magic, advanced thermodynamics, and early magical engineering. He understood the math.
>
> Rainbow Dash read the books and thought war was a glorious, individualistic sport (leading to her Atlas Complex). Twilight read the books and subconsciously internalized the idea that geopolitical conflicts are solved by a single, highly competent hero dropping in, grabbing the artifact, and leaving. Celestia simply offered him a comfortable, highly respected bureaucratic position, provided he never built prototypes or published his findings. He accepted the "Golden Handcuffs" because he believed Celestia was right: the ponies were not ready for the responsibility of this technology. He chose the safety of his family over the progress of his species.
>
> When Night Light tells Twilight he is proud of her, the emotional weight comes from the fact that Twilight actually built the things he was only allowed to read about.
>
> Rainbow Dash mentions the fakes, how the Tzinacatl warped their own culture to make money off the Daring Do legend
> Twilight Velvet says she knows, but didn't say anything. She acknowledges the flaw of their "walled garden" society and is proud of Twilight and her friends for actually connecting with changelings (Fluttershy), buffalo, and thestrals
> Rainbow asks why didn't you or anypony else write stories about the real problems of the world?
> Twilight Velvet admits "because I don't know what the real world is like. You have to write that story, Rainbow Dash"
>
> The family worries about the rest of their family trapped in the Crystal City

**Characters:**
* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** She isn't a naive fangirl; she used Daring Do as an idealized coping mechanism. She knows the real world is messy, but she wants to believe in the clear-cut, triumphant heroism of Twilight Velvet's novels.
>
> Rainbow Dash is hit with the scary truth that she has outgrown her childhood storybook hero (even knowing it was fictional, she embraced the spirit) and now is the one who has to write the story


**Codex:**
* **[[The Battle of Mount Aris]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Daring Do connection? Being a superhero in mount aris, facing reality

* **[[Rebuilding Ain Trotgourait]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** When Twilight arrives in the ruins of Ain Trotgourait (1007 ALB), she looks at the starving Zebras and applies her Canterlot Archive logic. She thinks: "They lack water and medicine. I have Alicorn magic. I will provide water and medicine." Because she learned about warlords from history books and her mother's novels, she treats the Storm King's devastation as a "completed chapter." She fails to realize that the geopolitical vacuum still exists. She builds glass hospitals and water filtration systems, completely ignoring the fact that without an armed, localized militia (Asset Specificity/Self-Determination), the next warlord (Colthage/Chiropterra) will simply walk in and take the infrastructure. Twilight burns out because she is trying to solve a macroeconomic supply-chain crisis using the logic of a fairy-tale savior. It is only when she meets Fleur Bloom and Comet Shine that she realizes infrastructure must be backed by industry and local ownership, not just Alicorn charity.

* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** This is the end of the nursery
> Celestia still thinks Twilight is a child who needs protection, but her actual parents view her as an adult who has surpased them
>
> Twilight says how could I have access to all the history books and not understand anything?
> Night Light says we all read the same books and all did not have the same experience as what we are experiencing now
>
> The Canterlot auditors, raised on the same sanitized history as Twilight, were only trained to look for "Monsters." When Rockfeller opened an oil derrick, he didn't look like a Griffon Warlord; he looked like an enthusiastic pony providing a service. The auditors lacked the macroeconomic vocabulary to recognize that wage-suppression and monopolistic extraction are just bloodless forms of feudalism.
>
> Chrysalis recognized that the Equestrian immune system was designed to fight dragons, not bankers. By using Skyfall shell companies to inject capital into these ambitious ponies, she bypassed the Friendship Seminars entirely. The tycoons passed the seminars because they genuinely believed they were "helping Equestria modernize," completely blind to the fact that they were acting as Chrysalis’s economic vanguard.


---
### SCENE 16.3: Reunion, Applejack sends a letter to the princesses
> **Synopsis:** Rarity, Spike, and Apple Bloom show up
>
> AJ asks where are Celestia and Luna?
> Rarity says they didn't come because the front is dangerous and they have to move the sun and the moon.
>
> AJ looks at Canterlot in the distance. She has Spike send the letter, the 2nd invitation, urgent. She hopes she can convince the princesses to somehow lend at least some magical assistance for the attack. Maybe a small part of her thinks Celestia can still just wave her horn and make the occupiers disappear. It's Celestia's home, after all (except in reality, it's Celestia's 1000 year prison)
>
> Rarity says darling, I really don't think the princess is in the right state to help
> Applejack says we can try to show her it ain't hopeless. That we're organized, we can win this, we can save Equestria. Maybe she'll wake up.
>
> I want the princess back.
>
> (I could be my parents' little filly again, now that I've forgiven them)
>
> Applejack asks Spike to send a letter with the time of the meeting at Twilight's Castle, asking them to please come because she needs them
>
> The letter Spike sends needs to thread the needle between "General giving orders" and "Daughter asking for Mom."
>
> Drafting the Letter (revise so it's not as threatening to Celestia's sovereignty, but rather implied!):
>
> Princess,
>
> Rarity says you can't come because it's unsafe. You're right. It is unsafe. My home is ash. My friends are tired.
>
> At 4 PM, we will be planning the liberation of Canterlot. We will be making decisions that will change this country forever. If you aren't in the room, I have to make them alone. And if I make them alone, I am the one leading Equestria, not you.
>
> I don't want to be the Princess. I want to be your loyal subject. But a subject needs a ruler who shows up.
>
> Please. Come to Ponyville. Don't make me do this by myself.
>
> - General Applejack, The Element of Honesty
>
> The Letter: The ultimatum that is secretly a cry for help.
>
> Why this works: It explicitly states the stakes: "If you aren't here, I am in charge." It forces Celestia to realize that her absence creates the Republic.
>
> To reinforce why the Princesses claim it's "too dangerous," remember the proximity.
>
> Ponyville is literally in the shadow of the enemy fortress. By demanding they come there, AJ is demanding they look the enemy in the eye.

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** Applejack is still a reluctant leader. If she can convince Celestia that there is hope, maybe she won't have to be the end all be all anymore. Carrying a nation on one's shoulders is hard for a mortal farmer. She wants to be her parents' little filly again.
>
> She WANTS the princesses to be there for the meeting even after being turned down the first time. Deep down, she wants both validation and an adult to look up to. In practice, she is the adult in the room, but in spirit, if Applejack has to say the quiet part out loud, the she is no longer "My Little Pony" then the mental struggle and weight skyrockets.
>
> If Applejack just orders them around, she is a "Girlboss General." But if she orders them around because she is secretly terrified of being the only adult in the room, she becomes a tragic hero. She is screaming for help, but she has to disguise it as a command because she knows they won't listen to a plea.

### SCENE 16.4: Rarity meets the side cast
> **Synopsis:** Rarity meets Henri. They should get along really well because they are both cultured. Maybe some flirting?
> Rarity busts out some Aquileian
> Applejack and Twilight say they didn't know Rarity knew Aquileian, and Rarity says of course, where do you think haute couture comes from? It's also the language of love, Rarity says, winking at Henri
> Rarity says Fleur could use a makeover, and Fleur is indignant. She is plain like Twilight and likes it that way

**Characters:**
* **[[Rarity]]**
> **Analysis:** Applejack and Twilight say they didn't know Rarity knew Aquileian, and Rarity says of course, where do you think haute couture comes from? It's also the language of love, Rarity says, winking at Henri
> This explains why Rarity is an "immoral" flirt in canon!!
> That's where she learned to take advantage of Spike and various other stallions, which is kind of taking advantage of them! It's a poor interpretation of Aquileian FJA hookup culture
> Even more specific, she learned it from the thestral parloirs
> So she immediately clicks with Henri the big blue bombastic griffon, to Applejack and Twilight's shock
>
> Henri would likely view Rarity not as a "damsel," but as a highly skilled tactical player in the game of social dominance. She understands that Posture (how you present yourself) creates reality.
>
> The Posture: She adopts the stance of the "Eagle" (graceful, delicate, in need of assistance).
>
> The Intent: She creates a situation where the stallion wants to serve her. She is actually the "Lion" (initiating the desire and controlling the outcome), but she masks it with the aesthetic of the "Eagle."
>
> Twilight is still annoyed that Rarity did this to Spike
> Henri says that is like asymmetric warfare


---
### SCENE 16.5: Rarity notices Twilight and Applejack
> **Synopsis:** Rarity says the two of you are too cute together
>
> Henri interjects and says when they got together they had a sleepover and braided each other's manes. We had to give the two lovestruck fillies a crash course in L'Art de Vivre. They were so... innocent
> Twilight and Applejack blush furiously, expecting Rarity to be scandalized
> Rarity giggles and says that's to be expected
> Twilight and Applejack's jaws drop. But Rarity, you're miss prissy! How could you of all ponies know...
> Mali says you've been to a parloir? Rarity says yes, she designed them, and the Aquileian glamour is what she was obssessed with
> Mali gently explains the institution to Twilight and Applejack, which reveals how Mali knows how to pleasure a mare so well and taught Twilight
> They stare at Rarity wide-eyed and Applejack asks Rarity if she was ever a client
> Rarity says a lady never tells
> So is there where you learned to bat your eyelashes at Spike and some stallions?
> Rarity makes some excuses
> Mali and Henri chastise Rarity for abusing the spirit of Aquileia
> Fleur says her mentor Coltbert set them up
> Rarity says you know THE Coltbert personally?
> Fleur is smug and says she was his student at the University of Pridea
>
> Twilight says she always thought parloirs were shady places. Now after Mali, Fleur and Henri taught them about passion, she can understand they are places for the magic of friendship. No wonder Mali is the better princess of friendship, but that's okay now

* **[[Spike]]**

> Rarity makes some excuses
> Mali and Henri chastise Rarity for abusing the spirit of Aquileia

> **Update:** Rarity saw back in season 2
>
> They realize the passion that Henri, Fleur and Mali taught them is standard in the real world and not some Aquileian craziness. Rarity of all ponies is validating their Aquileian friends!
