### SCENE 18.13: Applejack decides to send Trimmel to the POW camp in Tall Tale
> **Synopsis:** It's surreal hearing the voice on the radio. Back when I was hiding in a muddy trench, all I could picture was a monster. But your message was "Surrender, and you will be spared. That is a promise." I'm offering you the same.
>
> VOPS said you would fold
> But you're a lion
> How could you grant me mercy?
>
> Applejack slumped her shoulders looking down at her chipped hooves. "I reckon it's sad you thought I would execute you. I remember the exact words. You said Surrender, and you will be spared. That is a promise." She met his dull eyes with confusion. "Why attach your name to it? Why announce that you are the monster?"
>
> A nameless threat is just terror. I earned my rank, I hold myself to it
> Something about Statthalters
>
> I didn't sign up to be the monster.
>
> Your ponies look up to you. Don't make them look away.

**Characters:**
* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** Applejack expects Rainbow Dash to chew her out. Rainbow just shrugs and takes Applejack's word for it that Trimmel deserves to go to the camp. She says she saw Trimmel step out of his tank during the surrender to try and save his men (who didn't listen). That was loyalty.

* **[[Trimmel]]**
> **Analysis:** Trimmel is shocked that he's not going to be executed and is going to the POW camp instead. He surrendered alive, despite wholly expecting an execution. He is rational to a fault, and ultimately selfless. He was willing to take his execution to save his men (and many of his men chose to die for the Queen who abandoned them instead)


**Threads:**
* **[[Henri's Distaste of Trimmel]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** Henri is baffled that Applejack is letting THE changeling hivesmarshall live. The one whose voice was on the loudspeakers. Henri is actually fuming when Applejack says Trimmel is a decent bloke. Henri can't see past the voice of the speaker, thinking he is the ultimate poser and the face of the Herzland imitators. Applejack points out that Trimmel spoke in Equestrian. Henri is not pleased by the quip.


**Codex:**
* **[[Changeling Emotion Sense]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** When Applejack says Trimmel is going to the POW camp instead of execution, he says he can't believe she's not lying, but his emotion sense says so.

* **[[Applejack's Radio]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** The voice on the radio

* **[[AJ The Collaborator]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** The premise is that Applejack doesn't want the burden of leadership still; she wants Celestia to "wake up".
>
> Trimmel mentions that Synovial called Applejack "The Lioness of Tall Tale".
>
> Trimmel points out a VOPS profile on Applejack saying she will collaborate to keep her family safe. This will remind Applejack about the nightmare she had about the very topic.
>
> Trimmel should give some parting advice to Applejack which solidifies Applejack's decision to accept Rarity's uniform and replace "The Element of Honesty" with "The Lioness of Tall Tale" to show how she is permanently accepting her station by adopting the enemy's name for her instead of Celestia's name for her.


---
### SCENE 18.14: Applejack Flashbacks
> **Synopsis:** Explicit flashbacks to her backstory between canon FiM and the start of TLTT detailed here
>
> 1. Hiding behind Twilight's shield facing Tempest Shadow and the Warlords
> 2. Retreating to the farm
> 3. The Dotted Line Report, Applejack enlisting in the army, Luna asking her to be a general and Applejack refusing
> 4. Mastering the machine gun
> 5. Testing the friendship shield coming from the crystal empire
> 6. All the other volunteers looking up to her, Luna against asking to promote Applejack and she accepts this time
> 7. The Ju 87 Stuka Jericho Trumpet, Applejack seeing the front collapse
> 8. Running instead of surrendering, tearing off her uniform and covering herself in mud, hearing the radio address "This is Hivesmarshall Trimmel..." (Direct lead in to the very first line of chapter 1)
>
> Then return to the present day, with Applejack looking out at the truck that is taking Trimmel to Tall Tale just about to leave her line of sight
> She stares at her crystal in the ground as a mirror, at herself wearing the standard infantry uniform with a general's star slapped on, off center and lopsided. She tries to correct it with her hoof and the star falls off
>
> Applejack goes to Rarity to accept the new uniform, a proper general's uniform

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**

---
### SCENE 18.15: Applejack takes Rarity's uniform
> **Synopsis:** By accepting Rarity's new uniform, Applejack is fully embracing her role after Trimmel's talk
>
> General Applejack
> The Element of Honesty
>
> Can you change the title underneath?
> Why of course, darling. What do you want stitched?
> Applejack looks out the tent at the truck departing to the river to catch a raft. She looked at Mount Canterhorn in the distance, then back to Rarity.
>
> "The Lioness of Tall Tale."

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** By accepting Rarity's new uniform, Applejack is fully embracing her role after Trimmel's talk. She has decided she is not going back to being the farmpony. She has to continue being the General, and now the Lioness, because Celestia will not do what is necessary for Equestria.
>
> Being the monster during wartime is the easy part. Being a leader during peace is the part she is accepting the burden of now, to ensure Equestria is never preyed on the way VOPS described it again
>
> Applejack permanently sheds the "Element of Honesty" (a title bestowed by Celestia for maintaining the Nursery) and adopts a title forged in the crucible of modern, industrialized survival.


**Threads:**
* **[[Stagnation's Redemption]]**
> **Update:** Going back to the farm would be like being a filly again. Here, by taking the uniform, Applejack is permanently stepping into her leadership role. After the Trimmel talk, Applejack believes going back to the farm (stagnation) would be irresponsible and a morally bankrupt, isolationist, child-like choice.

* **[[The Meaning of Leadership]]**
> **Update:** The decision to become the leader instead of run from it


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** Applejack is now honestly presenting herself as the general, being honest about her authority and responsibility

* **[[Bottom Up > Top Down]]**
> **Commentary:** By accepting the title "The Lioness of Tall Tale," Applejack formally transitions from a Mascot to a Sovereign.
>
>     "The Element of Honesty" was a title bestowed Top-Down by Celestia, representing passive innocence.
>
>     "The Lioness of Tall Tale" is a title earned Bottom-Up, bestowed by her enemy (Synovial) and validated by her peers (Henri, Twilight, the FJA volunteers). It represents active, lethal responsibility.

## CHAPTER 19: Entrenchment
> **Chapter Note:** New angle to explore: how about if Flurry Heart didn't singlehandledly filter the crystal heart, but she gave a pinkie promise that the shield would not fall, and therefore the crystal ponies believed they would not fall.
>
> For the crystal shield, I wonder if ordinary crystal ponies, who I have established have emotion sense like changelings, can help with the emotional filtering to the heart, and Flurry Heart is just one of many teachers who was the first one that learned from Thorax. This lines up with the bottom up over top down theme, since instead of one alicorn doing it, the process is democratized. But Flurry Heart is the one who befriended the changeling jaeger (Thorax) who taught them how to filter emotion sense, so her core arc still holds
> **Chapter Note:** Aquileian volunteers went to yakistown after Mount Aris and defeated the changeling infiltrators helping Prince Bradford 
> Then Statthalters attack Yakistown alongside the Crystal Empire and some yaks make it to Crystal City 
> Their magic is agriculture in the snow, which helps the siege stay alive with calories while the crystal ponies keep up the shield

### SCENE 19.1: Twilight flashbacks
> **Synopsis:** The chapter opens up with a sequence of Twilight flashbacks to prewar, detailing her story explicitly between the break from canon FiM and the start of TLTT
>
> 1. Silverstream says she has to go home and Queen Novo is asking for help
> 2. Rainbow Dash doesn't show up to teach class. Twilight finds out Rainbow went with Silverstream and Zecora to Mount Aris
> 3. Twilight announces an unplanned recess, school will be off for a few days. Twilight, Applejack, Rarity, Fluttershy, Pinkie Pie, Starlight and Spike go to Mount Aris to retrieve Rainbow Dash
> 4. Twilight seeing the warlords, asks her friends to come with her to talk to Storm King (Queen Novo says it's suicide and Crack Lightning yells at her) her friends overwhelmingly tell Twilight to stay put, especially Spike
> 5. Applejack approaches Twilight afterwards and says she'll go with Twilight
> 6. The full scene from Twilight's point of view (with Applejack silent and cowering) where Twilight says she wants to speak to the Storm King. The warlords don't speak Equestrian and try shooting at her shield until eventually Tempest Shadow shows up and says "we don't negotiate, go away"
> Why are you doing this? You're a pony, just like me
> I'm nothing like you
> Open up your eyes, give up your sweet fantasy land. It's time to grow up and get wise (plus other lyrics from that song)
>
> 7. From Rainbow Dash's point of view, shooting down airships from a WW1 canvas biplane, seeing Reni and Minette fly invincibly, and witnessing Crack Lightning getting shot down and dying
>
> 8. Back to Twilight's point of view, she pulls Tempest Shadow out of the rubble and gives her a second chance
> 9. All of their friends are revolted by Tempest Shadow. Rarity and Rainbow Dash are really rude, Fluttershy is scared, even Spike and Applejack who are normally the grounded figures want Twilight to leave Tempest Shadow behind since she's a warlord. Only Pinkie Pie still has unwavering hope and joy, and Starlight as a former villain understands. The rest of them turn away, while Pinkie cheers up Twilight. This is where Tempest Shadow says her real name is Fizzlepop Berrytwist and Pinkie says it's awesome
>
> 10. The scene where Celestia yells at Twilight for going to Mount Aris and bringing a warlord home, saying Tempest Shadow is not welcome in Equestria. Twilight says if Celestia is banishing Fizzlepop, then Twilight will go with her to rebuild Ain Trotgourait and Celestia can't stop her. Twilight believes Celestia's methodology of top down harmony can work, even though Celestia herself does not believe it. Twilight is deeply hurt when Celestia says "we can't police the world" and condemns the zebras to their fate.
>
> 11. Twilight asks Spike to come with her, Starlight, and Pinkie Pie to rebuild Ain Trotgourait. Spike tries really hard to stop Twilight, being the reasonable one, but Twilight won't budge. Spike says Twilight can go, and he'll go to the Crystal Empire to help out Shining Armor and Cadance instead. Spike says Fizzlepop can keep track of Twilight's checklists because she's good at that, being the quartermaster of a warlord army.
>
> Ending on this note with Twilight and Spike's separation is the logical bridge to the next scene, which is Spike and Flurry Heart meeting Thorax, and the scene after that, which is present day with Twilight and Spike's reunion since the start of the war, since the siege if relieved

---
### SCENE 19.2: The Times they are a Changeling (Featuring Flurry Heart)
> **Synopsis:** Flurry's uncontrollable magic is initially just baby magic, which Sunburst calms down via a spell of his invention at the end of "The Crystalling" (canon episode), but then Flurry's magic still sometimes gets uncontrolled and Sunburst is still trying to figure out why. Turns out it is tied to fears of her being "The little menace" and being able to detect the crystal ponies being scared of her because of it because she has crystal pony emotion sense from the crystal heart
>
> Living in the Crystal Empire is torture for her. Crystal Ponies amplify love and light, but they also amplify anxiety. Every time a pony looks at her and thinks, "Oh no, don't explode," Flurry feels that fear. It scares her, which triggers her magic, which scares them more.
>
> When Flurry Heart is 5 (1008 ALB), Cadance reads her a bedtime story about jaegers.
>
> Cadance read her a bedtime story from Nova Griffonia about "yay-gers" protecting their home from monsters. (Readers can deduce that it's the changeling jaeger story passed down from Harmonist changelings, translated from native changeling to Herzlander, and then to Equestrian)
>
> Need to make the bed time story more elegant and parallel to the existing story, but not too obvious since in-universe no one connects it with the changeling hives
>
> The next morning she flares up from some crystal ponies being scared of her so she teleports out into the frozen north on her own. She practices her magic alone, outside the Crystal City. She is frustrated.
>
> Then she detects strong negative emotions (she has a very powerful emotion sense as an alicorn/crystal pony hybrid). Being a naive 5 year old child, she goes after the monster like a brave jaeger and encounters a changeling. She's about to blast him but he gets terrified; she feels it and stops, realizing he is a sentient creature (and bigger than her).
>
> It turns out the creature is Thorax, who is running for his life after VOPS killed all his friends. 
>
> What proceeds is the plot of the season 6 canon episode "The Times They Are a Changeling". Thorax doesn't want to meet Flurry Heart's parents right away since they rejected his friendly letter years ago, so Flurry Heart introduces Thorax to Spike.
>
> Spike possesses a profound, visceral understanding of what Thorax is experiencing. When Flurry Heart (operating on pure, unfiltered Emotion Sense) detects Thorax’s terror in the frozen wastes, she identifies that he is not a predator in that moment. However, as a five-year-old, she lacks the sociological vocabulary to defend him against a militarized state.
>
> Spike provides the translation. He looks at Thorax—a Jaeger fleeing Chrysalis’s purge of the Harmonist board game clubs—and recognizes the exact same biological terror he felt during his own rampages (Secret of my Excess). Spike can articulate to the adults: “He is not evil; he is trapped in a biological and chemical feedback loop. I know because I was trapped in one too. If I can be cured by Grace, so can he.” Spike becomes the ideological defense attorney for the Changeling race, utilizing his own trauma as the legal precedent for rehabilitation.
>
> When Thorax and Flurry reach the palace around evening, Cadance is so relieved that Flurry is safe. The love from the Princess of Love for her daughter is overwhelming and Thorax loses his disguise and starts hissing, but Spike and Flurry Heart protect him
>
> Spike uses his "Brave and Glorious" social capital to vouch for Thorax, which cuts through his big brother Shining Armor's terror of changelings
>
> Thorax trains Flurry Heart how to control her emotion sense like a jaeger and trains her as her Buncle. He teaches her not to not let the negative emotions overwhelm her.
>
> That leads to her special talent later. When Twilight and Applejack relieve the siege of Crystal City, they will find out that Flurry Heart got her cutie mark during the siege.
>
> The funny part is that Cadance read her a story about jaegers, and she actually met a "jaeger", Thorax. Maybe the bedtime story should actually talk about some allegory to the training jaegers did to prepare themselves for the hunt that they needed to do to protect their hives.

**Characters:**
* **[[Thorax]]**
* **[[Flurry Heart]]**
* **[[Spike]]**

**Codex:**
* **[[Pre-Industrial Changeling Society]]** (History)
> **Note:** The original jaeger story

* **[[Changeling Emotion Sense]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Flurry Heart's magic is like changeling emotion sense, because she inherited crystal pony biology due to all the magic flowing through Cadance?


---
### SCENE 19.3: Crystal City Siege
> **Synopsis:** No Mom, I'm not going! Flurry sobbed. What is the Princess of Love without her family?!
>
> Flurry Heart in my story and Shining Armor stay with Cadance during the siege no matter what. Flurry Heart won't leave Uncle Thorax, who is the first creature in the Crystal City besides her parents and Sunburst who sees her as not a "little menace" due to her uncontrollable magic, but a friend.
>
> During the Siege of the Crystal Empire, the panic is catastrophic. Spike tries to get the crystal ponies to stop projecting terror via promises, but the reality of the artillery and bombs crashing against the unstable shield is too much. The crystal ponies need the innocence of a 9 year old former "little menace" saying "cross my heart, hope to fly, stick a cupcake in my eye!" to believe that they will survive and recite the pinkie promise back
>
> When Twilight and Applejack arrive to break the siege, they find a city that isn't cowering, but standing resolute
> Crystal City lights up in a wild celebration and the crystal heart is overflowing with joy, like the liberation of Paris
> The shield has held for so long that the crystal ponies feel empathy for the freezing changeling conscripts outside, so they are brought in for rehab
> Flurry Heart and Thorax prove that changelings don't have to be predators, alongside Fluttershy's POW paradise

**Themes:**
* **[[P&K Subversion]]**
> **Commentary:** "Love is the death of duty" becomes "Love is the fuel of duty"
> Flurry stays and gives a pinkie promise to the crystal ponies that the shield will hold
> The Subversion: It rejects the grimdark idea that you must sacrifice your humanity to win. We are stronger together.

* **[[Bottom Up > Top Down]]**
> **Commentary:** Spike as a celebrity telling everyone to stay strong didn't work. He doesn't have emotion sense.
> Flurry Heart does, and a pinkie promise is a bottom up contract from everypony


---
### SCENE 19.4: Family Reunion and meeting Thorax
> **Synopsis:** Thorax thanks Applejack for sparing the changelings at the Tall Tale prison and apologizes for not stopping Chrysalis years ago
> He was too hesitant to act. He justified inaction by saying how could he launch a strike on Chrysalis while also talking about harmony? But the reality was he was scared until it was too late, and didn’t take the hard leap
> The truth is only strong can be merciful
> He should have acted with force sooner
> It might have felt wrong then but the inaction hurts more now
> This assuages Applejack and Twilight’s fears of leading a slaughter and killing so many changelings
>
> Crystal pony magic is similar to changelings. It's emotion sense with friendship as calories. When they crystallize, it's a sign of excess calories/friendship for storage for later.
> The crystal army led by Shining Armor was total solidarity. They drowned out the hateful emotions coming out them using their peers' courage and solidarity. Determination became fanatical resolve when in a self-sustaining courage loop
> That's why the statthalters are sent at them to commit war crimes and terror, plus drugged conscripts, since the crystal ponies are too unified to take a surrender offer, and they know Sombra enslaved them and wiped their memories and that's what happens if you submit
> They don't have the draining component to their biology, just the emotion sense and outward sharing of joy
> The crystal army took low casualties and inflicted high changeling casualties, but kept losing ground to save lives. A halt and stand ground order would fail because morale would shatter as lives were lost. That's how they were pushed back to the Crystal City and the shield getting unstable due to terror

**Characters:**
* **[[Princess Cadance]]**

**Themes:**
* **[[Strong to be Merciful]]**
> **Commentary:** Thorax says the inaction hurts more now


**Codex:**
* **[[Changeling Biology]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Crystal ponies are the "control group"
> Although they still needed jaegers to protect the hive, while Crystal ponies had the Crystal Heart to keep predators out


---
### SCENE 19.5: Twilight and Spike talk about the past
> **Synopsis:** Twilight tells Spike he was right to come to the Crystal Empire instead of following her and Pinkie Pie to Ain Trotgourait with Zecora and Fizzlepop
> She is sorry about all the times she ignored him back then and took his skills for granted
> Of course, Spike forgives her
>
> Spike says he did feel like Twilight was losing her understanding of real friendship and felt Celestia had a wrong idea of friendship, he made this realization around in-universe season 6
> It starts with Twilight's "Friendship Lesson" to Starlight with reuniting with Sunburst
> Spike takes over Starlight's friendship lesson trying to follow Twilight's list but feeling exasperated the whole time
> During the scene when Starlight points out the statue of Spike, he deflects and continues reading Twilight's list. Then the crystal ponies start admiring him and he starts to realize he has an identity outside of being Twilight's assistant
> This is because Celestia taught Spike to be humble (to a fault, since she has to nurture the dragon instincts out), just as she indoctrinated Twilight to be selfless
> Spike forcing Starlight to follow Twilight's lesson was also a beginning of him wanting to stop following her instructions, which ultimately come from Celestia's world view
> "I'm sure there's something on Twilight's list that can help here..." but there wasn't
> Spike literally says "it's not your fault. I'm the one who said all we needed was this list" and he literally burns the list on screen!
> Well, Twilight obviously thinks you're worth being friends with! And I do too! - this is where Spike starts to think that his intrinsic understanding of friendship is better than Celestia's
> Spike witnessed Sunburst and Starlight reconnect organically with honesty instead of needing the friendship lesson list
>
>     Spike: What's wrong, Twilight?
>     Twilight Sparkle: I don't know, Spike. I think I have a lot to learn about being a teacher.
>     Spike: What are you talking about? Your lesson went perfectly! Starlight and Sunburst got over their past and rekindled their friendship!
>     Twilight Sparkle: No thanks to me. I know a lot happened. I just wish I could have given my pupil the attention she deserves.
>     Spike: Well, I know she needed to be put on the right path, but giving her the space to make her own decisions worked pretty well. Isn't that how Celestia taught you?
>     Twilight Sparkle: You know, I never thought about it! But I guess it is!
>     Spike: Maybe you're a better teacher than you thought.
>
> Spike was happy with that, but the lesson didn't stick for No Second Prances because it was a dinner with Celestia
> Spike observed that Twilight regresses from first principles whenever Celestia's expectations are looming over her
> Spike then saw Twilight really taking the whole friendship lesson thing way too seriously, ending in Every Little Thing She Does
>
> In that episode, Twilight and Spike went to Canterlot for a day (to give a talk to the school of gifted unicorns)
> Before Spike leaves, he reads the flashcards, which are predefined activities with their friends. This is really rough...
> Spike even says "if you're nervous about your friendship lessons, it's totally okay to say so". At this point, he sees through all of Twilight and Starlight's BS
> When Starlight is wondering if she did the spell wrong, Spike says he think Starlight is missing the point
> Then when Twilight says the point of baking a cake with Pinkie Pie is to connect with her, Spike starts wondering if Twilight is missing the point too, that those things can't be forced. Celestia didn't force Twilight to make friends, the way Twilight is writing flashcards for Starlight. But he doesn't say it
> Especially when Spike watches Twilight follow Starlight around checking off the checklist. He kind of sees Twilight's point when she tells Starlight that she the friendship lessons are done from organically cleaning up the castle mess, but it still doesn't feel quite right
>
> Spike went to the crystal empire to confess his concerns with the way Twilight is acting, following Celestia's orders regarding the cutie map and being a "friendship teacher" to Shining Armor
> To Spike's surprise, Shining Armor supports him completely since he already processed the same issue of his upbringing at the parloirs. So he directs Spike to adult therapy with a thestral friend. That's how Spike began to come to terms with his upbringing with Celestia as an overbearing mother figure and how he finds peace in his role at the Crystal Empire. Spike processes the trauma of being a pacified predator. He learns to integrate his Dragon biology (Red Love/Logistical Ambition) with his Equestrian upbringing (Pink Love/Grace).
> This was a really hard realization for Spike to come to terms with, since Celestia raised him primarily, and so did Twilight as an occasional baby sitter
>
> For the next 3 years, Spike would then take occasional trips to the Crystal Empire without Twilight, slowly processing his role in the world and Celestia
>
> His maturity leads to the diplomatic success in Gauntlet of Fire
>
> Then Twilight said she wanted to open a School of Friendship to all creatures
> Celestia shut her down and said only 1 student from tusted nations, a total farce
> This is where Spike really started feeling estranged
> Culminating in the expedition to Mount Aris in 1006
>
> But he didn't have the heart to say that to Twilight straight to her face back in 1006. So instead he pretended he didn't like the desert and went to the Crystal Empire instead (where Shining Armor and Cadance were operating outside Celestia's rules), saying the crystal ponies would appreciate him there
>
> Moondancer also felt alienated from the top down friendship lesson in Amending Fences. Spike brought her to the Crystal Empire for therapy and she also works on Aquileian crystal tech now
> Moondancer and Twilight geeked out on Aquileian science and magic back at Celestia's school

**Codex:**
* **[[The Battle of Mount Aris]]** (Backstory)
* **[[Rebuilding Ain Trotgourait]]** (Backstory)
* **[[Canon Episode References]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** The Crystalling
> No Second Prances
> Every Little Thing She Does

### SCENE 19.6: Minette's Cutie Mark Story
> **Synopsis:** Minette marvels at Flurry Heart's cutie mark, since the last time they met she was still a blank flank
> Then Flurry Heart asks what is Aunt Minette's cutie mark story
> Cadance and Shining Armor freeze and try to say Flurry is too young for that, but Flurry says she's old enough for anything now
> Minette tells the story of being a servant who stood up. To make it "kid friendly" but in a way that Flurry will immediately get it, the metaphor for abuse/grooming is changelings using the draining spell without asking
> The metaphor for consensual passion is changelings sharing love/friendship and love donations
>
> After Flurry Heart goes to bed, Twilight asks what Minette meant by draining and sharing when it comes to griffons. Then they learn about the brutality of feudal Aquileia. This leads to the next plot point about why Celestia removed sex from Equestrian society

### SCENE 19.7: Twilight realizes Celestia intentionally removed sex from Equestrian society/The Canterlot Wedding Lioness Spell
> **Synopsis:** Shining Armor looks at Applejack's uniform. "The Lioness of Tall Tale. What does it mean?"
> Applejack explains
>
> Twilight and Applejack are modest around Shining Armor and Cadance
> Rarity spills the beans about how Applejack said "instead of being the princess, how about you be my princess?"
> Rarity says Applejack is une lionne
>
> Shining Armor and Cadance smirk at that. You two have grown up
> You know about parloirs too? Twilight accused
>
> Then Applejack makes the connection that Rarity's delusions from the gala had to do with the Aquileian view of a prince and princess
> Super awkward because Prince Blueblood is in the next room over, doing military communications
>
> When Twilight and AJ realize Celestia didn't just suppress "war" but also "passion," it creates a complex emotional reaction. It’s not just "Celestia lied," it’s "Celestia hide it from us to keep us safe." That is a much more mature realization than simple anger.
>
> They talk to Cadance about the Tirek plot
> Cadance participated in Celestia's scheme about giving Twilight all their magic
> Also the role Cadance plays in the Stagnation of Harmony, did you realize?
> Cadance said yeah, she started to figure things out once Celestia told her to reject foreign offers to set up mining operations for crystals in the crystal empire
> I went along with Celestia's plan for you on purpose, so that she would know I'm being a good niece and not notice the crystal mines or our Aquileian and thestral friends
> Cadance fed Celestia a massive dose of confirmation bias
>
> We never told Celestia about visiting the parloirs, but Luna knows
>
> The love spell to repel the changelings was an Aquileian consent spell that repels predators using their own predatory ambition
> We learned about it in a parloir and experimented
> We didn't tell Celestia
> Why didn't it work to stop the changelings?
> We tried. But Chrysalis drugged the conscripts to have no emotion, so the spell didn't work. The crystal ponies had to shoot all the conscripts
> The officers (statthalters) stayed in their vehicles, the spell probably pushed them against the back of the vehicle but it kept driving
> Chrysalis knew exactly how to fight us

**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]**
> **Update:** Did Celestia cripple science to keep Equestria "safe"?


**Codex:**
* **[[Lion and Eagle]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Rarity says Applejack is Une Lionne - the Aquileian romantic term of the lion initiator
>
> Then Applejack realizes Rarity's delusions with Prince Blueblood at the gala were her looking for a lion. Instead, he was a stagnant piece of work

* **[[Combat Drugs]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Total emotional removal, the suicide mix

* **[[Grover III's Enlightenment (854 ALB to 914 ALB)]]** (History)
> **Note:** The science textbooks came from Manehattan parloirs, and from Griffonia
> Applejack wonders if her parents know all about earth pony chemistry by now
> Abandoning traditions for industry

* **[[Canterlot Wedding]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Thorax pulls back the curtain on Chrysalis, why they thought she's a cartoon villain but she's totally methodical

* **[[Canon Episode References]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** The Best Night Ever

* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** "The Element of Honesty" represents a time when the element bearers save the day from a magical catastrophe at Celestia's wish, and then they return to the status quo. By swapping it with "The Lioness of Tall Tale" Applejack is acknowledging that the status quo is dead. She killed it when deciding to go on the Bluebell Spearhead.
> "Tall Tale" thematically represents "not stagnation" since the city of Tall Tale was the place where great, ambitious agricultural feats were shared at the harvest festivals, and they were so ambitious that the rest of stagnant Equestria thought they were lies/exaggerations, hence a tall tale
> Lioness being an Aquileian/Griffon concept and Tall Tale being an earth pony place, together in the title, represents harmonic republican synthesis
>
> "The Element of Honesty" is purely a pink love concept. "The Lioness of Tall Tale" according to Synovial is a brute with no grace, pure red love. This is what Applejack believes is needed to win the war. She will do the red parts so others can stick with the pink parts.
>
> Celestia thought Cadance and Shining Armor's magic spell was proof that a harmonic deux ex machina would always come to save the day
> The reality is it was an Aquileian consent spell empowered by both their special talents
> This deconstructs the mythical element of Canterlot Wedding


---
### SCENE 19.8: Applejack says she understands what it's like to have celebrity status and responsibilities you can't run away from
> **Synopsis:** Twlight hugs Flurry Heart and says it's sad how Flurry had to lose her innocence at such a young age
> Applejack says she knows what it's like now, and Flurry Heart had the same happen to her as still a filly

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]** (Impact: CoreValueDemonstration)
> **Analysis:** She expresses sadness that Flurry had to lose her innocence at a young age. There is some implied resentment of the system that forced Applejack and Flurry Heart to have to do this, because they are "chosen ones"


**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** Originally Applejack said "Instead of being THE princess, how about you be MY princess?"
> Now Twilight has come up with the equivalent: "You ARE the Lioness. But you are also my farmpony."
> This is because Twilight still loves the farmpony and sees the farmpony, even after Applejack has put on the name tag "The Lioness of Tall Tale" on her new uniform

* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]**
> **Update:** Applejack confides in Twilight she now understands what it's like to have celebrity status and responsibilities you can't run away from
> That's why Twilight is so stressed and anxious
> It's so hard knowing I can't fall for personal things like Sweet Apple Acres being destroyed, the possibility that my family or friends could be captured and held against me, that ponies' lives change based on my actions
> I can't pretend I'm just a soldier, drive out in the front line and if I die somepony else will continue the fight the right way, and the guilt that comes from not acting in front
> That's what Celestia lived with for 1000 years
> I don't want to do it
> Maybe I could for a few years, but my whole life? Eternity? I can't blame her, but that's why things need to change

* **[[Establishing the Equestrian Republic]]**

### SCENE 19.9: Talking to Cadance about what the Crystal Ponies are like after the war
> **Synopsis:** Should they talk to Cadance about it? Since Celestia's mission to Cadance was to acclimate the 1000-year-old crystal ponies to the modern Equestrian way of life (Stagnation of Harmony)

**Threads:**
* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]**

**Codex:**
* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Twilight thinks the crystal ponies may be broken from the war trauma. Cadance explains they already carried trauma, and that cannot be erased. Celestia told Cadance to acclimatize them to modern life, but Cadance only nodded along. She had no intention of giving an illusion that there were no more monsters.

### SCENE 19.10: The Eastern Front Collapses
> **Synopsis:** The changeling eastern front collapses from lack of supplies, some fight to the death and others surrender, Statthalters retreat to Canterlot
> Stalliongrad retains administrative control of some regions in northeast Equestria
> All conscripts remaining in Manehattan, Stalliongrad offers to take them for the gulags. The eastern cities are not equipped to take the POWs since they are overpopulated with refugees who lost their homes, so Blueblood and Celestia agree
> Flowing Current is regretful that their camp is already full but has no other recourse
> Applejack’s army liberates most of Northern Equestria behind the Bluebell-Quebuck river line, which is all plains, and finally surrounds Canterlot last

**Threads:**
* **[[The War in the North]]** (Triumph)
> **Update:** The war in the north of Equestria is won


**Codex:**
* **[[Dragon Lands]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** The dragons hunted the changelings retreating to Canterlot, still with unicorn helpers riding with them. After this, their egos are satisfied and they go home.

### SCENE 19.11: Canterlot is Entrenched
> **Synopsis:** The remnants of the predators and the most motivated love addicts flock to Canterlot. Canterlot is the last changeling occupied piece of Equestria east of the Bluebell River, but 2 million ponies are held hostage.
>
> Canterlot is fully entrenched
> Not sure how to end this chapter
> An attempt to get surrender, only to hear Pagala talking back?
>
> Maybe with guilt that they made the Canterlot situation worse?
> Or that Applejack knows they are winning, but it feels like they are still back at square one, back at Twilight's Castle

## CHAPTER 20: Encirclement
> **Chapter Note:** Connection between using the Night to mean adult life vs the Night will fall on every creature who dreams of conquest (leading to ponies shooting each other)?

### SCENE 20.1: Meeting Field Marshal Blueblood
> **Synopsis:** Preconception is that Blueblood gave up land to Stalliongrad, and also Trimmel said he was competent
> Applejack and Rarity are stunned by his professionalism and humbleness
> He gives a nod to Aunt Celestia but looks to Applejack for orders!
> He says how Pagala drained him personally in 1002 (she is now sitting on the throne) and that is when he woke up
> Henri probably makes a joke of him but then realizes he was a poseur who grew up (just like him)

---
### SCENE 20.2: Canterlot Intel
> **Synopsis:** The statthalters in Canterlot did not set up slave labor factories like in Vanhoover, Bales or Whinnipolis. They set up fiefdoms by block/neighborhood. Most Canterlot natives stay in their original homes and they get subjected to a daily love tax. Sometimes by harvester, sometimes a statthalter drains them personally.
>
> Luna and her thestrals have extensive intel on all one million ponies, where they live, what their needs are, etc.
> These suddenly become "judgement lists" where collaborators are obvious

---
### SCENE 20.3: The Paradrop plans
> **Synopsis:** Some ponies asked for guns in their dreams, Mali said
> Luna looks grim. They didn't answer requests for guns
>
> The gears start to turn in Applejack's head
> Luna panics
>
> Applejack and her friends and all commanders, including the princesses and Prince Blueblood, meet at Twilight's Castle to decide how they will liberate Canterlot.
>
> The logic is that all Celestia can do is wait. It's the slow death of the hostages, but it avoids direct, visceral violence. However flawed that is.
>
> Applejack says it's now or never. The longer we wait, the more ponies we lose. The changeling garrison under Pagala will not get weaker. They will just feed off of the hostages.
>
> Applejack decides on a plan of giving the changelings an ultimatum of 12 hours for any changelings to defect and be spared. Her reputation for Honesty will be worth something.
>
> Applejack wants to use the dreamscape aid network to deliver rifles to ponies. They will use the statthalters' arrogance against them, since many ponies are secure in their own houses. The servants can also get rifles.
>
> Then they will bomb all anti air emplacements, paradrop on the city, blanket the streets with detection spells, and shoot any remaining changelings on sight. Transport planes with unicorns will fly over and teleport a Luna Nova Rifle with a magic canister into every home along with instructions on how to use it. If the pony runs out of magic to fire, they can start draining themselves for more ammo in a dire situation of self defense against any changelings who try to use them as hostages.
>
> It's the only way to save as many hostages as possible.
>
> Before, they were too horrified to attempt an urban assault
> Now, they have practically won the strategic war. Canterlot is surrounded and they have overwhelming material advantage. Trimmel surrendered to Applejack and gave her the courage to make the decision to take charge and embrace her role, signified by the uniform and the title on it - "The Lioness of Tall Tale"

**Codex:**
* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** The ponies trapped in Canterlot are the ones who chose not to retreat further east where they would have to work in the factories for the war effort. These ponies are the ones who chose stagnation, thinking others (mainly Celestia) would solve the problem, or worse, they were resigned to the changelings and chose to be a servant through inaction.
>
> The Refusers (The Cynical/Scared): These ponies didn't go to Manehattan because they were afraid of the war or the industrial grind. They chose "Safety/Stagnation" (staying home) over "Ambition/Risk" (the war effort).
>
> After their abuse at the hooves of the statthalters, they have become radicalized. They are no longer cynical, they are desperate.
>
> Through the dreamscape aid network, they are forgiven; Luna is empathetic because she also retreated once (Tall Tale). The thestrals are empathetic. The ponies are given a second chance by accepting a rifle. They have to become adults now.


---
### SCENE 20.4: Celestia speaks up against the plan
> **Synopsis:** The details are set but the mood is grim. Celestia suddenly speaks up, coming out against the plan and begging Applejack to reconsider. She says everypony will be brimming with hate and it will be a crisis. Giving every pony a rifle that drains themself will take away their innocence.
>
> Applejack asks Celestia what she would do instead. When Celestia doesn't answer, even after a minute of silence with her eyes closed, Applejack yells at Celestia saying she would just let the ponies get drained to death by Pagala. That was what was going to happen to all of Equestria before Applejack found her courage and Twilight saved her from the trench.
>
> Applejack says they call us objects. Celestia says she knows, that's why she was silent.
>
> It's called the Predator's Dilemma. They are predators. We cannot be friends with them and expect or hope for the same friendship back. We must be strong to be merciful.
>
> Celestia says she has seen how strength leads to monsters. I see the magical beams of death, and do you know what it reminds me of?
>
> What?
>
> Nightmare Moon.
>
> "I didn't become Nightmare Moon because I was jealous that ponies enjoyed Tia's day and slept through my night," Luna said. "That's a fairytale my sister made up. It's rather nonsensical, considering thestrals are nocturnal... thus I found it rather amusing when I first read it, a few days after my return," Luna chuckled, allowing a brief respite from the tension. "But it was only a fairy tale," she sighed, her tone hardening.
>
> They were alicorns who could move the sun and the moon. They defeated Discord and Sombra. World Peace was the next logical step. The ambition corrupted her.
>
> Luna asks Rasti to divulge the Temberik myth that had been passed down through generations.
>
> Luna confirms the truth of the Temberik myth about the power crystals coming from the corruption of becoming Nightmare Moon. Luna says the crystals in the Luna Nova Rifles are the product of a 1000 year old evil of her own doing. They are weaponizing her mistake and putting it in the hooves of every pony in Canterlot. Literally, considering what the Temberik collectively chose to name the rifle. It's based on your myth, right? Not the town. Rasti solemnly nods.
>
> Applejack says okay, but that still don't make a lick of sense. We don't have to use the Temberik crystals, we can use the ones from the crystal empire or Griffonia. If those crystals are fine, what's wrong with the Temberik ones?
>
> Celestia speaks up and says the other crystals are windigos.
> Twilight says okay but math
>
> But what's the alternative? Let Pagala drain everypony to death?!
>
> Maybe the "sky will fall" line comes from Luna. She talks about how Nightmare Moon threatened the night will fall on every creature who dreams of conquest. Applejack sadistically likes the nightmare moon motto, and Luna is pissed
>
> Applejack chillingly remarks how "The night will fall on Canterlot is better thematically to scare the changelings" but it wouldn't be practical to assault at night since it would be harder to shoot the changelings. Shame. Applejack appears flippant of the morals, singularly focused on saving as many pony hostages as possible, essentially spitting in the face of Celestia's lecture
>
> Twilight makes her realization - Celestia banned her from the front not to selfishly protect her, but because she feared Twilight getting corrupted
>
> Celestia says yes, and describes how terrified she was when she heard of the reports that Twilight teleported all across the battlefield and decimated enemies. She sees Twilight, her faithful student, tapping into rage/desperation to commit mass violence (and also starlight and fizzlepop, who actually both do more individual damage than Twilight). It looks exactly like Nightmare Moon. Twilight should feel guilty and small from this description and the genuine pain in Celestia's expression.
>
> But then Twilight says Applejack was holding her hoof the entire time. It was Applejack receiving orders on the radio and directing Twilight. The love grounded the violence.
>
> Twilight briefly recounts the failure of the school of friendship, but then points to the Aquileians (Fleur and Henri), The Temberik (Rasti), The buffalo (Little Strongheart), and the Tzinacatl (Mali) and even points to Fluttershy, who has befriended changelings, as creatures who Celestia refused to admit to the school of friendship but are their friends now. (Also, Cadance and Shining Armor are here. Thorax, Sunburst and Flurry Heart are elsewhere in the castle.)
>
> She points at the cutie map, which is depicting the jungle and temberik mountains now
> Maybe it even shows Severyana and Nova Griffonia now since their spearhead move relieved their armies and they established mutual respect?
> Because the Equestrians are coordinating with the communists now. Stalliongrad/Nova are pushing through the northern front against penguin and polar bear land, and their divisions are lining up at the Bluebell River to prepare to liberate the rest of Equestria
>
> Twilight says she trusts Applejack's judgement over Celestia's, to the shock of everyone in the room.
>
> Celestia backs down.
>
> The paradrop planning continues.

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** Applejack chillingly remarks how "The night will fall on Canterlot is better thematically to scare the changelings" but it wouldn't be practical to assault at night since it would be harder to shoot the changelings. Shame. Applejack appears flippant of the morals, singularly focused on saving as many pony hostages as possible, essentially spitting in the face of Celestia's lecture
>
> Resentment for Celestia, the dismissal here, is a clear backdrop

* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]**
> **Analysis:** Twilight demonstrates a return to her past self that was based in skepticism and empiricism
>
> When Celestia says "you could have turned into Nightmare Moon" Twilight says where is the evidence of that? How did exactly did Luna turn into Nightmare Moon in the first place?
> They say they don't know (a lie) and just say "corruption"
> Also when Celestia says the crystals are windigos and they can come back, Twilight questions that too
>
> A lot of things are unexplainable, like Pinkie Sense
> But Twilight says Pinkie Sense is an observable outcome. There is empirical evidence, even if they don't understand it doesn't mean it's not true. Follow the evidence.
> My evidence is right here (nuzzles Applejack) I would never turn evil with Applejack with me (aww)

* **[[Princess Celestia]]**
> **Analysis:** Celestia does not have orders of magnitude more magic. She gets a discount for raising the sun. She does not get the discount for raising the moon. She facilitated moving the moon using the stored ambition of Nightmare Moon on the moon, which is why the crater pattern was on the moon


**Threads:**
* **[[Developing the Luna Nova Rifle]]**
> **Update:** The real reason why the Temberik chose the name "Luna Nova Rifle" is revealed. It's from a myth that was passed down through generations.

* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** Now Twilight understands why Celestia held Twilight back. It wasn't because Celestia thought she was a child in need of protection or because Celestia need her to survive to take over the throne for the next 1000 years. Celestia was scared of Twilight getting corrupted like Nightmare Moon. Twilight realizes and says "So that's why you said I can't use magic on the front. But you're wrong. Because I have Applejack to save me."
>
> You tried to keep me safe by isolating me. But I'm only safe when I'm connected. How is that friendship?
>
> It's also now clear why Celestia scrubbed the crystals from history. She uses them exclusively to move the moon for the public good for 1000 years. Twilight and everypony else always assumed Celestia as a 1000 year old alicorn does have orders of magnitude more magic than a unicorn, but it's not true. Only maybe 10 times as much outside of moving the sun, which she gets a discount for her special talent.
>
> Celestia never actually pondered the science of magic to the level Fleur does. It's just instinct and facts of life to her. She refuses to study magic after Luna's fall.

* **[[World Peace / Overcoming The Predator's Dilemma]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** It's called the Predator's Dilemma. They are predators. We cannot be friends with them and expect or hope for the same friendship back. We must be strong to be merciful.
>
> Applejack makes a stand here against Celestia


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** This is a "hard truth" moment for Celestia and the ponies who will be receiving rifles. The hard truth is they have to make an adult choice to survive the attack.

* **[[Strong to be Merciful]]**
> **Commentary:** It's called the Predator's Dilemma. They are predators. We cannot be friends with them and expect or hope for the same friendship back.
>
> We can't ask them nicely to leave the ponies alone. We have to arm the ponies.
>
> We have them surrounded. We are offering them mercy now. If they don't take it, we must not bluff and actually commit to following through.


**Codex:**
* **[[Luna's Banishment]]** (History)
> **Note:** Nightmare Moon is the source of the Temberik crystals

* **[[Alicorn Magic]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** This breaks down the mythology of Luna's magic. Her dreamwalking abilities are not divine. They are simply very advanced, high powered spells, which only Luna can conventionally cast due to her special talent discount and alicorn 3x biology. The reason her spells are so profound is not because she is a God, but because before she became Nightmare Moon, she was a magical innovator like Twilight and Star Swirl. She innovated for 130 years.

* **[[How names are perceived]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Applejack mentions "do you know how evil these changelings really are? They translate our names literally, like we are objects. We cannot reason with the predators who still occupy Canterlot; they must be given one final ultimatum, and then killed at gunpoint"
>
> Celestia's response is she knows about it, and she does not want Applejack to adopt such a "predatory" mentality. She wanted a happy, simple destiny and stability.
>
> Applejack says I like my name. I do lift apples. That's honest work.

* **[[School of Friendship]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** The contrast between the top-down mandate vs real friendship

* **[[Stagnation of Harmony (930 ALB - Present)]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Teleporting the rifles is the end of the nursery. Applejack is treating them as adults. "I trust you to decide your own fate, even if it’s dangerous." The innocence is gone forever. It forces the prey to stop being prey.
>
> Celestia is a parent who was okay with using the donated red love for "I will feed you and keep you safe in your room."
> Applejack now wants to say "You can't stay in your room forever. Here is a tool. Go handle your business." (Sending Rifles).
> The ponies loved the food (Childhood). They are terrified of the rifle (Adulthood). But they pick up the rifle because they have to.
> The Loss of Innocence: The moment the pony stops praying for bread and starts loading the gun is the moment the "Nursery" of Equestria dies.


---
### SCENE 20.5: Applejack delivers the ultimatum
> **Synopsis:** ◦"Chrysalis has left you. She took the air force to save herself, and left Trimmel, your best general, out to dry. She left you here to die to buy her time."
>
> Applejack says over the radio I know you destroyed my home to get me angry so I would make a mistake. I'm not angry anymore. I pity you. Your life revolves around hate and I pity that you'll never know friendship
> For all the other changelings who were forced to work in factories, then drafted into the army, fly down and surrender now and free yourself from the hive, free yourself from Chrysalis and Pagala and this mindset that you have to take love from others because you are not alone (like the Khala from Salvation)
> We can give you a second chance, but let there be no mistake, this is your last chance. At X, the sky will fall on Canterlot

**Themes:**
* **[[Strong to be Merciful]]**
> **Commentary:** When Applejack uses this line against the Statthalters in Canterlot, she is effectively tapping into the "Nightmare" to scare the "Predators." She "sadistically likes the Nightmare Moon motto" because it turns the tables: the prey (ponies) are now the ones bringing the darkness down on the predators (changelings).


**Codex:**
* **[[Applejack's Radio]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** She uses the same words as Trimmel
>
> "This is General Applejack. Surrender and you will be spared. That is a promise. Resist and you will be erased. That is a fact. You have 12 hours. At dawn, the sun will rise... but the night will fall on every creature who still dreams of conquest."


---
### SCENE 20.6: Sending the rifles
> **Synopsis:** Ponyville is filled to the brim with red love donations from all across Equestria so that all one million ponies can be reached at night and so the rifles can be teleported
> The official message is that there will be a full on assault at dawn (6 AM) and to stay in place or hide, and only use the rifle for self defense. Don't put yourself at risk. But they can't control what happens after.
>
> They go over the lists and mark which ponies have rifles
> They only have limited time to teleport all the rifles, so they go with the lowest risk of detection ponies first, who are in their own homes
> The ones who are in statthalter homes as servants are the most risky but they also must be contacted before they wake up
> Some servants aren't secure in their rooms (they may have been forced to sleep with their master...) so they are told to secure themselves and are woken up forcibly from their dream by the dreamwalker
> Any pony who is contacted and is in a dangerous position gets their rifle and woken up forcibly
>
> Applejack and Twilight roam the different buildings in Ponyville to check on the status of each neighborhood. Each building in Ponyville has a squad of dreamwalkers and rifles/red love stockpiles focused on a neighborhood in Canterlot, and there are huge checklists.
> Twilight's Castle (many rooms), Carousel Boutique, Sugarcube Corner, Barnyard Bargains, the Spa, the schoolhouse, Town Hall, etc.
>
> As the panic sets in, more changelings choose to surrender by flying down the mountain. They are allowed to live, but in strict prison. Others are leaving their homes in fear, but going to the castle for a last stand
> Some of the defectors are not statthalters, they are conscripts who were on the front and retreated

**Characters:**
* **[[Princess Luna]]**
> **Analysis:** She originally didn't want any part in teleporting rifles. However, the thestrals are going to do it anyway because the dreamscape and teleportation spells are democratized. She realizes she might as well help cast dreamscape spells to save red love for the army since she has the discount on her spell. To not contribute now would be counterproductive and shameful.
>
> This serves as Luna's realization of why Applejack is wearing the general's uniform. Applejack didn't want to be a general, but to not stand and fight would be counterproductive and shameful.
>
> Celestia's cynical viewpoint is different because she has actually lived through 1000+ years. She has to remember the sins of many mistakes. Luna has only lived through roughly 130 years.
>
> Should this plot point actually be from Luna's perspective?

* **[[Actia Pagala]]**
> **Analysis:** Pagala views it as an empty threat and her energy is on discipline and stopping changelings from defecting. She thinks it's a bluff to siphon off the garrison but they will never actually attack. She has convinced herself of this


---
### SCENE 20.7: Applejack and Twilight land at Canterlot Castle
> **Synopsis:** The commanders don't want Applejack to go for the castle since she's their leader, but Applejack says if I don't, who will? It ain't fair. Twilight and Starlight and Rainbow Dash are going too.
>
> They are in a transport plane, ahead of them are all the detection spells blanketing the city, ponies dropping on the streets, and rifles teleporting into every home
>
> It's brutal hallway to hallway warfare with a lot of detection spells. Constant shudders.

**Codex:**
* **[[Wings of Dew]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** How do I hold a gun with hooves? With wings! Twilight gives Applejack wings of dew again. This time it's purple alicorn wings
>
> This shows that Applejack loving Twilight is entirely organic within the story's timeframe, since before, the wing colors were for her new trench mates Tally Mark and Mali
>
> Tally Mark and Mali are in the plane with them too, since the four of them were in the tank together
>
> Twilight and Applejack are a chasseur pair


---
### SCENE 20.8: Applejack kills Pagala on the throne
> **Synopsis:** Pagala: "You tricked us! You said you would siege!"
> ◦AJ: "I said I would attack at dawn. I ain't a liar, Pagala. You just didn't believe a pony could be this hard."
>
> For the throne room, I'm thinking of Pagala hiding disguised amongst hostages. This quickly becomes ineffective because of the detection spells, so instead she's holding up guns to hostages' heads when AJ and Twilight break into the throne room. Except Twilight is so fast with her magic that she bursts all the guns in the room apart on the spot, including AJ's, and AJ bucks Pagala like an apple tree into the throne. That's when she cries for mercy. AJ doesn't give it, and kills her with a shovel since her gun is gone
>
> Mercy! You don't want to be a murderer do you? 
> I killed hundreds of changelings with a machine gun. It's too late for one more to affect me
>
> Applejack repeats Rainbow's line here, from Crack Lightning:
> "They are not creatures. They are animals."
> You ain't a general. You're a varmint, a parasite.
>
> I want a trial. I'll admit everything
> Varmints don't deserve trials
> She puts the shovel against Pagala's neck and presses her weight against the handle, then slams the crystal that expands the handle
>
> Applejack was speaking her honest thoughts with no filter. When she turns around after Pagala's last breath, she sees other soldiers staring at her, having heard her words to Pagala (including Rainbow Dash, who looks the most disturbed)
> Is the castle secured? She asked.
> Yes, General, they say

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** She calls Pagala an animal, not a creature, before killing Pagala with a shovel


**Themes:**
* **[[Strong to be Merciful]]**

**Codex:**
* **[[Applejack's Star Spade]]** (Technology)

### SCENE 20.9: Celestia walks through the ruined streets of Canterlot to her throne

### SCENE 21.3: Gilded Trust holds a rambling nationalistic rally

> His Aunt adopted Chrysalis


---
### SCENE 21.4: Applejack talks to Trimmel at Camp Fluttershy
> **Synopsis:** Applejack tasked Fluttershy with gathering intel on how they should invade the changeling lands. Fluttershy says all the POWs say the changelings are oppressed and secretly want to be nice, that's their nature. Applejack respectfully takes Fluttershy's perspective with a grain of salt.
>
> Applejack asks Henri to come with her to talk to Trimmel and ask how should they invade the Changeling Lands. Henri doesn't want to talk to Trimmel.
>
> When Applejack asks Fluttershy where is Trimmel, she takes her to a greenhouse
> Trimmel lives as a songbird most of the time at the camp
> He says he is not much of an extrovert despite being the face of the army and the voice on the radio
>
> Hivesmarschall Trimmel? Applejack asked
> Just Trimmel, he says. The parallel with chapter 1
> You have a new uniform?
> My friend made it for me
>
> What are you doing as a bird?
> The kindness is torture. I deserve execution.
> Aw, you poor thing, said Fluttershy
> Execution is the easy way out, said Applejack. Your friend is across the river and leads an army of terrified conscripts. You see what this camp is like. I want you to talk to him.
>
> Synovial is the original Hivemarshall who led the modern army that united the hives. Trimmel is the new generation of talent who grows up in the new order. But Synovial rejects Trimmel's bid to study under the master. So Trimmel studies on his own.
> Remember when I said I wanted to study under Synovial and he rejected me?
> Well I went to Aquileia to study Moriset Discret's new peasant army
>
> Trimmel praises Henri
> He says he learned his tactics from Discret's proto-FJA from the reconquest of the peripherie
>
> Henri is forced to admit the royalist roots of his people, and the Coltbert reforms
>
> He asks why she doesn’t follow the rivers west of Yeahcon Lake and Applejack says it’s not good terrain for the tanks, which Trimmel agrees
> Applejack asks how would Trimmel invade the Changeling Lands, for which Trimmel has no answer
> He says the tanks were designed for the plains of the Herzland in Griffonia, and they were well-suited for Equestria, but the forests and marshes of the Changeling Lands were dominated by Jaeger special forces and Queen’s guard
> You think your ponies will follow you into the Changeling Lands? It is a brutal place
>
> Trimmel explains some parts of the Great Leap Forward and its brutality and the destruction of the old changeling culture.
> Herzlander industry and language, Skyfall influences, FJA influences
> There's still only one Aquileia, no mention of the urban who were taxed. Just an Aquileia that grew out of royal patronage needed
>
> Trimmel finally mentions that the changeling front line across from Tall Tale is all fresh conscripts, led by his friend and comrade, Elvir Roland
> He asks Applejack to negotiate a surrender with Elvir
> He goes into his role in Severyana winter uprising and his comrades
> He mentions his friend Thranx speaking out against Chrysalis, staying in Griffenheim, and getting assassinated by VOPS
> That's why he put Chrysalis in Canterlot
> If Celestia attacked Canterlot and lost, then Chrysalis would march onward, and Trimmel planned to resign
> If the Equestrians followed through and won (which they did), then he would know he did his best for his people and the rest would be up to the Equestrians (because they are already winning)
>
> He participated in the Winter Uprising in Severyana and actually believes Equestria is a walled garden and a lie. He sees the oppression of the boyars and royal guards on the common ponies revolting, compares that to his meritocracy at home, and really believes Equestria is evil. His motivation is more ideological and based in justice, not predatory like the bugnazi Actia Pagala.
>
> You could have shot down Chrysalis during her flight. Your wonderbolts were capable
> We decided you were more important than her. And so were all the other ponies who needed air support
>
> So what is the plan now?
> We're going to liberate the hives. Then all of you will be free.

**Characters:**
* **[[Trimmel]]**

> Henri is not pleased at Trimmel for mentioning this nuance
> Applejack gives Henri a stink-eye but doesn't ask for elaboration
>
> The cognitive dissonance is that the Aquileian Revolutionaries always say monarchy is bad and Discret is an incompetent cartoon stick figure, but Trimmel suggests that Discret is a griffon of the people and charismatic
> He says Chrysalis copied Discret
>
> Trimmel says he resented Applejack (really it was Henri) for sniping his peers, but he understands now. It's Discret's chasseurs hunting traitor nobles to free the serfs. That's what they were doing, taking out jaegers who oppressed the drugged conscripts.
>
> Linguistic connection between how Chrysalis copied chaessur into jaeger for their protective class of warriors. They should really be called protectors
>
> Trimmel also says Chrysalis copied Moriset Discret's tactic of hunting the traitorous nobles and freeing the serfs. Chrysalis hooked the enemy nobles into love harvesters, turning them into resources/batteries instead of leaving corpses. The Queen does not waste.

>

The jaeger theater is Vanhoover because they can get a clean surrender of the city.

### SCENE 21.7: Trimmel explains what really happened on his side in the 2nd Battle of Tall Tale
> **Synopsis:** Warning from Trimmel: do not underestimate Chrysalis. She may seem like a petulant leader, but she is a rational actor. She uplifted them.
>
> Trimmel starts off saying how Olenia surrendered fast thanks to the overwhelming changeling force
> The bauleiters were given administrative control of Olenia. They collected love taxes but also invested in civilian infrastructure and industry
> The Olenian resistance goes to the statthalters, who extract from them punitively and enjoy it
> Which faction do you think brought more love back home to the hives?
>
> The Polar Bears submitted without a shot fired. They paid us in resources, we bauleiters invested in them. We taught them how to fight and build, they served us. No love taxes. In fact, they loved learning from us and becoming modern. That was what meritocracy was supposed to be.
>
> Vanhoover was a clean occupation. All surrendering combatants went to Vanhoover. All non-surrendering combatants who were taken alive went to black sites (need German name, I guess it's just concentration camp...) where the statthalters tortured them
>
> First Battle of Tall Tale was an eager error. Yes we saw the entrenchments, but after the success at the border, the generals thought your morale would shatter it didn't.
> It was a miscalculation. The officers were on Auftragstaktik/Mission Command. They made their judgement that all ponies are cowards are easily broken after Vanhoover, so they went with the infantry mass charge, flying over the river. Some doubts formed in my mind about the organized weather and destroying the dam, but others interpreted the dam blowing as desperation
> Some officers feared if you destroyed the dam, you would destroy the industrial infrastructure next so they went for the attack
> Tall Tale obviously proved that ponies could be very serious.
> Chrysalis questioned me, but I said it was a battle lost due to the high of previous victory [and the other reasons]. I just needed a few weeks to prepare a proper assault
> Then Princess Luna ordered the retreat. I smiled smugly. Chrysalis praised my foresight. The full plans weren't ready, but my hubris got the better of me, thinking how foolish Princess Luna was, and I ordered the assault.
> I resented you and the Aquileians that day. You targetted my officers, my peers
> I ordered all tanks and aircraft to come to Tall Tale. You were the Schwarpunkt, the Center of Gravity. If I defeated, or even better, captured heroes like you, Rainbow Dash, Twilight Sparkle, Starlight Glimmer and destroyed Star Energy Corporation, the rest of Equestria would surely surrender. We would have a clean occupation run by bauleiters. We would invest in you, just like we already did with those Skyfall and New Mareland shell companies.
> But Chrysalis overruled me. She told me, why waste our professional armies in a destructive battle? She said another army could pop up, emboldened. I couldn't argue with that. So she said start driving towards Manehattan. If we capture your family, surely you would surrender.
>
> I reckon I would, Applejack glared. That's why I sent my whole family to Manehattan before the war.
> So, you thought you would beat us?
> I believed so, said Trimmel. Starlight Glimmer was a huge threat, but only the most visible one. The true enabler was the Wonderbolts. With their air dominance, you could teleport and take out my officers with impunity.
> The battle would have been glorious, but that's besides the point. It would have been final. The war would have been over.
> Chrysalis didn't see it that way. She fixated on me seeking glory and avenging my peers. Called me emotional. She wanted to keep our tanks and planes to invade Griffonia next. She doesn't fight hard fights. And look what happened: you nationalized all our collaborators, united the Tzinacatl, copied our love harvester into a rifle, and blew a hole through the mountain, riding rafts straight to Quebuck. When I faced you in Ponytown, I didn't face Starlight Glimmer, I faced 50 Starlight Glimmers. You did the impossible - what the changelings did in 25 years, you did in 2.
>
> I said a military victory against the strong would compel the weak to surrender. Chrysalis chose to invade the weak to get leverage over the strong.
>
> So Chrysalis let me live?
> That was her mistake, but she doesn't make many mistakes. I have made many.
> Chrysalis doubled quotas on the bauleiters in Vanhoover after my defeat in the 2nd Battle. Then she tripled them. The bauleiters resigned and went home to the factories in shame, and then the statthalters took over.
> Chrysalis took my promise and corrupted it. I said surrender and you'll be spared. That is a promise. Resist and you'll be removed. That is a fact. Chrysalis began applying it to Equestria as a whole. I protested, saying she was violating my promise and the long-term productivity of our economy. Chrysalis said there wouldn't be a changeling economy if they don't win. She never promised she wouldn't raise expecations. She rewards results. She told me my peers chose to resign, so she had to fill the posts with whoever was willing to do the job.
> I turned a blind eye. I only thought of the officers you vaporized, kept my headset on, and continued marching towards Manehattan, ignoring the horrors to the north in Stalliongrad or what kind of fate awaited the surrounded Crystal City. I was at the gates of Manehattan when the magical rifles came. No changeling could predict that you would use our own terror machine against us...
>
> Vanhoover is a miserable place. The statthalters are committing atrocities I dare not even speak of.
> It is simply inefficient to force the ponies to work. Economically it would make more sense to just use them as love and let them rest wiht their families. It would yield more high quality love for the factory drone back home and the conscripts at the front. They are forcing the ponies to make changeling machines just so they can punish the ponies when the results are bad, and to assert their dominance
>
> It was a twisted corruption of the meritocratic laws. The ponies in Vanhoover did everything right, but because you resisted, Chrysalis punished them.
>
> So you're saying I caused this? Applejack said grimly
> No, I'm saying Chrysalis and I caused this. You must go in and kill them all. No radio promise. Give every pony a rifle, just like you did in Canterlot.
>
> There is no lying in the hives. You can't fake being a predator or love addict. If you are too soft or gaining empathy for the pony victims, you are sent to a factory floor back home to be an empathetic manager of drones. Every changeling in Vanhoover is complicit and cannot be rehabilitated. They are like the ones in the other ward.
> Basically those managers are the predators who are too cowardly to fight on the front (no honor)
>
> Trimmel explains how every professional unit has a statthalter there. They cannot override tactics but they can arrest soldiers who get too sympathetic to the ponies and move them to the factory using emotion sense
> Elvir will be executing all the statthalters in his ranks before surrendering. When he surrenders, drive straight to Vanhoover. Take blitz-essenz if you have to.
> We don't do that. We use sleep spells.
> Fine. As long as you surround the city. You asked me how I would invade the hives, I would start by killing all the statthalters in Vanhoover.
>
> My "promise" was only ever to lessers. In the hive wars, I commanded guns against sticks. In Severyana, I directed a mob. In Olenia, it was the same. Tall Tale was my first real fight. So was Manehattan against Blueblood.
>
> I am the voice on the radio who tricked ponies into Chrysalis's slaughter house. There is no such thing as a good occupation.
>
> We are not meritocrats. We are imperailists in fancy uniforms using slaves. You don't see jaegers or bauleiters or statthalters. You only see changelings.
>
> Trimmel says he doesn't know how to repent for enabling this, he was the one who captured Vanhoover. I deserve to be dead or in a love harvester
> Applejack says if you were dead, you wouldn't be helping now. If your friend surrenders and we don't have casualties, that's a first step
> Trimmel is giving Applejack a clean surrender through his friend Elvir. So Applejack is inclined to trust Trimmel.
> You're right, I don't see bauleiters or statthalters. I see changelings who took the promise, and changelings who must be removed.
>
> What else can I do?
>
> You can stop living like a varmint and start living like a changeling. I recommend starting with the coloring books before moving onto the board games, Applejack smirked
>
> Please liberate Vraks

**Characters:**
* **[[Trimmel]]**
> **Analysis:** Surrender and you will be spared, resist and you will be removed
> It is not just a threat, it is a political manifesto
> I can deliver Equestria without damaging the goods. The statthalters break the toys; I secure them
>
> He realizes the ponies only saw "changelings", so the worst of them represented all of them (statthalter torture, infiltrator deceit)

* **[[Queen Chrysalis]]**
> **Analysis:** Trimmel depicts her as a rational tyrant and a manipulator and a desperate CEO of a collapsing economy whose actions are bound by the elites and the system she created. Never a cartoon villain with arbitrary mood swings or a purely sadistic "dark lord" or "bugnazi". 
>
> He intentionally made her act like the cartoon villain that the Equestrians remember from the Wedding, by making her sit on Celestia's throne and gloat
>
> Trimmel treated the Queen of the Changelings like a shiny lure on a fishing hook. Worse, Chrysalis realizes she cannot punish the Statthalters for their decadence. On the Zebrican islands, she controlled them via the Love Quotas and the MEFO bills. But in Canterlot, they have direct access to millions of terrified Equestrian civilians. They don't need her money or her permission to eat anymore.

> Communism (Equality) is the antithesis of her Meritocracy (Hierarchy).
>
> Therefore, do not give any mercy to the statthalters in Vanhoover

His subordinate jaegers who aren't as ideologically pure and more poseur were terrified of facing the Aquileian aces at Tall Tale again so they want easy targets. This proves Henri's thesis correct, that the changelings had a terrifying aesthetic but once faced with a peer they flee
