### SCENE 23.7: Applejack recruits Trimmel and asks about the pony bauleiters
> **Stakes:** They receive news that Synovial is in the Reich. Applejack must intervene.
> **Synopsis:** She was already planning on going to Aquileia to pay back the debt to the FJA volunteers, now she wants to recruit some changelings who want to fight for her against Synovial, including Trimmel
>
> Applejack asks Trimmel did you know about the pony bauleiters?
> Trimmel honestly answers yes, but he didn't say it because he genuinely did not think the pony slaves would be an angry mob, and he wanted the statthalders wiped out and didn't want to mention any nuance
> Thorax is resentful at Trimmel again, saying he's corrupting Applejack's morals. But Applejack says thanks for not making me have to make the decision to go slower. They couldn't afford mercy before, but now they can (and must, to prove to Celestia)
>
> Thorax says in 1002 the plan was to take out Celestia and actually run a peaceful occupation of Equestria.
>
> The bauleiters had plans to educate the stagnant ponies on industry and set up a meritocratic system. There would only be light, non-invasive love taxes. This is the practical path that Chrysalis approved in order to power the invasion of Herzland (Chrysalis's actual enemies)
>
> Of course this bauleiter led vision is at odds with the racial superiority and fanaticism in the statthalders. But parts of this bauleiter vision from 1002 gets implemented in the statthalder concentration camps in Vanhoover (all the rural ponies who didn't escape to the eastern cities before the war are forcibly relocated to Vanhoover). The bauleiters offer industrial education to any pony who wants it, and they'll "enter the rat race" just like the 5 year old changelings. Not that many ponies take that offer.
>
> Thorax believed it up until they actually landed in Canterlot and the statthalters terrorized the city

**Characters:**
* **[[Rainbow Dash]]**
> **Analysis:** She welcomes Trimmel, to the surprise of Applejack. Because he surrendered (loyalty) and also helped Elvir Roland surrender bloodlessly (Rainbow oversaw the surrender from the sky)
>
> After that, Rainbow Dash spoke with Elvir and Trimmel, after the end of Stagnation

* **[[Trimmel]]**
> **Analysis:** He feels guilty for creating the Lioness and shame


**Threads:**

---
### SCENE 23.8: Starlight meets Trimmel
> **Synopsis:** Starlight should befriend Trimmel somehow. What would they talk about? As reformed villains. They also both associate with Stalliongrad, and possibly Caramel Marks
>
> I guess Trimmel recognizes her as the one who stopped him at the 2nd battle of Tall Tale
>
> He can remark about how VOPS intelligence said Celestia would never intervene on a battlefield. (He met her as a disguised pony and didn't like her for stagnation and aloofness?) There was a report about "If Celestia was on the battlefield, what do we do? How does it change the battle outcome?"
>
> Starlight both showed up, and exceeded the estimations of Celestia's power level and impact
>
> PTSD triggers are how to make his acceptance realistic and not "overly nice."
>
> •The "Voice" Problem:
> ◦Trimmel was the voice on the radio in Chapter 1: "Surrender and you will be spared. Resist and you will be eliminated."
> ◦The Scene: Trimmel walks into the SECEF mess hall to get coffee. He says something simple like, "Excuse me."
> ◦The Reaction: Half the room freezes. A veteran drops their tray. That specific timbre of voice is the soundtrack to their nightmares.
> ◦The Reality: He can never be "one of the guys." He will always be the Boogeyman who happens to be on their side now. He eats alone, not because he is shunned, but because he knows his presence kills the mood.
>
> Starlight Glimmer: The "Former Villains" Club
>
> Starlight is the character who bridges the gap between "Monster" and "Person" for Trimmel.
> •Shared History: ◦Trimmel: Served a Tyrant (Chrysalis) for the sake of an Ideology (Meritocracy).
> ◦Starlight: Was a Tyrant (Our Town) for the sake of an Ideology (Equality).
>
> •The Connection: Starlight is the only one who understands the seduction of control. She knows what it's like to do terrible things because you think you are "fixing" society.
> •The Interaction: ◦Starlight finds Trimmel isolating himself (the "Ghost in the Mess Hall"). She sits with him.
> ◦Trimmel: "You should not sit here. People stare."
> ◦Starlight: "Let them stare. They stared at me for months. They whispered that I was a monster who stole cutie marks. And they were right."
> ◦Trimmel: "How do you bear it?"
> ◦Starlight: "I don't. I just keep trying to be better than I was yesterday. Redemption isn't a finish line, Trimmel. It's a job."
>
> •Why this works: Starlight offers him a roadmap. She is living proof that you can come back from the brink. This gives Trimmel the hope he needs to join SECEF not just as a "Consultant," but as a believer in the future.

**Characters:**
* **[[Starlight Glimmer]]**
> **Analysis:** Going by the chasseur protocol, warlords/grifters who choose to be mean are irredeemable. Starlight isn't being mean, she really thinks equality helps ponies, she misinterpreted Caramel Marks' communist manifesto which was meant for the working class to rise up against industrialists, and Starlight's cult is actually a valid criticism of ego capitalism, because ponies joined because they felt their special talents were inadequate. 
>
> Trimmel is redeemable because he isn't a warlord or grifter, he was misled by Chrysalis since he was rescued from the ashes of Vraks
> He wasn't blown away by the Lioness Spell in Canterlot Wedding

### SCENE 23.12: Tank scrubbing scene
> **Synopsis:** Painting SECEF in big block corporate letters over the royal Equestrian insignia and the changeling heer/trident
>
> Scrubbing off the Royal Guard insignia is a heavy moment. It represents Private Citizens taking responsibility. They aren't fighting for the Crown anymore; they are fighting for the Corporation (which represents the Cooperative/Union).
>
> The Layers of Paint: ◦Bottom Layer: Changeling Black (Tyranny/Predation).
> ◦Middle Layer: Equestrian Gold/Sun (Monarchy/Stagnation).
> ◦Top Layer: SECEF Corporate Grey/Blue (Harmonic Capitalism/Action).

---
### SCENE 23.13: Luna speaks to Applejack and Twilight before they go to Aquileia
> **Synopsis:** Celestia went back to Canterlot, but Luna stayed in Tall Tale to help run the dreamscape aid network and work with Thorax to rescue the hostages
>
> As princess of the night, Luna is well versed in the language and customs of sex. She has to make the call between dreams of passion and dreams on conquest. She sympathizes with Applejack and Twilight's frustration with Celestia culturally neutering Equestria (though it's impermanent)
>
> In fact, Luna invented the contraception spell to facilitate safe passion. Celestia reversed it into a conception spell (which requires excessive pink love, she engineered it that way over years (decades?) as a safeguard to prevent abuse, as an example of her cautious approach to contrast with Luna and AJ's haste and recklessness) for family planning, not the other way around!
>
> Luna warns them to be safe. She hopes they can find a better way other than stagnation because she acknowledges it would be immoral to roll back the clock now (after thinking about it for the few weeks between the white peace and SECEF leaving).
>
> Send Celestia a letter. She would be happy to hear from you. Pinkie Promise

**Threads:**
* **[[Establishing the Equestrian Republic]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** A quite vote of confidence from Luna?

### SCENE 23.14: Applejack's Letter to Celestia about SECEF departure
> **Synopsis:** It should have the same tone as a season 1-3 friendship letter, sent via Spike in a scroll
>
> Applejack's points:
> She doesn't know how to lead ponies or fix the trauma of the war, but she still believes in honesty and in friendship, including her Aquileian friends
> So she is going to Aquileia to learn
> A lot of other ponies also want to learn. We're calling it SECEF
> SECEF is a pressure valve for ambition, just like New Mareland
>
> They carry on with preparations, then Spike burps out a response a few minutes later
>
> "Please come home safe. -Celestia"

## CHAPTER 24: Intervention

### SCENE 24.1: SECEF arrives in Pridea
> **Synopsis:** President Gaudreau and economic minister Verany personally welcome SECEF in Pridea. They are given a briefing. Synovial's attache is encroaching on the town of Ailmont in Verenia, which is Henri's hometown.
>
> Verany wants to talk economy and trade with Equestria with Applejack. Applejack is baffled at the lack of urgency for the war. She tells him to get in contact with Mayor Flowing Current of Manehattan for that kind of talk. She has a war to win.
>
> Henri explains that the FJA is only a plurality, the largest one, which is why Gaudreau is president, but they rule in a coalition with Verany's PdNA. Henri says Verany is Kemerskai's lackey. In Aqulileia, we call him the bourgeosie.
>
> Starlight gasps, Henri says you must be a student of Caramel Marks and Fire Angel, Starlight says yeah sheepishly
> It's nothing to be ashamed of, Henri laughed
> So is the FJA the proletariat?
> Ha, we are not boring city griffons. we are what Caramel Marks called the petty bourgeois. We have our land, our craft and we're proud of it. The workers are mostly PNdA
> But you said Verany is bourgeois. How could the proletariat ally with him?
> What is the one constant about griffons?
> A griffon mixes lion strength with graceful eagle flight? Applejack offered
> Ha, that is the ideal griffon. An impossible ideal. What is the real constant?
> Griffons are greedy, said rainbow
> Rainbow!
> What? He asked.
> Who did you hear that from?
> My friend gilda
> Where is she from?
> Griffonstone
> Henri did a double take, not exactly what I had in mind, but her native tongue is Herzlander, yes?
> I guess
> That's the Herzlander expression. Because Herzlander is a boring place. Same land, same weather, same cities, same factories, same griffons. How do you show you are the best? Have more land, more factories, more serfs. This is why Chrysalis copied them, it's easy because they are all the same, and she wants same
> But in Aquileia, we are different. We have different soil, different weather, different accents, and we have ponies.
> 20.4% pony, to be exact, said Fleur. Pridea is 39.3% pony.
> We show we are better with better products. Better clothes, better wine, better cheese,
> Better ponies, said Fleur
> Uh, I was not going to mention that
> Point is, all griffons want to be better than others. They don't want worker equality, they all want to be bourgeois
> Communism may work for ponies in the frozen severyana or griffons in the rice fields, but I doubt it works for griffons in a shining city with fertile fields around

**Threads:**
* **[[Perception of the Griffonian Republic]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** Verany is rather haughty and reeks of a businessgriff. Henri says Verany is Kemerskai's lackey. (But this is proven to be false. Verany is ideologically aligned with the textile industrialists who betrayed Kemerskai and the revolution in 981. There is no trace of that faction in the current day GR.)
>
> Also Henri snubs Herzlander culture as greedy again

* **[[Establishing the Equestrian Republic]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** Applejack is slapped with the realization that the FJA is not even a majority in Aquileia, only a plurality which has to compromise with Verany's PNdA (which isn't as sympathetic to ponies or terroir, since they are more urban). Applejack realizes that the harmonic republican ideology that Comet Shine/Fleur/Henri have been preaching is not even dominant in their own country. The task of proving Celestia that harmonic republicanism (the specific ideology that works for ponies, terroir, buffalo and thestrals) can work in Equestria gets even more daunting!


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** She sees smog, brutalist factories, and rushing workers who look and act exactly like the "Soulless Herzlanders" she was warned about.
> She has to confront Henri or Fleur Bloom: "You told me your way was the 'Aquileian Way.' But half your people live like machines. Were you lying to me, or to yourselves?"
>
> Applejack thought "soulless" meant foreign/imperial/evil. Now she realizes "soulless" was a class slur used be her friends against their own countrymen
>
> They shrug and say "yeah they are soulless but they make equipment and weapons for Aquileia and they are better than Skyfall or Herzlander junk"
>
> Fleur and Henri having conviction in the Aquileian way while omitting that FJA is only 40% of Aquileia is some posture?


**Codex:**
* **[[Moriset Discret's Aquileia/Coltbert Reforms]]** (History)
> **Note:** The origins of the FJA was from stealing from the urban griffons, but they redeemed themselves (should it be mentioned here? Maybe later... in Fraternity part 2)

### SCENE 24.3: The Battle of Ailmont
> **Synopsis:** Henri's town Ailmont is protected by a historical fortress on the border with Herzland called Verdame. Synovial took the fortress, it is a backdrop of the battle
>
> Henri insists that Applejack doesn't have to be at the tip of the spear anymore. This is his battle to win, it's Aquileia's battle, and it's Henri's hometown. Twilight flies with Rainbow Dash. Applejack sits on the command cloud with Henri and helps him with recon, giving a perspective of how Henri usually commands her.
>
> Trimmel is a mere tank commander, at the tip of the spear where Applejack usually is. He never asked for command of a unit. He provides recon, which is what Applejack usually does for Henri. Since Trimmel himself is usually the commander, he knows exactly what recon is helpful to Henri. Henri appreciates it but doesn't show it, but Applejack can tell.
>
> From the command cloud, Applejack watches as Henri directs the tanks to encircle. The changelings fire back, but magical shields prevent any damage. Trimmel's tank obliterates several enemy tanks in a magical explosion from the spellblast cannon. The wonderbolts wipe the enemy airforce, then dive bomb the changeling tanks who evaporate in magical explosions. They are like ants. It's like a video game. It's chilling.
>
> Trimmel's voice comes through the radio saying Synovial is surrendering. Several other tank commanders relay the same.
>
> Henri is jubilant. Applejack is quiet. (What's the right word for how Applejack feels?)

**Threads:**
* **[[Henri's Distaste of Trimmel]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Trimmel volunteers to be the tip of the spear (where Applejack usually is) as a humble tank commander and recon, not the commander he usually is. He puts his trust in Henri. Henri scoffs at him, but privately remarks to Applejack that he is flabbergasted that Trimmel agrees to be his subordinate.
>
> Since Trimmel himself is usually the commander, he knows exactly what recon is helpful to Henri. Henri appreciates it but doesn't show it, but Applejack can tell.


**Codex:**
* **[[Wings of Dew]]** (Technology)

### SCENE 24.4: Trimmel accepts Synovial's surrender
> **Synopsis:** "Gentlelings, you have fought like lions while being led by a donkey."
>
> You traitor to the hives, traitor to Vraks
> Traitor to Chrysalis, perhaps, but to myself? Not at all. To the Hives? To Vraks? That remains to be seen.
>
> Trimmel steps out of his tank hatch. The whole tank is shielded by the unicorn inside. Some changelings tell him in Herzlander not to be a coward and hide behind a pony's shield. Trimmel puts up a green shield and asks the unicorn to lower her shield. She says she won't because he is her comrade. Trimmel smirks.
>
> Applejack and Henri arrive in a shielded half track to formally accept Synovial's surrender. Twilight casts a detection spell and three changelings get revealed. One changeling tries to shoot Trimmel; he is killed. The second changeling surrenders and is spared. The third changeling makes a run for it, not wanting to surrender. Trimmel barks at him to stand down, then urges Synovial to tell him to stand down. Synovial gives the order. The runner doesn't stop. He is shot in the leg.
>
> Applejack remarks that they will need to keep the shields up until a supply truck arrives with inhibitor rings, since the changelings can't be trusted. Synovial bristles at the thought.

**Characters:**
* **[[Synovial]]** (Antagonist, Impact: CoreValueDemonstration)
> **Analysis:** He surrendered to save his men, proving he is rational (unlike the Changeling Vets). This makes the trial possible. Synovial even followed Trimmel's instruction to try and save the runner's life, despite Trimmel mocking him and their history of Synovial looking down on Trimmel.
>
> Synovial has more control over his loyal attaché than Trimmel had over predatory veterans. His attache is likely a bunch of old guard "friends". Some were even there back in 981. That's how exclusive the club is.


**Codex:**
* **[[AJ The Collaborator]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Synovial cackles seeing Applejack's title and reveals the parallel with Leonce du Roc The Lion of Verdame because of the VOPS profile
> That fortress back there is where he held back the Herzlanders, but he became a collaborator when Grover I came 26 years later with the Idol of Boreas. He locked King Discret in the dungeon to stop the king from ordering a suicide charge against Grover
>
> Then Henri laughs, saying Synovial thought he was Grover I, taking the fortress and advancing on Aquileia
>
> Synovial also meant it to call Applejack a whore (Aquileian Lioness)
> But the Aquileians view it as taming the beast. Applejack tamed the changelings at Tall Tale

### SCENE 24.5: Gaudreau informs Applejack that she is invited to deliver Synovial to his trial
> **Synopsis:** Verany suggests Applejack hand Synovial to the Griffonian Republic so that he can stand trial for "crimes against the republic" in 981 for atrocities committed during the Long March. Gaudreau agrees with Verany and convinces Applejack.
>
> Applejack asks what is SECEF's next assignment and Gaudreau tells her to rest. Applejack is dumbstruck.
>
> A return letter comes back (look into the magical messaging system griffon tech) saying Kemerskai is happy to accept Synovial, promising a fair trial, and wants Applejack and her commanders to give speeches prior to his trial. It's a griffonian tradition for the whole chain of command to be present for military ceremonies. The reason is because it's a mechanism to reward bold junior knights and let them gain clout and land a better marriage. Being a leader in the army (knightly order, or lord's retinue, or king's guard) isn't just about skill, it's also about decorum. So that means Applejack, Henri, and Trimmel are invited to give speeches in front of the entire republic at the Presidential Palace in Cloudbury.
>
> Applejack is reluctant. Gaudreau implores Applejack to go and establish diplomatic ties with Kemerskai. The rest of SECEF will rest in Ailmont, recuperate and celebrate their great victory. It's the Aquileian way. They get plenty of luxury wine and cheese from locals.
>
> it highlights the vanity and posture of flying the war criminal all the way over to the other side of the war for a performative trial and speeches. SECEF is a volunteer force to Aquileia specifically. AJ currently has no relationship with GR, but she has a deep relationship and debt to Aquileia thanks to the volunteers who taught them and led them to hold tall tale
>
> Applejack can't believe Gaudreau's orders are for her to go do a press conference in Cloudbury while her forces literally party and celebrate their victory. Isn't there a war to win? Aren't griffons dying?
> Gaudreau says you need to lighten up a bit! Go to Cloudbury and make some friends!

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]** (Protagonist)
> **Analysis:** Applejack can't believe Gaudreau's orders are for her to go do a press conference in Cloudbury while her forces literally party and celebrate their victory. Isn't there a war to win? Aren't griffons dying?


**Threads:**
* **[[Perception of the Griffonian Republic]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** Applejack is extremely dumbstruck. She scoffs at the farce.

* **[[Discovering the Elements of Liberty]]**
> **Update:** the "hidden element" parallel is emphasized by how SECEF went to Aquileia with the goal of "save their friends from the Reich". But after defeating Synovial's attaché in the field, the Aquileians tell Applejack to take a break and go to Cloudbury to fulfill Kemerskai's theatrical request to deliver Synovial and make a speech. Applejack hates the idea of giving a performative speech for a "perceived Herzlander Imperialist" (because of Aquileian bias). Applejack views it as a distraction from defeating the "evil Reich who funded Chrysalis, who Chrysalis copied". This is like Twilight's reluctance to "make friends" in Friendship is Magic part 1, viewing The directive as a distraction from researching the elements of harmony further
>
> In FiM Part 1, Twilight couldn't find the sixth element by searching for it because it wasn't an artifact — it was a relationship. The directive to make friends felt like a distraction from her real intellectual mission. The irony is total: her mission was the distraction. The friends she was reluctantly making were the elements she was urgently trying to find. She couldn't see this because she was looking outward for an external object when the thing she needed was already forming around her through the very activity she was dismissing.
>
> In TLTT, the parallel inverts by scale but preserves the structure perfectly. Applejack can't find the sixth element by fighting for it because it isn't a military objective — it's a civic reality. The directive to go to Cloudbury for Kemerskai's theatrical performance feels like a distraction from her real tactical mission. The irony is total: her mission was the distraction. The republic she was reluctantly being shown in Cloudbury was the thing she was urgently fighting to build. She couldn't see this because she was looking forward at the enemy when the thing she needed was already existing behind her in the society the war had made possible.


**Codex:**
* **[[Canon Episode References]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Friendship is Magic Part 1


---
### SCENE 24.6: They fly over Skyfall to Cloudbury
> **Synopsis:** Applejack, Twilight, Henri and Trimmel fly in a transport plane over neutral Skyfall to reach Cloudbury. Applejack witnesses the skyscrapers, factories, glamour, and the stolen Reich fleet in the harbor. It's the rugged individualist's paradise. It's more glorious than Canterlot and Manehattan combined.
>
> Applejack has a negative view of Skyfall throughout the whole story, it's like flim and flam to her. She heard bad things from before, like how Rockfeller got started from Skyfall, how the Manehattanites are from Skyfall, the Tzinacatl factory came from Skyfall investors...
>
> They reach Cloudbury, and it's also glorious skyscrapers and factories...

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** She feels a pit in her stomach from flying over the dense, smoggy city of the people she sold drugs to in order to enrich the ones living in the mansions in the heights above it


**Threads:**
* **[[Perception of the Griffonian Republic]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** Cloudbury looks like Skyfall, and Applejack already equates Skyfall with unchecked capitalism and their businessgriffs as the source of many Equestrian problems


**Codex:**
* **[[Skyfall Trade Federation]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Describe it as urban rot along with the heights for suburbs. Coltbert will break it down later

## CHAPTER 25: Fraternity
> **Chapter Note:** Maybe have a chapter split here and have Applejack spend some time in Aquileia (Pridea with Fleur? Twilight nerds out over the university. Maybe also meet her parents), and we learn more about the history of the Coltbert Reforms and the reality of the FJA-PNdA split (they are allies and symbiotic, one cannot exist without the other)

> Fraternity ends after Kemerskai invited them to his party room and says the Republic runs without me?

### SCENE 25.1: On the ground in Cloudbury
> **Synopsis:** It's gray and industrial. It looks soulless.
>
> But Henri notices something strange: He hears a griffon speaking Herzlander, which he understands, talking to another griffon talking a completely different language, Cloudburian. He sees they have translators, but can't imagine living like that.
>
> There's also social programs for housing and food, all in the name of freedom from the Reich

### SCENE 25.2: Synovial's trial
> **Synopsis:** Synovial AND his soldiers (whoever is left) are shipped off to Cloudbury. (In Cloudbury, Synovial will be on trial, plus any changelings who were with him back in 981. Since Synovial is a gilded dude, he's bound to have several bros still with him. The other changelings who don't have any crimes specifically committed on Herzland soil will simply be prisoners of war and fed. Even though it's plausible that they drained ponies in Equestria and other atrocities by virtue of their position, this cannot be proved by law. This should demonstrate that the machine's law is thoroughly ironclad.
>
> They get the new translators, Henri frowns at it
> Applejack tries speaking and Henri says oh, these are way better than the old models. I can hear your accent!
>
> The trial is boring and uneventful. Synovial is accused of overseeing the war crimes of his subordinates (draining prisoners to death)
>
> He doesn't offer any testimony and just accepts his sentence (because he expects a VOPS breakout)

### SCENE 25.3: Kemerskai gives the first bombastic speech
> **Synopsis:** They have the "glorious" press conference with Kemerskai, which is first contact. It's big, loud, bureaucratic, and very patriotic. Kemerskai gives the speech mocking the Reich for having allies as savage as Synovial.

**Codex:**
* **[[Herzland Republican Revolution of 978]]** (History)
> **Note:** Kemerskai says Gabriella Eagleclaw hired Synovial and his tanks to crush the Republic in the pivotal battle, and the Reich gave birth to Chrysalis's terror state

### SCENE 25.4: Applejack shudders at the fact that Chrysalis's role model is Kemerskai
> **Synopsis:** She's listening to Kemerskai's speech and is kind of rolling her eyes at all the performance. Shouldn't the truth speak for itself?
>
> She sees Trimmel on the edge of his seat, all ears. You like this stuff?
> He is honorable. I wish I was born here, not under Chrysalis
>
> "Huh." Applejack takes off the translator and suddenly Kemerskai yelling in Herzlander sounds like Chyrsalis to Applejack...
>
> Kemerskai's military equestrian voice through the universal translator is what makes AJ not like him. She has an epiphany that he literally actually sounds just like Chrysalis (who speaks fluent Equestrian and talks like that). And then second epiphany that it's the other way around. Chrysalis copied him! In Herzlander. She takes the translator off for a moment and hears the Herzlander guttural screaming voice (sounds like Hitler to us, put actual German in italics, an excerpt from a Hitler speech?? Maybe not word for word but the idea is there) which to AJ is indisguishable from Chrysalis because she doesn't understand the words!!
>
> Henri is leaning forward, hanging onto every word
> AJ asks Henri to take off the translator and asks him, "what does you hear?"
> He's confused, he says what are you talking about, it's still beautiful. He is a patriot, a true Republican. This is because Henri is fluent enough in Herzlander that he picks up on the meaning, that the speech is about civic virtues and Synovial's lack of them
>
> Is that not Chrysalis?
>
> Not at all, he speaks the opposite of her
> Henri says the griffons here a lot more eloquent than the "simplified" changelings
>
> AJ is still uneasy but takes Henri's word for it (especially since he was so against GR and the translator before. This also subverts Henri's demographic of being prone to propaganda, we know he is enlightened but his physical look tends to be the poser demographic. This is the leader he wish he met as a child
> Trimmel also enthralled because this is what he wished Chrysalis was (foreshadow that Trimmel will be the Kemerskai of the changeling lands later)
>
> This exchange between AJ and Henri should happen during the speech and we miss part of it, highlighting how long and performative it is
>
> She put the translator back on and listened.
> "... Continues"
>
> By Celestia, how much longer is this going to be?

**Characters:**
* **[[Henri Gourard]]**
> **Analysis:** Henri likes Kemerskai's speech. He also notices the Cloudburians in the crowd cheering. Maybe the GR isn't a Herzlander Imperialist state? Maybe not all Herzlanders are bad?
>
> He is a revolutionary intellectual by now. He knows political theory. He knows hate groups are just tools of the wealthy to divert class warfare into "us vs them" warfare. And yet, his lives experiences still cloud his perception of Herzlander culture, until this moment when he listens to Kemerskai.
>
> The GR is what his Supremacist group pretended to be (Strong/Proud) but without the racism.

* **[[Trimmel]]**

### SCENE 25.5: Private press conference on The Battle of Ailmont
> **Synopsis:** This narrative beat is a brilliant piece of misdirection. It uses the trope of "The Media" to play with Applejack’s prejudices against Kemerskai, only to subvert them with humor, and then subvert the humor with horror.
>
> Kemerskai should give a tongue and cheek warning about "the media" in between the speeches and the interview about tactics. AJ should feel disturbed, thinking he manipulates the narrative like a tyrant.
>
> Kemerskai: He is adjusting his medal rack. He looks annoyed.
> ◦Kemerskai: "General Applejack, a word. The press is out there. They are vultures. Watch your words. Do not let them bait you."
>
> •Applejack's Internal Monologue: She feels a chill. 'Watch your words.' That’s what dictators say. She assumes he means "Stick to the propaganda script or the secret police will get you."
> ◦AJ: "I only know how to speak the truth, Marshal."
> ◦Kemerskai: "Then Boreas help you. They will eat you alive."
>
> Then she finds out the problem - the reporters are insufferable. They ask stupid questions. Until the actual tactics come to light and the reporters and Kemerskai are horrified. This is where the script is flipped.
>
> The reporters are serious instead of annoying now. They talk about the magical anhilation and AJ says it's survival. But eventually they're is some genuine understanding as AJ and Trimmel convey the true horrors of changeling total war. But AJ decides they can't go on like this now that Synovial's attaché is gone.
>
> Griffon Perspective (The Fencing Match): War is a series of maneuvers, artillery exchanges, and flanking actions. It takes weeks. Casualties happen, but they are gradual.
> •SECEF Perspective (The Guillotine): Trimmel and Henri didn't "fight" Synovial. They removed him. ◦The Event: Magical CAS and Spellblast tanks vaporized the vanguard instantly.
> ◦The Horror: It’s not that they killed; it’s the efficiency. To a Griffon reporter, it looks like cheating. It looks like bringing a machine gun to a knife fight.
> ◦The Reaction: "You didn't offer them terms before you fired?"
>
> The Reporters: They shudder because it strips the "Glory" out of war. There is no glory in being vaporized by a purple beam from the sky before you can even load your rifle.

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]** (Impact: CoreValueChange)
> **Analysis:** Applejack realizes that she showed her strength. Now might be the time to be merciful before they become the monsters. (Connect to the lesson about the consequences of her actions/her power)
>
> Some of the reporters start interrupting with comments like "that's barbaric" and AJ realizes the startling truth that she is actually more of a monster than her perception of Kemerskai right now.

* **[[Queen Chrysalis]]**
> **Analysis:** 5 - Trimmel vocalizes it to Applejack, feeling ashamed now

* **[[Alexander Kemerskai]]** (Antagonist, Impact: CoreValueChange)
> **Analysis:** Kemerskai shudders at the description of the Battle of Ailmont, realizing that Applejack is not a soft and cuddly pony. He is humbled. He also realizes that he does not have Equestria's military capabilities.


**Threads:**
* **[[Perception of the Griffonian Republic]]** (Negative)
> **Update:** Kemerskai should give a tongue and cheek warning about "the media" in between the speeches and the interview about tactics. AJ should feel disturbed, thinking he manipulates the narrative like a tyrant.
>
> This restructuring creates a fantastic comedic beat that transitions into deep philosophical tension. It plays on Applejack's prejudice (expecting a Herzlander to be a Tyrant) and subverts it with the reality of a free, annoying press (Democracy).


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]** (Demonstration)
> **Commentary:** The reporters and Kemerskai are initially horrified by the recounting of the Battle of Ailmont. However, one reporter points out how the three of them were honest are bared their souls. The reaction in the room shifts from "They are barbarians" to "We take your word that it was a necessity from the war you fought and are currently still fighting against the changelings". They realize that these ponies and griffons walked through hell so the rest of the world wouldn't have to.

* **[[Stagnation can evolve into Harmonic Republicanism]]** (Demonstration)
> **Commentary:** It perfectly illustrates the Cultural Gap between a society at peace (or at least, a managed, civilized war) and a society emerging from an existential apocalypse. The consequence of stagnation is a rapid swing to the other extreme for survival. Society must be proactive instead. Always adapt to the new world/reality.

* **[[Strong to be Merciful]]**
> **Commentary:** Applejack realizes that she showed her strength. Now might be the time to be merciful before they become the monsters.

* **[[Embracing and Complementing Special Talents]]**
> **Commentary:** Kemerskai is humbled by the fact that, militarily, diverse specialization could go claw to claw with orderly superior firepower. And if that's the case for the military, it's certainly the case for the economy too. HIS world view (of Aquileia) experiences a crack.

### SCENE 25.6: Applejack gives a simple speech first
> **Synopsis:** It should be a quick life story of farm, friends, general, Lioness. Why she cares about Honesty (why is it, really?) She'll mention that Synovial called her "The Lioness of Tall Tale" and apparently that's an insult in Herzlander, but she'll take it in stride.
>
> "Well, he was right. I don't have grace. I don't know how to dance around a subject. I just know how to plant my hooves and hold the line. And if being a 'Lion' means protecting your friends when the wolves come knocking... then I reckon I'll take the name."
>
> She's floored by how all the griffons can understand her and cheer for her.
> **Outcome:** Henri sees that the translator actually works and the audience is moved by AJ's Equestrian speech. That's why he decides to give his life story passionately in Aquileian.

---
### SCENE 25.7: Henri gives a passionate speech about his life's story
> **Synopsis:** Henri tries to outdo Kemerskai. The crowd goes wild.
>
> Henri sees that the translator actually works and the audience is moved by AJ's Equestrian speech. That's why he decides to give his life story passionately in Aquileian.
>
> Henri's speech is his life story. This will be the first time he reveals the full story and the extent of his crimes against ponies (which really weren't much when compared against the backdrop of total war. The griffon supremacy group were a bunch of poseurs, as paramilitary strongman groups typically are)
>
> The Herzlander propaganda in Verenia appeals to Aquileian Henri's griffon side by saying he needs to purge Aquileian culture of ponies
> Pridea is the biggest example of ponies, e.g. policemare arresting griffon thugs who look like Henri's demographic (big loud griffon)
>
> Henri is big and strong. The griffon supremacists glazed him and said he must be better than those puny ponies with their stupid magic. Why are they rich and you're poor? Go knock them down a notch. You deserve it, because you're supposed to be big and strong. Also, the ponies are getting royal support from Discret (Coltbert reforms)
>
> He reveals how he repaid all the pony families he terrorized, which was months of labor (which he got fed for), and one family made him go to the University of Pridea to help their daughter with magical research.
>
> He joined a veterans club to actually learn how to be an honorable warrior
>
> That's how he got involved with the revolution in Pridea, where he volunteered as a tactical commander and integrated tactics with Fleur's magical inventions.
> He is a tactical commander because when he was a thug, he didn't actually do the physical vandilizing because he was too squeamish for it, but he planned and coordinated the operations and distractions so the other thugs could vandalize and terrorize
>
> It should also mention Moriset Discret and proto-FJA, or at least allude to it? Or is it already covered in the previous chapter?
>
> Henri didn't meet Fleur out of love at first sight. He didn't just apologize to the "pretty mare"; he apologized to everyone. He spent months fixing fences, repainting barns, and hauling debris for random pony families. It was boring, grueling, restorative justice.
> **Outcome:** Henri is no longer skeptical of the Griffonian Republic. He acknowledges them as different, but equally virtuous and forever an ally.

**Characters:**
* **[[Henri Gourard]]** (Protagonist, Impact: CoreValueChange)
> **Analysis:** He is stripping away the "Musketeer" glamour he built up for Applejack and showing her the ugly truth: "I was just like them (the Changelings). I was a bully who thought he was a king. And the Revolution saved me from myself."
>
> Henri has finally shed his bias against Herzlanders and their culture.


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** When he was a teenage thug harrassing pony farms and businesses, he was too cowardly to do damage so he would strategize how to distract the ponies so the other grunts could do the damage. He was the ultimate poseur - big blue griffon who didn't punch. It's also why he's a tactical commander, that's where he got those coordination skills
>
> Bullying is violence without consent/rules
> Dueling at the club is violence with strict consent and rules

* **[[Anti-Racism / Anti-Tribalism / Politics of Division]]** (Demonstration)
> **Commentary:** Henri was going to deliver a speech in broken Herzlander, but the Universal Translator allowed him to deliver his speech in passionate Aquileian. The crowd loved him. It shows that in the Griffonian Republic, where you are from matters less than who you are. There is no Herzlander imperialist bias like he once thought.
>
> Also, his speech reveals the full story of how he once was a griffon supremacist who harrassed ponies and grew out of it thanks to the hope of the republic.


**Codex:**
* **[[Universal Translator]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** The Universal Translator allows Henri to finally understand and accept Herzlanders as equals.

* **[[Moriset Discret's Aquileia/Coltbert Reforms]]** (History)
> **Note:** In Henri's speech, he mentions looking up the the chasseurs who liberated Verenia, and admits they were royalists
> This shocks Applejack, that Henri is admitting the Aquileian way was sponsored by royals

### SCENE 25.8: Trimmel tells his entire lifestory
> **Stakes:** Trimmel's thoughts: Henri just admitted he was a supremacist thug to the entire world. He trusted us with his shame. If I give the 'Safe Speech' now, I am a coward.
> **Synopsis:** Trimmel hears Henri's real voice in full form through the universal translator. Prior to this, he only heard Henri speak to him in broken Herzlander. He already respected Henri for tactics and being the commander. Also, now Trimmel realizes the universal translator will allow Henri to understand him!
>
> Trimmel matches Henri’s vulnerability with his own. He starts his speech with, "I was originally going to just talk about my history with Synovial and tell a story of the triumph of the drone over the aristocrat who stepped on the drone. It is a good story. It is the story you want to hear. But now... I am going to tell you the entire story."
>
> Not even AJ was expecting this.
>
> He starts with the storybook version of a jaeger, protecting the hives from manticore-tier predators
> Then says he wanted to train to be a jaeger but was denied. The reality is the jaegers are an elite club and drones can't join.
> Chrysalis showed up in Vraks with guns and strapped the old nobility to love harvesters, then offered Trimmel and his friends a place in the new order, in a Great Leap Forward
> He and his friends became jaegers and liberated the other hives in the name of Chrysalis, training other liberated drones to be jaegers too
> Meanwhile the other drones became bauleiters, building up the industrial economy
>
> The part about Stalliongrad, he changes to his revolutionary pony form and speaks Severyanan
> During this part, he talks about the "gang of four" which is Trimmel, Thranx, Elvir Roland and Ixodida who were the first and best neu-jaegers of honorable Vraks which Chyrsalis liberated them from the old jaegers and nobility
> They disguised themselves as ordinary ponies who took up arms, and due to their military tactical experience, naturally became leaders of local militias leading the fight against the boyars
>
> He talks about being an infiltrator in Canterlot and seeing the decadance of Equestria, Celestia neutering the ambition of the ponies, and he viewed Celestia as a tyrant in that sense
> He says he doesn't want to talk about the military glory of running a tyrant's army, but he must
>
> Trimmel says Ixodida was killed by a desperate pony civilian at the start of the war (dramatic irony for people who read The Harvest of Falldale, since Actia Pagala actually killed Ixodida. His reassignment of Pagala actually caused Ixodida's murder by Pagala...) I lied to myself and told myself that the pony must've been devoted to Celestia and it strengthened his resolve to end the war quickly
> But the war did not end quickly, and Applejack and her friends move the Equestrian war economy forward in a leap greater than the changelings could have ever done, because they used harmony instead of tyranny
> Trimmel talks about his capture, and Applejack granting him mercy
> Trimmel mentions finding out that Thranx was murdered by VOPS
> He got Elvir Roland to surrender to Applejack. He talks about the sad fact that he and Elvir were safer in a POW Camp in Tall Tale than Thranx was in Griffenheim.
> But he will not sit back in a POW camp. He will avenge Thranx and Ixodida, he will liberate Vraks, his home, and all the changelings from Chrysalis's tyranny!
>
> There will be a part of the speech near the end in native changeling with clicks. The universal translator won't pick up on it but the raw words will be preserved in the recording
>
> Maybe Kemerskai would put in some improvised additional remarks about how Trimmel is the future and Synovial is the evil past. This elevates AJ's view of Kemerskai. Would it be too crazy and bombastic for Kemerskai to say he will find a way to support Trimmel, during an improvised ending of a public press conference? Or would the griffons who love "The Republic" cheer even louder? Notice no teleprompter, or AJ can tell it's improvised by tone, or Trimmel says so with his VOPS training

**Characters:**
* **[[Trimmel]]**
> **Analysis:** Life story reveal
> Guilty - he forced a pacifist society to militarize. Now he must fix what he broke


**Threads:**
* **[[Perception of the Griffonian Republic]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** The crowd goes wild. AJ feels good. She thinks, Maybe this Republic isn't so bad.

* **[[Henri's Distaste of Trimmel]]** (Triumph)
> **Update:** Henri finally understands that Trimmel has nearly the same life story as him. Gaudreau rescued Henri early. Applejack gave Trimmel the same olive branch that Gaudreau gave him.

* **[[Discovering the Elements of Liberty]]**
> **Update:** Applejack's reaction to Trimmel throwing out his script. She shouldn't fully understand in the moment why it moved her so much. That unresolved feeling — why did that mean so much to me, when I've heard honest speeches before — is what carries forward into the plane ride and lands in Twilight's lap when they start reading Coltbert together. The epiphany is Twilight's to have, but the evidence is something Applejack felt and couldn't name.


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** The Prepared Speech: Was likely vetted by Kemerskai’s press office. It was going to be a standard "Meritocracy is great, Aristocracy is bad" speech. Safe. Political. Hearing Henri bare his soul about his Supremacist childhood and seeing AJ speak with zero filter. Trimmel realizes that sticking to the script would be Dishonest in the presence of the Element of Honesty. 
> "I stood here prepared to tell you a story about a Drone who defeated a Prince. It is a good story. It is the story you want to hear."
>
> (He looks at Applejack. She looks back, curious but steady.)
>
> Trimmel: "But the General taught me that a half-truth is a whole lie. So... I am going to tell you the entire story. The ugly parts. The parts where I believed the lie, and the parts where I helped build it."
>
> •AJ's Reaction: She is stunned. She holds her breath. She knows this could ruin him politically, but she also knows it is the bravest thing he has ever done.

* **[[Conscience]]**
> **Commentary:** Applejack's presence made performing feel impossible in the presence of the Element of Honesty
> It shows that Applejack's conscience is contagious. Trimmel didn't throw out his script because Applejack told him to. He threw it out because standing next to her made posturing feel like a lie. That is Conscience operating at the relational level. It propagates not through instruction but through presence.


**Codex:**
* **[[Universal Translator]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** The Universal Translator works perfectly even when Trimmel speaks Severyanan because it captures the same universal intent.
>
> The tech will allow the two wayward souls (Trimmel and Henri) to finally understand each other. The irony is that Trimmel and Henri use the tech to understand each other, but Applejack, who is already friends with both, doesn't like how the translator removes their equestrian accents, masking away their origins.

* **[[The Winter Revolution]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Trimmel reveals VOPS involvement in the communist revolution and his own personal involvement

### SCENE 25.9: Party, Kemerskai discusses his past
> **Stakes:** They are tense because AJ thinks Kemerskai is Chrysalis's model and doesn't like his posturing, and Kemerskai is scared of Applejack's magical might
> **Synopsis:** AJ is baffled, asking if he has a war to run. He explains that the country is running without him. AJ feels relief, seeing he's not a cult leader. Then Kemerskai expresses sorrow that AJ doesn't have this luxury and laments on the horrors of total war. He actually commits to working with the senate to approve some support if Equestria ends up at war with the changelings again. (Some aspect is realpolitik, other is genuine emotion after personally hearing the horrors of total war.)
>
> The reason Kemerskai is still in president is due to confidence in him. The legistature has had elections and debates since martial law ended. He is continuing to run for reelection for stability until the Reich is defeated, then he will step down (he does look forward to that day)
>
> Stuff about Celestia snub and asking why Twilight is here first
>
> I was born a noble, just like you
> Actually I wasn't a princess, I was a unicorn
> How did you get wings?
> Magic...
> But it's the same point, said AJ. We were appointed as elements of harmony, just like you were born a noble
>
> Twilight renounced her princess privilege and position on favor of a harmonic republic instead of ruling for the next 1000 years
> Kemerskai also renounced his noble title in order to lead the revolution for all griffons! His wife didn't approve and had to flee to Vedina with their 3 year old son, and that caused some resentment
>
> Applejack laughs and says Twilight does all the damage, I'm just the radiomare
> Applejack went from farmer to fake general to soldier to real general and soon will have to become president
> Kemerskai was supposed to be a farmer (overseer of the estate), chose to be a soldier, then had to become president
>
> Kemerskai will divulge his history with AJ and Trimmel during the private party so that AJ and Trimmel (as the prospective leaders of the Equestrian Republic and the liberated Changeling Lands) will know how to navigate the political world that they are about to have to navigate. Therefore, the chapter is going to consist of AJ, Henri, Trimmel (in public) and Kemerskai (in private) divulging their life stories.
>
> Kemeskai will talk about how he was once just an orator but took logistics seriously
>
> Cloudbury duke resorted to brutal tactics, razed fields and factories
> Cloudburian peasants joined the republican cause - this time, Kemerskai was determined to keep the alliance of rural peasants and factory worker revolutionaries alive for good
> They arrived in Cloudbury as liberators, the Cloudburians sacked their duke and welcomed us
> But then resource scarcity kicked in, as the land could not support both. It has always been the frozen north
>
> Kemerskai instituted marshal law and the flight of Sickleclaw in 986 (actually confirming Applejack's initial perception) plus Rosewing becoming a northern bandit then describes the great efforts to reform the republic.
>
> He talks about pouring resources into the universal translator development so that Herzlanders and Cloudburians could work together towards a shared future.
> Go through the development of the universal translator here
> They initially paid extortionist prices for crystals from the Sunstriker Mountains until they attacked with the buy in of the workers there in 995
>
> He also talks about the fall from grace of his friend Rosewing (who the rural northern Cloudburians turned to against Kemerskai's federal machine - Rosewing was an officer like Kemerskai) and how Kemerskai arrested him but gave him a fair trial. Rosewing is a free griffon and working some civilian job today.
> The Universal Translator was Kemerskai's political salvation. Before it, he was a foreign general barking orders. With it, he became 'Grandpa Kemerskai' to the Cloudburian youth. He used it to bypass the cynical Senate and appeal directly to the 'Forgotten Griffon' in the countryside, pressuring the legislature to pass the 'Economic Rights' bill
>
> Kemerskai absorbed the criticism of his friends and took on the burden in 985. He had already done this once in 978 when he executed his own father and his wife took his son to Vedina
> You killed your own father? Applejack shuddered. Do you regret it?
> If you want honesty, then No, said Kemerskai. He did not treat me or my brothers as sons, he viewed us as his pawns
> I do regret my wife leaving. She was a charity giver, that's how we met. In 978 I decided charity wasn't enough, and she left me for it.
> We did conquer Vedina in 1002. She didn't reunite with me, and I respected it. She still lives in Vedina. My son is here in Cloudbury, I worry he loves the Iron Marshal more than Dad.
>
> When Kemerskai talks about conditional love from his father based on results, Twilight and Trimmel connect it to how changelings are raised. Chrysalis copied the imperial patriarchy, but instead of just being a curse of nobility, it is for all drones
>
> Kemerskai had to use hard earned tax money to pay extortionary prices to warlords in the Sunstriker Mountains in order to get the crystals for the Universal Translator
> Once the design was finalized, they got buy in from the workers in those mines and invaded, liberating them in 994
> This gives Applejack some relief that the Skyfall drug deal had a precedent and the Republic turned out okay and cleaned up its mess

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** She realizes that Harmony isn't just about friendship (Trade). Sometimes, to protect the garden, you need a fence (Institutions).
>
> AJ learns that Herzland is NOT all standard Herzlander or Chrysalis's simplified version, and the universal translator was a means of shedding the dishonor of Imperialism
>
> AJ thought Kemerskai is a poser. In the end she is proved wrong
> The fact that Kemerskai went to great lengths to get them to deliver Synovial across a war zone is why AJ thinks he's a poser or a tyrant. But the posture is part of the practicality
> There should be a joke that Synovial gets such special treatment, better than how Chrysalis treats him
>
> AJ ends up enjoying Cloudbury a lot and having faith in democracy
> Also sees a need to defend Aquileian way of life by getting Equestria in the pact
> Kemerskai "absorbed the criticism." He lives with the fact that people call him a tyrant, knowing he saved them.
> Applejack, as the Element of Honesty, wants a "Perfect Truth."
> Kemerskai teaches her that Leadership is taking the blame. There will be backlash. Traditionalists will hate her. Celestia will be disappointed.
>  She realizes she can live with being called a "traitor" by the Crown, as long as she knows she is a "Savior" to the People. This is the death of her "People Pleaser" flaw.
>
> Kemerskai's story about needing to be the Iron Marshal and it coming from Coltbert (therefore Henri is in agreement, it's a consensus between the Herzlander and the Aquileian) relieves Applejack of the guilt of her "Nightmare Moon" phase from the moment she put on "The Lioness of Tall Tale" on her uniform after her talk with Trimmel to the Canterlot paradrop to the Vanhoover liberation. She doesn't have to do that again. The next phase is the democracy phase because the resource scarcity and cultural unity of Equestria is already solved. Applejack's goals are to maintain those prerequisites and not let the rot set in from the war or backslide to celestia's Stagnation.
>
> Applejack’s anxiety stems from Celestia’s accusation that the "Lioness" persona is a transformation into a monster (Nightmare Moon). Kemerskai teaches her that the "Lioness" is actually scaffolding.
>     Celestia’s View (The Trap): Celestia believes that once you pick up the sword (Authoritarianism/Force), you can never put it down. To her, the "means" define the "ends." If you act like a Nightmare, you are a Nightmare.
>     Kemerskai’s Reality (The Tool): Kemerskai shows that Martial Law was a specific tool used to build a specific structure (Economic Security).
>         He proves that you can put the sword down. The fact that he instituted elections in 996 and is preparing to step down for Sunglider proves that the "Lioness" phase is not a terminal illness; it’s a construction phase.
>     Applejack’s Relief: This de-fangs the "Nightmare Moon" accusation. She realizes she isn't sliding down a slippery slope to evil; she is simply doing the "dirty work" required to stabilize the foundation. Once the foundation is set (The Republic), she can take off the "Lioness" armor.
>
> this invigorates Applejack's resolve to return to Equestria and begin the referendum process, which is another reason why she decides to take SECEF home and just donate the weapons
>
> Applejack thought a leader had to be an absolutist (Celestia, Chrysalis, Trimmel). When she refused the uniform, it was because she thought she didn't deserve it. When she put on the uniform, she thought she had to carry the burden. Now she is learning from Kemerskai that the leader is not an absolutist. A president is just the leader who leads the rest. She was being a healthy president all along. She doesn't have to go back to the iron marshal phase.

* **[[Henri Gourard]]**
> **Analysis:** Henri validates the effectiveness of Kemerskai's posturing by discussing his own fall by less virtuous posers
>
> Seeing Henri nod along and shed his own prejudices confirms to Applejack that she isn't just hearing self-serving propaganda. She is hearing historical fact.

* **[[Alexander Kemerskai]]**
> **Analysis:** Kemerskai introduces some posture to his personality out of necessity
>
> Kemerskai thinks AJ is a soft and cuddly pony, ready for a return to the stagnation of harmony. He is proved very wrong
>
> Kemerskai using a Royalist’s critique to dismantle his own democratic ideals in order to save them—establishes him as one of the most complex figures in your lore. He is the "Sin Eater" of the Revolution.
>
> Kemerskai is not a tyrant; he is a Martyr of Reputation. He consistently destroys his own happiness/image to do what is necessary.


**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** Kemerskai asks if Twilight is the Princess which is why she's here. Twilight says officially I'm General Applejack's bodyguard, but I'm also HER princess, not THE princess. But no one can get to her when she's between my hooves <3
>
> Then Kemerskai mentions how Celestia ignored him, which is why he doesn't like princesses
>
> Then Kemerskai talks about his wife and son, not sure how to handle this
> Do I go the Roosevelt path where the wife is estranged? Need to look at the focus about his wife.

* **[[Perception of the Griffonian Republic]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Applejack meets the real Kemerskai behind all the performance and posturing. She realizes the republic is perfect for the griffons it was built by and built for. All the misconceptions are cleared.
>
> The Griffonian Republic is essentially one giant, hardened family (The Long March Survivors). Their solidarity is not soullesness, it is barn-raising on a national scale.

* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
> **Update:** Martial law, the original visionary tyrant

* **[[Establishing the Equestrian Republic]]** (Positive)
> **Update:** Applejack realizes that Harmony isn't just about friendship (Trade). Sometimes, to protect the garden, you need a fence (Institutions). Now her resolve to challenge Celestia's stagnation has returned.
>
> It's not just going to be a governing structure. The Republic and pact between republics is fundamentally about establishing a shared burden of managing the griffon's/world's ambition, to contrast Luna/Nightmare Moon's singular burden and Celestia's walled garden to keep the ambition out.
>
> He warns Applejack not to make his mistake. "Do not trust the 'Moderate' Monarchist (Celestia/Blueblood) to protect your Republic. They will tolerate you in war and dismantle you in peace."
>
> Equestria won't fall into the traps of the 1st Herzland and Aquileian revolutions. It is already primed to succeed, according to Kemerskai and Henri's consensus.

* **[[The Meaning of Leadership]]**

**Themes:**
* **[[Generosity and Equity]]**
> **Commentary:** Kemerskai's wife gave naive charity, but it wasn't enough

* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** Kemerskai introduces some posture to his personality out of necessity
>
> They don't lie about griffons being greedy. They ensure the need to be better serves the common good
> They funnel collective ambitions to be better than the Herzland who kicked them out, and aspire to conquer their former overlords

* **[[Stagnation can evolve into Harmonic Republicanism]]**
> **Commentary:** Kemerskai rejects Stagnation (Celestia) and Predation (Chrysalis). His philosophy is 'Constant Maintenance.' Liberty is like a heated road in winter; if you stop powering it for one hour, the ice (Tyranny) returns. This justifies the high taxes and constant activity.

* **[[Isolationism is Immoral]]**
> **Commentary:** The annexation of Vedina in 1002 is the perfect litmus test for the difference between Imperialism (Chrysalis/Celestia’s fear) and Integration (Kemerskai’s reality).
>     The "Imperialist" Narrative (Celestia/Henri):
>         They look at a map. They see borders changing. They see a Republic absorbing a Kingdom.
>         Conclusion: "They are conquering distinct cultures just like the Empire. They are power-hungry."
>     The "Economic" Reality (Kemerskai):
>         Vedina was "Harmonic" but "Stagnant." They were likely suffering from their own version of the Predator’s Dilemma (isolation/scarcity).
>         By absorbing them, Cloudbury didn't erase them; it gave them Universal Translators, the trade routes, and the industrial base.
>         Result: A Vedinan citizen today is fed, warm, and understood. A Vedinan citizen in 1001 was free but freezing.
>     The Takeaway: Applejack learns that Celestia hyper fixates on "Sovereignty" even when it leads to suffering (we cannot police the world). Applejack decides that results (quality of life) matter more than the aesthetic of independent borders.

* **[[Rugged Individualism / Wedge Issues Deconstructed]]** (CentralConflict)
> **Commentary:** Kemerskai is extremely performative and bombastic because he has to compete with "posers" like the rich industrialists who flaunt their wealth and sold them out, and nobility like Eagleclaw, and any big and tough male griffon who thinks he can preach to vulnerable male peasants (like Henri once was) to abuse female peasants and divide the commoners against each other (like Andrew Tate and the "manosphere" in the online world in 2025)
>
> He is fighting against a "Manosphere" style narrative peddled by the elites. The Industrialists and Eagleclaw are telling young, poor male griffons: "You aren't poor because the system is rigged; you are poor because you aren't 'Alpha' enough. Dominate your wife, hate the Federalists, and act tough." He has to be more masculine, more loud, and more aggressive than them to win over those young males. If he acts like a calm intellectual, he looks weak compared to the "Rugged Individualist" fascists.
>
> The Long March: When the Barons sold out, Kemerskai realized that Unregulated Greed (Rugged Individualism) was just as dangerous as Feudalism.
> •Cloudbury: He built the new state in the north by purging the Rugged Individualists. He established strict laws (The Federal Machine) to ensure that the Industrialists served the State, not the other way around.


**Codex:**
* **[[Herzland Republican Revolution of 978]]** (History)
> **Note:** Full history of bourgeois betrayal unveiled here

* **[[Universal Translator]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Allowed Kemerskai to speak directly to the Cloudburians

* **[[Clothing in Equestria and Griffonia]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Kemerskai's "Clothing for All" mandate is a Civil Rights Act. It legally defines every citizen as "Inviolable." He isn't just building roads; he is enforcing Bodily Autonomy. He contrasts his centralized, bureaucratic federal state as a necessary defense against the betrayal of the textile barons.

* **[[The Predator's Dilemma (Coltbert's Paper)]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Kemerskai read the paper, and it's why he instituted martial law in 986. He lost the support and trust of his friends Sickleclaw and Rosewing, but accepted the burden and built the industry and universal translator, which allowed him to resume elections in 996.
>
> He is deeply struck by the Aquileian royalist saying Democracy is not a magic spell, it's a privilege that comes on top of industrial/economic stability and cultural connection
> Instead of getting angry at the royalist, Kemerskai internalizes the critique
>
> The Failure of Good Intentions (981–986): Even with a population of "good" people, Democracy failed. Why? Because of Coltbert’s Law: Morality is a function of Capacity. Due to resource scarcity, the predator's dilemma was settling in.
> Refugees were starving.
> The "Natives" of Cloudbury resented the "Invaders." despite being liberated of their lords
> Neighbor turned on neighbor not because they were evil, but because they were hungry.
>
> Sickleclaw/Skynavia = purism, form over function, premature democracy
> Rosewing = disillusionment -> banditry and tribalism (must take from others to help ourselves)
>
> Most Democrats looked at Coltbert’s paper and saw an attack: "He says we can't govern!"
> Kemerskai looked at the paper and saw a manual: "He’s not saying we CAN'T govern; he’s saying we can’t govern WHILE POOR."
>
> Coltbert argued that the King (or the State) must treat subjects as "Assets" to be nurtured, not exploited.
>
>     The Feudal Lords (Kemerskai’s father) viewed sons/peasants as disposable assets (Cannon Fodder).
>
>     The Rugged Individualists viewed workers as extractable assets (Labor).
>
>     Kemerskai (Post-986) viewed the Citizens as Capital Investments. You don't starve your investment. You feed it, educate it (Translator), and build it up.

* **[[Banking]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Kemerskai studied Coltbert's paper and the Skyfall and Aquileian banking systems and determined that money is a social contract, not a shiny rock
> Skyfall market fiat works, but it's amoral. It relies on trade and piracy and serves the oligarchs.
> Coltbert's 1 to 1 conversion to gold is a lie to serve the king's ego and punish the bourgeois.
> His Conclusion: "We cannot base our survival on Gold (which we don't have) or Piracy (which we despise). We must base it on Us."
>
> Kemerskai instituted martial law and abolished the gold standard in the republic. He and the legislature passed laws saying from now on, money is backed by tax obligations. To make the financial reforms work and build an industrial base, there will be a pseudo-command economy with price controls and directed investment in order to prevent a collapse. The pseudo-command economy is a rejection of Skyfall's unchecked greed and free market.
>
> Sickleclaw and Rosewing abandoned the republic, but the majority believe in Kemerskai (idealist refugees and liberated Cloudburian peasants) and he articulates everything through speeches.
>
> The republic will print money to buy productive capacity, paying the salaries of workers who work on infrastructure and giving out loans to griffons who want to build firms.
> Taxes serve a dual purpose - a drain to prevent inflation, and creating demand for the dollar, since everyone must pay taxes in the dollar otherwise they go to jail.
>
> There are price controls and rationing to prevent price gouging and inflation on the new federal currency during martial law. At the end of the 10 year period, there is enough bread being produced that the price controls can be dropped. Since supply exceeds demand, the market now serves the customers instead of the owners by keeping prices down.
>
> Trimmel now sees exactly how Chrysalis became the "Visionary Tyrant."
>     She sees that he forced value into existence. He didn't find gold; he pointed a gun at the economy and said, "Work."
>     Her Critique: She thinks he is weak because he eventually gave up the gun (ended Martial Law and restored democracy). She believes the "Visionary Tyrant" phase was his peak, and his return to democracy is his decline. She picks up the gun (MEFO bills/War Economy) where he put it down.
>
> Sickleclaw left to Skynavia because as an agrarian communalist he believes the land belongs to those who till it. The state directing things is another form of slavery.
>
> Rosewing become a disillusioned Robin Hood militia leader in the rural north because he hates corruption and lies, and thinks Kemerskai's fiat currency and martial law are too similar to the tools of Skyfall and the emperor. Rosewing becomes a warlord of justice who thinks the dream is dead and only tribalism will work. "I can't trust the State, so I will only trust my platoon and my neighbors."

* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Kemerskai left his wife and son behind to build the future. It's like what Applejack's parents did.
>
> They live in Vedina without him, even after Vedina was integrated into the Republic. He did not force them to Cloudbury.
>
> Applejack realizes that Progress is often resented by the people it saves.
>
>     Her parents saved her from poverty/stagnation, and she resented them for it.
>
>     Kemerskai saved his son from serfdom/stagnation, and his son resents him for it.
>
>     The Foreshadowing: This prepares Applejack for the backlash in Equestria. She realizes that even if she saves the ponies (via Harmonic Capitalism), they might hate her for destroying the "Innocence" of the Cutie Mark stagnation. And she has to be okay with that.

### SCENE 25.10: Education System
> **Synopsis:** How do you get all these griffons to get a long and work hard?
>
> Kemerskai says their education system
> Standard curriculum, but the mantra is No Griffon left Behind
> He says Imperial Herzland once factories came about started schools based on the factory model
> Cubs sorted by date of manufacture, 12 grades, if you fail you work or become a scoundrel
> The griffonian republic had a fresh start. They teach for mastery, not a set number of years
> You take as long as you need to master, then get a job and be productive and patriotic
>
> Won't cubs be lazy? Applejack asked
> Then they're missing out on money and freedom! Kemerskai laughed. Most griffons work hard and finish early
> Griffenheim was crime ridden from the dropouts and those who were wronged. The rich built exclusive neighborhoods to keep out the low lifes. We don't do that here. The rich cubs go to the same schools as the average cub and the immigrants and refugees. We run the same schools in newly integrated areas. No griffon left behind.
>
> Twilight takes furious notes
>
> Trimmel realizes Chrysalis took the worst of both worlds. There's a hard cutoff where all the failing drones become permanent workers or conscripts. Once you get into engineering or military school you are given as much time as you need to finish the curriculum. Extreme care for the best, destitution for those not good enough

## CHAPTER 26: Dilemma
> **Chapter Note:** Need to determine the order of Astler, Sickleclaw and Rosewing that makes the best progression and message
>
> Astler is about forward-looking idealism. Applejack and Twilight realize how privileged and ignorant they once were and feel crushed by Applejack's parents' adult choices, but Astler tells them don't wallow in the past, look towards th efeture
>
> Sickleclaw is about saying Celestia's way isn't obsolete, even some griffons want simple lives. That doesn't make them prey animals
>
> Rosewing is about rehabilitation/reconciliation, universal love and respect (whether you believe in Boreas or the Republic) and breaking down barriers

### SCENE 26.1: Private Party with Kemerskai
> **Synopsis:** Then Kemerskai cracks out the drinks and goes loud and jovial. Henri is all in, seeing that Kemerskai is truly a griffon to respect and just as loud and jovial as himself. He is finally free of hate of Herzlanders.
>
> Trimmel is more moody. This might be the first time he ever realizes that changelings copied Herzland's weapons without their rules of war. Total war has always been the changeling reality. But suddenly, Henri says he understands Trimmel now and offers an olive branch (a drink). He says they are not so different after all.
>
> Applejack sees them all getting along and is really happy, and happy that the GR is not what she thought. Through the whole thing, she has her universal translator taking the Herzlander into Equestrian. Henri has been speaking his native Aquileian the whole time because he can be more bombastic in Aquileian, not that he isn't bombastic in Equestrian or Herzlander. But Applejack does notice that while Henri's Aquileian translated into Equestrian does still have his accent, it is still not the same as Henri speaking Equestrian with his Aquileian accent. The aspects that make the voice "Henri" are still there, which is the remarkable part. But the aspects that make the voice "Aquileian" are gone, as if magicked away.

**Threads:**
* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
> **Update:** Applejack asks Kemerskai why the Universal Translator has Equestrian. He says Equestrian businessponies are in Skyfall, plus some Nova Griffonians speak Equestrian. Applejack never knew that Equestrian business ponies did business overseas. This is a hint that Chrysalis spurred them


**Codex:**
* **[[Universal Translator]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Henri's means of speaking Equestrian contains two custom flairs: A "Henri" factor which is the way he talks, boisterous and bombastic, and an "Aquileian" factor which features Aquiliean renderings of Equestrian sounds (like the iconic one: th becomes s or z). When AJ hears Henri speaking to her in Equestrian, she hears both flairs. When Henri speaks Aquileian, what AJ hears in the translator is Equestrian with the "Henri" flair, but NOT the "Aquileian" flair.
>
> Henri can scream in Aquileian and be understood by a Vedinan. It fulfills the promise of the Republic: "Your background does not limit your reach."
> Applejack finds it uncanny. She hears the "Henri" factor (The Soul), but misses the "Aquileian" factor (The Heritage).
>
> •The Thematic Clash: Aquileia (Applejack’s Preference): Values the Friction. The struggle to understand an accent is part of the bonding process. It validates the origin of the speaker.
> Griffonian Republic (Kemerskai’s Preference): Values the Flow. Friction is inefficiency. They engineered a solution that keeps the passion but removes the barrier. It is "Optimized Harmony."
>
> Conclusion: The GR checks all the boxes for a "Good Society," but to AJ, it feels like listening to a remastered album where the crackle of the vinyl has been scrubbed out. It’s "too clean."


---
### SCENE 26.2: AJ and Henri discuss his past at the party
> **Synopsis:** Henri should mention in the speech that he went to EVERY pony family that he vandalized. Most of them had them do a simple job to repay them. Only Fleur's parents gave him a more complex one: go help our daughter at the University.
>
> Fleur was giggling because she knows Henri's real story about once being a poseur but outgrowing it, but Applejack doesn't. And then, at the party with Kemerskai way, way later, Applejack thinks back to the first time Henri used "Poseur" at the Star Energy factory. And she will realize that Henri and Fleur were hooking up right behind her back (because at this point she knows about their long-standing Aquileian-style relationship).
>
> When AJ brings it up in the party, Henri mentions that he and Fleur were originally just going to talk about new military tech (because she is a Star Energy engineer and he is a tactical commander) but AJ getting Henri to talk about why he was in Tall Tale, and him saying "because they are poseurs", made Fleur giggle and give him "the look". But since AJ is busy contemplating poseurs and wondering if she is one, and is unaware of Aquileian passion culture, she doesn't see "the look"; the narrative doesn't mention it, only a light giggle. All the narrative mentions is Fleur saying, "Come, Henri, we need to talk about the new tech and tactics" and the narrative specifically mentioning that they left in a hurry. AJ doesn't think twice.
>
> Fleur's giggle is an inside joke. Henri rants about changelings being poseurs and that he's here to stop them. Fleur knows that it's because Henri used to be a poseur due to Herzlander influence, just like the changelings. It takes one to know one. She is proud that he isn't that griffon anymore. He is being a hyprocrite, but a righteous one.
>
> Applejack finally understands the inside joke about Henri's past.
>
> She realizes that while she was standing there pondering Henri's words about poseurs, Fleur and Henri were sharing a deeply intimate look and sneaking off to express their "Red Love." She feels like a fool, but it underscores how blind she was to the concept of passion.

**Threads:**
* **[[Fleur & Henri's Backstories]]**

**Themes:**
* **[[Intimacy and Liberty]]** (Motif)
> **Commentary:** Applejack was so distracted by Henri calling the changelings poseurs and contemplating whether she was a poseur or not that she completely missed Fleur and Henri flirting


**Codex:**
* **[[Moriset Discret's Aquileia/Coltbert Reforms]]** (History)
> **Note:** Henri admits the truth, that the FJA volunteers, the Coltbert reforms, the Aquileian way, all started from royal roots. It's just that they own it now
> (Should this come before the "conditioning for president" accusation or after?)
