### SCENE 26.3: The Scene
> **Synopsis:** She acts like she was caught and must submit because he's so big and powerful
> He plays along by fumbling undressing her, and then fumbling undressing himself
> It's a symphony of mutual teasing and drawing it out, both mocking the pretend poseur
> Then when the posseur is finally ready to take her, she lightly bucks him away, showing how the "captured" earth pony wasn't even in danger after all the whole time
> Henri recovers and changes his attitude, flipping the script by dropping the poseur persona for a passionate griffon of conviction. He says "you know what? I think you're the one who wants something from me, not me wanting something so desperately from you" and he pins her down again. "How about you tell me what you want."
>
> Afterwards, he's holding her. He's caressing her body with his paws, she's caressing his wings with her hooves. Their tails are intertwined.
> I'm so glad to be the real thing (an Aquileian revolutionary) and not a posseur, Henri jokes 
> And then the biggest twist: Fleur says she's also glad she's the real thing, an Aquileian pony. Equestrian ponies don't know what they're missing, and it's a tragedy.
>
> On the surface level, Fleur is in Tall Tale working with Star Energy to protect the ponies here. Henri is here to stop the changeling posseurs.
> They remind each other that they aren't in Tall Tale just to save the Equestrian ponies from the changeling horror machine. They are both here to spread the ideals of the 2nd Aquileian revolution and all its benefits (it's about unlocking the ambition of the Equestrian ponies in a healthy way. Industrial ambition to complement special talent without greed or obsolescence. Sexual ambition for pleasure between loving partners without predatory behavior.)
> These are spoilers for the story, thus it should be read after Henri's tell all speech in Cloudbury.
>
> It ends with: so what are we supposed to do about tech and tactics?
>
> Context: Henri and Fleur in the factory. Theme: Deconstructing Fear through Play.
>
> This scene works because it reclaims the narrative of "The Predator."
> •The Changeling/Posseur: Uses the threat of capture to take love. It is non-consensual and parasitic.
> •The Aquileian Roleplay: Henri and Fleur act out the "Capture," but because it is safe and consensual, it mocks the Changelings. It proves that Power isn't scary when it is held by someone you trust.
>
> The "Twist" (Ideological Victory): When Fleur says, "Equestrian ponies don't know what they're missing," she frames sexual liberation not as "sinful" (as Celestia might fear) but as Human (Pony) Flourishing.
> •Celestia protects ponies by keeping them innocent (Childlike).
> •The Aquileian Republic protects ponies by empowering them to be adults (Lion/Eagle).
>
> Ending Beat ("Tech and Tactics"): The transition from afterglow back to work is crucial. It shows that their passion isn't a distraction from the revolution; it is the fuel for it.
>
> Henri: "We fight so every pony can have this freedom. Now... what are we supposed to do about tech and tactics?"
>
> We cannot show the Equestrians this side of the revolution until they are ready
> They will run back to the nursery

**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** Deconstructing fear through play

* **[[Intimacy and Liberty]]** (CentralConflict)
> **Commentary:** Fleur's twist

### SCENE 26.5: Rikard Astler at the Industrial Bakery
> **Synopsis:** The workers do not eat "state gruel".
> The bakery perfects the traditional Cloudbury Meat Pies and sells them hot to all the workers coming in.
>
> It isn't a Michelin-star Aquileian masterpiece, but it is excellent. It is hot, perfectly seasoned, and incredibly dense. Astler proves that if you treat the workers with absolute dignity, you do not need "Special Talents" to produce greatness. You just need a good system and mutual respect. By using Astler—a young, ideological product of the Republic—you establish that "Standardized Excellence" isn't a temporary war measure forced by Kemerskai. It is a permanent, viable culture that successfully redeems the industrial machine from the greed of Skyfall and the cruelty of the Empire.
>
> The "Solidarity Mess Hall"
>
> This is the Republic's answer to the Aquileian Parloir and the Skyfall Club.
>
>     The Contrast: In Aquileia, the FJA Master Chef cooks to prove he is better than you, and you eat to prove you are cool enough to be there. In Skyfall, you eat to prove you are rich.
>
>     The Republic Reality: In Cloudbury, President Kemerskai, a frontline soldier, and a factory worker all sit at the same long wooden table in a massive, brightly lit hall. They all eat the exact same meal—a perfect cut of cured pork, a slice of dark rye bread, and a pint of living ale.
>
>     The Vibe: It is loud, egalitarian, and fiercely proud. The cooks are paid excellent wages. There is zero ego, only absolute execution.
>
> the Herzlander refugees and Nordic peasants took the industrial factory—the very thing Skyfall uses to crush souls—and turned it into a machine for Dignity.
>
> The Republic proves that you don't need a "Special Talent" or an "Ego" to make a masterpiece. You just need to care about the griffon sitting next to you. This provides Applejack with the final blueprint for her Equestrian Republic: take the vibrant individual magic of Aquileia, but anchor it to the unshakeable, standardized solidarity of the North.
>
> Trimmel (The Logistician): He is mesmerized. Trimmel spent his career in the Changeling Hive trying to achieve exactly this level of efficiency, but Chrysalis relied on fear and exhaustion to get it. Astler shows Trimmel that you can achieve perfect military-industrial logistics through Solidarity and Fair Wages instead of terror. It shatters Trimmel's belief that tyranny is necessary for efficiency.
>
> Twilight (The Engineer): She looks at the magical/mechanical pressure cookers and the exact temperature controls. She realizes that the magic here isn't used to make someone "special" (like a Cutie Mark); it is used to guarantee consistency. It is the ultimate application of her "Science of Magic."
>
> Henri looks at the massive bakery and sneers that it lacks "Passion" and "Asset Specificity." He argues that Coltbert's system is better because it allows a genius to become a millionaire and earn a "Seal."
>
> Astler’s response isn't about state control; it's about Dignity.
>
>     Astler's Rebuttal: "Your Coltbert is obsessed with geniuses. But what about the griffon who isn't a genius? What about the griffon who just wants to turn a wrench, go home, play with his kids, and not be treated like a loser? In Aquileia, you make the 'ordinary' griffon feel invisible. In Skyfall, they starve him. Here, we pay him a living wage and call him a Citizen. You don't need a 'Special Talent' to deserve a good life."
>
> This is the exact refutation of the "Aquileian Ambition Barrier." Astler proves that a society doesn't need to be fueled by Ego and Cutie Marks to be great.
>
> Solving the Predator's Dilemma via "The Union"
>
> Coltbert's Predator's Dilemma states that to survive a predator, you must make yourself a unique, indispensable asset (Asset Specificity). If you are generic, you get eaten.
>
> Astler provides the Democratic Socialist counter-argument: Collective Bargaining.
>
>     Astler realizes that not everyone can be a unique genius. Some jobs are inherently generic (shoveling coal, basic assembly).
>
>     If a single generic worker faces a Skyfall Tycoon, the worker gets eaten.
>
>     But if ten thousand generic workers form a Union, they become an Indigestible Mass.
>
>     Astler tells Twilight: "Coltbert teaches you to grow thorns so the wolf won't bite you. I teach the sheep to form a union. A wolf can eat one sheep; he cannot eat a union."
>
> This is the moment Applejack's entire worldview crystallizes.
>
> She looks at Astler's operation and realizes it is the industrialized, legal version of what her parents did with Standard Agricultural Amalgamated (SAA) and the EEEE! movement in Manehattan. Her parents treated their workers like family and paid them well, relying on personal morality. Astler has codified that morality into Law.
>
>     From Aquileia: She will take the innovative drive and the celebration of Special Talents (Cutie Marks).
>
>     From Equestria (Celestia): She will take the fundamental kindness and the baseline of Harmony.
>
>     From Astler (The GR): She will take the Structural Safety Net. She realizes that "Harmony" cannot just be a feeling in your heart; it must be written into the labor laws.
>
> Applejack leaves Cloudbury knowing exactly what the Equestrian Republic must be: A society where the exceptional are free to soar (Aquileia), but where the floor is raised so high that the "ordinary" pony never has to fear hitting the ground (Astler).

**Characters:**
* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]**
> **Analysis:** Astler says don't regret your privilege or feel crushed about the scale of global injustice, just look towards improving the future and do your part, all creatures are in it together
> This is the ultimate answer to her guilt of failing to fix Ain Trotgourait, and that the future will be different


**Codex:**
* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Rikard Astler remembers eating SAA cans when he was a teenager
> Many griffons are permanently stunted and can't fly
> Standardized industrialization gives them dignity
> No griffon left behind
>
> Applejack is devasted by how she was annoyed her parents were mashing up bad apples into flavorless paste when those calories provided nourishment to the world
>
> Astler says don't regret your privilege or feel crushed about the scale of global injustice, just look towards improving the future and do your part, all creatures are in it together


---
### SCENE 26.6: Bessemer Steel
> **Synopsis:** "Your father banned the bessemer process, claiming it was soulless. But look at Severyana! The ponies are using our science to build an empire in the ice. Are Griffons to be outpaced by herbivores?" Grover IV, desperate to escape his father's shadow and prove Griffon superiority, lifts the bans. The Griffonian Gilded Age begins.

**Codex:**
* **[[Grover IV's Gilded Age (914 to 970)]]** (History)
* **[[Pre-Revolution Severyana]]** (Unset)

---
### SCENE 26.7: Meeting Sickleclaw and Rosewing the day after the Party
> **Synopsis:** They find Sickleclaw and Rosewing and hear from them at a veterans club
> Cloudbury soldiers rotate from the front for rest and rejuvenation
>
> Sickleclaw said he wanted the farmer to own the land with no state intervention
> He got 15% of the vote in the very first election in 978 while Kemerskai got 77%
>
> Kemerskai immediately abolished slavery, serfdom, and servitude. Improved welfare reforms, industrial changes like minimum wage laws and steep taxation of nobles and oligarchs. Policies massively popular
>
> Then the bourgeois betrayed them
>
> Kemerskai's martial law and industrialization made him flee to Skynavia to seek out new land to live out his dream
> Ultimately Skynavia voted to join the republic peacefully in 1005
>
> the FJA (Aquileia) are the "Petty Bourgeois" farmers who want to own their land and sell goods. In the GR notes, the rural peasants wanted socialism. This highlights a fundamental cultural difference you can exploit:
>     Aquileian/Equestrian Peasants: Want Market Access (to sell their terroir goods).
>     Cloudburian/Interriver Peasants: Wanted Communal Ownership (to survive the harsh north/nobles).
>
> Agrarian Communism was a survival instinct against predators. They are not even at the stage of considering terroir/pride/export. It is subsistence farming. That's the default state with no industry. They didn't trust industrialists because they have always been abusive. This is actually just like the stagnation of harmony view, thinking all industry is evil and banning it.
>
> Sickleclaw left on a second long march because he thought Kemerskai was forcing every griffon to serve the state at high capacity, and Sockleclaw's followers just wanted to reject the rat race and be left on their own on their land
>
> From each their ability, to each their need. The republic isn't perfect but it's good enough at meeting needs, Sickleclaw admits
> It is always a continuous struggle to maintain your principles. Never give up and let the others rule you, always participate
>
> Hard marksism is a trauma response of tyranical times, Rosewing said
>
> He talks about being an officer in the imperial army with Kemerskai
>
> The Monarchy to our West, while it claims to be "harmonic" in nature, is still an aristocratic, noble state, with little democracy and rights for the common-folk
>
> "Claus Rosewing, once Kemerskai's most trusted friend in the battle against reaction, was born in 941 in the city of Griffing, Angriver.\n\n At the age of 24, he joined the Imperial Army, and soon became a mentor and comrade to Kemerskai when he joined the army soon after. They both were staunch supporters of Grover IV and his new reforms - but they also believed in radical republicanism and democracy. Rosewing, ever the pragmatist, believed that a regime like Grover IV's would make revolution unnecessary - and that the people should only revolt when monarchy becomes oppressive and decadent. When Grover V took power, however, Rosewing became a supporter of revolution, participating in the mutiny of the Imperial Army during the March on the Imperial Palace. Rosewing participated in campaigns in Angriver and Katerin, and was appointed in late 978 as Kemerskai's official vice president.\n\nRosewing eventually fled north with Kemerskai, but by that point, the two had suffered many disagreements about Republican rhetoric and ideals. In 995, Rosewing was soon removed as Chief of Staff and Vice President - for Vice President, Kemerskai appointed Schnabel Sunglider, a staunch Republican, and for Minister of Defense, Reinhard Suntail, an openly far-right but skilled general.\n\nRosewing was sent North to keep the peace with a sizable force, but instead of keeping the peace, Rosewing began to undermine it. His warlord band soon built connections with ethnically Cloudburian and monarchist groups, and Rosewing engaged in the looting of Republican armies and stockpiles. However, Rosewing was not fully anti-government, and always made sure never to attack towns - any soldier who did so would receive the death penalty. And there, in the north, Rosewing has stayed, "defending the peace" for 10 years and counting."
>
> Rosewing describes Vedina as always independent from Herzland Empire, totally separate culture because they are so far away
> It is a stagnant "benevolent" monarchy of performance and low ambition
> He thought that was admirable for a time. It was stable, isolated, not worth the Kaiser's time
> It's why he became a robin hood figure in the far north of Cloudbury. He just wanted a banditless small and stable self-sustaining area. He decided scale was the problem
>
> Vedina was not mobilized into a war economy. The ambitious patriotic Vedinans went to Cloudbury, the stagnant are allowed to stay as farmers who sell to market humbly. Many Herzlanders and Cloudburians moved to the open Vedinan countryside, with tools (harmonic capitalism!) for a non hyper industrial life
> This is why Skynavia voted by referendum to be annexed into the Republic. Their agrarian simplicity was accepted, and they get the votes and influence to keep the system. The citizens in Cloudbury don't look down on them, they support them. It's no griffon left behind
>
> He remembers a moment when he was vice president when Kemerskai reached out to Celestia for an endorsement of being the rightful government of Herzland and was snubbed
> Her reply had something to do with disturbing the Cloudburians
> But Rosewing actually started thinking about small local rule with a strong leader after that
> Cloudburians were the majority in the countryside, Herzlanders in the city
> Cloudbury was very sparsely populated due to the cold before the Herzlander Republicans arrived from the Long March
>
> Rosewing wanted a return to simple feudal times but with respect. He is like Celestia, remove the ambition and every griffon will be humble, solidarity through hardship. He is a true believer of the Archons' teachings about morals and chivalry (from Grover III's compromise). He viewed greed as a moral failing after the industrialists turned on them, which is why he was a Robin Hood warlord figure
>
> Rosewing describes how he was arrested and served his time
> During this time, Kemerskai's universal translator was perfected
> Kemerskai gave radio addresses in Cloudburian to the countryside and reminded the peasants of Rosewing's former domain of the republican cause
>
> Trimmel should be the one to ask the hard, cynical questions. He shouldn't believe it at first. He should expect Sickleclaw to be a puppet or Rosewing to be a broken husk.
> Trimmel sees that Sickleclaw formed a breakaway state and was reintegrated. Rosewing was a bandit, he served his time, and then came out of jail and continues life as a civil servant. These are in sharp contrast to Thranx (assassinated by Chrysalis)
>
> They are all looking at Sickleclaw and Rosewing and realizing: "This system works because it allows you to fail and come back."
>
>     Celestia's System (Stagnation): Prevents failure by preventing action. (Safety).
>
>     Chrysalis's System (Tyranny): Punishes failure with death/erasure. (Terror).
>
>     The Republic/Alliance: Absorbs failure and turns it into experience. (Resilience).
>
> Rosewing sees Twilight taking frantic notes and says you should get a copy of Kemerskai's memoir, and points to the bookstore.
> They go to the bookstore which is full of books. Twilight realizes how much of the world she hasn't read yet!
> They have Equestrian copies
> "The Triumph of Liberty"
> Rosewing buys it for her
> It's like the friendship journal equivalent
>
> Then Twilight realizes Cloudbury must have a library
> She has to come back one day
> (Henri asks is she always like this? Applejack says it's been a while since Twilight has encountered NEW books)
>
> She reads it on the plane ride back to Aquileia

**Characters:**
* **[[Twilight Sparkle]]**
> **Analysis:** Up to this point, Twilight’s only reference for a functioning society is Celestia’s "Stagnation of Harmony" (Biological Determinism/Cutie Marks) or the "Soulless Industry" of the Changelings. She fears that without a Princess or Cutie Marks, society collapses into greed or chaos.
>
> The Solution in Cloudbury: Twilight serves as the intellectual witness to the Republic's institutions. She needs to see that systems can replace destiny.
>
> Twilight should realize that the GR has industrialized education the way Chrysalis industrialized war. However, unlike Chrysalis who discards the weak, the GR invests in them until they are strong. This validates Twilight's goal to democratize magic via the Luna Nova Rifle/Crystal Enhancers. It proves that "power for everyone" doesn't lead to chaos if you have the right education system.
>
> She leaves Cloudbury convinced that mass-produced magic is not dangerous if it is paired with mass-produced education and civic virtue
> This will be the moral basis of the magic economy. It gives her the resolve to invent civilian spell matrices and recruit others to help too


**Threads:**
* **[[Stagnation's Redemption]]**
> **Update:** Even some griffons want stagnation, not all griffons are greedy


**Codex:**
* **[[Parloirs/Pre-War Manehattan]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** This explains why the Manehattan Parloirs feel different from the Pridea Salons.
> • The Aesthetic (Aquileian): On the surface, it looks like Pridea. Velvet curtains, tea, sexual liberty, "The Night." It celebrates Asset Specificity (Thestrals as specialized hosts, ponies finding their true selves).
> • The Foundation (Griffonian): Under the surface, it runs on Solidarity.
>     ◦ Coltbert’s Pridea: "You need a Seal to enter." If you are boring/unambitious, you are excluded (The Wallflowers).
>     ◦ Manehattan’s EEEE!: "No Creature Left Behind." The Parloirs became soup kitchens and shelters for refugees. If you are a broken refugee with no ambition, you are invited in to heal.
> • The Synthesis: EEEE! proved that you can have the Safety Net of the North (GR) without losing the Passion of the South (Aquileia). This is the prototype for Applejack’s Republic.

* **[[Applejack's Parents]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Rosewing says his parents were apple farmers in Angriver
> He joined the army and was hoping the king could make reforms that helped the common griffon
> They were in an industrial revolution that was making tons of food
> But Grover IV was doing all the wrong laws, he was making it harder for the common griffon, not easier
> Rosewing was skeptical of democracy too since griffons are greedy. He wanted a constitutional monarchy where the monarch was a moral check
> He finds Celestia ok
>
> While he was in jail, he ate SAA rations
> He says how Equestrian ponies would come with cans and cans of food to feed the griffons, paying Skyfall shipping, and they accepted the new Riks as payment
> These ponies are why Equestrian is in the Universal Translator
> The ponies learned solidarity and said they formed a union back home (EEEE!) and solidarity
>
> To a Skyfall accountant, this is financial suicide. To a Harmonic Capitalist, this is a Moral Investment. Bright Mac was betting that Liberty would win.
> • The Cultural Exchange: The ships didn't just bring back worthless paper money; they brought back Values.
>     ◦ SAA workers saw the "Solidarity" of the Cloudburians. They saw a society where people suffered together to build a future.
>     ◦ They brought this "Bunker Mentality" back to the soft luxury of Manehattan.
>     ◦ Result: EEEE! adopted the GR’s "No Creature Left Behind" ethos. They view Martial Law not as tyranny, but as "Kemerskai becoming Celestia" (Intervening to save the people).
>
> AJ thought her parents left the farm because they were greedy "Skyfall Sellouts".
> 2. The Reveal: She learns that SAA was helping keep the Griffonian Republic alive during the dark years. Her parents weren't selling out; they were exporting Harmony via industrial canning


---
### SCENE 26.8: Rosewing's Workshop
> **Synopsis:** This is the Idol of Boreas, said Rosewing, holding up a small statue
> Come again? I thought the Idol of Boreas was a legendary artifact
> That's what the legends say, but science says it's just a naturally occuring composite crystal lattice that glows in the presence of ambition. It was a propaganda tool
> Grover III studied it and was going to publish with his printing press that the Archons' religion is a lie
> But he stopped because he decided science had no moral boundaries, but religion does
>
> Republicans deduced the truth out of the contradictions and published Common Sense, which started the revolution
> Rosewing says after Kemerskai instituted martial law, he left Cloudbury as a robin hood figure, reverting back to religion as a moral gate to shame bandits
>
> Kemerskai eventually arrested Rosewing, but employed his men to build the universal translators
> It is built upon the very composite material that the idol of boreas was made of, optimized and foreged by bilingual artisans

**Codex:**
* **[[Universal Translator]]** (Technology)
* **[[Grover III's Enlightenment (854 ALB to 914 ALB)]]** (History)

### SCENE 26.9: Henri talks about Herzlander supremacist propaganda
> **Synopsis:** The Propaganda Wedges (The "Poser" Playbook)
>
> The Supremacists (who indoctrinated Henri) are masters of hypocrisy. They attack both sides to fuel their narrative of "Only the Empire is Pure."
>
> •The Attack on Aquileia (The "Pony Pet" Narrative):
> ◦They point to the Aquileian nobles dressing up their ponies. "Look at them! They dress cattle in silk and pretend they are people. It is a mockery of our heraldry."
> ◦They attack Pridea (where ponies have land). "Those ponies didn't fight for the Revolution; they just squatted on land that belongs to Griffons. The Republic is giving away your birthright to animals."
>
>
> •The Attack on the GR (The "Weak Male" Narrative):
> ◦They latch onto the Paternity Leave laws. "In the Empire, a father protects his cubs by killing their enemies. In the Republic, a father protects his cubs by warming up milk bottles. Who do you think will win the war?"
> ◦They frame the GR's solidarity as "Socialism that castrates the strong."

**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]** (Demonstration)
* **[[Anti-Racism / Anti-Tribalism / Politics of Division]]** (Demonstration)
> **Commentary:** Wedge issues


---
### SCENE 26.10: Trimmel says he wants to stay behind
> **Synopsis:** Trimmel says he was thinking about the press conference. Applejack agrees that she felt out of place.
>
> Plot twist: Trimmel suggests the fact that his old rival, Synovial, actually wanted the rules of war too. He is traditional. Trimmel's new mobile warfare changed the game. He surpassed Synovial (whose doctrine was based on the old and sluggish rules of war) by breaking all the rules with new tech that was faster, but also more brutal strikes, because that was what Chrysalis asked of him. Trimmel wants to stay behind in the GR so he can properly learn the rules of war. An honorable warrior is who he aspires to be. (He also looks up to Kemerskai as what he wished Chrysalis was.)
>
> This perfectly explains that weird feeling that Applejack felt, saying she was out of place at the interview. She is currently Chyrsalis's puppet.
> **Outcome:** Applejack realizes her doctrine of magical combined arms is an evolution of Trimmel's mobile warfare doctrine, which was developed at Chyrsalis's behest. Applejack is perpetuating Chrysalis's means of war. She must stop!

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]** (Impact: CoreValueChange)
> **Analysis:** Applejack realizes she is now the monster, and must change to avoid becoming a perpetuator of total war, as if she is Chrysalis's puppet

* **[[Trimmel]]**
> **Analysis:** Kemerskai shows him it's not just harmonic ponies but also Herzlander griffons (the original selfish hoarders and industrialists) that can do things right, morally

* **[[Synovial]]**
> **Analysis:** The "tragedy" or plot twist on Synovial is that perhaps he always wanted to fight a gentlegriff's war. He doesn't drain enemies like Pagala (it's beneath him) and doesn't use unrelenting breakthroughs like Trimmel and Applejack/Henri who learned from Trimmel. He fights a slow gentlegriff's war not because he is arrogant but because he misses the old days, when war was glorious.
>
> Applejack outmaneuvering him using Trimmel's tactics in the 3rd Battle of Tall Tale and then accepting all surrenders is the biggest power move. She used Chrysalis's doctrine for good.
>
> His war crimes are that he commanded prisoners under him who he neglected to reign in because he served a genocidal regime.
>
> He does not divulge these details and his personal grievances with Chyrsalis during his trial because he fears VOPS retaliation. His trial was mundane, in which he did little to refute the charges against him, a total forgotten media piece compared to Henri and Trimmel's tell-all speeches.
>
> Synovial doesn't like total war. He likes honorable posture and maneuver, the old griffon way
> He is literally in his element when he arrives to fight for Eros. Then shattered by Trimmel and AJ's total war tactics. His villainy and enabling coming to bite him
>
> This recontextualizes "The Lioness of Tall Tale". Synovial uses the term to say that Applejack has no honor or moral grace (!)


**Threads:**
* **[[Magical Combined Arms]]** (Disaster)
> **Update:** Applejack realizes her doctrine is a descendent of Chrysalis's total war. She is effectively Chrysalis's puppet

* **[[Camp Fluttershy]]**
> **Update:** Trimmel asks Applejack to send the changeling linguists from the camp to the Cloudbury so they can work on adding native changeling to the Universal Translator


**Codex:**
* **[[Knightly Code / Rules of War]]** (WorldRules)
> **Note:** Trimmel plans to stay behind in the GR so he can properly learn the rules of war.


---
### SCENE 26.11: Applejack decides to withdraw SECEF and donate all their weapons to BOTH Aquileia and GR
> **Synopsis:** Applejack comes to terms with the fact that they don't belong in this war. They defeated Synovial. Continuing to fight the way changelings forced them to is not what Applejack wants to do. Twilight agrees and says "this is why you're our president"
>
> Applejack and Twilight meet Kemeskai, explain themselves and offer the donation of their magical weapons. Kemerskai is humbled by the fact that Applejack is trusting them with her weapons even though they just met.
>
> Kemerskai is skeptical of ponies because he tried reaching out to Celestia to get her to recognize the Republic as the rightful government of Herzland long ago but Celestia ignored him. Plus, there are not really any ponies in Herzland. That's why he thought ponies were soft and cuddly and not important. Kemerskai admits the shock when he hears how exactly they wiped Synovial and what Applejack is capable of. Now AJ is donating her weapons to him? After only being in the GR for one day?
>
> Applejack says her heart tells her it's the right thing to do and she can trust him.
>
> Kemerska jokingly calls AJ Madame president or a southern equivalent (need to think about it)
>
> Huge gesture of good faith, especially to the GR who previously viewed SECEF/AJ as too in bed with Aquileia or biased against them because of the cultural trauma of the changeling invaders being Herzlander posers (I think AJ and Twilight and Henri and Trimmel will come up with this plan and give it Kemerskai ahead of time before leaving Cloudbury, and Kemerskai expresses extreme gratitude and humbleness at their trust. Not even noble houses of old who were allies of convenience or longstanding would ever share their weapons.)
> It signals that AJ/SECEF's enemy and quarrel is with Chrysalis, not the Reich
> The split donation is also practical, because they start a joint campaign to cut off occupied Skyfall from the rest of Herzland using the weapons. While following their rules of war. The Reich generals generally retreat pretty fast when faced with the magical weapons. It's mostly power projection. It shows that posturing actually saves lives.

> **Synopsis:** Twilight should read the paper ahead of meeting him, she got a copy in Equestrian from the bookstore (bestseller! Not many Equestrian books) in Cloudbury and read it on the plane ride
>
> Applejack, would you read it with me?
> I'd love to, Twi
>
> Then they get disturbed by how the paper details Coltbert's promiscuity 
> It's the full paper plus the whole newspaper feud with Vérany

### SCENE 26.13: Reuniting with SECEF at Pridea
> **Synopsis:** Applejack goes up to Rainbow Dash and says "I know I'm soft, hit me with it"
> Rainbow says no, she's glad she's not flying anymore. She's realized that the griffons aren't the enemy.
> Synovial

---
### SCENE 26.14: Meeting Coltbert at the University of Pridea
> **Synopsis:** The Commission: The Thugs are likely paid a commission on what they collect (or they just keep what they can steal).
> The "Poseur" Dynamic: They don't understand economics. They just see a busy factory and think, "That guy is rich. I'm going to shake him down"
> The Turf War: Because the "Wine Bribe" is more valuable than their actual salary, Thugs fight each other for the "Right" to audit the wealthy districts.
>     Scenario: Thug A is collecting bribes from the PNdA steel mill. Thug B wants that turf. They have a knife fight in the alley. The Industrialist watches from his window, sipping wine, and bets on the winner.
> The "Thug Market" (The Race to the Bottom) Commoditized corruption
>     The Supply/Demand Imbalance:
>         Supply: There are thousands of Thugs (Poseurs who want easy money).
>         Demand: There are only a few dozen Factory Owners (The "Whales" with the wine).
>     The Bidding War:
>         Thug A: "I'll let you smuggle for 5 bottles a month."
>         Thug B: "I'll do it for 3 bottles, and I'll guard your truck."
>         Thug C: "I'll do it for 2 bottles, and I'll break Thug B's legs."
>     The Result: The Industrialist sits in his office, swirling a glass of wine, watching the police beat each other to death in the alley for the privilege of taking his bribe.
>         The Thugs think they are the predators.
>         The Industrialist knows the Thugs are just Private Security that he pays in alcohol.
>
> Coltbert doesn't weep for Discret. Discret was a useful idiot who funded Coltbert’s cultural experiment.
>
> Applejack learns that the "Soulless Industrialists" (PNdA) were actually forged in the fire of Coltbert’s "Cultural Crusade." The Aquileian Republic isn't a compromise between Good and Evil; it is a Synthesis created by a mad pony genius who wanted to see if he could force greed to serve art. And he did.
>
> Applejack learns that the PNdA are not the enemy (unlike what she first thought upon arrival). They are business partners who operate with harmonic capitalism even though the outward aesthetic is "soulless capitalist". The real enemy is the MPA who are waiting for the market to crash to install martial law.
>
> Dignity ain't something you get from being clever, it's something you get for being alive

**Characters:**
* **[[Fleur Bloom]]**
> **Analysis:** Fleur once gently mocked Twilight for her worship of Celestia. But the reality is... she looks up to Coltbert like a god the same way Twilight once did to Celestia (instead of Princess! it's Professor! and the pride of praise). Twilight should point this out tongue in cheek
>
> When they enter Coltbert's splendid office, Fleur says Professor! and Coltbert says "Fleur! My faithful student" but it's in French
> Just like Friendship is Magic Part 2
>
> Her development from here onward will be about eclipsing her mentor's "Aquileian superiority complex" and really embracing world peace without cynicism or mocking amusement
>
> When Coltbert reveals the 2nd Aquileian Republic started from a bank run and a bloodless coup, Twilight suggests Fleur was being a poseur for being such a dyed-in-the-wool revolutionary and anti-monarchist when in reality everyone in Aquileia loved King Moriset Discret
> Fleur says Coltbert's Reforms are the revolution. It was cultural, not political


**Threads:**
* **[[Twilight Breaking from Celestia]]**
> **Update:** Twilight realizes Fleur has been mentoring Twilight all along, and yet Fleur puts herself in an impossible pedestal of ambition to impress Coltbert just like Twilight once did for Celestia. She seeks praise from Coltbert. Twilight applies the lessons she learned (some from Fleur herself) to convince Fleur that she doesn't have to be 100% ambition and arrogant like Coltbert.


**Codex:**
* **[[The Predator's Dilemma (Coltbert's Paper)]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Eagleclaw and Synovial call it a deviant paper because its origin is from the noble griffonesses and 2nd gen royalist stallions
>
> The Flaw: The Universal Ambition Myth
> This is the critical error that leads to the Wallflower Tragedy (and Applejack’s eventual victory).
> • The Assertion: Coltbert asserts that Ambition is Universal. He claims that Aquileian Ponies are biologically just as ambitious as Griffons, but they were "socially oppressed" by Feudalism. He argues that Celestia "nurtured away" ambition in Equestria, implying it is the natural state of all sentient life.
> • The Error: He projects his own personality (a thrill-seeking, deviant intellectual) onto the entire population.
>     ◦ He assumes everyone wants to be a "Player" in the Game.
>     ◦ He ignores the 25% (Pink Population) who just want safety and community.
> • The Consequence: He builds a society (Ego-Capitalism) that rewards the Lion (Initiator) and the Eagle (Graceful Receiver) but has no place for the "Turtle" (The Content). This creates the "Wallflowers"—citizens who have food but no dignity because they lack the "Seal" of ambition.

* **[[2nd Aquileian Republic]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** To defeat Gilded Trust and prevent the rise of Equestrian fascism, Applejack must reject the FJA's elitism.

* **[[Moriset Discret's Aquileia/Coltbert Reforms]]** (History)
> **Note:** Twilight and Henri say Kemerskai based his martial law reforms from reading Coltbert's predator's dilemma paper. Twilight wants to read it. Coltbert's ego goes up even more
>
> Twilight reads the original debate between Verany and Coltbert in the newspapers
>
> Coltbert explains the nuances of Verany's pivot in mindset after his pardon, and what he perceives as his ultimate cultural victory
>
> Fleur and Henri admit their FJA worldview missed the nuance

* **[[Banking]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Seeing how bartering becomes paper money gives more confidence in the war bonds system they built. They are not predatory Skyfall, they are harmonic Aquileia. They don't need a gold standard, in fact the 1:1 fixed conversion was a lie
>
> Coltbert describes the bank run that started the 2nd revolution
> Fixed Peg/The lie: one royal note = one gold bit
> As long as the citizens believe the lie, the note is worth 1 bit. The moment the belief breaks, the note is worth 0 bits. The moment Discret fired Coltbert, the bank run started, and Discret suspended conversions. That was the day the 2nd revolution started.
> By contrast, the new Aquileian value based franc is a floating fiat. If the economy/country struggles, the currency gets weaker (inflation). It bends, but doesn't break. It allows for soft landings, not collapse. That is also how the Equestrian war bonds will work now.
>
> "Franc" means "Free" in old French. That's the etymology of the historic coin.


---
### SCENE 26.15: The Lioness Game, Part 3 of Coltbert's Game
> **Synopsis:** An Aquileian lioness could be walking down the street and two griffons who have been with her before both want to sleep with her tonight. She says she accepts both of their honorable requests (this is what Henri meant by a lion having the honesty and grace to say "I want you", and this dynamic of the prince serving the princess is from Aquitaine, Gascony) but they have to negotiate with each other. Instead of beating each other in the street which would invalidate both of them, they have to negotiate with each other gracefully and coming to a sharing agreement in front of the lioness. Seeing how she civilized the two thugs into eagles gives the biggest ego trip and arousal imaginable
>
> Fleur, have you done that?
> No, I'm too antisocial for that. I only go for a fling when stressed or need a break from research 
> ...

**Characters:**
* **[[Minette]]**
> **Analysis:** The ones who take the moral beating and learn grace through the contempt end up getting jobs, perfecting crafts and becoming bourgeois themselves... And then they too look down on the thugs who don't fix themselves!
> It's the most brutal version of "we give you infinite carrots (free sex without biological consequences!), if you don't take it it's your fault" while ignoring the harsh cultural contempt complex
>
> This is why she failed to help the drugged changeling POWs back in chapter 6. The changelings have raw, untrained emotion sense and drug withdrawal. They physically could not handle the moral beating and contempt. Only Fluttershy's kindness could break through
>
> After this conversation, Minette and Reni, now humbled by their experiences in Equestria, seek to have honest conversations and connect with the left-behind Aquileians (to be featured at the end of their side story)


**Threads:**
* **[[Stagnation's Redemption]]**
> **Update:** Coltbert's arrogant "ego-capitalism" solidifies Applejack's resolve that she won't copy Aquileia or the Griffonian Republic, but she will combine the merits of all three societies
>
> Coltbert fixed the predator's dilemma by arming everyone. It requires high ambition. Being stagnant is culturally demoralizing (whereas in Equestria and Rosewing/Sickleclaw, being stagnant is a valid life choice)
>
> Twilight and Applejack conclude the the whole system is barbaric. They cannot imagine having to sleep around to build a scorecard before being eligible for a long term relationship built on feelings, like what they have
> This is a big criticism of the modern dating world and the French and Western realities
> The scorecards are transparent but more absolute than dating apps
> Honesty alone is not enough to fix society, you need the Stagnation of Harmony's unconditional dignity
>
> There is a difference between people who want to live simple lives with their family and friends vs people who are want to strive but can't
> In Stagnation of harmony, most are content and the ambitious ones are suppressed
> In Skyfall and las pegasus, capacity only goes to the rich
>
> Pink population are those who are happy with just family and friends 
> Red popu
>
> Kemerskai's martial law forced all griffons to work together, but also to grow up and leave the nursery for at least 10 years, until they were strong enough to feed everyone and survive an attack from the Reich and have universal translators. That is what Applejack had to do in Equestria with total war. The Equestrian war story matches the Griffonian civilization exactly in an incredibly compressed timeline. Now Applejack sees she must finish the job


**Themes:**
* **[[Intimacy and Liberty]]**
> **Commentary:** Henri and Fleur outright tell Coltbert they taught Twilight and Applejack sexual liberation. They taught his deviance to Celestia's faithful student and the honest family-friendly farmpony


---
### SCENE 26.16: Henri and Fleur explain their relationship to Applejack and Twilight
> **Synopsis:** Fleur and Henri are close friends who see each other a lot from their shared history at Pridea, the revolution, Star Energy and Tall Tale. They would not form a familial unit because their interests and personalities are different - Fleur is a nerdy and eccentric pony who studies obscure earth pony magic and loves being a teacher. Her mentor is Coltbert. Henri is a loud and boisterous griffon who drinks jovially and is a tactical commander. His mentor is Gaudreau. But they both love the revolution and the way it freed them from their very different destinies. Fleur was supposed to be a farmer (a free and independent farmer, but still just a farmer). The reforms allowed her to be a researcher and teacher and visionary in the field of earth pony magic. Henri, born a griffon peasant, was supposed to be either a serf, or a factory worker. Then became a poser thanks to propaganda. The reforms (Gaudreau fixing up his home town) changed his life. So Henri and Fleur have deep shared history. They both have poweful ambitions, including sexual ambitions by biology. So they have a robust sexual relationship despite having no plans to form a familial unit (and due to their biology of being one pony and one griffon, they don't have the baggage of biological consequences that griffons in Herzland or ponies in pre Celestia equestria or the Riverlands have).

**Threads:**
* **[[Fleur & Henri's Backstories]]**

---
### SCENE 26.17: The Wallflower Problem
> **Synopsis:** What if someone just wants to live a simple life instead of fill out their tableau de chasse?
> They get food and housing
> A lot of them enlisted in the army as infantry, and Coltbert is glad they are finally serving the country despite having no asset specificity
> Applejack accuses Coltbert of having no conscience, being no better than the imperialists in Herzland
> He has no right to mock Kemerskai's republic for being soulless

**Themes:**
* **[[Conscience]]**

### SCENE 26.18: Breaking down Verany
> **Synopsis:** The industrialists promised "clothes for all" and it was subconciously interpreted as "civil rights for all" due to the meaning of clothes in pre-industrial Herzlander society. The federalist faction are the true believers of this marketing. Then the industrialists betrayed the federalists. They did not betray their own principles of why they launched the revolution in the first place: to get rich by unlocking a new market that the nobility was holding back.
>
> This is the pitch of Verany's first revolution and the Griffenheim revolution which Kemerskai sympathized with
>
> Coltbert describes how Verany wanted to make the industrial cities even more industrial and polluted (the basin), and to motivate the workers, they had to save up money to buy a detatched home (all the same, boring and soulless) and car outside the city (the heights) and commute in. This would ruin the farmland outside the city and they would make up for it by industrializing farming.
> This is the reality of Skyfall that they saw out of the plane
> It's the 1950s American Dream (lie)
> This what Las Pegasus, as a Skyfall city, is moving towards
>
> like clothes for all, it's also cars for all
>
> Also lots of crime in the rugged cities as a product of the conditions, to cope with the "temporarily embarassed millionaire" phase
>
> Coltbert ensured Pridea became an artisan hub of culture as a counterpoint to that soulless vision for Aquila, Rila, and Pelis. In the end he won, because even the most urban cities today are more artisan than they used to
>
> Artisan cities: Pridea (because ponies, even though it's global on the coast), Vinovia, Pomovarra, Flowena, Flowerino, Falcontown, Eagleton (all inland)
> Every city should stand out for their local culture and embrace it, just like cutie marks and special talents
>
> The city is the center of culture. It's where everyone wants to live, where Aquileians (both ponies and griffons) share the fruits of their special talents in open markets, where the light industry is available to help everyone use their special talent to the fullest. There is heavy industry corridors but only the ones who view it as their talent/calling willingly work there. All land is specialized terroir owned and worked at the local level by small business owners and workers strive to gain the skills and savings for their own plot of terroir (southern California model)
> Land near the cities is not easily given up for industry or suburban sprawl because that land is valuable to make specialty products to bring to market in the close city. Cars and trucks are for logistics and tourism, not commuting, because almost everyone lives in the city
> In rural areas there are big towns for this purpose where the FJA social clubs are
>
> Kemerskai and Cloudbury (the idealists who were betrayed) - heavy industry in the city. Large farms in the countryside for efficiency. These are essential for the GR's survival against the Reich. There is no terroir or special talents. But there are no individualist walled off and gated suburbs. The griffons live in the city in dense housing, and they are proud of the machine they built. Communal social services everywhere. Weather control of the industrial pollution where the byproducts are captured via magic and reused as inputs.
>
> Then go into how Coltbert got Verany to change, his cultural victory

**Themes:**
* **[[Intimacy and Liberty]]** (CentralConflict)
> **Commentary:** The Federalists (True Believers): They thought "Clothes" meant Dignity. They interpreted the slogan philosophically.
> •The Industrialists (Cynics): They meant "Clothes" literally. They just wanted to sell textiles to 100% of the population instead of just the 1% nobility.


**Codex:**
* **[[Clothing in Equestria and Griffonia]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** "clothes for all" was subconciously interpreted as "civil rights for all"

* **[[Moriset Discret's Aquileia/Coltbert Reforms]]** (History)
