### SCENE 27.9: Eros Unconditionally Surrenders to the Republican Pact
> **Synopsis:** Since Eros is a rational actor, he should see the magical combined arms obliterated Synovial, then see the fact that the weapons were donated but the tactics were not, and understand that it is the ultimate olive branch of mercy. He isn't stupid, he is incredibly wise. He hears the honest speeches and the honest interviews, parses through the grandeur and performance, sees the actual result, that the combined arms has stopped and only the weapons remain, sees the exhausted populace, and that is why he unconditionally surrenders. Living as a citizen in the Republic (bleh) is preferable to anihilation, and the "enemies" who came in and wiped half of Synovial's attaché off the map, who HE invited, and then are leaving, is profound on the Archon. It's the ultimate gentleman's gesture.
>
> The "Gentleman’s Gesture": To a Knight like Eros, this is a language he understands perfectly.
> •If they stayed, it would be an occupation. He would have to fight to the death to protect the Emperor’s dignity.
> •Because they left, he can surrender without shame. He is yielding to a superior but Honorable force. ◦Eros's Internal Monologue: "They killed the monster I invited in (Synovial), and then they went home. They are better knights than we are."
>
> News reaches Equestria of Archon Eros broadcasting an unconditional surrender and for all Imperial forces to lay down their arms. Applejack is shocked that a "villain" would do such a humbling thing just because she gave the republicans some weapons. The weapons were allowing huge gains and the Reich troops were retreating en masse.
>
> Applejack receives a letter from Kemerskai, an invitation to oversee the public trials of Archon Eros and Gabriella Eagleclaw. She decides to take a break from campaigning to go to Griffenheim.
>
> Applejack and Twilight are checking in on the changeling refugees and rescued hostages when they get the letter with the invitation to Griffenheim. Flurry Heart wants to go to Griffenheim with Aunty Twilight.

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]** (Impact: CoreValueChange)
> **Analysis:** Applejack realizes that posturing while being authentic actually saves lives. The griffon nations don't do total war, they have a "gentlegriff's war" which seems non-sensical to Applejack who was only subjected to total war. But the war ends with little casualties. She was right to withdraw and let them sort it out themselves.
>
> Posturing is actually how to resolve industrialized war without mass deaths (like on the changeling/equestrian front that had mass death)

* **[[Archon Eros]]** (Impact: CoreValueChange)
> **Analysis:** Two weeks after the weapons donations, and a few pivotal engagements, Archon Eros will surrender unconditionally. It shows that he is not irrational the way Chrysalis is. He even apologizes for accepting Synovial's help again, declaring that it was a sin and Kemerskai was right.
>
> This is because Eros actually does care about the griffons. He is a true believer in peace through strength. He is not a supermacist that weaponizes inter peasant struggle to divert class struggle. He wanted to build a strong and stable empire for Grover VI.


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
> **Commentary:** Applejack realizes that posturing while being authentic actually saves lives. The griffon nations don't do total war, they have a "gentlegriff's war" which seems non-sensical to Applejack who was only subjected to total war. But the war ends with little casualties. She was right to withdraw and let them sort it out themselves.

* **[[Strong to be Merciful]]** (Demonstration)
* **[[Bottom Up > Top Down]]** (Demonstration)
> **Commentary:** Eros is the Griffonian equivalent of Celestia, but militarized.
> •His Philosophy: "The Emperor protects the people. I protect the Emperor."
> •His Fear: He feared the Republic not because he hates freedom, but because he fears Chaos. He looks at the messy debates of Aquileia and the cold machinery of Cloudbury and thinks, "This will destroy the soul of the Griffon. Only a Father (The Emperor) can hold us together."
> •The Surrender Logic: Once SECEF destroys his main armies and Synovial falls, Eros realizes that continuing the war only kills the subjects he is sworn to protect. ◦Chrysalis fights to the death because she owns the people.
> ◦Eros surrenders because he serves the people (in his own twisted way).


**Codex:**
* **[[Knightly Code / Rules of War]]** (WorldRules)
> **Note:** Archon Eros surrender unconditionally to save his people, demonstrating the "Knightly" nature of Griffonian warfare and prove that while the Reich was wrong, its leader was not a monster—just a man on the wrong side of history.


---
## CHAPTER 28: Kinship

### SCENE 28.1: Applejack arrives in Griffenheim
> **Synopsis:** Applejack destroyed Synovial on Griffonian soil. It was a sneak peak into total war. Eros and Eagleclaw must face their roles in faciliating Herzland's support of Chyrsalis's regime, which led to the terrorization of Equestria and the development of magical weapons. Applejack's presence compels Eros and Eagleclaw to tell the whole truth with no lies, because appealing to honesty is the only way to save Herzland from the wrath of Equestria.
>
> But still there's some moral obligation. Eros is inherently moral and must atone for his sins, while Eagleclaw was once immoral but is now extremely humbled by Grover VI's pardon.
>
> Before the trial, Eros and Eagleclaw meet in a holding cell. They are no longer rivals for the throne, but two defeated elders terrified for their country's survival. To save the Herzland, they must destroy their own reputations.
> Grover is wise beyond his years. He can handle the truth.

**Themes:**
* **[[Strong to be Merciful]]** (Demonstration)
> **Commentary:** Because Applejack showed strength, the defeated Herzland leadership is being honest. Now Applejack can be merciful.


**Codex:**
* **[[Changeling Emotion Sense]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** Trimmel uses emotion sense to judge the honesty of the defendants.


---
### SCENE 28.2: Eros admits that accepting Chyrsalis's help is his undoing
> **Synopsis:** Eros’s "Absolute Meritocracy" (The Purge)
>
> This is the terrifying climax of the Imperial arc. Eros looks at Grover V's Stagnation where he was pushed around by nobles and sees rot. He decides to burn it all down to save the Emperor.
> The "Chrysalis-Lite" Method: Wealth Redistribution: Eros seizes the factories from the "Rugged Individualists" and the estates from the "Nobles." He nationalizes everything in the name of the Emperor.
> He doesn't use Love Harvesters, but he uses the Firing Squad. "You are hoarding resources meant for the Army? Treason. Shot."
>
> The State Reason (What Chrysalis told Eros/Synovial): "Eagleclaw is a 'Rugged Individualist' sympathizer. She is weak. If she leads Herzland, the PNdA or the Communists (like Severyana) will infiltrate and destroy the Empire. You, Eros, are a strongman. You can hold the line. That's why I'm betraying my friend for you. Do not let Kemerskai win or let communists take over."
> This effectively sold Eros on the betrayal.
>
> The Appeal: The common Griffon loves it at first. The "Fat Cats" and "Arrogant Dukes" are finally getting punished. Eros channels the class rage of the GR but directs it toward a Dictator (The Lord Protector) rather than a Democracy.
> The Climax (The Execution of Auntie Gabriella): Eros puts Duchess Gabriela on trial, not for "War Crimes" (like the later international trial), but for "Treason against the State" (i.e., being a rich noble).
> 5 year old Grover VI steps in instinctively to save his Auntie Gabriella, and also Duke Gerlach of Feathesia and Count Ignatius of Bronzehill. This gave Eros pause, and he followed Grover's wishes
>
> Eros talks about how Chrysalis murdered Thranx on Griffonian soil because he spoke out against her regime. He was a citizen of the Reich. Eros was told ahead of time about the assassination and if he refused to authorize then Chrysalis would do it anyway and also break all ties and military support, that's how much the stakes were for Eros and how desperate Chrysalis is to stop the "good changeling" image of Thranx from gaining power. This is where Eros takes the blame, for continuing to work with Chrysalis and for lying to Grover.
>
> Eros talks about Grover's purity in order to save him from trial. He talks about five year old Grover's mercy for his aunt to show Applejack that the soul of Herzland is not evil. They can be better than Chrysalis.
>
> Accepting Chrysalis’s help was an “ends justify the means” move. In fact, Grover VI distinctly does not like Chrysalis, and Eros can’t argue with it. It’s a point of contention between them. There’s also a subplot about one of the changeling tank commanders in the 1007 attache named Thranx. He is a product of meritocracy like Trimmel and is from Vraks. Grover meets Thranx during the trial of Eagleclaw when Synovial delivers Eagleclaw to Eros. Thranx, who loves Herzlander culture (the real deal in Griffonheim, not the hollow shell back at the hives) is unhappy with his role as a changeling officer under Chrysalis. As Synovial’s attache is in Griffonheim to support Eros, Thranx mentors Grover VI on many things, becoming a foster uncle. Thranx likes Eros’s teachings about top down morality - it’s what the Herzlander “empty shell” back home lacks. When the great war starts in 1011, Synovial’s attache is recalled back to the hives in order to fight against Equestria. Thranx refuses to go home to help murder and drain ponies, and begs Grover to stay. As a result, VOPS agents assassinate him a year later in Griffonheim, which devastates Grover. Teenage Grover was told Thranx died of a natural changeling disease (like cancer) but deduces the truth that Chrysalis had VOPS kill him, even though Grover cannot figure out the exact mechanism. Eros is aware of the truth and the cost of keeping Chrysalis as an ally. It is deeply shameful to him, but he has to stay strong in order to fend off the GR and Aquileia.
>
> The war in Griffonia is in a stalemate. Chrysalis and Celestia agreed on a white peace on Equus. Eros shamefully accepts Chrysalis’s offer of assistance, thinking once the war is over they can break ties, and Synovial’s attache arrives. Synovial’s troops are expressly forbidden from committing war crimes (but there are still cracks, because they are predators). Then SECEF’s troops land in Pridea and face Synovial in the Battle of Ailmont. Applejack, Henri and Trimmel brutally wipe half of Synovial’s force off the map in minutes and accept Synovial’s surrender. Eros sees the cost of his actions - that for a few hours, total war had arrived on Griffonia. He listens to the speeches of Applejack, Henri and Trimmel, talking about their honest selves, during the press conference about Synovial’s trial. He listens to the interviews about tactics and why they were necessary. Eros sees how SECEF donated their magical weapons to the GR and Aquileia but went home. He remembers 981, when Chrysalis whispered in Eagleclaw’s ear. He remembers 1007 when Chrysalis supported him against Eagleclaw, her “friend”, because he wanted to learn from her methods of “eat the rich”. He sees that Grover VI is pure and free from Chrysalis’s influence (Eros in 1007 accepted that he would do the dirty work to deliver an empire for Grover’s pure and moral heart to protect, even if history views him as a villain.) Eros decides the war, and Chrysalis’s influence, must end with him. So Eros unconditionally surrenders to Kemerskai and Gaudreau. He accepts that his approach to morality lost and theirs won (because SECEF left, while Chrysalis/Synovial was a mockery of Eros).
>
> Eros is a tragic figure. He is "The Dark Knight" of Griffonia—willing to damn his own soul to save the city.
> •The Compromise: He hates the Changelings, but he uses Chrysalis to destroy the "Rugged Individualist" Nobles who were starving the people.
> •The Burden: He hides the truth of Thranx’s assassination from Grover VI because he wants the boy to remain pure. Eros takes the sin of the alliance onto himself so Grover doesn't have to.
> •The Realization: When SECEF arrives, Eros sees that the "Bottom-Up" morality of the Republic produced warriors (Applejack, Henri, Trimmel) who are more honorable than his own ally (Synovial). He realizes his "Top-Down" method failed because it required him to get in bed with monsters.
>
> The sequence of events for the surrender is now thematically perfect:
> 1.The Battle: SECEF annihilates Synovial in Pridea/Rottendedam with "Honesty" and efficient brutality, then spares the survivors and leaves.
> 2.The Contrast: Eros compares the honor of the Republicans (who helped without conquering) to the treachery of the Changelings (who killed Thranx and demand total war).
> 3.The Choice: Eros looks at Grover VI. If they keep fighting, Grover becomes a puppet of Chrysalis. If they surrender, the Reich dissolves, but Grover lives and retains his moral soul.
> 4.The Surrender: Eros surrenders to Kemerskai. He admits that the "Bottom-Up" system has proven it can protect the weak better than his "Top-Down" system could.
>
> I told you we would strip the rich and powerful in the name of Boreas, and we did. But it was built on two fundamental contradictions with Boreas. The empire itself was built on an unsurmountable contradiction with Boreas, that Herzland was better than its subjects, that Herzland deserved to rule over Cloudbury and Aquileia and Wingbardy and the Evi Valley and Griffonstone. And second, I had to accept the help of morally bankrupt changelings who spit in the face of Boreas.
>
> When Chrysalis murdered Thranx and I could not speak out about it, I realized I was her puppet. I had made the Reich Chrysalis's vassal state.
> I lied to Grover to protect him. He surely deduced it. I realize now I did not lie to protect his innocence. I lied to hide my shame.
> I wanted to unite the continent so Grover would not have to.
>
> I pray that the new Republic can carry on the true spirit of Boreas free from devil influence and dignifying any and all griffons, nay, all creatures, whether they believe in Boreas or not. I ask you to do so.
> **Outcome:** Eros is given the appropriate prison sentence, a few years, for being complicit in war crimes.

**Characters:**
* **[[Archon Eros]]**
> **Analysis:** Eros's core philosophy was, "The Emperor protects the people. I protect the Emperor". He did not fight the Republic because he hated freedom or wanted to oppress the working class; he fought the Republic because he feared the chaos of modern political machinery. Much like Celestia's Stagnation of Harmony, Eros believed that his people were vulnerable and that only a singular, benevolent "Father" figure (the Emperor) could keep the griffon soul intact.
>
> When the Republican forces shatter his main armies and Synovial falls, Eros realizes that continuing the war will only slaughter the subjects he swore to protect. He chooses to surrender unconditionally because his love for the griffon people outweighs his personal pride.
>
> Eros’s surrender proves he is not a poseur. He publicly apologizes for the sin of accepting help from the changelings (Synovial) and admits that Kemerskai was right. By laying down his arms to save lives, Eros demonstrates the very moral superiority he always preached. This act earns him Kemerskai's respect; Kemerskai publicly praises Eros's morals, acknowledging that Eros’s only true crime was allowing Chrysalis’s influence to root itself in the Herzland.


**Threads:**
* **[[The Intertwined Fates of Equus and Griffonia]]**
> **Update:** Eros says he decided to take down the nobility and institute an absolute meritocracy, and his methods were inspired by Chrysalis's model and the results she had on the changeling unification.

* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
> **Update:** Chrysalis got Eros to adopt restoration bills, which are MEFO bills
>
>  It solves Chrysalis's fear that the Reich will fall to communism or Kemerskai, which is a harder opponent to conquer once she is done with her planned occupation of Equestria because they offer a moral alternative. She is forcing the Reich to rearm and mobilize and literally forcing a war, which weakens her destination (Herzland) and her ideological enemies/ancestors who she believes she has surpassed (Kemerskai's federal Republic and Discret's cult based on Coltbert reforms which turns into the 2nd Aquileian Republic)
>
> He thought he could mobilize the masses through Boreas's will. He thinks that because his cause is righteous, the economics will magically work out. Boreas provides. and defeat GR and Aquileia fast, then he could pay off the loans, assimilate the griffons back into the Reich and be strong, hand the Reich for Grover to rule divinely and virtuously. When he failed to win the war, he realized he was Chrysalis's puppet. The unconditional surrender was a realization that he mortgaged the Emperor to a monster. He didn't save the empire for Grover, he sold it to Chyrsalis. His unconditonal surrender is an attempt to default on the loan and dependence on Chrysalis before she can get ahold of Grover.
>
> Thirdly, this fully explains why Chrysalis chose Eros over her "best friend" Eagleclaw, betraying her. Eagleclaw would be weak and lose a war, but also she views Chrysalis as a partner or sister. Eros would view Chrysalis as a savior.


**Themes:**
* **[[Bottom Up > Top Down]]**
> **Commentary:** Eros concedes that bottom-up republic prevailed over top-down emperor for morality


**Codex:**
* **[[Grover III's Enlightenment (854 ALB to 914 ALB)]]** (History)
> **Note:** Eros and Kemerskai both want what's best for the griffons. Eros follows Grover III's thinking that griffons need religious morals and chivalry to prevent societal collapse. Kemerskai built a legal system and cultural understanding to prevent it.


---
### SCENE 28.3: Eagleclaw reveals the truth of why the industrialists betrayed the republic in 981 and her history with Chrysalis
> **Synopsis:** Gabriella Eagleclaw, who is still alive and living a lower-key life thanks to her young nephew’s intervention, decides to get revenge for Chrysalis’s betrayal by exposing Chrysalis’s true personality and motivation.
>
> Gabriella Eagleclaw reveals how when she was a teenager, she met another teenage griffoness named Krista Sterling, who was the adopted prodigy of Equestrian tycoons in Skyfall. Eagleclaw believed Chrysalis came to the academy genuinely hoping to fit in and learn. The Herzlander nobles bullied her relentlessly for being Skyfall new money, but Gabriella bonded with Krista over shared intellect and hatred towards the incompetent nepo babies. They studied together and pledged that they would help each other overcome the powerful male griffons who look down on them. They studied engineering, industry and tactics together for 7 years (971-978).
>
> The most catastrophic blow to Krista's ego was in martial arts/forging class. She couldn't enchant her sword or armor very well (she must've used a disguise to make it look like the sword was enchanted) and they would shatter against even the mediocre nobles. The instructor and their classmates chalked it up to Krista's heritage as a Skyfall orphan instead of a true blood Herzlander noble, and said because she was new money and only cared about money and grades, she had no pride in her sword or armor. This led to even further humiliation.
>
> Then there was biology class, where changelings were mentioned as a footnote and labeled parasites. The nobles started using "changeling" as an mocking insult to each other when calling others out for being parasites or fake. Eagleclaw wanted to use the insult against their incompetent classmates, but Krista who was usually gleeful to insult their classmates turned cold at the insult. These two events plus Krista's extraordinary origin story alibi led Eagleclaw to deduce that Krista was a genius changeling, but she kept it to herself, viewing having a changeling for a best friend as a great plus. She offered more hugs without explanation. Defensive posturing against bullies. Sharing notes. The Intent: Pure, if slightly condescending, affection.
>
> This was Chrysalis's "Count of Mont Borée" phase. She smiles, takes notes, and memorizes their weaknesses while secretly despising them.
>
> In 981, Krista Sterling arrived in Strawberry after a 3 year disappearance and revealed her true identity as Queen Chrysalis, ten years older than the griffon she posed as. She was a glorious and powerful queen. Gabriella trusted her friend, her fellow ambitious lady, completely back then.
> "I knew what you were, and I loved you anyway."
> In a school full of idiots, Chrysalis is the only other "Genius." Gabriella values that connection more than her species loyalty.
> Chrysalis offered a changeling attache led by her cousin Synovial, with the latest tanks copied from new designs. In exchange, Eagleclaw would provide technical expertise to develop the love harvester after Herzland was reconquered.
>
> Eagleclaw is the only griffon on Griffonia who knew Chrysalis personally before 981. She is the only griffon in the world who knows that Krista Sterling was always Queen Chrysalis. Chrysalis revealing herself to Eagleclaw and trusting her friend was the only time Chrysalis was ever vulnerable. They had a sisterly bond.
>
> She reveals the private conversations from 981. She explains how Chrysalis manipulated her by framing the Changelings as "oppressed brilliant women" just like Gabriella.
>
> Chrysalis didn't want to kill their classmates. She wanted to dismantle their lives piece by piece, using their own vices against them, and loot everything for her own Hive unification wars back home. (This is even better than the Count of Monte Cristo because Chrysalis is the shadow count with infinite resources, but her resources are through engineering and conquest and financial schemes, not random gold treasure!)
>
> Just as she turns rival Hive Nobles into living batteries (Love Harvesters) to fuel her economy, she turns the Herzland Nobles into Political Batteries.
> She keeps them alive and in power (on the Regency Council) specifically to extract value from them.
> She isn't just killing them; she is making them complicit in the destruction of their own nation. She is feeding on their incompetence just as she feeds on their love.
>
> I helped her manipulate all our stupid classmates. They were all on the regency council with me, we made sure of it. They thought they were Boreas. Over 26 years (281-1007), she stripped them of all their diginity and everything they held dear as I watched and laughed. We designed every economic scheme on our former classmates. She bribed them and looted our country; I let her do it because she was my sister. She would make them flounder, seize their assets for her mining company which was a front for Vesalipolis, then I would come in and fix the economy using her resources. I would get the glory and she would get the spoils. That's why Grover V's regency council was so incompetent.
>
> Gabriella Eagleclaw would have shown them mercy. Eros promised them a firing squad. That's why Chrysalis chose him.
> Eros let her gloat at them while they were in their cells before execution, including me
> I said I supported Chrysalis ever since they met
> Chyrsalis said she didn't need my hugs, then left
>
> Therefore, Eagleclaw deduced:
> Chrysalis looked back at the hugs in school.
> The Realization: "Wait. She didn't hug me because she needed me. She hugged me because she thought I was a frightened child. She wasn't worshipping me; she was petting me."
> The Shame: The memory of those "sisterly hugs" curdles instantly. Every warm memory becomes a moment of humiliation where the Great Queen was treated like a charity case by a teenager.
>
> I was a 15 year old teenager giving my honorary fellow teenage sister a hug.
> "I thought I was protecting her. I thought my love was a shield."
> "I didn't know she was ten years older. I didn't know she looked at my hugs and felt disgust. I treated a Wolf like a Puppy, and she hated me for it."
> She was a narcissistic adult feeling insulted
>
> Eagleclaw never spoke out about Chrysalis even after the 1007 betrayal because she feared VOPS assassinating Grover. She bowed to Eros to get permission to help raise Grover (like a governess/servant, the very thing she and Chrysalis promised they would never be when they met, subservient) and kept her beak shut, humiliated and humbled. Grover asked for mercy for his aunt, it was her duty to raise him.
>
> Eros's logic: "You used to feast while the people starved. Now, you will wash the Emperor’s clothes and teach him his letters. You will learn the dignity of labor."
> Thranx was the changeling Chrysalis could have been, the friend Chrysalis pretended to be. He was an uncle to Grover. Chrysalis assassinated him too.
> She is speaking out now to save the future of Herzland and because Grover is wise beyond his years and can bear it
>
> Archon Eros started with her stated reason for supporting him over me - prevent Kemerskai or communism. Now I end with the real reason.
> The Real Reason (Internal Narcissism): "Eagleclaw looks at me and sees the insecure girl who was bullied. She knows I am not a Goddess. As long as she lives, I am vulnerable."
> The 1007 betrayal - "she chose to be the manticore they laughed at instead of be my sister"

**Characters:**
* **[[Queen Chrysalis]]**
> **Analysis:** When Chrysalis betrayed her in 1007, it wasn't just political. Chrysalis realized that Gabriella pitying her (by keeping her secret) was the greatest insult of all. Chrysalis doesn't want a friend who "accepts" her despite being a bug; she wants subjects who worship her.
> Eagleclaw says "She chose to be the manticore they laughed at instead of be my sister"
>
> Eros looks at Chrysalis and sees an apex predator. He fears her. He negotiates with her as a dangerous superpower. Gabriella looks at Chrysalis and sees The Girl She Hugged. She sees the vulnerability. A narcissist will always choose the person who fears them (Validation of Power) over the person who pities/loves them (Reminder of Weakness). Chrysalis betrays Gabriella because she wants to erase the version of herself that needed a hug.

* **[[Gabriella Eagleclaw]]**

**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** When Gabriella Eagleclaw describes giving Chrysalis a hug and Chrysalis probably feeling it was humiliating, Twilight and Applejack instinctively hug each other due to tragedy of it.
>
> Twilight saved Applejack in the trench (vulnerability), and Applejack loved her for it.
> comforted Chrysalis in the classroom (vulnerability), and Chrysalis hated her for it.
> It proves that Love requires Humility, which Chrysalis lacks completely.

* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
> **Update:** Eagleclaw knows exactly how Chrysalis used finance to screw over their classmates because she laughed along with Chrysalis for 26 years. Eros was supposed to execute her, but Grover begged him to spare his aunt. Eagleclaw stays quiet because she hates Eros for opposing her but also because Chrysalis threatened her with killing Grover through infiltration if she ever spoke out.


**Themes:**
* **[[Generosity and Equity]]** (Demonstration)
> **Commentary:** The betrayal shows that the industrialists didn't care about liberty for all griffons. They only cared about the liberty to enrich themselves. That's not the kind of liberty that can sustain a free and just society. They don't want Equity, only Rugged Individualism. They are the poseurs who are the true enemy of the Republic.

* **[[Loyalty and Kinship]]**
> **Commentary:** The Eagleclaw revelation is a Kinship story at its core. Eagleclaw's testimony is built on a specific memory: "I knew what you were, and I loved you anyway." That is the purest possible definition of chosen kinship: not despite difference but in full knowledge of it. Eagleclaw chose Chrysalis as kin. Her testimony is the account of having that kinship betrayed and destroyed.

* **[[Rugged Individualism / Wedge Issues Deconstructed]]**
> **Commentary:** The patriarchy will also get highlighted when Chrysalis appeals to Gabriella because they are both brilliant and powerful women who deserve their place in the spotlight. Chrysalis, posing as an elite and rich teenage griffoness (even though Chrysalis is already in her 30s by this point), would have met Gabriella (a teenager) during their studies. They will instantly bond over shared ambition. Chrysalis will sell her race inferiority complex as a gender inferiority complex to charm Gabriella.
>
> The Industrialists used "Rugged Individualism" as a weapon. They dismantled Kemerskai's coalition by bribing the leaders and telling the common male griffons that "true strength" comes from individual dominance (patriarchy), not collective bargaining (unions/democracy).


**Codex:**
* **[[Herzland Republican Revolution of 978]]** (History)
> **Note:** Chrysalis revealed as an instigator of the counterrevolution

* **[[The Predator's Dilemma (Coltbert's Paper)]]** (Concept)
> **Note:** Chrysalis read the paper and suggested how to run the counterrevolution
> Eagleclaw explicitly mentions Chrysalis being obsessed about a paper from some Aquileian pony deviant professor

* **[[Magical Engineering]]** (MagicSystem)
> **Note:** The start of Chrysalis's branch of magical engineering that led to the love harvester.

* **[[Banking]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Chrysalis wants to ruin the specific nobles who bullied her. She does not need to drain the entire nation's gold to do this; she only needs to target their gold. The Herzland nobles are "Rugged Individualists" who believe in the Gold Standard because they want to hoard wealth.
> Chrysalis's Tactic: She uses her Skyfall shell companies to offer these nobles massive loans denominated in Skyfall Marks (Fiat) to modernize their factories.
>
> The Hook: "You need new machinery to compete with the Republic. We will lend you the money. The interest rate is low."
> The Squeeze: The loans are easy to get, but the collateral is their Land and Titles.
> The "Monte Cristo" Twist: Chrysalis manipulates the market. She floods the market with cheap goods (using her slave labor in the Hives) to crash the price of whatever those specific nobles are selling (e.g., textiles or steel).
> The Result: The nobles go bankrupt. They default on their loans. Chrysalis’s shell banks seize their estates. She effectively evicts the bullies from their ancestral homes before Eros even fires a shot. She proves they are "bad capitalists."
> Chrysalis then packs up all the machinery and ships it all to the hives
>
> When Eros takes power and executes the remaining nobles (after Chrysalis gets the last laugh in their jail cells), he inherits a broken economy. He cannot use the Gold Standard (because the gold is gone/hoarded) and he hates the "Liberal" Fiat of Kemerskai.
> He creates a Theocratic Command Economy. Eros creates a currency backed by Divine Providence and State Violence. The Backing: It isn't backed by gold or laws. It is backed by the Seized Assets of the Traitors. These are Restoration Bills, and they function just like MEFO bills.
> Whenever it approaches insolvency, Chrysalis buys them up and provides investment and military aid. Eros has to pay the bills later.
> She went from manipulating just her stupid to classmates to manipulating all of Herzland, all to keep them weak so she can invade them later after she is done with Equestria.

* **[[The Count of Mont-Borée]]** (Unset)
> **Note:** Revealed here


---
### SCENE 28.4: Trimmel picks up where Eagleclaw left off
> **Synopsis:** Probably a private conversation with just Applejack and Twilight
>
> Trimmel says he recalls Chrysalis brooding after returning from Griffenheim in 1007. She was less engaging in the Olenia invasion plans
>
> I thought she was just feeling jittery about the economy collapsing if the invasion failed or if the occupation wasn't fruitful - if the MEFO bills weren't made solvent, the hives would revolt and break apart after the last 5 years of hard work
>
> The "Monster" Solution: By betraying Gabriella, Chrysalis proves (to herself) that she is no longer the girl who needed a hug. She is a cold, rational Queen who sacrifices anyone for the objective.
>
> The Twist: She expects this to make her feel powerful. Instead, it leaves her hollow. She killed the only person who actually liked her to satisfy her ego. This hollowness drives her later obsession with "conquering the world"—she is trying to fill the void left by the friendship she murdered.
>
> And she did, with the Olenia occupation, love tax and investment. She stopped brooding and started beliving the idea that she would liberate the world. She got out of the slump from the resolution of the petty high school revenge of 1007 and into the Megalomanical World Conquest of 1011
>
> 1007 (Herzland) was Hollow: Killing her classmates and betraying Gabriella felt good for a moment, but it was dirty. It was about the past. It left her with ghosts.
> 1008 (Olenia) was Regenerative: Creating a functioning client state is about the future.
>     Seeing a Olenian factory churn out tank parts for the Hive gives her a dopamine hit that revenge never could. It validates her Intellect, not just her Power.
>     The Pivot: She stops thinking, "I want to hurt the people who hurt me," and starts thinking, "I have the formula to fix the world's inefficiency."
>
> Trimmel says this is when Chrysalis sold the country on the belief that they were liberators
> The existence of Coltbert’s parloirs is her smoking gun.
>     She sees ponies sneaking into the dark to drink wine, have affairs, and feel intensity.
>     Her Conclusion: "See? They want to be like us. They want ambition. They want passion. But Celestia has lobotomized their society so they have to hide it in the shadows."
> The Cure: She believes she is breaking down the walls of the Walled Garden. She is forcing the ponies to "grow up."
>     "I am not enslaving you; I am giving you a Job. I am giving you Utility. Under Celestia, you were a pet. Under me, you are a Cog in the Machine of History. That is an upgrade."
>
> She didn't sell the war as "Eating Ponies." She sold it as "Exporting the Revolution."
> Because the deer were raiding savages (vikings). She "civilized" them and they shed their pride (at gunpoint)
>
> That is the mindset of Chrysalis and the meritocrats when the invasion of Equestria began
> That is why Chrysalis was so insulted when Applejack resisted

**Characters:**
* **[[Queen Chrysalis]]**
> **Analysis:** She views herself as the Great Modernizer. The fact that the ponies scream and fight back is, to her, just the tantrum of a child who doesn't want to go to school.
>
> She isn't fighting to be evil. She is fighting to prove that her way—The Chrysalis Way—is the only way to fix a broken world. Applejack's job is to prove that the world wasn't broken, just different.
>
> Chrysalis's takeaway from Coltbert's paper was to always fight an asymmetric war!


**Themes:**
* **[[Bottom Up > Top Down]]**
> **Commentary:** Both Applejack and Chrysalis agree on one thing: Celestia’s Stagnation was wrong.
>
>     Chrysalis’s Solution: "Liberate them by forcing them into the Machine." (The Factory as a Cage).
>
>     Applejack’s Solution: "Liberate them by giving them the keys to the Machine." (The Factory as a Co-op).


---
### SCENE 28.5: Kemerskai gives the final speech about national unity before stepping down as head of the NRP
> **Synopsis:** Now that the Republican dream has been achieved, Kemerskai is stepping down effective immediately and Sunglider is taking over the party.
> Kemerskai is actually crying in his triumph and farewell speech. He is speaking from the heart, with no performative posturing required.
>
> Kemerskai praises Eros's morals despite them being enemies. Eros is not the poseur; Chrysalis is. Eros's crime is allowing Chrysalis to operate in Herzland.
>
> In the unified GR, worship of the traditional Herzlander gods like Boreas will be allowed and synthesized with morality and the Republic, in an idealistic commentary about how Christianity has a lot of fundamental good teachings, but a lot of it is abused by poseurs in real life
> This is the ultimate pragmatic move
> If the Republic simply banned Boreas worship (like the Communists/Equalists might), the Herzland would revolt.
> By co-opting it, they pacify the conservative Herzlanders. They say, "We aren't killing your god; we are freeing him from the corrupt nobles who used his name in vain."
>
> Kemerskai calls for all of Herzland, and all of griffonkind, to unite against Chrysalis, who divided them. They will go to war with the Changeling Hegemony (whether Equestria is in or not)
>
> Kemerskai also demands that Skyfall extradite all war criminals or else there will be a trade embargo. The merchants of death must finally face consequences for fueling the war on both sides and enabling Chrysalis.
>
> Once the Reich is integrated into the Republic, the resulting GR still carries on the fundamental ethos of the (idealized) Reich, Grover I and II's image, Grover III's legacy, and Herzlander culture, which is the "Honor" aspect.
>
> The story isn't about the Republic destroying the Reich; it is about the Republic rescuing the Reich's soul from the corrupt nobility and the meddling changelings.
>
> The conflict between "Boreas/Honor" and "Liberty/Republic" was always artificial—a wedge driven by corrupt nobles and exploited by Chrysalis.
> •The Old Reich: "Honor" meant obedience to a superior.
> •The Old Republic: "Liberty" meant rebellion against authority.
> •The New Synthesis: "Honor" means obedience to your own conscience and your fellow citizen. You don't fight for the Emperor because you have to; you fight for your neighbor because it is the Honorable thing to do.
> The "Atomic Family" unit is no longer just a fortress against the world; it is the "School of Honor" where young griffons are taught to be good citizens.
>
> The Republicans don't tear down the statues of Grover I and II. Instead, they re-brand them.
> •Grover I (The First Emperor): He is no longer celebrated as a "King who conquered the tribes." He is celebrated as the First Patriot—the first griffon who saw a "Nation" where others saw only warring clans. He is the grandfather of Griffonian unity.
> •Grover II (The Crusader): He is honored not for his title, but for his Spirit. He represents the tenacity of the Herzlander who refuses to be subjugated.
> •The Visuals: In the new Republic, you might see a statue of Grover I standing next to a statue of Kemerskai. One represents the Body of the nation (Unity), the other represents the Mind (Democracy).
> Grover III (The Unifier) is celebrated as the father of the modern Griffonian State and the Enlightenment.
>
> The end of the great Griffonian War goes from a subjugation (Republic conquering Reich) into a re-contextualization. The moment the Griffons realize they weren't fighting for "Destiny" or "Liberty" as much as they were dancing to a foreign tune, their national pride demands retribution against the puppetmaster.
>
> The Republican Realization: The die-hard Republicans realize that the Reich wasn't just "evil reactionaries"; they were desperate hostages. The "madness" of the Regency wasn't inherent Griffon incompetence; it was sabotage.
> The Imperial Realization: The Imperial loyalists realize that their "honor" was weaponized against them. They thought they were serving Boreas and the Emperor, but they were actually serving a bug Queen who viewed them as meat shields against Equestria.
>
> This shared humiliation births a new, terrifying resolve. The two ideologies don't merge, but they align against a common existential threat.
> •The Republicans: They want to export the Revolution and destroy tyrants. Chrysalis is the ultimate tyrant.
> •The Imperialists: They want to restore honor and protect the Fatherland. Chrysalis disgraced their honor and threatened the Fatherland.

**Characters:**
* **[[Alexander Kemerskai]]**
> **Analysis:** By handing power to Sunglider voluntarily when he could have easily been "President for Life" in a reunited Herzland, he retroactively validates his entire 35-year rule.
>
>     It proves the "Martial Law" and the "Long Presidency" were means to an end, not the end itself.


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]** (Demonstration)
> **Commentary:** Kemerskai makes clear the distinction between moral Eros and immoral Chrysalis

* **[[Rugged Individualism / Wedge Issues Deconstructed]]**
> **Commentary:** In the unified GR, worship of the traditional Herzlander gods like Boreas will be allowed and synthesized with morality and the Republic, in an idealistic commentary about how Christianity has a lot of fundamental good teachings, but a lot of it is abused by poseurs in real life


**Codex:**
* **[[Griffonian Republic]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** The future of the republic

* **[[Skyfall Trade Federation]]** (Nation)
> **Note:** Kemerskai is belligerent to Skyfall and implies punitive action


---
### SCENE 28.6: Sunglider speech
> **Synopsis:** Sunglider says there will be an integration period of Herzland into the republic, but also a war against Chrysalis. It will be volunteer only. (the goal is national unity)
> Once the war is over, there will be new nationwide elections (This is very in character for Sunglider, who is an idealist and militant harmonist. Also the realpolitik of using the war against Chrysalis as a unifying force, given Eros and Eagleclaw's testimony, and allowing buffer time for economic and cultural integration)
>
> Upon the next election, Sunglider is instituting term limits for president and Senate (although Senate you can come back after a gap)
>
> Big twist is that Eros's new Reich wasn't so far from the GR's civic virtues (equivalent to the elements of liberty) because he actually cared about the griffons, and the war crimes trials are simply about letting the posers in and the reggued individualists take influence and accepting Chrysalis help
> This is the fuel for AJ to take over and Luna to back AJ
>
> Of course there's going to be national unity against Chrysalis who copied and weaponized the worst parts of their culture, which helps integration and reconciliation
>
> Sunglider mentions the "hollow copy of our culture" angle in his speech

**Themes:**
* **[[Loyalty and Kinship]]**
> **Commentary:** Kinship at a national scale


---
### SCENE 28.7: Kemerskai and Grover meet (maybe merge with next)
> **Synopsis:** Kemerskai meets Grover at a memorial for Thranx.
>
> Grover is entirely onboard with the republic and launching a unifying crusade against Chyrsalis in Thranx's memory
>
> Grover VI, who is nearing adulthood by now, says he is proud of both his auntie and his "old man" mentor for owning up to their pasts. He becomes a private citizen. Obviously there is no trial for him. Kemerskai goes to great lengths to announce Grover as a public citizen in some bombastic display in order to shame any remaining rugged individualists or nobles who might be getting ideas.

---
### SCENE 28.8: Grover, Thranx, and Trimmel
> **Synopsis:** Thranx is the first creature who views child Grover as not "the emperor" but a friend (because he is not a griffon, so he doesn't have to see the "emperor of all griffonkind". He just sees a lonely and intelligent and empathetic child.)
>
> Trimmel and Grover meet at a memorial service for Thranx. They talk about Thranx, who was Trimmel's comrade in the Winter Revolution.
>
> The Role of Thranx (The Uncle Who Knew Too Much)
> Thranx is the emotional anchor for Grover VI.
> •The Lesson: Thranx teaches Grover that culture and morality are choices, not biology. A Changeling can be "Herzlander" in spirit.
> •The Death: When Thranx refuses to return to the Hive to fight Equestria, Chrysalis has VOPS kill him to prevent a "Good Changeling" precedent from spreading.
> •The Impact: Grover VI deducing the truth (that his "Ally" killed his "Uncle") is the moment he mentally breaks with the Changelings. It sets the stage for him to accept Eros's surrender later—he knows the Reich is on the wrong side of the war because of who their friends are.
> Thranx aligns with Eros's philosophy a lot. He sees that top-down authority "worked" because Chrysalis imposed a meritocracy. He buys into Eros's rhetoric about a moral emperor setting an example to all the griffons, which Chrysalis is a sham of. That's why Thranx refuses to go home and why he gets assassinated. It's why Thranx takes it upon himself to raise Grover as his uncle and teach him about meritocracy while advising him against the crippled morals and lies of Chrysalis.
>
> Trimmel expresses regret that Thranx spoke out and died for it while he continued to lead the Heer, intending to be executed at the end, only for Applejack to grant him mercy.
>
> Grover says he guessed Chrysalis was responsible, but didn't have any proof
> Grover did not confront Eros about the lie because he realized Eros, the Lord Protector, was powerless and scared of Chrysalis. It made Grover realize the Reich is not invincible.
>
> Grover had listened to Trimmel's speech as well as Henri's and Applejack's and forced Eros to listen to them too.
> Grover says Thranx told him about the Winter Revolution, and Trimmel's part of the speech about the Winter Revolution mentions Thranx, who was killed by Chrysalis. This is what Grover listened to and latched onto. Since Thranx was the good changelings and murdered by Chrysalis, and the GR is hosting Thranx's comrade, doesn't that make us the villains? The fools with no dignity, her colony?
>
> It shifts the power balance between Eros and Eagleclaw.
>     Before, Eros was the Jailer and Eagleclaw the Prisoner.
>     Now, they are Fellow Prisoners.
>     Eros might even tolerate Eagleclaw slightly more after this, because they now share a secret hatred of the "Ally" who controls them both.
>
> Eros admitted the truth to Grover ahead of the unconditional surrender announcement.
> Eros and Eagleclaw spilled everything to Grover, agreeing to tell the whole truth to save Herzland from Applejack who values honesty, and whose weapons are leading the way to Griffenheim as they speak, whose country was destroyed by the bug queen who copied their culture and got everything from them. They were the enablers of the horror that befell Equestria, and now Equestria's weapons were pointed at them
> That's why the surrender came
>
> Thranx said the statthalters prove his species are just thugs
> He had to speak out, otherwise he was implictly letting Chrysalis label him to the world. And he died for it.

**Characters:**
* **[[Trimmel]]**
* **[[Grover VI]]** (Protagonist, Impact: CoreValueLearned)

**Themes:**
* **[[Loyalty and Kinship]]**
> **Commentary:** Thranx represented Kinship and Chrysalis murdered him for it


---
### SCENE 28.9: Applejack, Twilight and Flurry Heart meet Grover
> **Synopsis:** There was an open line of communication between Eros and Kemerskai regarding the rules and laws of the potential occupation (integration) upon the unconditional surrender, and eleven year old Grover will articulate his grievance on chrysalis, why he is asking Eros to surrender honorably, and his plea that all griffons are treated with dignity. Kemerskai is blown away by the 11 year old emperor prodigy's eloquence and humility, when he was expecting a spoiled brat, and this establishes a lot of trust and good faith. This is only possible because Eros invited the snake (Synovial) back, who brought total war to their gentleman's war, got chopped, and now Grover has the moral upper hand over Eros (and Benito, whose stance of protecting the emperor and his divine right at all costs let him tolerate the changelings). So AJ was indirectly responsible for the peace in Griffonia. This will be layed out during "Authenticity"
>
> Grover mentions his meeting with Kemerskai
>
> Flurry meets Grover at Thranx's memorial. They have both heard about each other and their great deeds and great responsibilities for being children.
>
> Grover says how amazed he is that Flurry Heart held up the crystal heart's shield. He has done nothing compared to her.
>
> Flurry Heart says how Grover holds himself high even without parents to support him.
>
> Grover talks about how when he was five, he stopped Eros from executing his Aunt, and that made him realize the weight of the Reichstone on his head. Flurry can relate about everyone looking at them.
>
> Grover mentions how Thranx was the first creature who saw him as just a scared child. They both realize they had bug uncles who changed the course of their lives.

**Characters:**
* **[[Flurry Heart]]**

---
### SCENE 28.10: Applejack meets with Gaudreau again

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** This should be a lesson to AJ: at first she thought Aquileia was a monolith, an FJA paradise that her friends who saved her in Tall Tale sold her on. Then upon arrival she thought the FJA is under threat from Vérany and Kemerskai. Finally she learns that the FJA and PNdA actually love each other economically while squabbling on only surface level and compromising on policy, and that's how democracy actually works.
>
> Rulership in the modern world is not a "good vs evil" fight like it was with Chrysalis
> It's not soul vs soulless either
> Democracy sounds like a fight but acts like a team.
> In a Monarchy (Equestria), silence implies unity.
> In a Republic (Aquileia), noise implies health. The shouting matches in Parliament are the "steam valve" that prevents civil war. The friction is where the compromise happens.
>
> This is actually really reasuring to her because it means she won't be The Princess 2.0 and won't be responsible for everything
>
> Also, AJ's friendship with Kemerskai where he teaches her realpolitik (the truth does not speak for itself, you have to roar) also has an effect on him: harmonic capitalism is viable, and Aquileia should not be annexed. The pan Griffonian dream stops at Aquileia (but can still move onto other regions who want to join the GR, which isn't just Herzland, it's Herzland + Cloudbury + Vedina with universal translators)
> He shifts his vision from a Unitary Superstate (One Republic to rule them all) to a Federal Alliance (The Republican Pact).


---
### SCENE 28.11: Meeting Felix Birkeland and Longsword
> **Synopsis:** They hear about Longsword's liberation from the reformisten, reconstruction
> There can be some connection with Polish apples here?
> Felix Birkleland is a true believer in harmony, unlike Velvet. He approaches Applejack wishing to be the leader of the Olenian liberation, vouched for by Starry Night and other Longsword ponies and griffons

**Codex:**
* **[[Olenia]]** (Nation)

---
### SCENE 28.12: Applejack speaks with Kemerskai
> **Synopsis:** Kemerskai won't pass away of old age as in EaW canon shortly after Griffenheim. But there will be signs of deterioration as AJ talks to him. Now that the dream has been achieved, Kemerskai isn't running on adrenaline anymore, and 35 years of struggle are catching up to him all at once. AJ expresses regret that Kemerskai felt the need to drink so much at the party. Kemerskai admits he cannot be performative without being genuine (blurring the lines between honesty and posture, showing how the intent is authentic even if some posturing is required to outflank the posers), and that includes alcohol and long hours. Especially to impress powerful foreign leaders (wink).
>
> You didn't have to drink at the party, look at the consequences 
> But I wouldn't be myself if I didn't, even if I know I'm too old for it. And I had to win over you and your friends (powerful foreign leaders). I must do it to strip away the allure of the greedy griffon so we can build a state that works for all (even at the cost of my own health)
>
> This is the deconstruction and evolution of honesty into Authenticity, which is honesty with a healthy dose of posture to get the point across and win minds, in order to achieve the level of power and buy in required to enact real change and peace. It's realpolitik. Applejack uses the lesson here in the next chapter to effectively dismantle the ponies first bloc by espousing the greatness of harmonic capitalism and multi species equality.
>
> I need to verify that "Authenticity" is the right word for "Honesty + healthy posture" for the purpose of defeating actual posers and enacting real change (realpolitik)
>
> AJ bans him from any more drinking and vows to get him the magical tech necessary for a good retirement. Kemerskai says if AJ wants to help him then she has to help all the griffons (with a wink)
> AJ glares at him and says surely you have a good pension? Can't you come to Equestria for some medical treatment if you need it? This isn't communism and the country isn't poor.
> Kemerskai laughs and says that was a lesson in authenticity/healthy posture/realpolitik to reaffirm a commitment on economic trade between their countries. Don't worry, I do have a pension and will be seeking out the best treatment in Herzland. Looking forward to seeing the intersection of medicine across all their republics
>
> AJ takes some serious notes.
>
> And no more alcohol, I promise. Then Kemerskai speaks in horribly accented Equestrian, "Cross my heart, hope to fly, stick a cupcake in my eye" and does the motions. (Speech should be normal until then, then italics with und my eye etc.) Since AJ's universal translator is configured for Equestrian and it's picking up Equestrian (however horribly accented), Kemerskai's voice is unaltered. And he does the motions! It shows that Kemerskai looked into Equestrian culture as a genuine friend, and it subverts AJ's one critique of the Universal Translator from the Cloudbury chapter. Usually AJ hears a perfectly eloquent and loud and passionate Equestrian military general voice (like General MacArthur), but in this moment, she is hearing an extremely Herzlander accented voice reciting the silly phrase.
>
> He'll get some robust Equestrian medical treatment for his retirement. In the epilogue, AJ asks Grover how the old man is doing and he said splendid, since he's not allowed to have any alcohol and the universal healthcare system is robust, especially since Equestrian tech/research is integrated with Herzlander industry and medical research.
> This paints the point that in canon, Kemerskai gave his all and died a hero. In my story, Kemerskai gave his all, and the Equestrian alliance allows him to retire and flourish and enjoy the fruits of his 35 year struggle for many more years.

**Characters:**
* **[[Applejack]]**
> **Analysis:** She is humble. She hates the spotlight. She thinks speeches don't dig trenches. But now Kemerskai has taught her that the truth does not speak for itself; you have to roar. Posture is not evil; propaganda is. The posture can be decoupled from the propaganda and used for Conviction, just like how industry can be decouple from evil for prosperity.


**Threads:**
* **[[TwiJack]]**
> **Update:** "If you take your time to do things the right way, your work will speak for itself."
> That's what let Twilight relax in the first place
> The truth does not speak for itself, you have to roar. This is the opposite of the cider episode lesson
>
> Friendship lessons aren't black and white, fact or things to worship


**Codex:**
* **[[Universal Translator]]** (Technology)
> **Note:** Kemerskai recites the pinkie promise in Equestrian. Since Applejack's translator is configured for Equestrian, the Herzlander accent still comes through.
>
> Just because you have the translator doesn't mean you can't try to learn and speak another's language in a way that is meaningful to them if you want to put in the effort to show that you care.


---
## CHAPTER 29: Ambition
> **Chapter Note:** Maybe for the first election cycle, it will be only party lists with no follow up for the mixed member election because they need to get the government going? And individual candidates will have a full cycle to campaign? Maybe 1 year gap? Or is this against the spirit of the Republic?
>
> Tribal isolationist thestrals, who would they vote for? A big majority of thestrals are already full on Harmonic Republicanism for economy and fraternity. Especially considering my new lore about how they were not isolationist, but actually black market drug lords on the global maritime economy
> Would the isolationists just not vote? But since it's compulsory, they technically have a fine, so I guess would they ironically vote for ponies first to stay isolationist? No, they would vote for Celestial, despite hating her (or being indifferent to her) since Celestia would roll back the clock and leave them alone.
> **Chapter Note:** After destroying Gilded Trust in the debate, Applejack rides the momentum with Kemerskai style speeches about the glory of Republic, harmony, using magic and industry for everyone to solve problems improve life, healing and strength, and combining every creature's unique talents for everyone's glory 
>
> When asked if she'll keep the POWs (a wedge issue) she will just repeat "I will work to make life better for you than even before the war, and that's the authentic truth" while not giving the wedge issue any air
> **Chapter Note:** With so much commoditized magic and industry, being an alicorn isn't so drastic anymore. Any creature can fly and use magic. Any creature can use the Alicorn of love's spells - conception, gender swap, which was the role of the princess of love (and contraception, fufufu). They even have the collective might to move the sun and moon now through industry and love donations - and Celestia and Luna still doing it because it's their special talent and comparative advantage proves harmonic capitalism works. The fact that they CAN replace the sisters but don't NEED to is the whole point. And also a thesis about never stop improving even when life is good. Never stagnate. Just because someone raises the sun and moon for you doesn't mean you should sit back and just live your life passively. Having ambition is good
> **Chapter Note:** Celestia agrees to the referendum rules not because she wants to win, but because if the overwhelming majority of the populace legally demands she remain their protector, she has to accept the burden to avoid causing a panic. It removes the ambiguity.
>
> Celestia’s silence during the campaign is not aloofness; it is an act of passive resistance against her own voting bloc. She is intentionally starving her own party of political oxygen, desperately hoping Applejack can successfully sell Harmonic Capitalism to the masses so Celestia is legally permitted to step down. Applejack’s frustration should stem from realizing she isn't fighting Celestia—she is fighting the terrified psychology of her own neighbors.

### SCENE 29.1: Updated Polling Results 35/44/21
> **Synopsis:** Applejack arrives home and sees updated polling results
>
> The Impact on the Voters:
> ◦Harmonists: See that she brought Peace without Conquest.
> ◦Ponies First: See that she destroyed the enemy's will to fight.
> ◦Celestials: See that she respected the Sovereignty of nations (by leaving).
>
> It's still not enough. Now that she learned from Kemerskai, she is ready to apply his lessons on posture to rip apart the PFP leader

---
### SCENE 29.2: Applejack and Gilded Trust have a debate
> **Synopsis:** If you vote for him, you are voting to be replaced by a machine. If you vote for me, you are voting to be irreplaceable.
>
> The commentary is that Crisis Absolutism (The idea that a strongman is needed in emergencies) creates a tyrant who won't give up power when the emergency ends. He treats peace like a business merger he needs to win.
>
> Gilded Trust is the "Cool Boss" who becomes the "Nightmare Boss." He represents the seductive danger of Hyper-Capitalism: it builds amazing things very fast, but it burns people as fuel to do it.
>
> Applejack has to destroy Gilded Trust with both facts and theatrics. She has to show strength
> She should point out replacing "The Element of Honesty" with "The Lioness of Tall Tale"
> Gilded Trust is in a glamourous suit, Applejack is in her general uniform
>
> Applejack internalizes the strengths of all the factions she met:
> Aquileia - the ideology and asset specificity
> GR - the industrial power, political will and conviction, solidarity
>
> Gilded Trust says Rockfeller and the other industrialists sold out to Skyfall and Chrysalis, now Applejack wants to sell out to Aquileia, she wants to sleep with griffons, and be greedy like the griffons in Herzland
> Rockfeller let Chrysalis colonize Las Pegasus, I kicked him out
> Applejack wants to let griffons colonize Equestria, we can't have that
> Make Equestria Safe Again
>
> The buffalo are saboteurs who want chaos
> These buffalo are big, very tough, very nasty folks
> Applejack surrendered our soverign oil to them
> If you vote for her, you are voting to give your home to thefirst savage who throws a rock at it
>
> Satirizes the "Great Man Theory": The billionaire claims to be the inventor, but he actually just bought the invention (or in this case, the intel) from someone else and put his name on it.
> You aren't the great man. You had help from buffalo. They helped you because you offered them guns. That's the honest truth. You had friends who weren't ponies. That ain't a bad thing! But you ain't the great pony you say you are!
>
> Thestrals are drug dealers
> Don't even get me started on the drug dealers
> Applejack didn't stop them, she helped them sell more drugs to Skyfall
>
> I thought you hate Skyfall
>
> Gilded Trust says we can become an Equestrian Empire and dominate the world economically with our power, no one will ever be able to touch us again.
> Applejack says he wants to use the machinery of the Republic to build a new tyranny and be the next Chrysalis and turn you all into drones. I want World Peace instead. Gilded Trust tries to say the rest of the world will never love us, they are vicious and nasty like Skyfall griffons

**Characters:**
* **[[Princess Celestia]]**
> **Analysis:** Gilded Trust calls her "Sleepy Celestia"
> Applejack respects Celestia's chair
>
> The debate audience think Celestia is being negligent or out of touch, but the reality is Celestia is staying out of the debate on purpose so that Applejack can earn her legitimacy
>
> Gilded Trust is the exact manifestation of the 15% Tycoon class that caused the 930 ALB lockdown. He is a rugged individualist running a company-scrip empire. If Celestia debates him, she legitimizes him as a peer. By refusing to attend, she is executing a moral quarantine.
>
> When Gilded Trust says Celestia is sitting in her castle and ignoring everypony, Applejack counters by saying Celestia is busy giving therapy

* **[[Gilded Trust]]**
> **Analysis:** Gilded Trust claims to be a rugged individualist who built his empire from scratch. The materialist truth is that he is driven by the burning resentment of a nepotism-beneficiary who was denied his payout. He didn't build his empire out of pure innovation; he built it out of spite. He doesn't care about you, he is only fighting the ghost of his aunt
>
> His hatred of Skyfall is not rooted in a moral objection to their predatory insurance or their sweatshops; his hatred is rooted in the fact that a Skyfall Griffon stole his money. He equates "Foreigner" with "Thief." He is setting up the same sweatshops in Equestria with is face on it
>
> Applejack counters by saying Gilded Trust is only a ponies first nationalist because his Aunt Gilded Lily gave her company to a griffon instead of Gilded Trust
> His aunt sold the country to Chrysalis
>
> Gilded Trust capitalizes on that and says yes, my aunt sold out to Chrysalis and adopted her, and I refused the whole way. I am a patriot
> Applejack panics a little, then pivots and says that just shows why we can't have big corporations that are unregulated. In my republic, Gilded Lily would not be able to exploit Equestria the way she did. That is what Gilded Trust wants to do


**Threads:**
* **[[Stagnation's Redemption]]**
> **Update:** she can treat the ponies of Celestia's party with respect and use this in the debate to get votes
> We are not the only victims of Chrysalis. The Griffonian Republic will attack Chrysalis either way. We can either close our eyes, or we can help our friends.
> We will not be going to a war economy anymore. No more mandatory factory work or love donations. Everypony who wants to go back life before can do it. Everypony who wants to work towards a future can also do it. We won't be like Aquileia or Kemerskai's early phase or the total mobilization anymore. A vote for me is a vote to choose your old life again. That is a promise.
>
> Therefore, Applejack won't be adopting Coltbert's reforms which made everyone a noble/predator/snob and made the cost of conquest too high. She will increase capacity and use laws and systems to ensure the ambitious don't intentionally or accidentally hurt the content (Kemerskai's model). No creature left behind, every creature is an Equestrian 
> Using Aquileia's economic model but Griffonian Republic's cultural model


**Codex:**
* **[[Banking]]** (SocietalDifferences)
> **Note:** Applejack defeats Gilded Trust not just by calling him a poseur, but by exposing the Gilded Bit as a cage.
>
>     The Argument: "He paid you in money that only works at his store. He didn't make you rich; he made you inventory. A War Bond is a promise from the entire Republic. A Gilded Bit is a coupon for a casino that might close tomorrow."
>
> She says the Gilded Bits are Serfdom
> But she also says the Gilded Bits will be honored by war bonds. Currently Gilded Trust holds the war bonds that back the gilded bits.
> The workers can exchange all their gilded bits for war bonds, then Applejack has all the gilded bits and can demand Gilded Trust's war bonds in exchange
>
> His xenophobia saved him financially. Because he didn't trust "Griffon Paper," he wasn't exposed to the Skyfall crash.
>
> The Twist: He is still a "Poseur" because he claims he did it for patriotism. The truth is he did it for control. He didn't want to be a small fish in Skyfall's pond; he wanted to be the Whale in the Las Pegasus pond.

### SCENE 29.3: Thorax reports on the state of the Hives
> **Synopsis:** most of the fanatics who followed Chrysalis to abuse ponies died on the front or in Canterlot because they were in the occupied lands that were cut off. The occupiers of Vanhoover and Acornage made it back though
> Most of the civilian population is turning against Chrysalis and is receiving harmonist propaganda over the radio, so VOPS is cracking down to keep the country militarized
> More drafts, more factory work
> Changeling civilians start fleeing their homes and going to Tall Tale
> The ponies across Equestria start to feel more sympathetic to the changelings and there is a slow shift from wanting to continue the war as revenge towards wanting to continue the war to liberate the changelings from Chrysalis’s oppression

---
### SCENE 29.4: The Skyfall Resolution
> **Synopsis:** Kemerskai and Gaudreau are ready to invade Skyfall to punish the merchants of death who bled them dry to end their tyranny
> The Skyfall council tries to draw a wedge saying Applejack funded the drugs, and they have the valve production for the magical economy
> Applejack apologizes, owns it, and says she'll donate pink love and bring rehab experts to solve the crisis once and for all
> The council refuses help because the underclass is their profit margins
> Applejack broadcasts the meeting minutes to Skyfall and says the offer is still on the table
> The Patrotten overthrow the trade council and establish a harmonic republic, nationalize Kessler's company and joins the Republican Pact
> Ships and trucks of pink love and reformed changelings from Camp Fluttershy arrive to help cure the population

**Threads:**
* **[[Camp Fluttershy]]**
> **Update:** The changelings graduate from Camp Fluttershy and become the rehab mentors, also spreading kindness and harmony


**Themes:**
* **[[Isolationism is Immoral]]**
> **Commentary:** A real superpower cleans up their own mess (criticism of Western imperialism)


**Codex:**
* **[[Caramel Marks' Communist Manifesto]]** (Backstory)
> **Note:** Caramel Marks and Fire Angel have spent decades in the smog of Skyfall, enduring arrests, beatings, and poverty, waiting for the inevitable collapse of late-stage capitalism. They view themselves as the intellectual saviors of the working class, agitating for a workers' revolution but get completely sidelined by harmonic capitalism
>
> The historical failure of Marxist revolutions in advanced, industrialized democracies stems from the introduction of the social safety net and labor unions (the New Deal/Social Democracy). When the working class is offered a tangible path to dignity, ownership, and upward mobility, the appetite for violent, totalizing state control evaporates.
>
> In Skyfall, the workers have been indoctrinated by the "temporarily embarrassed millionaire" myth. They endure the sweatshops because they dream of becoming Tycoons. Caramel Marks and Fire Angel offer them a system where no one gets to be a Tycoon, and everyone becomes a standardized, equal cog in a state machine.
>
> When the Trade Council rejects Applejack’s aid, the breaking point is reached. But when the Patrotten rise up, backed by Equestrian logistics and Aquileian FJA ideology, they do not offer the workers a command economy. They offer Asset Specificity and Equity. They offer the workers ownership of their own lathes, the right to form cooperative syndicates, and the immediate influx of Pink Love (rehab) and food. Because Harmonic Capitalism solves the resource scarcity through abundance and trade rather than rationing and purges, the material conditions that necessitate a Communist vanguard vanish overnight.
>
> The workers gain the clarity and resilience to demand fair contracts and dismantle the Trade Council, but they lose the bloodlust required to set up guillotines and gulags. The Equestrian intervention literally tranquilizes the violent phase of the revolution, leaving Marks and Angel with a pacified, constructive populace that has no use for their radicalism.

### SCENE 29.6: Chrysalis funded the boyars of Severyana for their industrial development
> **Synopsis:** After Eagleclaw's unraveling of Chrysalis's personal story and Skyfall's collapse, some former boyars reveal the truth. They do it because Eagleclaw set a precedent, the world will listen, and they want revenge after realizing they were puppeted, and validation or pity
>
> Trimmel pieces together how he was in the first level of VOPS, the surface level, and fought for the revolution. The second level of VOPS, top secret, were behind the boyars in the first place and tried and failed to faciliate a counterrevolution. They were all disguised as griffons from skyfall.

**Characters:**
* **[[Trimmel]]**
> **Analysis:** Trimmel is deeply humbled by his level of naivety.
> He recalls when he returned from the newly formed Stalliongrad and Chrysalis gave him a medal and promotion. In retrospect, she was plotting to do undo his work.


**Threads:**
* **[[The True Actions and Motivations of Chrysalis]]**
> **Update:** She wanted to destroy Stalliongrad to erase the evidence of her first failure, just as she wants to destroy Eagleclaw to erase the evidence of her first friendship.
>
> She also started the civil war in Nova Griffonia, this time choosing to back militant Highhill instead of stagnant Teafeather (the establishment, like the Boyars), but Highhill still lost due to direct Stalliongrad intervention to support the griffon communists, despite the species difference. Chrysalis cannot comprehend the fact that pony communists were welling to stick their necks out aggressively for griffon communists who were getting crushed.


**Codex:**
* **[[Luna's Banishment]]** (History)
> **Note:** At the time that Luna invented the dream altering/ambition draining spell, Severyana had not become part of Equestria yet. After Nightmare Moon's banishment, the ponies there were so scared of Nightmare Moon that Celestia left them alone. That's why they are separate from Equestria. They became a vassal in 507 ALB when they threw out the tsar and asked Celestia to be their tsarina on paper. So they could go to her to arbitrate disputes.

* **[[The Winter Revolution]]** (Backstory)

### SCENE 29.7: Thorax unveils the depth of Chrysalis's economic scheme
> **Synopsis:** They say it's extremely likely that Chrysalis operates most of the Skyfall banks that lent heavily to Equestria, according to cross checking all the seized companies and collaborators
> She copied Coltbert's investments, ever the poseur
> She undercut the other Skyfall bankers

**Codex:**
* **[[Chrysalis's Economic Scheme]]** (Backstory)

### SCENE 29.9: Luna talks to Celestia then backs Applejack in the referendum
> **Synopsis:** Big twist is that Eros's new Reich wasn't so far from the GR's civic virtues (equivalent to the elements of liberty) because he actually cared about the griffons, and the war crimes trials are simply about letting the posers in and the reggued individualists take influence and accepting Chrysalis help
> This is the fuel for AJ to take over and Luna to back AJ
>
> Luna sees how Applejack was able to help secure peace in Griffonia and decides that Applejack is going to achieve what she failed 1000 years ago: world peace. Luna talks to Celestia about it, and decides to throw her support for Applejack in spite of Celestia’s wishes, because if Applejack is right, then Celestia is free.
>
> Luna will see that the Griffonian Republic will invade Chrysalis with or without Equestrian help. That's her practical reason for trying to change Celestia's mind and backing AJ to form the Equestrian Republic.
>
> The even bigger factor is how Applejack used the pink love to fix Skyfall.
> Applejack used systemic care to prove to Luna that redemption and harmony are possible at scale. This is truly what Nightmare Moon failed to achieve. Applejack saved the creatures of Skyfall from the nightmares of addiction (griffon, pony, zebra, hippogriff, all creatures)
>
> Luna talks to Twilight and Applejack, saying how she and her sister were once unicorns too, and their special talents dramatically reduced crystal mining requirements. Star Swirl invented the alicornization spell with a one time huge crystal use so that they wouldn't need any crystals day-to-day anymore. They are only immortal because of the sun and moon. Luna has decided that that doesn't mean they have to be heads of state anymore, they can just focus on their special talent, just as the Harmonic Republic is all about, enhancing everypony's special talents.
>
> Luna also apologizes for waiting for a magical miracle to save them (and forcing Discord to try and solve it) The Republic is the miracle. Some miracles don't happen in a day.
>
> Twilight says wait, Celestia WASTED that many crystals on my wings?!
> They were new crystals from the Crystal Empire
>
> But... but... think of how many spell matrices we could've had
> Twilight, I assure you, the number of crystals spent on your wings is a fraction of the number of crystals that you turned into spell matrices
>
> After Luna leaves, Twilight mutters "I still think my wings were a bad investement"
> Applejack says "I like how fluffy they are"
> "I could have made a spell matrix for these and given you a pair too"
> "But you forgot the earth pony part. You can help me regrow Sweet Apple Acres. Uh, only if you want to. I know you have a million spell matrices to invent"
> Twilight smiles widely.
>
> Luna's announcement moves the needle 5 points from Celestial into Harmonic Republicanism

**Characters:**
* **[[Princess Luna]]** (Protagonist, Impact: CoreValueChange)
> **Analysis:** Luna realized her mistake wasn't "Ambition," but Solitude. She supports the Republic because she sees it as the "Correction" of her own failed experiment 1000 years ago.
>
> Luna mentions how when she came back from corruption, she was small, demure and isolated with only her older sister to comfort her. She felt guilty of her crimes and unwilling to see other ponies. She wrote off all ambition because of the Nightmare Moon corruption. But she also felt a total loss of identity because she was once a Warrior Princess and the Princess of the Night. Now Equestria had been scrubbed free of "warriors" and "night culture/adult culture", at least in public.
>
> Mali joining the royal guard in 1001 let Luna see that her identity still existed, just hidden away. Mali healed Luna a lot through empathy and connection. She was a master at comfort, emotional breakdowns,
> She showed me that ponies do not hate the Night. Actually, they craved it.
>
> You mean, you and her...
>
> Yes, Luna chuckled. She brought the Night back to me.
>
> I traveled across the jungle and Manehattan and the mountains. Mali was with me.
> I didn't go through with her proposed list of reforms. I chose Celestia's policies of much slower, safer ways
> Keep the thestrals separate but help them so that there's less conflict
> In doing so, I treated the thestrals just as I and Celestia treated you, as little ponies to be protected. They were already forced to be adults by the world.
>
> Mali left amicably instead of challenging me to change.
> I am now ready to change. You have proven yourself. I want you to lead


**Threads:**
* **[[World Peace / Overcoming The Predator's Dilemma]]**
> **Update:** Luna sees how Applejack was able to help secure peace in Griffonia and decides that Applejack is going to achieve what she failed 1000 years ago: world peace.


**Themes:**
* **[[Bottom Up > Top Down]]**
> **Commentary:** Luna says she bore the shame of the retreat and didn't tell Applejack about the nightmares. She held the guilt of what was happening in Vanhoover so that Applejack would not have to bear it. Now she realizes the system Applejack is trying to set up will relieve her of her singular duty and Celestia's duty to rule.
>
> She has decided that her democratization of the dream spell worked, she shared her singular duty to others. She supports Applejack’s Republic because she has tasted the freedom of not being a singular, overburdened deity. She wants a system where the "Night" is a shared responsibility, not a royal curse
>
> If Applejack and her allies can bring world peace, the world's suffering will end, and she won't have to feel the kinds of nightmares and guilt with the occupation ever again


**Codex:**
* **[[Luna's Banishment]]** (History)
> **Note:** It happened in a blink of an eye to me, said Luna
> One moment she was losing control of her dream-altering spell, the next moment she was on the ground
