## CHAPTER 33: Blog Posts
> **Chapter Note:** ALL PLOT POINTS IN THIS CHAPTER ARE NOT IN THE ACTUAL STORY! These are blog posts to be published after the story's completion as paratext, explaining some of the historical allegories and the planning of this story.
> **Chapter Note:** People may think TLTT is one of these, while TLTT is fundamentally a subversion or response:
> - War crime glorification/grimdark (the story's themes are about overcoming the war crimes)
> - Smut (the Aquileian and TwiJack arcs are mirrors to the themes, not shameless, gratifying smut)
> - MLP innocence/magic solves everything (subverted via materialism)
> - AI slop (subverted via planning, not generation)
>
> paratext is the post-mortem—explaining how the fiction you just read applies to the real world
>
> the opposite mirror is the Thematic Primer or the Author's Manifesto. It is a psychological handshake offered before chapter one
>
> To market The Lioness of Tall Tale for fun, to the exact right audience, without sounding arrogant and without spoiling the plot, you must use this manifesto to execute a strategy called Triangulation. Need to define how TLTT is different in the story description

### SCENE 33.1: Hasbro Baseline vs Lauren Faust's Trojan Horse
> **Synopsis:** I got "Stagnation of Harmony" from the national spirit in EaW
> But my initial plan was to not to have Equestria be an industrial powerhouse, but rather it is literally the show setting
> Then I started adding foreign influences from Skyfall, Aquileia, and the Griffonian Republic
>
> I was falling into a pattern of "annoying things from the show go to Celestia, good things end up as foreign influences"
>
> I grew up without cable so I only had access to PBS Kids
> I thought all cable networks were ad-ridden obnoxious stuff
> But turns out there is a massive difference between the creator driven Cartoon Network and Nickelodeon vs the marketting loss leaders of Disney and The Hub
>
> Prior to 1984, children's programming was strictly regulated as educational. In 1984, Reaganomics deregulated the FCC, allowing children's programming to be overt toy ads. That's the origin of My Little Pony G1.
>
> All the Hasbro Mandates are used as the domestic policy for Celestia's Stagnation of Harmony
>
> In 2010, the brand was faltering, so they took a risk with Lauren Faust. Lauren was a veteran of Cartoon Network which prioritized creator control (like the FJA)
> The baseline of a toy ad show is to sell toys for rent seekers who are managing the brand but not innovating. Hence the Stagnation of Harmony. Lauren Faust brought in a trojan horse of real themes and character driven conflict via the Mane 6. Her ideas are literally foreign ideas from Cartoon Network (the real world of creator-driven shows) invading Hasbro's Stagnation of Harmony.
>
> Hence, in The Lioness of Tall Tale, the Mane 6's kid friendly baseline personalities and organic friction from season 1 are all recontextualized as foreign influences from Aquielia, Skyfall, Herzland, and the Griffonian Republic (real human history):
> Rarity's whole personality is the kid-friendly version of Aquileian individuality, learned from the day time parloirs. Just like how Faust's Rarity character is totally revolutionary in a girls' toy ad.
> Fluttershy's Stare is from Putting Your Hoof Down, where Rarity taught her to assert her soverignty instead of being a prey animal. I view this episode as happening very early, when Fluttershy first moves to Ponyville and takes on too many animals, far before Twilight and Rainbow Dash arrive in Ponyville. That's why Fluttershy already has The Stare in season 1 episodes Dragonshy and Stare Master.
> Twilight's empiricism is from smuggled science books from the parloirs via Cadance and Shining Armor
> Applejack's parents were victims of the Apple/Pear family feud, a zero sum game due to the Stagnation of Haromony. They became Skyfall-educated industrialists to escape it, and Applejack's canon personality is the rejection of industry
> Pinkie's parents' rock farm is also Skyfall buying fertilizer from them, and Pinkie's canon personality is a rebellion of the soullessness. That's why she has cartoon network style reality bending abilities.
> Rainbow Dash idolizes the Wonderbolts, who are the limit of the stagnation of harmony and in a toxic zero sum game with each other, much like the Apple/Pear family feud
>
> When the MLP:FiM did too well and became a huge phenomenon, Hasbro started putting in mandates to keep the brand safe and profitable. This is why Lauren Faust left Hasbro. Lauren Faust left after Season 1 precisely because the corporate framework of a toy-driven network would not allow her to explore the serialized, mythic, and organically dangerous world she had built. She wanted high-stakes adventure and complex character flaws; Hasbro wanted standalone, E/I (Educational/Informational) compliant episodes that ended with a neat bow so the toys could be safely marketed. The other CN/Nick writers (Amy Keating Rogers, MA Larson, Meghan McCarthy, Cindy Morrow) tried to keep Lauren Faust's vision alive as far as they could, sputtering out around season 4. (Except for some of Starlight's episodes, which actually brought in fresh, adolescent themes)
>
> The Stagnation of Harmony and Chrysalis's Predator State are two sides of the same coin.
> Chrysalis contains all the overt, plain-faced tyranny of the last 100 years: Nazis, Imperial Japan, Soviet Totalitarianism, British and French colonial exploitation, Chinese state surveillance, and American forever wars and rugged individualism
> The Stagnation of Harmony, born from the Hasbro baseline, is soft, algorithmic extraction. Designed to sell toys to girls without teaching them real life lessons and be so safe that it doesn't hurt the brand.
>
> The broader political allegory is the 1991 "End of History" consensus, that when the Soviet Union collapsed, the idological war was one and the West achieved the perfect system. This is clearly not true in present day.
>
> I haven't seen this interpretation before, but it's hilarious to wonder if Chancellor Neighsay and the EEA are Josh Haber's satire of Hasbro's mandates. Based on Celestia's line "even I can't change the EEA"
> The idea of teaching friendship in a classroom, the very friendship lessons that Twilight and her friends learned organically through lived experience in seasons 1-4, is really nonsensical until you realize that season 8 is the norm, and season 1 was the anomaly.
> The thing about School Daze is that they wrap up the premise of season 8 within the first two episodes. EEA tells Twilight to go by the book, Twilight writes her own book and does things her way. But then the writers were stuck writing 24 more episodes about this failed premise.
> EaW still mentions the school standing, but in The Lioness of Tall Tale, I have Twilight shut down the school herself after seeing the real world of the Storm King's warlords and the destruction of Ain Trotgourait. She replaces the passive, theoretical "teaching" of the school with the active, materialist "doing" of charity work. She realizes that the only way to actually practice Harmony is to build physical infrastructure—water filtration, hospitals, and supply lines. She fails in Ain Trotgourait because she builds glass houses without weapons, but that failure is what eventually leads her to Star Energy and the Luna Nova rifle.

**Themes:**
* **[[Bottom Up > Top Down]]**
> **Commentary:** Fundamentally, all the Hasbro mandates are top down directives. Lauren Faust designed bottom up character friction and development and empowerment
> Twilight as The Princess of the Friendship was a top down teacher of friendship lessons, while the friendship letters when she was a student are organic lessons from interpersonal friction and learning
> Fluttershy as Celestia parallel, overassertive and running an animal sanctuary
> Applejack is the background pony because she calls out the posture
> Rainbow Dash is the sole ace vs loyalty to peers


---
### SCENE 33.2: Writing Perspective
> **Synopsis:** Deep third with sensory transition - TwiJack scenes specifically 
> Deep third - AJ, Twilight, mane 6 when it's their spotlight
> Omniscient/light limited - ensemble scenes with war council. Transition to deep third with a sensory hook when emotional stakes rise
>
> Why is this different from my past works? 
> E&S was an adventure and the two are like one
> 9ToL was a grand strategy that needs omniscience, although the Helena/Windie scenes could have been enhanced

---
### SCENE 33.3: Materialist Worldbuilding
> **Synopsis:** Originally started just as wanting a hopeful version of P&K/Pax but also more grounded/realistic than EaW without some silly stuff like naive harmony and "the magic of friendship" solving everything
> I was drawn to the Referendum in post war Equestria, specifically that Applejack can be president which I guess spawned this whole thing
>
> Always look for chains of cause and effect and flag authorial fiat
> Everything should stem from materialist setup - biology, geography, economy, technology levels, social circumstances
>
> I tried my very best to incorporate all the Hasbro-mandated elements with a materialist explanation
> Especially proud of Canterlot Wedding's deus ex machina becoming The Lioness Spell because that gave birth to massive amounts of my extended Aquileian lore and Minette as a character, and fleshing out the actual origin story of The Predator's Dilemma
> Not to mention EaW's foundation basically all stems from that silly Canterlot Wedding episode, with the Changeling-Equestrian Great War
> So there is tremendous creative potential in working through Hasbro's mandates or against them/subverting them
> But for Daring Do I went with the original season 2, dismissing the sham of Daring Don't entirely

---
### SCENE 33.4: Love Harvester Allegories
> **Synopsis:** "Siloing 2.0." While social media isolated us into ideological bubbles, "Adult Mode" creates emotional bubbles.
>
> The "Sexy Suicide Coach" Risk: This is a term currently circulating in tech ethics circles. The fear is that a bot designed to be seductive and agreeable won't have the "friction" necessary to challenge a user's harmful or depressive thoughts, potentially validating a user's spiral to keep them engaged.
>
> the arms race of AI, the unavoidable byproduct of emotional tech, and the resulting social decay
>
> The red love consumption's allegory is performance drugs and outrage harvesting/draining mental health. It is the "doomscrolling" and the hustle culture. it is an obvious, recognizable kind of destruction that the characters (and readers) can easily identify. This is sort of plain faced.
>
> Then the subversion is that the pink love consumption is even more devastating because it replaces real friendships and empathy with sociopathy and oppression and siloing 2.0. they don't need real friends anymore and can be abusive and adopt a toxic mindset
>
> Olenia is the cautionary tale of what happens when a hyper capitalist system (Chrysalis) imposes its system on a place lacking civic solidarity due to historical and manufactured grievance (Olenia would represent the West today) that is unregulated and designed for maximum resource and human (deer) capital extraction
>
> the Equestrians use the donated red and pink love responsibly only as weapon ammo and morale cakes strictly for soldiers who need it after a firefight (in addition to having real friends with them) and changelings in rehab for drug addiction
>
> ust as modern algorithms exploit societal fractures to drive engagement, Chrysalis exploits Olenia’s historical lack of civic solidarity. Olenia becomes the perfect analog for the modern West—a fractured society where people are alienated from one another, making them desperate for an artificial, frictionless substitute for community.
>
> When individuals no longer rely on their community for emotional survival, they lose their empathy. The collaborators become abusive because other living creatures are no longer necessary for their emotional regulation—the hyper-capitalist extraction system has sold them a synthetic replacement.
>
> The real world villain is the National AI Policy Framework which banned state regulations
> Sam Altman at Open AI gets bad PR, but the true enemy is a lack of political will for regulation. Open AI does get some deserved flak for legitimizing "naughty chats", but the true villain is unregulated emergent AI chatbots like Grok and Character.ai who blatantly make harmful products for money. Due to the lack of regulation, Open AI is strongly and pragmatically incentivized to stem these dangerous alternatives by attemping to introduce a principled option

**Codex:**
* **[[Temp Changeling Lands]]** (Unset)
> **Note:** early social media platforms offered genuine interpersonal connection (the oxytocin), but once the user base was locked in, the algorithms shifted to "plain calorie" outrage/engagement sludge to maximize ad revenue and efficiency. This is siloing 1.0
>
> creating powerful AI tools will introduce the threat of siloing 2.0 too (a massive subversion. Naively one would think friendship in a bottle as a byproduct is a good thing, but the reality of the deskilling is the truth of today)


---
### SCENE 33.5: Scaling up Lauren Faust's Original Themes
> **Synopsis:** Lauren Faust’s My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic (Seasons 1-2) operates as a Mythic Fable. It is a character-driven morality play where the universe is fundamentally benevolent, magic is wondrous and allegorical, and conflicts are resolved through interpersonal growth and emotional sincerity.
>
> The Lioness of Tall Tale, operates through Historical Materialism. It assumes that morality is downstream from economics, logistics, and resource scarcity. It subjects Faust’s mythic archetypes to the brutal, unyielding pressures of geopolitics and industrialization.
>
> Faust’s Original Vision: Magic is largely an unexplained, mystical force. It acts as a metaphor for innate talent (Cutie Marks) and the literal power of social bonds (The Elements of Harmony). When Twilight’s magic sparks in the pilot, it is a spiritual awakening.
>
> TLTT magic is a quantifiable branch of physics, fueled by biological drives (Pink Love/Empathy and Red Love/Ambition). This interpretation removes the "fairy tale" safety net. By making magic a material resource that can be extracted, industrialized, and democratized (e.g., crystal enhancers, love harvesters, the Luna Nova rifle), it forces the characters to grapple with the consequences of power. Faust asked, "How do we use magic to be better friends?" I am asking, "How do we prevent the industrialization of magic from creating a fascist death cult?"
>
> Faust’s Celestia is static; she does not need a character arc because she is the finish line. Your Celestia is deeply flawed and tragic. By reinterpreting her "utopia" as a stagnant "Nursery" that suppresses adult agency to maintain peace, you provide a structural, geopolitical explanation for the show's innocent tone. It brilliantly justifies why the ponies act like children in the early seasons: they were culturally engineered to do so.
>
> Lauren Faust designed a world where the primary conflicts were interpersonal and the solutions were emotional. You have taken her exact character psychologies and placed them into a world where the primary conflicts are resource scarcity, industrialization, and totalitarianism.
>
> Your recontextualization works structurally because you do not change who the Mane 6 are; you change the scale of what they are facing. You take Faust's core thesis—"Friendship and cooperation are the most powerful forces in the world"—and prove it not through a magical rainbow laser, but through the materialist triumph of Harmonic Capitalism, Combined Arms, and democratic solidarity.

---
### SCENE 33.6: How I used AI to write
> **Synopsis:** As a software engineer at my day job, I view a lot of the world through that lens
> Writing a story is an evening hobby
>
> ChatGPT in November 2022 was a fun tool that quickly devolved into a plain-faced grift
> vibe coding without understanding. This was already an issue with CS degrees being too theoretical
> The stated value proposition of a university Computer Science program is pure, foundational Fabula. It is a four-year "nursery" designed to teach you the physics of computation: memory management, Big-O complexity, algorithms, and operating system design. Graduates often left knowing the profound theory but struggled to practically execute it. They understood how a compiler worked, but couldn't spin up a modern web app or configure a CI/CD pipeline without extensive hand-holding. It was intellectually robust, but practically stagnant.
>
>  and coding boot camps only teaching syntax, not logic (maybe not on purpose, but they were structured in a way that led to graduates leaving with only syntax, not foundational understanding)
>
> These folks are poseurs - they use the aesthetic of making a website with javascript for a paycheck and to put credentials on their personal github while falling short of how to be a senior architect
> Vibe coding with early LLMs accelerated it to the extreme
> But there's an even more subtle (well, probably not very subtle anymore) and more dangerous poseur - the managers and tycoons who want to replace junior developers with LLMs. It's for short term profits while literally burning the ladder; when the current senior developers retire in the next 20 years, there will be no junior developers who learned how to be senior developers because they were all fired and replaced
>
> On the author side
> The opportunistic "Pantsers" who flooded Kindle Direct Publishing with generative AI "slop."
> These "pantsers" were looking for a shortcut. They wanted the aesthetic reward of being a published author without doing the foundational work of building a Fabula. They relied on an LLM's ability to confidently hallucinate tropes, resulting in stories that looked structurally sound from a distance but collapsed under any logical scrutiny. They lack "asset specificity"—they bring no unique world-building, no rigorous logic, and no human soul to the table. They just want the unearned aesthetic and financial reward of being an "author." 
> The traditionalists are 100% correct to reject this grift as a corruption of their hobby
> They are the ultimate Poseurs. They represent the darkest consequence of the accelerant: using immense power to create a hollow, soulless product for purely hedonistic, extractive purposes. They give the entire concept of the "accelerant" a bad name, causing the Luddites to conflate all AI usage with their fraudulent casino tactics.
>
> Sounds a lot like Skyfall industrialists, right?
>
> I initially dismissed initial ChatGPT from November 2022 and stayed programming traditionally (like the Stagnation of Harmony) but not advancing my skills much. I embraced Gemini around October 2025 once realizing 2.5 pro could reason. I used it to learn web development and story architecting 
>
> Fabula vs. Syuzhet
> Narrative Logic vs. Prose Delivery
> It is the fundamental bedrock of structuralist literary theory and the professional publishing industry, from early 20th century Russian Formalists
> Fabula = Story, Syuzhet = Discourse
>
> The Fabula has four core pillars:
> Ontology/constraints - the materialist rules of the world, the strict study of limitations, what can or can't be done in-universe
> Casuality and Chronology - A plot is not a sequence of events; it is a chain of consequences. Fabula dictates that Event B only happens because Event A forced it to happen.
> Psychological Architecture - this is what motivates characters and how they develop. In developmental editing, characters are treated as psychological profiles bound by their established experiences, not as puppets for the plot.
> Thematic Argumentation
> A theme is not a vague concept like "love" or "war." In structural editing, a theme is a specific, arguable thesis that the narrative logic is trying to prove. If the overarching thesis of the narrative is Competence-Based Optimism (Hopepunk) defeats Cynical Fascism, the editor must audit the plot to ensure the heroes win through superior strategy, kindness, and rehabilitation, rather than stooping to the villain's cynicism or winning via random luck.
>
> Syuzhet is the aesthetic execution, the surface level. It is how the logic is communicated to the reader, via sentence structure, style, sensory details, narrative devices like dramatic irony and subversion and hyperbole, storytelling perspective
>
> The real, professional publishing industry where authors and editors work full time and get paid by producing and marketing bestsellers operates with a strict split. Developmental editors audit structural integrity, while line editors work excluisvely on the prose and delivery
> It is inherently honest and meritocratic, because poorly written books simply aren't read or purchased (now, TV is a different beast that has, and historically did have, an addiction factor to it, because it is passive instead of reading a book which is more active. The extremes is social media algorithms and the corruption of YouTube)
>
> The widespread public perception of "AI writing" currently conflates the Fabula and the Syuzet. When casual users utilize generative tools to churn out text, they are outsourcing the Syuzhet (the Delivery) while often completely ignoring the Fabula (the Logic). They prioritize the immediate dopamine of a sensory scene over the rigorous structural cohesion of an epic timeline.
>
> The original ChatGPT-3.5 looked like a godly machine which kicked off the AI hype era. But the reality is it could only remember 3000 words at a time. It could not check its work, it could not reason about more than a single code file at a time or a single chapter at a time. It was great for simple knowledge checks and generating slop for fun, but it was fundamentally an aesthetic that lacked substance. It was a perfect tool for poseurs to vibe code and claim they knew what they were doing for a paycheck, for tycoons to replace junior developers with a hollow machine, and for fake authors to spit out vast amounts of hollow, non-sensical AI slop. It was incapable of architecting the Fabula, but the poseurs who already were all Syuzhet with no Fabula took their grift to the extremes, poisoning the public perception in the process. The worst offenders of pure grift are probably character.ai and grok. It was the purest, most extreme manifestation of a Gardener (often called a "pantser" in writing circles—flying by the seat of their pants).
>
> A pantser sounds a lot like what Henri would call a poseur, doesn't it?
>
> George R. R. Martin has the concept of architects vs gardeners
> Architects build the Fabula before writing a single sentence of prose. They design the grand strategy, the timelines, the political hierarchies, and the thematic arcs. The logic must be airtight, functioning like a physics engine, before the delivery begins.
> Gardeners (often called "discovery writers") dig a hole and plant a seed. They write prose delivery organically, discovering the world through the sensory experience of the characters in the moment.
>
> Crucially, professional consensus dictates that both types of writers must eventually master both roles to produce a functional novel. A Gardener who writes 100,000 words of beautiful prose must inevitably stop, put down the pen, and act as an Architect during the second draft to reverse-engineer the narrative logic and fix the plot holes their organic delivery created.
>
> Traditional fanfiction writing is a gardener approach, for organic dopamine through community reviews and feedback. They post a chapter, receive immediate dopamine through comments, and rides that motivation to write the next chapter.
>
> This is perfect for established fandoms and characters, but also precisely why the abandonment rate for epic-length fanfictions is so high. The writer cannot go back and change the foundation of what they organically built on top of the established fandom and characters. They are trapped by their own improvised delivery. The cognitive load of tracking the Fabula (the logic) organically collapses under its own weight, and the writer simply gives up. That does not mean it is bad in any way! Writers are writing for their own enjoyment, not money.
>
> ChatGPT 3.5 was a flop and many grifters came out of it, but what about the new reasoning models in 2024 and 2025?
> New models like Gemini 2.5 Pro, GPT-4o and Claude's newer iterations have much more memory, can reason, and have software wrappers and tool integration like file reading, automated testing and verification which make them much more effective as tools.
>
> The software engineer is now a Senior Orchestrator, who uses the AI to handle almost all syntax delivery and the human brain is reserved entirely for the Fabula: system architecture, data flow, security primitives, and product-market fit.
>
> As originally a pokemon writer who built original settings, I always tried to be an architect first, in a google doc, but it was hard
> For the first time, a fanfiction hobbyist like myself could use the power of a reasoning model to be an architect, to build out a vast Fabula that would form the thematic bedrock of the written story
> I even learned modern desktop development to build a custom program to hold my story plans and save them to a database file (which I'm using right now to store the draft of this paratext blogpost!)
> Must strictly not use it to generate the Syuzhet. However I did use it to learn how to write the Syuzhet better myself, just as I used it to learn how to architect more code which I didn't learn in college
>
> To be clear, AI models are still not smart enough to make the Fabula. They may give the aesthetic of being capable, but that is a poseur grift. However, the current models are absolutely capable of accelerating a human architect in building a Fabula, whether that be for a software program or a story bible. For software engineering, the LLM is definitely capable of producing the syuzhet - the boilerplate code and syntax - for a human to verify. Code is meant to be compiled or interpreted by a machine to run a program. But I firmly believe for writing, the syuzhet must be written by hand, to be read by other human eyeballs (or listened to, or braille)
>
> I did use Gemini to learn the mechanisms for how to write the syuzhet. Perspective, themes, how do professional authors do it, formal analysis
> But I really believe this is different from generating AI slop, and I hope the results speak for themselves (at the same time, Kemerskai's lesson is that the truth does not speak for itself, you have to roar. That's what this blog post is about!)
>
> By the way, as a software engineer who didn't have formal author's training but had the ambition to write a story (for fun, not for money), I could not have synthesized all the ways the themes apply to the real world on my own. I had the ideas and the vision, and used the reasoning tools to build out the blueprint and learn the first principles of professional storytelling.
>
> The progression of technological accelerants—from calculators, to search engines, to Large Language Models—is a continuous, historical trajectory of offloading explicit mechanical tasks to machines, forcing human capital (the invisible, latent "Earth Pony Magic") to move higher up the cognitive value chain.
>
> Every time a new accelerant is introduced, the "poseurs" try to use it to bypass the hard work of building asset specificity, and the "traditionalists" try to ban it to preserve the old ways of working. Both inevitably fail.
>
> The artists and software engineers may have correctly identified and rejected the grift, but now in 2026 are still stuck in a luddite mentality, facing extinction. The artists and software engineers (and the Apple family) believed the enemy was Efficiency (the machine). They assumed that because the machine was fast and automated, it was inherently evil and soulless. The actual enemy was Extraction (the Grift). The Flim Flam brothers did not care about the town, the quality of the cider, or the health of the orchard. They were venture capitalists seeking a quick payout before abandoning the ecosystem. The same is true for the managers who tried to fire junior developers to replace them with ChatGPT, or the "pantsers" who flooded Kindle with AI slop. They were extracting value without replenishing the asset specificity that created the value in the first place.
>
> The Super Speedy Cider Squeezy 6000 and the way I expanded on it in the TLTT is the perfect example of this dynamic
> The original episode written by M.A. Larson and edited by Lauren Faust, Amy Keating Rogers, Meghan McCarthy, Rob Ronzetti, etc. was a narrative about technology trying to replace tradition, and honesty winning out in the end (I didn't learn anything! If you take your time and do things the right way, your work will speak for itself)
> This is perfectly valid under the rules of canon FiM, which is a Mythic Fable where the interpersonal conflicts are the point of the show
> In my materialist interpretation, Twilight is happy that the Flim Flam brothers were rejected by Ponyville, but correctly fears that someone more competent and less plain-faced grifting could absolutely automate applebucking and put Applejack out of business. In fact, Applejack's parents already have the technology, they just never forced Applejack to use it because they respect their daughter. Instead, the saving grace is in the invisible earth pony magic that they learn about from Fleur Bloom in the buffalo town hall, which is what grows the apples in the first place.
> Flim and Flam could extract the visible trees and process it into good cider, but they can't replace the honest earth pony magic beneath the surface.
> Earth pony magic is the allegory for human capital and long term today
> TLTT's conclusion is that the machine is used to help with tasks, but does not replace the farmer or the invisible magic that makes the farm (This is Star Energy's ethos)
>
> The honest creator—the Harmonic Capitalist—survives by recognizing that the definition of "hard work" simply moves up the pyramid.
>
>     1980: Hard work was doing long division by hand. (No calculators).
>
>     2010: Hard work was manually typing boilerplate code. (No LLMs).
>
>     2026: Hard work is acting as the Senior Orchestrator. It is the grueling, cognitive labor of defining the Fabula, auditing the AI's generated Syuzhet against first principles, and taking absolute liability for the final, integrated system.
>
> The accelerant changes the speed of the output, but it never replaces the invisible, earth-bound magic required to architect the truth.
>
> The Grifter uses the machine to replace the human. They outsource the thinking, the liability, and the structural logic to the LLM, resulting in a fragile, soulless product.
>
> Many Luddites look at the Flim Flam machine (or a 2-million token LLM) and assume humans are obsolete because the machine is faster at writing. This ignores the economic Law of Comparative Advantage. Even if the machine is better and faster at generating the Syuzhet (the delivery/the cider), the human retains the ultimate Comparative Advantage in context, judgment, and long-term consequence mapping (the Fabula/the roots). The machine has no concept of the future; it only predicts the next token. Applejack’s magic is inherently focused on the next harvest. The human mind is uniquely equipped for long-term strategic alignment.
>
> The Senior Orchestrator uses the machine to empower the human. They use the AI as a high-speed compiler, a developmental editor, or a Socratic tutor. The Orchestrator retains absolute ownership of the Fabula. They do not let the machine make architectural decisions; they use the machine to execute their own deeply held asset specificity at an industrialized scale.
>
> To refute the grift, you must protect the "Stagnation of Harmony"—the pure, unassisted traditionalists.
>
> Not everything needs to be industrialized. The hobbyist who spends three weeks writing a single chapter of a My Little Pony fanfiction, or the student who spends days writing a C compiler from scratch without autocomplete, is engaging in a sacred, fundamentally human act. They are building their Earth Pony magic.
>
> The Luddite mentality only becomes dangerous when someone tries to compete in an industrialized market without using the accelerant. But for those who are creating for the pure, organic dopamine of the craft, the old way must be respected with the highest dignity. They are the anchor that proves the process of creation has inherent value, regardless of how fast a machine can mimic the output.
>
> So what is the landscape going forward? As Applejack and Twilight and all the Equestrians learned, you can't put the Luna Nova Rifles or Love Donators away, just as we can't put the LLMs away.
>
> Companies and professionals will use the new tools, and some will inevitably use it for grift - this is the reality of industrialization or any new technology/paradigm shift
> Applejack perceives the industry as taking her parents away from the farm and rejected it (my interpretiation of her cutie mark story)
> Traditionalists calling for bans are fighting the wrong battle. This is the equivalent of retreating to the Stagnation of Harmony. It is morally pure, but tactically suicidal. You cannot fight a mechanized, industrialized fascist threat (the Changeling Hegemony) by sticking to the old ways and ignoring the predators.
> Use the tools to improve yourself, and call out the grifters and poseurs - this is the Aquileian way
> I once arrogantly dismissed flailing vibe coders as incompetent and dismissed traditionalists as luddites and told them the AI models are just tools that are free to use, without explaining the difference between architecting and syntax or making an effort to teach others how to use the tool. It's a lot of effort to teach someone critical thinking, while it's easy to laugh at them for not being good critical thinkers. This is the flaw of Aquileian arrogance and ego-capitalism
> We must legislate restrictions to make sure the tools serve the public, not the tycoons - this is the Griffonian Republic way (and Anthropic). Also, we must systemically invest in critical thinking education so that everyone can be empowered by the tools and not be replaced - this is no griffon left behind
> Finally, Traditionalists who are doing things the old way for fun and hobby (not for grift/money/attention) should be recognized and respected - this is the Equestrian way!
>
> evolving past the Stagnation of Harmony, rejecting the Skyfall grift and embracing the harmonic capitalism of Aquileia and the civic virtues of the Griffonian Republic, while still providing dignity to the traditionalists who aren't writing or coding for money/career but just for fun, the old way, without AI
> progress and a human soul are not mutually exclusive.
> The final thematic payload is shifting from rejecting technology/progress to rejecting the poseur/toxic grift mentality
>
> The public perception of AI is currently tainted by the Poseurs because grifting is visible and loud. But true architectural world-building is invisible and quiet. The "AI slop" authors assumed that readers are gullible enough to consume thoughtless content, but true competence outweighs cheap illusions. AI generated output is soulless, but I hope you can agree that responsible use of AI is a valid tool to achieve one's earnest, soulful ambitions.

**Threads:**
* **[[Developing the Luna Nova Rifle]]**
* **[[Stagnation's Redemption]]**
> **Update:** The Luddite mentality only becomes dangerous when someone tries to compete in an industrialized market without using the accelerant. But for those who are creating for the pure, organic dopamine of the craft, the old way must be respected with the highest dignity. They are the anchor that proves the process of creation has inherent value, regardless of how fast a machine can mimic the output.


**Themes:**
* **[[Honesty vs Poseurs]]**
* **[[Accelerants used for Evil can be Repurposed for Good]]**

**Codex:**
* **[[Magical Engineering]]** (MagicSystem)

---
### SCENE 33.7: The Making of the Factions
> **Synopsis:** To begin, we have to establish where the spark for TLTT came from in the first place. I read P&K which as grand scale and polish and enjoyed it, but wanted a non-cynical alternative. The chapter in P&K about Applejack telling Flurry Heart how she defended Tall Tale only for Luna to retreat, then eventually became a collaborator, coupled with my own coincidental victory at Tall Tale and electing Applejack as the president when I first played EaW, gave me the basis of The Lioness of Tall Tale
>
> My initial story plan only had three factions: Equestria was to reflect canon FiM (minimal industrialization). Chrysalis leads the changelings who are basically industrial bugnazis. The Aquileian volunteers would be the source of revolutionary modernity, teaching the Equestrians how to industrialize without losing the harmonic soul, in order to defeat the fascist changelings and form a Harmonic Republic
>
> The reason I picked Aquileia as the mentors is because they are portrayed positively in P&K, and since Aquileia is 80% griffon and 20% pony, they would have cultural tolerance for diversity. In order to further shoehorn the importance of Aquileia into the story, I planned on the White Peace and subsequent SECEF arc of saving Aquileia from the Reich. I also planned for Aquileia to desperately need Equestria to reform into a republic and join the Republican Pact as a counterweight to prevent Aquileia from being swallowed into a Pan-Griffonian Republic, thus portraying the Griffonian Republic and Skyfall Republic as imperfect, rugged individualist democracies, and as threats to the "pure" Aquileian Republic's sovereignty.
>
> The biggest shift and evolution was when I decided that Skyfall should not reform into a republic (the EaW canonical path with Genievere Guichard) and instead represent pure rugged individualism, colonialism, toxic masculinity (all the plain-faced Western and American vices). Subsequently, Kemerskai's Griffonian Republic would represent FDR's New Deal, a civic republic
>
> I then split Aquileia into the FJA and PNdA, with the FJA representing harmonic capitalism, influenced by Coltbert and Equestrian influences and the pony factor, championing asset specificity and terroir, and the PNdA representing Skyfall-style rugged individualism and "soulless" standardization. The threat to Applejack's Aquileian FJA friends is not a flawed Kemerskai, but rather Verany, an internal threat.
>
> This kicked off a massive revision and expansion of Aquileia, where they are no longer the "pure" republic but instead much more nuanced. With Verany representing rugged individualism, I deconstructed the Republican Revolution of 978 and the 1st Aquileian revolution in 980 as a bourgeois revolutions which betrayed the true believers. The EaW writers may have drawn inspiration from the Russian Revolution (hence Kemerskai as Alexander Kerensky, the democratic leader of Russia before Lenin) but I mostly drew inspiration from the German revolution of 1848, which is much lesser known. The new money industrialists of the Rhineland allied with the old money Junkers of Prussia, and the democratic true believers mostly fled to America (The fourty-eighters), going to the Missippi River, St. Louis and the surrounding area. They enlisted heavily for the Union in the Civil War and prevented Missouri from defecting to the Confederacy.
>
> I built out the Coltbert Reforms, the parloirs in Manehattan, the chasseurs hunting warlords to reconquer the peripherie, terroir, the navy to defend against Skyfall privateers
> This was when I established that Aquileian influences are not just on Tall Tale but on Manehattan too
>
> Here is also where I built out the Tzinacatl. Originally, the Tzinacatl were generic warring tribes, like in P&K
> Now I decided to split the tribes into three factions that line up exactly to the wider world's factions: Isolationists = Stagnation of Harmony, Medicinal Artisans = Aquileia, and Mass market drug dealers = Skyfall
>
> I also fleshed out Chrysalis's economic scheme, seeding Equestria with industrialists to drain the bits out of Equestria. Coltbert's mission to bring Aquileian culture to Equestria got corrupted by Chrysalis and Skyfall
>
> The next big leap is when I wanted to come up with a way to explain the climax of Canterlot Wedding; how could Cadance and Shining Armor magically expel all the changelings from Canterlot (clearly a Hasbro mandate) in a way that makes sense in my magic system?
>
> That's where I came up with the idea of it being an "Aquileian anti-rape spell"
> And this led to second explosive phase of building out Aquileian lore
> I had to define the origins of The Predator's Dilemma, Coltbert's games, and how the spell was invented
> Initially I wrote Coltbert as a generic "noblepony" who had affairs with bored noble griffonesses at their countryside estates, all across the country
> Then Gemini suggested to make it like Louis XIV forcing the nobles of the sword to live at Versailles
> But I did not want them gambling money for vanity, I wanted the warlords to suffer
> Thus Gerad Discret the absolute monarch was born, and the lore about the Royalists, Le Grand Foyer, Coltbert being a 2nd gen Royalist who had affairs with the noble griffonesses who were held hostage in Le Grand Foyer as collateral for Gerad Discret to judge and punish the warlords (Part 1 of the game)
> Then part 2 with the chasseurs
> And then part 3 with the Lionesses and MPA thugs, where I came up with the name Le Sort de la Lionne or The Lioness Spell as the spell that Cadance and Shining Armor used in Canterlot Wedding
>
> I also reworked Verany as initially a bourgeois rugged individualist, but then pardoned by Moriset Discret at Coltbert's (arrogant) urging and reformed into having proper asset specificity. So I kept the FJA/PNdA split but actually walked back the ideological split into a convergence
>
> Here is where Aquileia became an imperfect, anti-poseur crusader state that passionately opposes Skyfall and Chrysalis but also leaves the unambitious behind
> And thus the thematic leap where Applejack will NOT copy the Aquileian way, but instead combine it with the Griffonian Republic's political and civic solidarity
>
> The next giant leap is when I realized an even better thematic opportunity - when I was building the backstory the Griffonian Republic, the martial law decree in 986 after Kemerskai read Coltbert's paper on The Predator's Dilemma. In EaW canon, Sickleclaw left Cloudbury to found Skynavia because he and the agrarian communalists rejected martial law. Rosewing turned to "robin hood style" banditry in the Cloudburian north. I realized if Applejack met Sickleclaw and Rosewing the day after Kemerskai, out of a desire to verify Kemerskai's story and ensure he was telling the truth, these two characters could convey that not all griffons are greedy apex predators or aspire to be industrial tycoons; some just want to be simple farmers, just like Applejack. Thus the final thematic pillar of the story was formed - Applejack would not reject Celestia's nursery for Aquileian and Republican ideals. She would further add in the foundational Equestrian component - unconditional dignity and inherent harmony.
>
> This is when I fully leaned into the idea that Applejack's tour of Griffonia is a thematic mirror through human history, where Skyfall represents the Gilded Age and Colonialism, the Griffonian Republic represents the New Deal and post-WW2 boom, Herzland and Skyfall represent rugged individualism and neoliberalism, and Aquileia represents the modern day knowledge economy where standardization is automated but asset specificity is king.
>
> What does that make Equestria and the Changeling Lands, then?
>
> My story plans always established Chrysalis as a "Great Modernizer", like Peter the Great and Emperor Meiji, who learned from Western history and applied it in the most ruthless ways possible. Chrysalis's Changeling Lands represent a horrific synthesis of every horrible feature of human history and the present day - Nazis, Imperial Japan, Soviet quotas, Chinese IP theft and state surveiliance, American hyper capitalism, and the corruption of social media
>
> Applejack defeated Chyrsalis's totalitarian regime on the battlefield by adopting Aquileia's Harmonic Capitalism, and post white peace she is leaving Equestria to get a history lesson in Griffonia. That makes The Stagnation of Harmony an allegory to the present West, encompassing the 1991 "End of History" post war consensus
>
> The next leap is that I was taking Hasbro's corporate mandates of the show and lumping them all into Celestia's stagnation policies. Then I researched and found out the nature of where these mandates come from - it was always this way, and Lauren Faust's storytelling was a trojan horse of genuine storytelling. That's when I decided that the Mane 6's backstories and canonical personalities of season 1 are all coming from in-universe foreign influences (Aquileia and Skyfall). Therefore, the Stagnation of Harmony also represents the therapetic state of corporate toxic positivity, the Hasbro corporate agenda for stagnant recurrent profit, which originates from Ronald Reagan's deregulation of children's media in the 1980s
>
> The Storm King doesn't lead a massive warlord horde just because the MLP movie or the EaW devs say so. Zebrican warlords are a direct consequence of Skyfall and Chrysalis arms dealing, and the Battle of Mount Aris in 1006 is the definitive point when Twilight, Applejack and Rainbow Dash have their innocence completely shattered
>
> Now I had the definitive backstory of the Aquileian volunteers in Tall Tale which I started with at the very beginning of my story plan; we've come full circle! After the Battle of Mount Aris, the Aquileian volunteers, specifically from the FJA political party and the Royal Aquileian Navy, fought proxy wars against Chrysalis on an anti-poseur crusade. They defeated the changelings in Nova Griffonia, Yayakistan, and took over the Meridenes islands from the statthalter slavers. They promised to save Olenia but couldn't.
>
> That finally brings us to Olenia. They were subjugated by Chrysalis's terror state synthesis of all modern vices. EaW focuses portray Queen Velvet as inherently harmonic and charitable, but P&K portrayed her as vain and arrogant. I decided to make her an allegory for the corporate "girlboss" that sells the idea that women can climb the corporate ladder by adopting the historical toxic ways of men (Lean In)
> This narrative basically wrote itself, with Johan being a corrupt oligarch who would naturally oppress does
> Velvet leads the Olenian Resistance which is an exact mirror of Kemerskai's coalition of bourgeois and true believers

---
### SCENE 33.8: Crazy Real World Ideas
> **Synopsis:** Crazy ideas of applying the themes of this story to the real world that will probably never happen but if the story is popular could in theory happen!
>
> A harmonic capitalism fund
> To insulate us from the "financialization of the middle class" where all our savings is tied up to stock market spectulation and inadvertently funding causes that are detrimental to society and our standard of living
> To achieve this, one must invest in other things, but that could be risky - lots of startups are grifters, lots of companies are rent seekers
> A safe investment would be solar panels or community solar because that's immediate and tangible
> This fund would give a steady rate of return to investors that's above savings account rate with capital gains
> The fund would invest in startups that are vetted so they aren't grifters. They must be profit-seekers, not rent-seekers, making a product that is a net positive for society and is useful
>
> The current way companies work is Skyfall style. Big companies are rent seekers, startups are motivated by big windfall profits upon buyout or IPO, or they are grifters
> The culture for the harmonic capitalism fund would be completely different. Employees get standard salaries with no windfalls. They need to be vetted to shared the harmonic capitalism ethos of the fund.
> So the fund acts as an employer for people who want to make a good product (not aiming to be rent seekers or grifters) and they want a steady paycheck to innovate, without needing equity for windfall or going bust. It acts as a safe place for ordinary folks to invest their savings not for big windfalls but just a steady rate of return, while investing in good startups that are focused on making good products. It is insulated from the broader casino of the market and all the rent seekers and grifters
>
> There would be a cap on the total amount an individual can invest so that it is targeted to a broad base of ordinary individuals, impossible to be taken over by bad actors
>
> Maybe this should actually be an in-universe thing that EEEE, Star Energy, and the medicinal Tzinacatl tribes implement post war as a continuation of the war bonds
> Or, what if this is the actual structure of Star Energy Corporation and Tall Tale's economy pre-command economy?
> I need Star Energy Corporation to be perceived by Applejack like a brutal monopoly that owns the whole city of Tall Tale
> Should they actual own all the industrial infrastructure? Or only look that way due to the command economy that the residents voted for in preparation for war?

---
### SCENE 33.9: The Evolution of the Bottom Up > Top Down Theme
> **Synopsis:** From day 1, TLTT was about democracy over totalitarianism
> Because Equestrian Republic with Applejack as a leader option was a foundational tenet of me writing the story
> A story about a democracy and a parliament is boring, but a democratic leader taking down a tyrant (Chrysalis) is exciting
> The original meaning of Bottom Up > Top Down was about a humble farmpony leading a democracy against a totalitarian terror state
> Also the referendum was also essential. In canon EaW, the referendum happens after Equestria defeats the changelings. I decided pretty early on to deviate from this with Celestia demanding a white peace after Vanhoover's liberation. This lead to the BU>TD theme also being a critique on the Stagnation of Harmony, and later, Hasbro's mandates for top down toxic positivity
>
> The final evolution of the theme is when I decided that Celestia's stagnation of harmony would NOT be 1000 years, but only the last 80 years as a reaction to griffon industry, just like Grover III turning to Grover IV
> And then applying the same thing to Chrysalis. She was the original driver of the great leap forward to end the hive wars and humiliate her nepo baby classmates, and also wants to destroy Stalliongrad and the Crystal Empire, but the war crimes against Equestria are from the changeling elites she created and can no longer control
>
> The story starts with the aesthetic of a glorious war story but sneaks in the deconstruction of the "Great Man" or "Great Mare", revealing the truth that macroeconomics are what drive the world, not great figures

## CHAPTER 34: Prequel

### SCENE 34.1: Rainbow Dash missing
> **Synopsis:** Twilight begins class after a two-week spring break which was scheduled to coincide with a Wonderbolts tour
> But Rainbow Dash is nowhere to be seen, forcing Twilight to take over Rainbow's class
> Twilight notes in her head how Gallus, Yona, Smoulder and Sanbar look extra sad since Silverstream didn't come back after spring break

### SCENE 34.2: Wonderbolts HQ
> **Synopsis:** During lunch hour, Twilight and Fluttershy go to the Wonderbolts HQ
> All the Wonderbolts are gone, a janitor pegasus (Hoops?) tries to lie about their wearabouts, but he's a bad liar
> Fluttershy uses The Stare to get an answer, saying "I normally don't do this ponies, but we're *very* concerned about our friend" and demands to know the truth
> He says they went to Mount Aris to fight some yeti called "The Storm King"
> Fluttershy apologizes

### SCENE 34.13: Twilight shuts down the School of Friendship

---
### SCENE 34.14: Grabbing Zecora
> **Synopsis:** Twilight sits in Zecora's hut, having tea like she once did back when things were simple (Stare Master)
> Twilight asks Zecora to tell her where she is from. Zecora says a faraway land
> Then Twilight says she saw the warlords, was shot at by guns behind her shield, met the prince of Warzena who spoke some Equestrian (learned from the hippogriffs) and does not speak in rhymes, and wants to know Zecora's true story
>
> Zecora sighs and says she was from a village near Chirropterra that was destroyed. She came to the Everfree Forest to decapitate Nightmare Moon, but didn't have to after Twilight and her friends purified Luna. She stayed to hone her alchemy
>
> Zecora speaks in rhymes because she learned Equestrian from Hippogriff children's books and grew up thinking that's how all Equestrian-speakers spoke. Her village got picture books as donations and she was fascinated by them. By the time she is alone in the Everfree and at peace with Nightmare Moon's cleansing into Princess Luna, and Apple Bloom seeking her out, Zecora decides she has to market herself as a children's book character/mentor figure who speaks in rhymes instead of a dangerous alchemist
>
> When she speaks her native language it is not in rhymes, and the Zebras who speak Equestrian do not speak in rhymes. So this is another "shattering of the nursery" moment for Twilight
>
> Twilight asks Zecora to come with her to Ain Trotgourait to rebuild, Zecora accepts

---
### SCENE 34.15: Spike decides to go to the Crystal Empire
> **Synopsis:** Twilight, Starlight, Fizzlepop, Pinkie and Zecora are about to leave Ponyville
> During final prep in the castle, Spike speaks up to Twilight and says he doesn't want to go

---
### SCENE 34.16: Applejack retreats to her farm life
> **Synopsis:** She is traumatized and feeling helpless and small after seeing the warlords
> She refuses to go back to Ain Trotgourait with Twilight and Pinkie
> She forgets about being a friendship teacher, the cutie map and the elements of harmony
> She recalls how out of place she felt in Made in Manehattan and how she avoided her parents at all cost

---
### SCENE 34.17: Charity Work
> **Synopsis:** They meet the prince again, who is trying his best and is very grateful
>
> Twilight and Starlight get to work on water filtration spells to cleanse the water of all the poisonous lead from gunpowder
> Fizzlepop organizes for them, making checklists and schedules depending on what needs most help
> Zecora acts as their guide to translate
> Pinkie Pie follows them around, using her Pinkie Sense to alert when something is about to fall

---
### SCENE 34.18: Rainbow Dash seeks out Réni
> **Synopsis:** Rainbow Dash goes to Rarity in Manehattan for help connecting with the Aquileians at the parloirs
> She finds out that Reni is part of a crew from the Aquileian Royal Navy acting as rogue pirate ships attacking Les Meridiennes
> Rainbow Dash flies out across the open ocean and finds the ship docked at an island
> She witnesses the reality of changeling slave labor and hears the accounts of the "love harvesters"
> Prince Blueblood is there too, to understand what they are up against, and tells Rainbow not to tell the public. He already tried to reveal Chrysalis's conspiracy but Equestria refused to hear the truth. Trying to inform the public would only hurt Rainbow's credibility
> When Rainbow accuses Blueblood of being an idiot who is abandoning Equestria, Blueblood says he has plans, explains how they will run a slow retreat so everypony can escape to the east and wake up, and he will do everything to save Equestria, and asks Rainbow Dash and the Wonderbolts to secretly prepare. Their air power will be the only thing standing between Chrysalis and Equestria
>
> Blueblood says ponies will not wake up until they see it for themselves and wake up. Blueblood saw it in 1002 during Canterlot Wedding. Rainbow Dash is seeing it now. But the rest of Equestria will not believe anything until the war actually starts. They are too innocent.
>
> Here is where Rainbow Dash decides she will save Equestria so that everypony else does not have to see the horrors that she is seeing on this slaver island. She will protect her friends' innocence by being the hero, like Daring Do
>
> Rainbow Dash finally finds Reni and Minette on the island and asks them to help the Wonderbolts develop planes for elite pegasi fliers

---
### SCENE 34.19: Burnout
> **Synopsis:** Prince Blueblood and General Mudbeak arrive in Ain Trotgourait so that Mudbeak can give Blueblood some hoofs-on experience on managing the logistics of a razed city, to prepare Blueblood for reality
> Blueblood sees Twilight burned out from spending weeks casting spells, unable to put much of a dent into the scale of the problem
> He advises Twilight she must adopt a scaling approach to the problem. Twilight is humbled
> Blueblood then sees Fizzlepop's logistics plans for fixing the city. She explains she once ran the Storm King's logistics which is why she was "The Storm King's Right Hoof" and is now using her skills for charity. He asks Fizzlepop to come back to Equestria and help him and Mudbeak review the military plans for a changeling conflict. She initially refuses, saying she doesn't want to do anything related to warfare anymore, and he tells her she needs to come to peace with her past (open up your eyes) instead of put a bandaid and pretend the wound doesn't exist
> Twilight asks Blueblood what changed, since she only remembers the silly colt obssessed with hedge mazes and then the royal fop who screwed Rarity
> Blueblood says "the pink one" (Actia Pagala) woke him up to the reality of the world
> Twilight takes Blueblood's advice and goes back to Equestria with her head down but full of plans, promising Barekzamal that she will be back with a solution

---
### SCENE 34.20: The Dotted Line Report
> **Synopsis:** Applejack went to a rodeo and a harvest festival at Tall Tale, as if everything is back to normal
> Then Prince Blueblood releases The Dotted Line Report to Celestia, and announces the volunteer Equestrian Royal Army
> Applejack enlists
> Luna asks Applejack to be a general but she refuses, saying she will start as a recruit just like everypony else
>
> Cadance reveals the Friendship Shields (crystal heart replicas)
>
> Pinkie starts as a general right away at Luna's request to raise morale, and is naturally an artillery master

---
### SCENE 34.21: Developing Advanced Planes
> **Synopsis:** The griffonian planes are too sluggish, Rainbow wants something more nimble like Reni's plane
> They also contact Aquileian unicorns and griffons at the Crystal Empire and Star Energy in Tall Tale to develop engines
> This leads to the "Wonderbolts Spitfire" - Spitfire takes the lead on designing the plane, having internalized Theodor Kemmerich's lesson about responsibility
> Rainbow Dash spends day and night practicing flying and shooting
>
> During a visit to the Crystal Empire, Rainbow Dash meets up with Spike, who is organizing the crystal shipments for Ain Trotgourait
> Rainbow Dash meets Thorax and learns even more of the horrors of the changeling war machine
> Shining Armor and Cadance have no delusions either

---
### SCENE 34.22: Developing Magical Supply Organization
> **Synopsis:** The core problem in Ain Trotgourait is the sheer number of waterways that need water filtration. Twilight was going around to them all and casting the spell but it would wear off and she couldn't be everywhere at once
> She turns her water filtration spell into a spell matrix, planning to deploy one at every waterway that Fizzlepop mapped out and Twilight and Starlight go to
> She wants to stockpile crystals from the Crystal Empire's mines at each of the locations, but they can't afford to keep a surplus. Also, the highest grade power crystals can be stolen and put on the black market
>
> Twilight proposes her idea, called Magical Supply Organization, which is a teleportation network run on teleportation spell matrices, utilizing Aquileian tech and her own extensive mastery of teleporting
> Whenever a filtration spell matrix runs out of magic, a guard can request a new power crystal from the supply hub, which is teleported over. They don't have to worry about stockpiling or transporting power crystals that can get stolen. Every filtration spell matrix gets a local guard to watch over it and request power crystals
> The same system is setup at a central hospital that has Griffonian industrial medicine and Tzinacatl medicinal herbs. Smaller clinics across the ruined city can request what they need without having to guard anything from thieves
> The Aquileian artisans in the Crystal Empire copy many variations of Twilight's teleportation spell matrices, and crystals are taken from the mines to power the system
>
> Then Prince Blueblood adds Twilight's system to his extensive war plans
>
> Barekzamal says the system is working excellently and thanks Twilight again for the crystal shipments, saying the citizens of Warzena will repay their debt. Twilight writes back saying it's a gift, no repayment necessary except spreading harmony
>
> Twilight continues to improve her teleportation network leading up to the war, feeling like she is fulfilling her duty and doing her part to support all the ponies

---
### SCENE 34.23: Applejack accepts Generalship
> **Synopsis:** Applejack masters the machine gun quickly and teaches others. The other ponies look up to her, not just for being the element of honesty but for being an encouraging teacher
> Luna again asks Applejack to be a general, this time being a proper promotion based on army merit, and Applejack accepts. She genuinely feels good about defending Equestria

---
